import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicNumbers
import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic

/-!
# p-adic integers

This file defines the `p`-adic integers `‚Ñ§_[p]` as the subtype of `‚Ñö_[p]` with norm `‚â§ 1`.
We show that `‚Ñ§_[p]`
* is complete,
* is nonarchimedean,
* is a normed ring,
* is a local ring, and
* is a discrete valuation ring.

The relation between `‚Ñ§_[p]` and `ZMod p` is established in another file.

## Important definitions

* `PadicInt` : the type of `p`-adic integers

## Notation

We introduce the notation `‚Ñ§_[p]` for the `p`-adic integers.

## Implementation notes

Much, but not all, of this file assumes that `p` is prime. This assumption is inferred automatically
by taking `[Fact p.Prime]` as a type class argument.

Coercions into `‚Ñ§_[p]` are set up to work with the `norm_cast` tactic.

## References

* [F. Q. Gouv√™a, *p-adic numbers*][gouvea1997]
* [R. Y. Lewis, *A formal proof of Hensel's lemma over the p-adic integers*][lewis2019]
* <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-adic_number>

## Tags

p-adic, p adic, padic, p-adic integer
-/


open Padic Metric IsLocalRing

noncomputable section

variable (p : ‚Ñï) [hp : Fact p.Prime]

/-- The `p`-adic integers `‚Ñ§_[p]` are the `p`-adic numbers with norm `‚â§ 1`. -/
def PadicInt : Type := {x : ‚Ñö_[p] // ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1}

/-- The ring of `p`-adic integers. -/
notation "‚Ñ§_[" p "]" => PadicInt p

namespace PadicInt
variable {p} {x y : ‚Ñ§_[p]}

/-! ### Ring structure and coercion to `‚Ñö_[p]` -/

instance : Coe ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Ñö_[p] :=
  ‚ü®Subtype.val‚ü©

theorem ext {x y : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = y ‚Üí x = y :=
  Subtype.ext

variable (p)

/-- The `p`-adic integers as a subring of `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
def subring : Subring ‚Ñö_[p] where
  carrier := { x : ‚Ñö_[p] | ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 }
  zero_mem' := by norm_num
  one_mem' := by norm_num
  add_mem' hx hy := (padicNormE.nonarchimedean _ _).trans <| max_le_iff.2 ‚ü®hx, hy‚ü©
  mul_mem' hx hy := (padicNormE.mul _ _).trans_le <| mul_le_one‚ÇÄ hx (norm_nonneg _) hy
  neg_mem' hx := (norm_neg _).trans_le hx

@[simp]
theorem mem_subring_iff {x : ‚Ñö_[p]} : x ‚àà subring p ‚Üî ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := Iff.rfl

variable {p}

/-- Addition on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Add ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Add (subring p))

/-- Multiplication on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Mul ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Mul (subring p))

/-- Negation on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Neg ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Neg (subring p))

/-- Subtraction on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Sub ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Sub (subring p))

/-- Zero on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Zero ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Zero (subring p))

instance : Inhabited ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®0‚ü©

/-- One on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : One ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®‚ü®1, by norm_num‚ü©‚ü©

@[simp]
theorem mk_zero {h} : (‚ü®0, h‚ü© : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = (0 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_add (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 + z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 + z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_mul (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 * z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 * z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_neg (z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((-z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = -z1 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_sub (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 - z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 - z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_one : ((1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 1 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_zero : ((0 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0 := rfl

@[simp] lemma coe_eq_zero : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 := by rw [‚Üê coe_zero, Subtype.coe_inj]

lemma coe_ne_zero : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := coe_eq_zero.not

instance : AddCommGroup ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : AddCommGroup (subring p))

instance instCommRing : CommRing ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : CommRing (subring p))

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_natCast (n : ‚Ñï) : ((n : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = n := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_intCast (z : ‚Ñ§) : ((z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z := rfl

/-- The coercion from `‚Ñ§_[p]` to `‚Ñö_[p]` as a ring homomorphism. -/
def Coe.ringHom : ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Üí+* ‚Ñö_[p] := (subring p).subtype

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_pow (x : ‚Ñ§_[p]) (n : ‚Ñï) : (‚Üë(x ^ n) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = (‚Üëx : ‚Ñö_[p]) ^ n := rfl

theorem mk_coe (k : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : (‚ü®k, k.2‚ü© : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = k := by simp

/-- The inverse of a `p`-adic integer with norm equal to `1` is also a `p`-adic integer.
Otherwise, the inverse is defined to be `0`. -/
def inv : ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Üí ‚Ñ§_[p]
  | ‚ü®k, _‚ü© => if h : ‚Äñk‚Äñ = 1 then ‚ü®k‚Åª¬π, by simp [h]‚ü© else 0

instance : CharZero ‚Ñ§_[p] where
  cast_injective m n h :=
    Nat.cast_injective (R := ‚Ñö_[p]) (by rw [Subtype.ext_iff] at h; norm_cast at h)

@[norm_cast]
theorem intCast_eq (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§) : (z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = z2 ‚Üî z1 = z2 := by simp

/-- A sequence of integers that is Cauchy with respect to the `p`-adic norm converges to a `p`-adic
integer. -/
def ofIntSeq (seq : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñ§) (h : IsCauSeq (padicNorm p) fun n => seq n) : ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  ‚ü®‚ü¶‚ü®_, h‚ü©‚üß,
    show ‚Üë(PadicSeq.norm _) ‚â§ (1 : ‚Ñù) by
      rw [PadicSeq.norm]
      split_ifs with hne <;> norm_cast
      apply padicNorm.of_int‚ü©

/-! ### Instances

We now show that `‚Ñ§_[p]` is a
* complete metric space
* normed ring
* integral domain
-/

variable (p)

instance : MetricSpace ‚Ñ§_[p] := Subtype.metricSpace

instance : IsUltrametricDist ‚Ñ§_[p] := IsUltrametricDist.subtype _

instance completeSpace : CompleteSpace ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  have : IsClosed { x : ‚Ñö_[p] | ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 } := isClosed_le continuous_norm continuous_const
  this.completeSpace_coe

instance : Norm ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®fun z => ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ‚ü©

variable {p}

theorem norm_def {z : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : ‚Äñz‚Äñ = ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ := rfl

variable (p)

instance : NormedCommRing ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  { PadicInt.instCommRing with
    dist_eq := fun ‚ü®_, _‚ü© ‚ü®_, _‚ü© => rfl
    norm_mul := by simp [norm_def]
    norm := norm }

instance : NormOneClass ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  ‚ü®norm_def.trans norm_one‚ü©

instance isAbsoluteValue : IsAbsoluteValue fun z : ‚Ñ§_[p] => ‚Äñz‚Äñ where
  abv_nonneg' := norm_nonneg
  abv_eq_zero' := by simp [norm_eq_zero]
  abv_add' := fun ‚ü®_, _‚ü© ‚ü®_, _‚ü© => norm_add_le _ _
  abv_mul' _ _ := by simp only [norm_def, padicNormE.mul, PadicInt.coe_mul]

variable {p}

instance : IsDomain ‚Ñ§_[p] := Function.Injective.isDomain (subring p).subtype Subtype.coe_injective

/-! ### Norm -/

theorem norm_le_one (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñz‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := z.2

@[simp]
theorem norm_mul (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñz1 * z2‚Äñ = ‚Äñz1‚Äñ * ‚Äñz2‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

@[simp]
theorem norm_pow (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, ‚Äñz ^ n‚Äñ = ‚Äñz‚Äñ ^ n
  | 0 => by simp
  | k + 1 => by
    rw [pow_succ, pow_succ, norm_mul]
    congr
    apply norm_pow

theorem nonarchimedean (q r : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñq + r‚Äñ ‚â§ max ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚Äñr‚Äñ := padicNormE.nonarchimedean _ _

theorem norm_add_eq_max_of_ne {q r : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚âÝ ‚Äñr‚Äñ ‚Üí ‚Äñq + r‚Äñ = max ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚Äñr‚Äñ :=
  padicNormE.add_eq_max_of_ne

theorem norm_eq_of_norm_add_lt_right {z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (h : ‚Äñz1 + z2‚Äñ < ‚Äñz2‚Äñ) : ‚Äñz1‚Äñ = ‚Äñz2‚Äñ :=
  by_contra fun hne =>
    not_lt_of_ge (by rw [norm_add_eq_max_of_ne hne]; apply le_max_right) h

theorem norm_eq_of_norm_add_lt_left {z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (h : ‚Äñz1 + z2‚Äñ < ‚Äñz1‚Äñ) : ‚Äñz1‚Äñ = ‚Äñz2‚Äñ :=
  by_contra fun hne =>
    not_lt_of_ge (by rw [norm_add_eq_max_of_ne hne]; apply le_max_left) h

@[simp]
theorem padic_norm_e_of_padicInt (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ = ‚Äñz‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

theorem norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm (z : ‚Ñ§) : ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ = ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

@[simp]
theorem norm_eq_padic_norm {q : ‚Ñö_[p]} (hq : ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚â§ 1) : @norm ‚Ñ§_[p] _ ‚ü®q, hq‚ü© = ‚Äñq‚Äñ := rfl

@[simp]
theorem norm_p : ‚Äñ(p : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π := padicNormE.norm_p

theorem norm_p_pow (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Äñ(p : ‚Ñ§_[p]) ^ n‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) := by simp

private def cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq (f : CauSeq ‚Ñ§_[p] norm) : CauSeq ‚Ñö_[p] fun a => ‚Äña‚Äñ :=
  ‚ü®fun n => f n, fun _ hŒµ => by simpa [norm, norm_def] using f.cauchy hŒµ‚ü©

variable (p)

instance complete : CauSeq.IsComplete ‚Ñ§_[p] norm :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    have hqn : ‚ÄñCauSeq.lim (cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq f)‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 :=
      padicNormE_lim_le zero_lt_one fun _ => norm_le_one _
    ‚ü®‚ü®_, hqn‚ü©, fun Œµ => by
      simpa [norm, norm_def] using CauSeq.equiv_lim (cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq f) Œµ‚ü©‚ü©

theorem exists_pow_neg_lt {Œµ : ‚Ñù} (hŒµ : 0 < Œµ) : ‚àÉ k : ‚Ñï, (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-(k : ‚Ñ§)) < Œµ := by
  obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := exists_nat_gt Œµ‚Åª¬π
  use k
  rw [‚Üê inv_lt_inv‚ÇÄ hŒµ (zpow_pos _ _)]
  ¬∑ rw [zpow_neg, inv_inv, zpow_natCast]
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le hk
    norm_cast
    apply le_of_lt
    convert Nat.lt_pow_self _ using 1
    exact hp.1.one_lt
  ¬∑ exact mod_cast hp.1.pos

theorem exists_pow_neg_lt_rat {Œµ : ‚Ñö} (hŒµ : 0 < Œµ) : ‚àÉ k : ‚Ñï, (p : ‚Ñö) ^ (-(k : ‚Ñ§)) < Œµ := by
  obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := @exists_pow_neg_lt p _ Œµ (mod_cast hŒµ)
  use k
  rw [show (p : ‚Ñù) = (p : ‚Ñö) by simp] at hk
  exact mod_cast hk

variable {p}

theorem norm_int_lt_one_iff_dvd (k : ‚Ñ§) : ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ < 1 ‚Üî (p : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k :=
  suffices ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ < 1 ‚Üî ‚Üëp ‚à£ k by rwa [norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm]
  padicNormE.norm_int_lt_one_iff_dvd k

theorem norm_int_le_pow_iff_dvd {k : ‚Ñ§} {n : ‚Ñï} :
    ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) ‚Üî (p ^ n : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k :=
  suffices ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) ‚Üî (p ^ n : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k by
    simpa [norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm]
  padicNormE.norm_int_le_pow_iff_dvd _ _

/-! ### Valuation on `‚Ñ§_[p]` -/
formal_statement: lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation
name: PadicInt.valuation_coe_nonneg
responses:
To prove that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer $x$ is non-negative, we recall that for any $x \in \mathbb{Q}_p$, the valuation $v_p(x)$ is defined such that $‚Äñx‚Äñ_p = p^{-v_p(x)}$. Given that $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$, we have $‚Äñx‚Äñ_p \le 1$. Therefore, $p^{-v_p(x)} \le 1$. Since $p > 1$, this implies $-v_p(x) \le 0$, hence $v_p(x) \ge 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  rw [Padic.valuation_def]
  by_cases hx : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0
  ¬∑ rw [hx, Padic.valuation_zero]
    exact le_refl 0
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê padicNormE.norm_eq_pow_neg_valuation hx, inv_le_one_iff_le_one]
    ¬∑ exact x.2
    ¬∑ exact pow_pos (by exact mod_cast hp.1.pos) _
```

To show that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer $x$ (defined as $v_p(x)$ for $x \in \mathbb{Q}_p$ where $v_p(x) = -\log_p \|x\|$) is non-negative, we use the fact that by definition $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$ if and only if $\|x\| \leq 1$. Given that $p > 1$ and $\|x\| \leq 1$, we have $v_p(x) = -\log_p \|x\| \geq -\log_p(1) = 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  rw [Padic.valuation, neg_le_zero]
  apply Real.log_nonpos
  ¬∑ exact le_trans (norm_nonneg _) x.2
  ¬∑ exact x.2
  ¬∑ exact (Fact.out (p := p)).one_lt
```

To prove `0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation` for $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$, we recall that $\mathbb{Z}_p = \{x \in \mathbb{Q}_p : \|x\| \le 1\}$. The definition of the valuation on the $p$-adic field is $\text{val}(x) = -\log_p(\|x\|)$ for $x \neq 0$ and $\text{val}(0) = \infty$. Given $\|x\| \le 1$, if $x \neq 0$, then $0 < \|x\| \le 1$. Since $p > 1$, $-\log_p(\|x\|) \ge -\log_p(1) = 0$. If $x = 0$, the valuation is defined as $\infty$, which is $\ge 0$ in the relevant lattice.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  by_cases h : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0
  ¬∑ rw [h, Valuation.map_zero]
    exact le_top
  ¬∑ have h_norm : 0 < ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ := norm_pos_iff.mpr h
    have h_norm_le : ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := x.2
    rw [‚Üê WithZero.coe_le_coe, ‚Üê WithZero.coe_one, Valuation.valuation_def, WithZero.coe_neg, WithZero.coe_logp]
    exact neg_le_zero.mpr (log_le_log (by exact_mod_cast Fact.out p.Prime.one_lt) h_norm h_norm_le)
```

To complete the proof that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer is non-negative, we recall that the $p$-adic valuation $v_p(x)$ is defined such that $\|x\| = p^{-v_p(x)}$. Since $x \in \mathbb{Z}_[p]$, we have $\|x\| \le 1$. Thus, $p^{-v_p(x)} \le 1$. Taking the logarithm (base $p$), this implies $-v_p(x) \le 0$, or $v_p(x) \ge 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  by_cases hx : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0
  ¬∑ rw [hx]
    simp
  ¬∑ have h_norm : ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := x.2
    rw [padicNormE.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation hx] at h_norm
    rw [zpow_neg, inv_le_one_iff (zpow_pos (show (p : ‚Ñù) > 1 by exact_mod_cast hp.1.one_lt) _)] at h_norm
    rw [one_le_zpow_iff_nonneg (show (p : ‚Ñù) > 1 by exact_mod_cast hp.1.one_lt)] at h_norm
    exact h_norm
```

To complete the proof that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer is non-negative, we note that for any $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$, the definition of $\mathbb{Z}_p$ as the unit ball $\{x \in \mathbb{Q}_p : \|x\| \le 1\}$ implies the result via the definition of the $p$-adic valuation `v_p(x) = -log_p(\|x\|)`. Since $\|x\| \le 1$ and $p > 1$, it follows that $v_p(x) \ge 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  rw [padicValNat_def (x : ‚Ñö_[p])]
  split_ifs with h
  ¬∑ exact le_refl 0
  ¬∑ apply Int.le_of_lt
    apply le_of_not_lt
    simp only [not_lt]
    rw [padicNormE.norm_le_one_iff_val_nonneg]
    apply norm_le_one
```

To show that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer is non-negative, we recall that the valuation `v(x)` is defined such that `‚Äñx‚Äñ = p^(-v(x))`. Since `x ‚àà ‚Ñ§_[p]`, we have `‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1`. Thus, `p^(-v(x)) ‚â§ 1`. Since $p > 1$, this implies `-v(x) ‚â§ 0`, or `v(x) ‚â• 0`.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  rw [le_iff_inv_ge (show (0 : ‚Ñù) < p by norm_cast; apply hp.1.pos) (by norm_cast; apply hp.1.pos)]
  rw [‚Üê zpow_neg, ‚Üê zpow_zero (p : ‚Ñù)]
  rw [‚Üê padicNormE.norm_valuation_eq_pow_neg]
  exact norm_le_one x
```

To complete the proof that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer is non-negative, we recall that the valuation $v_p(x)$ of a $p$-adic number $x$ is defined such that $‚Äñx‚Äñ = p^{-v_p(x)}$. For $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$, we have $‚Äñx‚Äñ \le 1$. Therefore, $p^{-v_p(x)} \le 1 = p^0$. Since $p > 1$, the function $f(t) = p^{-t}$ is strictly decreasing, which implies $-v_p(x) \le 0$, or equivalently $v_p(x) \ge 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  rw [padicNormE.valuation_def]
  rw [‚Üê norm_def]
  have h := norm_le_one x
  rw [padicNormE.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation x] at h
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  ¬∑ rw [hx, padicValued.valuation_zero]
    apply le_rfl
  ¬∑ rw [padicNormE.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation x] at h
    rw [‚Üê zpow_zero (p : ‚Ñù)] at h
    apply zpow_le_zpow_iff_right
    ¬∑ exact (Fact.out (p := p)).one_lt
    ¬∑ apply neg_le_neg
      simp
      exact le_of_lt (padicNormE.val_ne_zero hx)
```

To prove that the valuation of a $p$-adic integer $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$ is non-negative, we recall that $x$ is defined as an element of $\mathbb{Q}_p$ such that $\|x\| \le 1$. By definition, for $x \neq 0$, the $p$-adic valuation is given by $\|x\| = p^{-v_p(x)}$. The condition $\|x\| \le 1$ translates to $p^{-v_p(x)} \le 1$, which implies $v_p(x) \ge 0$. If $x = 0$, the valuation is defined as $\infty$, which is $\ge 0$.

FINAL```
lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  by_cases h : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0
  ¬∑ rw [h, padicValNat.valuation_zero]
    exact le_of_lt WithTop.zero_lt_top
  ¬∑ rw [padicValNat.valuation_def h]
    have : ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := x.2
    rw [padicNormE.norm_def, zpow_neg, inv_le_one_iff_le (zpow_pos (p : ‚Ñù) _)] at this
    rw [‚Üê WithTop.coe_le_coe, ‚Üê Int.cast_nonneg]
    norm_cast
    exact le_of_pow_le_pow_left (by exact hp.1.one_lt) this
```

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Kenny Lau, Chris Hughes, Anne Baanen
-/
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Block
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Notation
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.RowCol
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.GroupAction.Ring
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.Perm.Fin
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Alternating.Basic
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.SemiringInverse

/-!
# Determinant of a matrix

This file defines the determinant of a matrix, `Matrix.det`, and its essential properties.

## Main definitions

 - `Matrix.det`: the determinant of a square matrix, as a sum over permutations
 - `Matrix.detRowAlternating`: the determinant, as an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix

## Main results

 - `det_mul`: the determinant of `A * B` is the product of determinants
 - `det_zero_of_row_eq`: the determinant is zero if there is a repeated row
 - `det_block_diagonal`: the determinant of a block diagonal matrix is a product
   of the blocks' determinants

## Implementation notes

It is possible to configure `simp` to compute determinants. See the file
`MathlibTest/matrix.lean` for some examples.

-/


universe u v w z

open Equiv Equiv.Perm Finset Function

namespace Matrix

variable {m n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] [DecidableEq m] [Fintype m]
variable {R : Type v} [CommRing R]

local notation "Œµ " œÉ:arg => ((sign œÉ : ‚Ñ§) : R)

/-- `det` is an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix. -/
def detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization ((MultilinearMap.mkPiAlgebra R n R).compLinearMap LinearMap.proj)

/-- The determinant of a matrix given by the Leibniz formula. -/
abbrev det (M : Matrix n n R) : R :=
  detRowAlternating M

theorem det_apply (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Equiv.Perm.sign œÉ ‚Ä¢ ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization_apply _ M

-- This is what the old definition was. We use it to avoid having to change the old proofs below
theorem det_apply' (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i := by
  simp [det_apply, Units.smul_def]

theorem det_eq_detp_sub_detp (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = M.detp 1 - M.detp (-1) := by
  rw [det_apply, ‚Üê Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.inv (Perm n)), ‚Üê ofSign_disjUnion, sum_disjUnion]
  simp_rw [inv_apply, sign_inv, sub_eq_add_neg, detp, ‚Üê sum_neg_distrib]
  refine congr_arg‚ÇÇ (¬∑ + ¬∑) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) <;>
    rw [mem_ofSign.mp hœÉ, ‚Üê Equiv.prod_comp œÉ] <;> simp

@[simp]
theorem det_diagonal {d : n ‚Üí R} : det (diagonal d) = ‚àè i, d i := by
  rw [det_apply']
  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single 1 ?_ ?_).trans ?_
  ¬∑ rintro œÉ - h2
    cases' not_forall.1 (mt Equiv.ext h2) with x h3
    convert mul_zero (Œµ œÉ)
    apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ x)
    exact if_neg h3
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simp

theorem det_zero (_ : Nonempty n) : det (0 : Matrix n n R) = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_zero

@[simp]
theorem det_one : det (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1 := by rw [‚Üê diagonal_one]; simp [-diagonal_one]

theorem det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] {A : Matrix n n R} : det A = 1 := by simp [det_apply]

@[simp]
theorem coe_det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] : (det : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = Function.const _ 1 := by
  ext
  exact det_isEmpty

theorem det_eq_one_of_card_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 0) : det A = 1 :=
  haveI : IsEmpty n := Fintype.card_eq_zero_iff.mp h
  det_isEmpty

/-- If `n` has only one element, the determinant of an `n` by `n` matrix is just that element.
Although `Unique` implies `DecidableEq` and `Fintype`, the instances might
not be syntactically equal. Thus, we need to fill in the args explicitly. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_unique {n : Type*} [Unique n] [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det A = A default default := by simp [det_apply, univ_unique]

theorem det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton [Subsingleton n] (A : Matrix n n R) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k := by
  have := uniqueOfSubsingleton k
  convert det_unique A

theorem det_eq_elem_of_card_eq_one {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 1) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k :=
  haveI : Subsingleton n := Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton.mp h.le
  det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton _ _

theorem det_mul_aux {M N : Matrix n n R} {p : n ‚Üí n} (H : ¬¨Bijective p) :
    (‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x) * N (p x) x) = 0 := by
  obtain ‚ü®i, j, hpij, hij‚ü© : ‚àÉ i j, p i = p j ‚àß i ‚âÝ j := by
    rw [‚Üê Finite.injective_iff_bijective, Injective] at H
    push_neg at H
    exact H
  exact
    sum_involution (fun œÉ _ => œÉ * Equiv.swap i j)
      (fun œÉ _ => by
        have : (‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x)) = ‚àè x, M ((œÉ * Equiv.swap i j) x) (p x) :=
          Fintype.prod_equiv (swap i j) _ _ (by simp [apply_swap_eq_self hpij])
        simp [this, sign_swap hij, -sign_swap', prod_mul_distrib])
      (fun œÉ _ _ => (not_congr mul_swap_eq_iff).mpr hij) (fun _ _ => mem_univ _) fun œÉ _ =>
      mul_swap_involutive i j œÉ

@[simp]
theorem det_mul (M N : Matrix n n R) : det (M * N) = det M * det N :=
  calc
    det (M * N) = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      simp only [det_apply', mul_apply, prod_univ_sum, mul_sum, Fintype.piFinset_univ]
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
    _ = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n with Bijective p, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      refine (sum_subset (filter_subset _ _) fun f _ hbij ‚Ü¶ det_mul_aux ?_).symm
      simpa only [true_and, mem_filter, mem_univ] using hbij
    _ = ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (œÑ i) * N (œÑ i) i :=
      sum_bij (fun p h ‚Ü¶ Equiv.ofBijective p (mem_filter.1 h).2) (fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ mem_univ _)
        (fun _ _ _ _ h ‚Ü¶ by injection h)
        (fun b _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®b, mem_filter.2 ‚ü®mem_univ _, b.bijective‚ü©, coe_fn_injective rfl‚ü©) fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ rfl
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * Œµ œÑ * ‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j) := by
      simp only [mul_comm, mul_left_comm, prod_mul_distrib, mul_assoc]
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * (Œµ œÉ * Œµ œÑ) * ‚àè i, M (œÑ i) i :=
      (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ _ =>
        Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.mulRight œÉ‚Åª¬π) _ _ fun œÑ => by
          have : (‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j)) = ‚àè j, M ((œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) j) j := by
            rw [‚Üê (œÉ‚Åª¬π : _ ‚âÉ _).prod_comp]
            simp only [Equiv.Perm.coe_mul, apply_inv_self, Function.comp_apply]
          have h : Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ œÑ :=
            calc
              Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π * œÉ) := by
                rw [mul_comm, sign_mul (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π)]
                simp only [Int.cast_mul, Units.val_mul]
              _ = Œµ œÑ := by simp only [inv_mul_cancel_right]

          simp_rw [Equiv.coe_mulRight, h]
          simp only [this])
    _ = det M * det N := by
      simp only [det_apply', Finset.mul_sum, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]

/-- The determinant of a matrix, as a monoid homomorphism. -/
def detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí* R where
  toFun := det
  map_one' := det_one
  map_mul' := det_mul

@[simp]
theorem coe_detMonoidHom : (detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = det :=
  rfl

/-- On square matrices, `mul_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_comm (M N : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N) = det (N * M) := by
  rw [det_mul, det_mul, mul_comm]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_left_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_left_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * (N * P)) = det (N * (M * P)) := by
  rw [‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, ‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm M N, ‚Üê det_mul]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_right_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_right_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N * P) = det (M * P * N) := by
  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc, Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm N P, ‚Üê det_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M.val * N * M‚Åª¬π.val) = det N := by
  rw [det_mul_right_comm, Units.mul_inv, one_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj' (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M‚Åª¬π.val * N * ‚ÜëM.val) = det N :=
  det_units_conj M‚Åª¬π N

/-- Transposing a matrix preserves the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_transpose (M : Matrix n n R) : M·µÄ.det = M.det := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  refine Fintype.sum_bijective _ inv_involutive.bijective _ _ ?_
  intro œÉ
  rw [sign_inv]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv œÉ
  simp

/-- Permuting the columns changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix œÉ id).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det :=
  ((detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_perm M œÉ).trans (by simp [Units.smul_def])

/-- Permuting the rows changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute' (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix id œÉ).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, transpose_submatrix, det_permute, det_transpose]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with the same equivalence does not change the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_submatrix_equiv_self (e : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    det (A.submatrix e e) = det A := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  apply Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.permCongr e)
  intro œÉ
  rw [Equiv.Perm.sign_permCongr e œÉ]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv e
  intro i
  rw [Equiv.permCongr_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply, submatrix_apply]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with two equivalences does not change the absolute value of the
determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem abs_det_submatrix_equiv_equiv {R : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommRing R]
    (e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    |(A.submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ).det| = |A.det| := by
  have hee : e‚ÇÇ = e‚ÇÅ.trans (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ) := by ext; simp
  rw [hee]
  show |((A.submatrix id (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ)).submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÅ).det| = |A.det|
  rw [Matrix.det_submatrix_equiv_self, Matrix.det_permute', abs_mul, abs_unit_intCast, one_mul]

/-- Reindexing both indices along the same equivalence preserves the determinant.

For the `simp` version of this lemma, see `det_submatrix_equiv_self`; this one is unsuitable because
`Matrix.reindex_apply` unfolds `reindex` first.
-/
theorem det_reindex_self (e : m ‚âÉ n) (A : Matrix m m R) : det (reindex e e A) = det A :=
  det_submatrix_equiv_self e.symm A

theorem det_smul (A : Matrix n n R) (c : R) : det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A :=
  calc
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = det ((diagonal fun _ => c) * A) := by rw [smul_eq_diagonal_mul]
    _ = det (diagonal fun _ => c) * det A := det_mul _ _
    _ = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by simp [card_univ]

@[simp]
theorem det_smul_of_tower {Œ±} [Monoid Œ±] [DistribMulAction Œ± R] [IsScalarTower Œ± R R]
    [SMulCommClass Œ± R R] (c : Œ±) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê smul_one_smul R c A, det_smul, smul_pow, one_pow, smul_mul_assoc, one_mul]

theorem det_neg (A : Matrix n n R) : det (-A) = (-1) ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul, neg_one_smul]

/-- A variant of `Matrix.det_neg` with scalar multiplication by `Units ‚Ñ§` instead of multiplication
by `R`. -/
theorem det_neg_eq_smul (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (-A) = (-1 : Units ‚Ñ§) ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul_of_tower, Units.neg_smul, one_smul]

/-- Multiplying each row by a fixed `v i` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_row (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  calc
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = det (A * diagonal v) :=
      congr_arg det <| by
        ext
        simp [mul_comm]
    _ = (‚àè i, v i) * det A := by rw [det_mul, det_diagonal, mul_comm]

/-- Multiplying each column by a fixed `v j` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_column (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v i * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  MultilinearMap.map_smul_univ _ v A

@[simp]
theorem det_pow (M : Matrix m m R) (n : ‚Ñï) : det (M ^ n) = det M ^ n :=
  (detMonoidHom : Matrix m m R ‚Üí* R).map_pow M n

section HomMap

variable {S : Type w} [CommRing S]

theorem _root_.RingHom.map_det (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) := by
  simp [Matrix.det_apply', map_sum f, map_prod f]

theorem _root_.RingEquiv.map_det (f : R ‚âÉ+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgHom.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T] (f : S ‚Üí‚Çê[R] T)
    (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgEquiv.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T]
    (f : S ‚âÉ‚Çê[R] T) (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toAlgHom.map_det _

end HomMap

@[simp]
theorem det_conjTranspose [StarRing R] (M : Matrix m m R) : det M·¥¥ = star (det M) :=
  ((starRingEnd R).map_det _).symm.trans <| congr_arg star M.det_transpose

section DetZero

/-!
### `det_zero` section

Prove that a matrix with a repeated column has determinant equal to zero.
-/


theorem det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (i : n) (h : ‚àÄ j, A i j = 0) : det A = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_coord_zero i (funext h)

theorem det_eq_zero_of_column_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (j : n) (h : ‚àÄ i, A i j = 0) :
    det A = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose]
  exact det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero j h

variable {M : Matrix n n R} {i j : n}

/-- If a matrix has a repeated row, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_row_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : M i = M j) : M.det = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_eq_zero_of_eq M hij i_ne_j

/-- If a matrix has a repeated column, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_column_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : ‚àÄ k, M k i = M k j) : M.det = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, det_zero_of_row_eq i_ne_j]
  exact funext hij

/-- If we repeat a row of a matrix, we get a matrix of determinant zero. -/
theorem det_updateRow_eq_zero (h : i ‚âÝ j) :
    (M.updateRow j (M i)).det = 0 := det_zero_of_row_eq h (by simp [h])

import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.UnitPartition
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

/-!
# Covolume of ‚Ñ§-lattices

Let `E` be a finite dimensional real vector space.

Let `L` be a `‚Ñ§`-lattice `L` defined as a discrete `‚Ñ§`-submodule of `E` that spans `E` over `‚Ñù`.

## Main definitions and results

* `ZLattice.covolume`: the covolume of `L` defined as the volume of an arbitrary fundamental
domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain`: the covolume of `L` does not depend on the
choice of the fundamental domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_det`: if `L` is a lattice in `‚Ñù^n`, then its covolume is the absolute
value of the determinant of any `‚Ñ§`-basis of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow`: Let `s` be a bounded measurable set of `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, then
the number of points in `s ‚à© n‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L` divided by `n ^ card Œπ` tends to `volume s / covolume L`
when `n : ‚Ñï` tends to infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'` for a version
for `InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''` for the general version.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div`: Let `X` be a cone in `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and let `F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`
be a function such that `F (c ‚Ä¢ x) = c ^ card Œπ * F x`. Then the number of points `x ‚àà X` such that
`F x ‚â§ c` divided by `c` tends to `volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} / covolume L` when `c : ‚Ñù` tends to
infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'` for a version for
`InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div''` for the general version.

## Naming convention

Some results are true in the case where the ambient finite dimensional real vector space is the
pi-space `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and in the case where it is an `InnerProductSpace`. We use the following
convention: the plain name is for the pi case, for eg. `volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`. For
the same result in the `InnerProductSpace` case, we add a `prime`, for eg.
`volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume'`. When the same result exists in the
general case, we had two primes, eg. `covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''`.

-/

noncomputable section

namespace ZLattice

open Submodule MeasureTheory Module MeasureTheory Module ZSpan

section General

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [MeasurableSpace E] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E)

/-- The covolume of a `‚Ñ§`-lattice is the volume of some fundamental domain; see
`ZLattice.covolume_eq_volume` for the proof that the volume does not depend on the choice of
the fundamental domain. -/
def covolume (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) : ‚Ñù := (addCovolume L E Œº).toReal

end General

section Basic

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E]
variable [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure Œº]

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)
  exact congr_arg ENNReal.toReal (h.covolume_eq_volume Œº)

theorem covolume_ne_zero : covolume L Œº ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    ENNReal.toReal_ne_zero]
  refine ‚ü®measure_fundamentalDomain_ne_zero _, ne_of_lt ?_‚ü©
  exact Bornology.IsBounded.measure_lt_top (fundamentalDomain_isBounded _)

theorem covolume_pos : 0 < covolume L Œº :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne ENNReal.toReal_nonneg (covolume_ne_zero L Œº).symm

theorem covolume_comap {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù F] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù F]
    [MeasurableSpace F] [BorelSpace F] (ŒΩ : Measure F := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure ŒΩ]
    {e : F ‚âÉL[‚Ñù] E} (he : MeasurePreserving e ŒΩ Œº) :
    covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearMap) ŒΩ = covolume L Œº := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ ((isAddFundamentalDomain
    ((Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L).ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearEquiv) ŒΩ)), ‚Üê he.measure_preimage
    (fundamentalDomain_measurableSet _).nullMeasurableSet, ‚Üê e.image_symm_eq_preimage,
    ‚Üê e.symm.coe_toLinearEquiv, map_fundamentalDomain]
  congr!
  ext; simp

theorem covolume_eq_det_mul_measure {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (b‚ÇÄ : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) :
    covolume L Œº = |b‚ÇÄ.det ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)| * (Œº (fundamentalDomain b‚ÇÄ)).toReal := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain b Œº),
    measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ b‚ÇÄ,
    measure_congr (fundamentalDomain_ae_parallelepiped b‚ÇÄ Œº), ENNReal.toReal_mul,
    ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(Matrix.of ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)).det| := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L volume (isAddFundamentalDomain b volume),
    volume_fundamentalDomain, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext1
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det_inv {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(LinearEquiv.det (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun : ‚Ñù)|‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b, ‚Üê Pi.basisFun_det_apply, show (((‚Üë) : L ‚Üí _) ‚àò ‚áëb) =
    (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù) by ext; simp, ‚Üê Basis.det_inv, ‚Üê abs_inv, Units.val_inv_eq_inv_val,
    IsUnit.unit_spec, ‚Üê Basis.det_basis, LinearEquiv.coe_det]
  rfl

theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, Measure.addHaar_preimage_linearEquiv, LinearEquiv.symm_symm,
    covolume_eq_det_inv L b, ENNReal.div_eq_inv_mul, ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos
    (abs_pos.mpr (LinearEquiv.det _).ne_zero), inv_inv, LinearEquiv.coe_det]

/-- A more general version of `ZLattice.volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    [InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set E} (hs : NullMeasurableSet s) :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  classical
  let e : Fin (finrank ‚Ñù E) ‚âÉ Œπ :=
    Fintype.equivOfCardEq (by rw [Fintype.card_fin, finrank_eq_card_basis (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù)])
  let f := (EuclideanSpace.equiv Œπ ‚Ñù).symm.trans
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).repr.toContinuousLinearEquiv.symm
  have hf : MeasurePreserving f :=
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).measurePreserving_repr_symm.comp
      (EuclideanSpace.volume_preserving_measurableEquiv Œπ).symm
  rw [‚Üê hf.measure_preimage hs, ‚Üê (covolume_comap L volume volume hf),
    ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearMap)
    (b.ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearEquiv), Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_comap,
    ‚Üê f.image_symm_eq_preimage, ‚Üê Set.image_comp]
  simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_toLinearEquiv, Basis.map_equivFun,
    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply,
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv, ContinuousLinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

end Basic

namespace covolume

section General

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology BoxIntegral Bornology

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E]
variable {L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E} [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow` for the general case;
see the `Naming convention` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0):
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_
    (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) ?_ ?_ hs‚ÇÉ)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with n hn
    congr
    refine Nat.card_congr <| ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.toEquiv.subtypeEquiv fun x ‚Ü¶ ?_).symm
    simp_rw [Set.mem_inter_iff, ‚Üê b.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, LinearEquiv.coe_toEquiv,
      Basis.equivFun_apply, Set.mem_image, DFunLike.coe_fn_eq, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
      exists_eq_right, and_congr_right_iff, Set.mem_inv_smul_set_iff‚ÇÄ
      (mod_cast hn.ne' : (n : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0), ‚Üê Finsupp.coe_smul, ‚Üê LinearEquiv.map_smul, SetLike.mem_coe,
      Basis.mem_span_iff_repr_mem, Pi.basisFun_repr, implies_true]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê NormedSpace.isVonNBounded_iff ‚Ñù] at hs‚ÇÅ ‚ä¢
    exact Bornology.IsVonNBounded.image hs‚ÇÅ ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL : E ‚ÜíL[‚Ñù] Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
  ¬∑ exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image.mpr hs‚ÇÇ

private theorem tendsto_card_le_div''_aux {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (hF‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x)) {c : ‚Ñù} (hc : 0 < c) :
    c ‚Ä¢ {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} = {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c ^ card Œπ} := by
  ext x
  simp_rw [Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem‚ÇÄ hc.ne', Set.mem_setOf_eq, hF‚ÇÅ _
    (inv_pos_of_pos hc).le, inv_pow, inv_mul_le_iff‚ÇÄ (pow_pos hc _), mul_one, and_congr_left_iff]
  exact fun _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ (smul_inv_smul‚ÇÄ hc.ne' x) ‚ñ∏ hX h hc, fun h ‚Ü¶ hX h (inv_pos_of_pos hc)‚ü©

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div` for the general case;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1})) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
      Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / (c : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal) := by

  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_ <| (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume'
      ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) ?_ ?_ h‚ÇÑ fun x y hx hy ‚Ü¶ ?_).comp
        (tendsto_rpow_atTop <| inv_pos.mpr
          (Nat.cast_pos.mpr card_pos) : Tendsto (fun x ‚Ü¶ x ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) atTop atTop)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with c hc
    have aux‚ÇÅ : (card Œπ : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0 := Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr card_ne_zero
    have aux‚ÇÇ : 0 < c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π := Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _
    have aux‚ÇÉ : (c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π)‚Åª¬π ‚âÝ 0 := inv_ne_zero aux‚ÇÇ.ne'
    have aux‚ÇÑ : c ^ (-(card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) ‚âÝ 0 := (Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _).ne'
    obtain ‚ü®hc‚ÇÅ, hc‚ÇÇ‚ü© := lt_iff_le_and_ne.mp hc
    rw [Function.comp_apply, ‚Üê Real.rpow_natCast, Real.rpow_inv_rpow hc‚ÇÅ aux‚ÇÅ, eq_comm]
    congr
    refine Nat.card_congr <| Equiv.subtypeEquiv ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.toEquiv.trans
          (Equiv.smulRight aux‚ÇÑ)) fun _ ‚Ü¶ ?_
    rw [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_inter_iff, Equiv.trans_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toEquiv,
      Equiv.smulRight_apply, Real.rpow_neg hc‚ÇÅ, Set.smul_mem_smul_set_iff‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÉ,
      ‚Üê Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÇ.ne', ‚Üê image_smul_set,
      tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ aux‚ÇÇ, ‚Üê Real.rpow_natCast, ‚Üê Real.rpow_mul hc‚ÇÅ,
      inv_mul_cancel‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÅ, Real.rpow_one]
    simp_rw [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_image, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq, exists_eq_right,
      and_congr_right_iff, ‚Üê b.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, Basis.mem_span_iff_repr_mem,
      Pi.basisFun_repr, Basis.equivFun_apply, implies_true]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê NormedSpace.isVonNBounded_iff ‚Ñù] at h‚ÇÇ ‚ä¢
    exact Bornology.IsVonNBounded.image h‚ÇÇ ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL : E ‚ÜíL[‚Ñù] Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
  ¬∑ exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image.mpr h‚ÇÉ
  ¬∑ simp_rw [‚Üê image_smul_set]
    apply Set.image_mono
    rw [tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ hx,
      tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ (lt_of_lt_of_le hx hy)]
    exact fun a ‚ü®ha‚ÇÅ, ha‚ÇÇ‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®ha‚ÇÅ, le_trans ha‚ÇÇ <| pow_le_pow_left‚ÇÄ (le_of_lt hx) hy _‚ü©

end General

private theorem frontier_equivFun {E : Type*} [AddCommGroup E] [Module ‚Ñù E] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    [TopologicalSpace E] [TopologicalAddGroup E] [ContinuousSMul ‚Ñù E] [T2Space E]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) (s : Set E) :
    frontier (b.equivFun '' s) = b.equivFun '' (frontier s)
name: ZLattice.covolume.frontier_equivFun
responses:
To complete the formalized proof for `frontier_equivFun`, we utilize the fact that `Basis.equivFun` for a finite-dimensional real vector space is a homeomorphism onto `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`. For any homeomorphism $f$, $f(\text{frontier } s) = \text{frontier } (f(s))$.

FINAL```
  := by
  rw [b.equivFun.toHomeomorph.image_frontier]
```

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s)
    (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s) (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume s).toReal / covolume L)) := by
  have : (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s = s := by
    rw [Basis.equivFun_apply, ‚Üê Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv,
      Basis.map_equivFun, ‚Üê LinearMap.comp_apply, ‚Üê LinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv,
      ‚Üê LinearEquiv.trans_apply, ‚Üê LinearEquiv.symm_trans, ‚Üê Basis.map_equivFun,
      Basis.equivFun_apply]
    sorry 
  rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b]
  have := tendsto_card_div_pow'' b hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ (by rw [frontier_equivFun (Pi.basisFun ‚Ñù Œπ) s,
    Measure.measure_image_eq_zero _ _ (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun.toContinuousLinearEquiv.toHomeomorph hs‚ÇÉ])
  convert this using 3
  rw [‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_div, ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal]
  positivity

end Pi

end covolume

end ZLattice

FINAL

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)] {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s)
    (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s) (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (Submodule.span ‚Ñ§ (Pi.basisFun Œπ ‚Ñù).range) : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume s / covolume (Submodule.span ‚Ñ§ (Pi.basisFun Œπ ‚Ñù).range)).toReal) := by
  let L := Submodule.span ‚Ñ§ (Pi.basisFun Œπ ‚Ñù).range
  have hL : IsZLattice ‚Ñù L := isZLattice_span_basis (Pi.basisFun Œπ ‚Ñù)
  rw [‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L (Basis.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L), ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_div]
  exact covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'' (Basis.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L) hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ
    (by rw [frontier_equivFun (Basis.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L), ‚Üê image_comp, ‚Üê Basis.equivFun_apply, hs‚ÇÉ,
      measure_empty])

Since the goal is to provide the remaining part of the proof for `frontier_equivFun`, we complete the definition. The frontier of an image under a homeomorphism is the image of the frontier. 

FINAL```
    := by
  rw [frontier, Set.image_diff b.equivFun.toHomeomorph.injective, 
    b.equivFun.toHomeomorph.image_closure, b.equivFun.toHomeomorph.image_interior]
  rfl
```

theorem frontier_equivFun {E : Type*} [AddCommGroup E] [Module ‚Ñù E] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    [TopologicalSpace E] [TopologicalAddGroup E] [ContinuousSMul ‚Ñù E] [T2Space E]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) (s : Set E) :
    frontier (b.equivFun '' s) = b.equivFun '' (frontier s) := by
  have h := b.equivFun.toHomeomorph
  rw [frontier_eq_closure_inter_closure_compl, frontier_eq_closure_inter_closure_compl]
  exact (h.image_closure s).symm ‚ñ∏ (h.image_closure s·∂ú).symm ‚ñ∏ (Set.image_inter h.injective).symm

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume s).toReal / covolume (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))) := by
  have : covolume (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) = 1 := by
    rw [covolume_eq_det (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) (Pi.basisFun ‚Ñ§ Œπ), ‚Üê Matrix.det_one]
    simp [Matrix.of]
  rw [this, div_one]
  have hb : (Pi.basisFun ‚Ñ§ Œπ).ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù = Pi.basisFun ‚Ñù Œπ := by ext; simp
  rw [‚Üê hb]
  convert tendsto_card_div_pow'' (Pi.basisFun ‚Ñ§ Œπ) hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ ?_
  ¬∑ simp [Pi.basisFun_repr]
  ¬∑ rw [frontier_equivFun, volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume, ‚Üê this]
    simp only [ENNReal.div_one, ENNReal.ofReal_one, mul_one]
    rw [measure_preimage_linearEquiv, hs‚ÇÉ, ENNReal.zero_toReal]
    rfl

end Pi

end ZLattice

FINAL

To complete the proof of `frontier_equivFun`, we use the fact that `b.equivFun` is a homeomorphism between `E` and `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`. For any homeomorphism `f : X ‚âÉ‚Çú Y`, the image of the frontier of a set `s` is the frontier of the image of that set.

```lean

  exact (b.equivFunL.toHomeomorph).frontier_image s

end Pi

end covolume

end ZLattice
```

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s)
    (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s) (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume s).toReal / covolume L)) := by
  have hb := (Basis.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L)
  rw [‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L hb, ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_div, ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  apply (tendsto_card_div_pow'' hb hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ _).congr'
  ¬∑ exact (frontier_equivFun hb s).symm ‚ñ∏ (measure_preimage_eq_zero_of_image_eq_zero _
      (continuousLinearEquiv_toHomeomorph _).toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image hs‚ÇÉ)
  ¬∑ refine eventually_of_forall fun n ‚Ü¶ ?_
    rw [‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_div, ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
    congr 2
    exact volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L hb

end Pi

end covolume

end ZLattice

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')
    (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi'' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    have : (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom ‚â´ f' = f'' := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      rw [assoc, hf', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, assoc, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hi'' hj']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom, comp_zero, ‚Üê this, assoc]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      simp only [zero_comp, hf', K.shape _ _ hij]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      dsimp
      rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, hf'', zero_comp, zero_comp,
        K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj']
      rw [hi]
      exact hij
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hi hi' in
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 :=
  lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' cone hcone _ (hcone.fac _ _) _
    (IsLimit.fac _ _ WalkingParallelPair.zero) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def cokernelCofork :
    CokernelCofork ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ cocone.œÄ (by
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone]
    exact cocone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork :
    IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff' hcocone _
    (lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone)

end leftHomologyData

open leftHomologyData in
/-- The left homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a left homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def leftHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).LeftHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').LeftHomologyData where
  K := h.K
  H := h.H
  i := h.i ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv
  œÄ := h.œÄ
  wi := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk']
    exact h.wi
  hi := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' _ h.hi
  wœÄ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi]
    exact h.wœÄ
  hœÄ := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi _ h.hœÄ

namespace rightHomologyData

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (K.d i j)) (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

/-- The cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a cokernel
cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def cokernelCofork : CokernelCofork ((K.extend e).d i' j') :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ) (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' cocone.œÄ, cocone.condition])

/-- The colimit cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a
colimit cokernel cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork : IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff hcocone ((K.extend e).d i' j')
    (extendXIso K e hj') (d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi')

variable (cone : KernelFork (hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcone : IsLimit cone)

include hk hk' hcocone in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ K.X k)
    (hf' : cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f' = K.d j k)
    (f'' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ (K.extend e).X k')
    (hf'' : (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f'' = (K.extend e).d j' k')
    (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ f' = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ f'' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk'' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    have : f' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv = f'' := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [reassoc_of% hf', ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv, zero_comp, assoc, this]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      simp only [hf', comp_zero, K.shape _ _ hjk]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'', comp_zero, comp_zero,
        K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj']
      rw [hk]
      exact hjk
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hk hk' in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) = 0 ‚Üî
      œÜ ‚â´ ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) = 0 :=
  d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hk hk' cocone hcocone _ (hcocone.fac _ _) _ (by
    simpa using (isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).fac _
      WalkingParallelPair.one) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def kernelFork :
    KernelFork ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ cone.Œπ (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone]
    exact cone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork :
    IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff' hcone _
    (d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone)

end rightHomologyData

open rightHomologyData in
/-- The right homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a right homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def rightHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').RightHomologyData where
  Q := h.Q
  H := h.H
  p := (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ h.p
  Œπ := h.Œπ
  wp := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi']
    exact h.wp
  hp := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' _ h.hp
  wŒπ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp]
    exact h.wŒπ
  hŒπ := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp _ h.hŒπ

/-- Computation of the `g'` field of `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
lemma rightHomologyData_g' (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) (hk'' : e.f k = k') :
    (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).g' = h.g' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv := by
  rw [‚Üê cancel_epi h.p, ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom]
  have := (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).p_g'
  dsimp at this
  rw [assoc] at this
  rw [this, K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'', h.p_g'_assoc, shortComplexFunctor'_obj_g]

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def homologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').HomologyData where
  left := leftHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.left
  right := rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.right
  iso := h.iso

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc j'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def homologyData' (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc j').HomologyData :=
  homologyData K e hj' hi rfl hk rfl h

end HomologyData

lemma hasHomology {j : Œπ} {j' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j') [K.HasHomology j] :
    (K.extend e).HasHomology j' :=

    (homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl ((K.sc j).homologyData))

instance (j : Œπ) [K.HasHomology j] : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) :=
  hasHomology K e rfl

instance [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] (j' : Œπ') : (K.extend e).HasHomology j' := by
  by_cases h : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h
    infer_instance
  ¬∑ have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' (by tauto)
    exact ShortComplex.HasHomology.mk'
      (ShortComplex.HomologyData.ofZeros _ (hj.eq_of_tgt _ _) (hj.eq_of_src _ _))

The naturality of the homology map follows from the naturality of the cycles map and the fact that the construction of the homology isomorphism is compatible with these maps. We finish the proof by applying the `homology_œÄ_Œπ` lemma and properties of the isomorphisms constructed in `extendHomologyIso`.

Since `homologyMap œÜ j` is defined such that `homologyœÄ ‚â´ homologyMap œÜ = cyclesMap œÜ ‚â´ homologyœÄ`, and the isomorphism `extendHomologyIso` is constructed from the cycles and opcycles isomorphisms, the requested naturality follows from the naturality of those isomorphisms.

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Continuity
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Complex.LogDeriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Extend
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Prod
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Deriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Trigonometric.Deriv

/-!
# Derivatives of power function on `‚ÑÇ`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñù‚â•0`, and `‚Ñù‚â•0‚àû`

We also prove differentiability and provide derivatives for the power functions `x ^ y`.
-/


noncomputable section

open scoped Real Topology NNReal ENNReal
open Filter

namespace Complex

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p := by
  have A : p.1 ‚âÝ 0 := slitPlane_ne_zero hp
  have : (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    ((isOpen_ne.preimage continuous_fst).eventually_mem A).mono fun p hp =>
      cpow_def_of_ne_zero hp _
  rw [cpow_sub _ _ A, cpow_one, mul_div_left_comm, mul_smul, mul_smul]
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero A, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_smul, add_comm, smul_add] using
    ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.clog hp).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).cexp

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow' {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (hp : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((y * x ^ (y - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (x ^ y * log x) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) (x, y) :=
  @hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow (x, y) hp

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® y ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun y => x ^ y) (x ^ y * log x) y := by
  rcases em (x = 0) with (rfl | hx)
  ¬∑ replace h := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
    rw [log_zero, mul_zero]
    refine (hasStrictDerivAt_const y 0).congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
    exact (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem h).mono fun y hy => (zero_cpow hy).symm
  ¬∑ simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero hx, mul_one] using
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id y).const_mul (log x)).cexp

theorem hasFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p :=
  (hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow hp).hasFDerivAt

end Complex

section fderiv

open Complex

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚ÑÇ E] {f g : E ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ}
  {x : E} {s : Set E} {c : ‚ÑÇ}

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).comp_hasStrictFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by
  convert
    (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hg : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ g x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivAt h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (hg : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ g s x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableWithinAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s) (hg : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ g s)
    (h0 : Set.MapsTo f s slitPlane) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).cpow (hg x hx) (h0 hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableOn_const c) h0

theorem Differentiable.cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f) (hg : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ g)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x, f x ‚àà slitPlane) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).cpow (hg x) (h0 x)

theorem Differentiable.const_cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x, f x ‚âÝ 0) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiable_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] :
    Differentiable ‚ÑÇ fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s :=
  differentiable_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiableAt_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] (t : ‚ÑÇ) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s) t :=
  differentiableAt_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

end fderiv

section deriv

open Complex

variable {f g : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {s : Set ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' x c : ‚ÑÇ}

/-- A private lemma that rewrites the output of lemmas like `HasFDerivAt.cpow` to the form
expected by lemmas like `HasDerivAt.cpow`. -/
private theorem aux : ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight f' +
    (f x ^ g x * log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight g') 1 =
      g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * log (f x) * g' := by
  simp only [Algebra.id.smul_eq_mul, one_mul, ContinuousLinearMap.one_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.smulRight_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul']

nonrec theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa using (hf.cpow hg h0).hasStrictDerivAt

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (h : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h).comp x hf

theorem Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (h : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ c) (c * x ^ (c - 1)) x := by
  simpa only [mul_zero, add_zero, mul_one] using
    (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).cpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x c) h

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivAt

theorem HasDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') s x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivWithinAt

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') s x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

/-- Although `fun x => x ^ r` for fixed `r` is *not* complex-differentiable along the negative real
line, it is still real-differentiable, and the derivative is what one would formally expect.
See `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` for an alternate formulation. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ -1) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x := by
  rw [Ne, ‚Üê add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ‚Üê Ne] at hr
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- easy case : `0 < x`
    -- Porting note: proof used to be
    -- convert (((hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)).cpow_const _).div_const (r + 1)).comp_ofReal using 1
    -- ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel, id.def, mul_one, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel _ hr]
    -- ¬∑ rw [id.def, ofReal_re]; exact Or.inl hx
    apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal (e := fun y => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1))
    convert HasDerivAt.div_const (ùïú := ‚ÑÇ) ?_ (r + 1) using 1
    ¬∑ exact (mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr).symm
    ¬∑ convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
      ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, mul_comm]; exact (mul_one _).symm
      ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)
      ¬∑ simp [hx]
  ¬∑ -- harder case : `x < 0`
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x,
        (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1) = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) * exp (œÄ * I * (r + 1)) / (r + 1) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-‚Üëy) ^ (r + 1) * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * (r + 1)))
        ((r + 1) * (-‚Üëx) ^ r * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * r)) x by
      convert this.div_const (r + 1) using 1
      conv_rhs => rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr]
    rw [mul_add ((œÄ : ‚ÑÇ) * _), mul_one, exp_add, exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm (_ : ‚ÑÇ) (-1 : ‚ÑÇ),
      neg_one_mul]
    simp_rw [mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (‚Üë(-y) : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) (-(r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) x by
      convert this.neg.mul_const _ using 1; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) (-x) by
      convert @HasDerivAt.scomp ‚Ñù _ ‚ÑÇ _ _ x ‚Ñù _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ this (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      rw [real_smul, ofReal_neg 1, ofReal_one]; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) ‚Üë(-x) by
      exact this.comp_ofReal
    conv in ‚Üë_ ^ _ => rw [(by ring : r = r + 1 - 1)]
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
    ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, add_sub_cancel_right]; exact (mul_one _).symm
    ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id ((-x : ‚Ñù) : ‚ÑÇ)
    ¬∑ simp [hx]

theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :

/-
