import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Hom.Monoid
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Maps
import Mathlib.Tactic.TFAE

/-!

# The basics of valuation theory.

The basic theory of valuations (non-archimedean norms) on a commutative ring,
following T. Wedhorn's unpublished notes ‚ÄúAdic Spaces‚Äù ([wedhorn_adic]).

The definition of a valuation we use here is Definition 1.22 of [wedhorn_adic].
A valuation on a ring `R` is a monoid homomorphism `v` to a linearly ordered
commutative monoid with zero, that in addition satisfies the following two axioms:
 * `v 0 = 0`
 * `‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y)`

`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`is the type of valuations `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`, with a coercion to the underlying
function. If `v` is a valuation from `R` to `Œì‚ÇÄ` then the induced group
homomorphism `Units(R) ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is called `unit_map v`.

The equivalence "relation" `IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Prop` defined in 1.27 of [wedhorn_adic] is not strictly
speaking a relation, because `v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÅ` and `v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÇ` might
not have the same type. This corresponds in ZFC to the set-theoretic difficulty
that the class of all valuations (as `Œì‚ÇÄ` varies) on a ring `R` is not a set.
The "relation" is however reflexive, symmetric and transitive in the obvious
sense. Note that we use 1.27(iii) of [wedhorn_adic] as the definition of equivalence.

## Main definitions

* `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of valuations on `R` with values in `Œì‚ÇÄ`
* `Valuation.IsEquiv`, the heterogeneous equivalence relation on valuations
* `Valuation.supp`, the support of a valuation

* `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of additive valuations on `R` with values in a
  linearly ordered additive commutative group with a top element, `Œì‚ÇÄ`.

## Implementation Details

`AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` is implemented as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`.

## Notation

In the `DiscreteValuation` locale:

 * `‚Ñï‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñï)`
 * `‚Ñ§‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñ§)`

## TODO

If ever someone extends `Valuation`, we should fully comply to the `DFunLike` by migrating the
boilerplate lemmas to `ValuationClass`.
-/

open Function Ideal

noncomputable section

variable {K F R : Type*} [DivisionRing K]

section

variable (F R) (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued valuations on `R`.

When you extend this structure, make sure to extend `ValuationClass`. -/
structure Valuation extends R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max' : ‚àÄ x y, toFun (x + y) ‚â§ max (toFun x) (toFun y)

/-- `ValuationClass F Œ± Œ≤` states that `F` is a type of valuations.

You should also extend this typeclass when you extend `Valuation`. -/
class ValuationClass (F) (R Œì‚ÇÄ : outParam Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]
  [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ]
  extends MonoidWithZeroHomClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ : Prop where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max (f : F) (x y : R) : f (x + y) ‚â§ max (f x) (f y)

export ValuationClass (map_add_le_max)

instance [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ] [ValuationClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ] : CoeTC F (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    { toFun := f
      map_one' := map_one f
      map_zero' := map_zero f
      map_mul' := map_mul f
      map_add_le_max' := map_add_le_max f }‚ü©

end

namespace Valuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì''‚ÇÄ : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì''‚ÇÄ]

section Basic

variable [Ring R]

section Monoid

variable [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]

instance : FunLike (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe f := f.toFun
  coe_injective' f g h := by
    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®_,_‚ü©, _‚ü©, _‚ü© := f
    congr

instance : ValuationClass (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  map_mul f := f.map_mul'
  map_one f := f.map_one'
  map_zero f := f.map_zero'
  map_add_le_max f := f.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem coe_mk (f : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) (h) : ‚áë(Valuation.mk f h) = f := rfl

theorem toFun_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : v.toFun = v := rfl

@[simp]
theorem toMonoidWithZeroHom_coe_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    (v.toMonoidWithZeroHom : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

@[ext]
theorem ext {v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : ‚àÄ r, v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÇ r) : v‚ÇÅ = v‚ÇÇ :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ h

variable (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_coe : ‚áë(v : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

theorem map_zero : v 0 = 0 :=
  v.map_zero'

theorem map_one : v 1 = 1 :=
  v.map_one'

theorem map_mul : ‚àÄ x y, v (x * y) = v x * v y :=
  v.map_mul'

-- Porting note: LHS side simplified so created map_add'
theorem map_add : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  v.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem map_add' : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ v x ‚à® v (x + y) ‚â§ v y := by
  intro x y
  rw [‚Üê le_max_iff, ‚Üê ge_iff_le]
  apply map_add

theorem map_add_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x + y) ‚â§ g :=
  le_trans (v.map_add x y) <| max_le hx hy

theorem map_add_lt {x y g} (hx : v x < g) (hy : v y < g) : v (x + y) < g :=
  lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) <| max_lt hx hy

theorem map_sum_le {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) ‚â§ g) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) ‚â§ g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ zero_le')
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_le hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g ‚âÝ 0)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ (zero_lt_iff.2 hg))
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_lt hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt' {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : 0 < g)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g :=
  v.map_sum_lt (ne_of_gt hg) hf

theorem map_pow : ‚àÄ (x) (n : ‚Ñï), v (x ^ n) = v x ^ n :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_pow

-- The following definition is not an instance, because we have more than one `v` on a given `R`.
-- In addition, type class inference would not be able to infer `v`.
/-- A valuation gives a preorder on the underlying ring. -/
def toPreorder : Preorder R :=
  Preorder.lift v

/-- If `v` is a valuation on a division ring then `v(x) = 0` iff `x = 0`. -/
theorem zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 :=
  map_eq_zero v

theorem ne_zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 :=
  map_ne_zero v

lemma pos_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : 0 < v x ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem unit_map_eq (u : RÀ£) : (Units.map (v : R ‚Üí* Œì‚ÇÄ) u : Œì‚ÇÄ) = v u :=
  rfl

theorem ne_zero_of_unit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : KÀ£) : v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simp only [ne_eq, Valuation.zero_iff, Units.ne_zero x, not_false_iff]

theorem ne_zero_of_isUnit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) (hx : IsUnit x) :
    v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simpa [hx.choose_spec] using ne_zero_of_unit v hx.choose

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
def comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  { v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.comp f.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := v ‚àò f
    map_add_le_max' := fun x y => by simp only [comp_apply, map_add, f.map_add] }

@[simp]
theorem comap_apply {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (s : S) :
    v.comap f s = v (f s) := rfl

@[simp]
theorem comap_id : v.comap (RingHom.id R) = v :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`.
-/
def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ :=
  { MonoidWithZeroHom.comp f v.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := f ‚àò v
    map_add_le_max' := fun r s =>
      calc
        f (v (r + s)) ‚â§ f (max (v r) (v s)) := hf (v.map_add r s)
        _ = max (f (v r)) (f (v s)) := hf.map_max
         }

@[simp]
lemma map_apply (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (r : R) :
    v.map f hf r = f (v r) := rfl

/-- Two valuations on `R` are defined to be equivalent if they induce the same preorder on `R`. -/
def IsEquiv (v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ r s, v‚ÇÅ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÇ s

@[simp]
theorem map_neg (x : R) : v (-x) = v x :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_neg x

theorem map_sub_swap (x y : R) : v (x - y) = v (y - x) :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_sub_swap x y

theorem map_sub (x y : R) : v (x - y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  calc
    v (x - y) = v (x + -y) := by rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
    _ ‚â§ max (v x) (v <| -y) := v.map_add _ _
    _ = max (v x) (v y) := by rw [map_neg]

theorem map_sub_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x - y) ‚â§ g := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
  exact v.map_add_le hx (le_trans (le_of_eq (v.map_neg y)) hy)

variable {x y : R}

theorem map_add_of_distinct_val (h : v x ‚âÝ v y) : v (x + y) = max (v x) (v y) := by
  suffices ¬¨v (x + y) < max (v x) (v y) from
    or_iff_not_imp_right.1 (le_iff_eq_or_lt.1 (v.map_add x y)) this
  intro h'
  wlog vyx : v y < v x generalizing x y
  ¬∑ refine this h.symm ?_ (h.lt_or_lt.resolve_right vyx)
    rwa [add_comm, max_comm]
  rw [max_eq_left_of_lt vyx] at h'
  apply lt_irrefl (v x)
  calc
    v x = v (x + y - y) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ max (v <| x + y) (v y) := map_sub _ _ _
    _ < v x := max_lt h' vyx

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x + y) = v y :=
  (v.map_add_of_distinct_val h.ne).trans (max_eq_right_iff.mpr h.le)

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x + y) = v x := by
  rw [add_comm]; exact map_add_eq_of_lt_right _ h

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x - y) = v y := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_right, map_neg]
  rwa [map_neg]

open scoped Classical in
theorem map_sum_eq_of_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {j : Œπ}
    (hj : j ‚àà s) (h0 : v (f j) ‚âÝ 0) (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s \ {j}, v (f i) < v (f j)) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) = v (f j) := by
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton hj]
  exact map_add_eq_of_lt_left _ (map_sum_lt _ h0 hf)

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x - y) = v x := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_left]
  rwa [map_neg]

theorem map_eq_of_sub_lt (h : v (y - x) < v x) : v y = v x := by
  have := Valuation.map_add_of_distinct_val v (ne_of_gt h).symm
  rw [max_eq_right (le_of_lt h)] at this
  simpa using this

theorem map_one_add_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one] at h
  simpa only [v.map_one] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

theorem map_one_sub_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 - x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one, ‚Üê v.map_neg] at h
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg 1 x]
  simpa only [v.map_one, v.map_neg] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

/-- An ordered monoid isomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces an equivalence
`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`. -/
def congr (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := map f f.toOrderIso.monotone
  invFun := map f.symm f.toOrderIso.symm.monotone
  left_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp
  right_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp

end Monoid

section Group

variable [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : R}

theorem map_inv {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x, v x‚Åª¬π = (v x)‚Åª¬π :=
  map_inv‚ÇÄ _

theorem map_div {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x y, v (x / y) = v x / v y :=
  map_div‚ÇÄ _

theorem one_lt_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 < v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  simp [inv_lt_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem one_le_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 ‚â§ v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  simp [inv_le_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_lt_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x < 1 ‚Üî 1 < v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_lt_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_le_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî 1 ‚â§ v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_le_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_eq_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 1 ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π = 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [map_inv‚ÇÄ, inv_eq_one]
  ¬∑ simpa only [le_antisymm_iff, And.comm] using and_congr (one_le_val_iff v h) (val_le_one_iff v h)

theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, zero_lt_one, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_lt_val_iff v h, le_or_lt]

/--
This theorem is a weaker version of `Valuation.val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one`, but more symmetric
in `x` and `x‚Åª¬π`.
-/
theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_le_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_le_val_iff v h, le_total]

/-- The subgroup of elements whose valuation is less than a certain unit. -/
def ltAddSubgroup (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (Œ≥ : Œì‚ÇÄÀ£) : AddSubgroup R where
  carrier := { x | v x < Œ≥ }
  zero_mem' := by simp
  add_mem' {x y} x_in y_in := lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) (max_lt x_in y_in)
  neg_mem' x_in := by rwa [Set.mem_setOf, map_neg]

end Group

end Basic

-- end of section
namespace IsEquiv

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÉ : Valuation R Œì''‚ÇÄ}

@[refl]
theorem refl : v.IsEquiv v := fun _ _ => Iff.refl _

@[symm]
theorem symm (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ := fun _ _ => Iff.symm (h _ _)

@[trans]
theorem trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ) : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ := fun _ _ =>
  Iff.trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ _ _) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ _ _)

theorem of_eq {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : v = v') : v.IsEquiv v' := by subst h; rfl

theorem map {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (inf : Injective f)
    (h : v.IsEquiv v') : (v.map f hf).IsEquiv (v'.map f hf) :=
  let H : StrictMono f := hf.strictMono_of_injective inf
  fun r s =>
  calc
    f (v r) ‚â§ f (v s) ‚Üî v r ‚â§ v s := by rw [H.le_iff_le]
    _ ‚Üî v' r ‚â§ v' s := h r s
    _ ‚Üî f (v' r) ‚â§ f (v' s) := by rw [H.le_iff_le]

/-- `comap` preserves equivalence. -/
theorem comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) :
    (v‚ÇÅ.comap f).IsEquiv (v‚ÇÇ.comap f) := fun r s => h (f r) (f s)

theorem val_eq (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r s : R} : v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r = v‚ÇÇ s := by
  simpa only [le_antisymm_iff] using and_congr (h r s) (h s r)

theorem ne_zero (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r : R} : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ 0 := by
  have : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÅ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÇ 0 := not_congr h.val_eq
  rwa [v‚ÇÅ.map_zero, v‚ÇÇ.map_zero] at this

end IsEquiv

-- end of namespace
section

theorem isEquiv_of_map_strictMono [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] [Ring R] {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ)
    (H : StrictMono f) : IsEquiv (v.map f H.monotone) v := fun _x _y =>
  ‚ü®H.le_iff_le.mp, fun h => H.monotone h‚ü©

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x y : K}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y := by
  simp only [IsEquiv, le_iff_le_iff_lt_iff_lt]
  exact forall_comm

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_iff_lt, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val

theorem isEquiv_of_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ}
    (h : ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1) : v.IsEquiv v' := by
  intro x y
  obtain rfl | hy := eq_or_ne y 0
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê div_le_one‚ÇÄ, ‚Üê v.map_div, h, v'.map_div, div_le_one‚ÇÄ] <;>
      rwa [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1 :=
  ‚ü®fun h x => by simpa using h x 1, isEquiv_of_val_le_one‚ü©

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.le_one_iff_le_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simpa using @IsEquiv.val_eq _ _ _ _ _ _ v v' h x 1
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply isEquiv_of_val_le_one
    intro x
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v'.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v' (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v'.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [‚Üê h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [‚Üê h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.eq_one_iff_eq_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simp only [lt_iff_le_and_ne,
      and_congr h.le_one_iff_le_one h.eq_one_iff_eq_one.not]
  ¬∑ rw [isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one]
    intro h x
    by_cases hx : x = 0
    ¬∑ simp only [(zero_iff _).2 hx, zero_ne_one]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.2 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
          rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
          exact hh.not_lt (h.2 ((one_lt_val_iff v' hx).1 h_2))
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.1 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
        rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
        exact hh.not_lt (h.1 ((one_lt_val_iff v hx).1 h_2))

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_one_iff_lt_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 := by
  rw [isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one]
  exact (Equiv.subRight 1).surjective.forall

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.val_sub_one_lt_one_iff, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_tfae [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
    (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ) :
    [ v.IsEquiv v',
      ‚àÄ {x y}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 ].TFAE := by
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 2 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 3 := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 4 := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 5 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 6 := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one
  tfae_finish

end

section Supp

variable [CommRing R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

/-- The support of a valuation `v : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is the ideal of `R` where `v` vanishes. -/
def supp : Ideal R where
  carrier := { x | v x = 0 }
  zero_mem' := map_zero v
  add_mem' {x y} hx hy := le_zero_iff.mp <|
    calc
      v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) := v.map_add x y
      _ ‚â§ 0 := max_le (le_zero_iff.mpr hx) (le_zero_iff.mpr hy)
  smul_mem' c x hx :=
    calc
      v (c * x) = v c * v x := map_mul v c x
      _ = v c * 0 := congr_arg _ hx
      _ = 0 := mul_zero _

@[simp]
theorem mem_supp_iff (x : R) : x ‚àà supp v ‚Üî v x = 0 :=
  Iff.rfl

/-- The support of a valuation is a prime ideal. -/
instance [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] [NoZeroDivisors Œì‚ÇÄ] : Ideal.IsPrime (supp v) :=
  ‚ü®fun h =>
    one_ne_zero (Œ± := Œì‚ÇÄ) <|
      calc
        1 = v 1 := v.map_one.symm
        _ = 0 := by rw [‚Üê mem_supp_iff, h]; exact Submodule.mem_top,
   fun {x y} hxy => by
    simp only [mem_supp_iff] at hxy ‚ä¢
    rw [v.map_mul x y] at hxy
    exact eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero hxy‚ü©

theorem map_add_supp (a : R) {s : R} (h : s ‚àà supp v) : v (a + s) = v a := by
  have aux : ‚àÄ a s, v s = 0 ‚Üí v (a + s) ‚â§ v a := by
    intro a' s' h'
    refine le_trans (v.map_add a' s') (max_le le_rfl ?_)
    simp [h']
  apply le_antisymm (aux a s h)
  calc
    v a = v (a + s + -s) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ v (a + s) := aux (a + s) (-s) (by rwa [‚Üê Ideal.neg_mem_iff] at h)

theorem comap_supp {S : Type*} [CommRing S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) :
    supp (v.comap f) = Ideal.comap f v.supp :=
  Ideal.ext fun x => by rw [mem_supp_iff, Ideal.mem_comap, mem_supp_iff, comap_apply]

end Supp

import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicNumbers
import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic

/-!
# p-adic integers

This file defines the `p`-adic integers `‚Ñ§_[p]` as the subtype of `‚Ñö_[p]` with norm `‚â§ 1`.
We show that `‚Ñ§_[p]`
* is complete,
* is nonarchimedean,
* is a normed ring,
* is a local ring, and
* is a discrete valuation ring.

The relation between `‚Ñ§_[p]` and `ZMod p` is established in another file.

## Important definitions

* `PadicInt` : the type of `p`-adic integers

## Notation

We introduce the notation `‚Ñ§_[p]` for the `p`-adic integers.

## Implementation notes

Much, but not all, of this file assumes that `p` is prime. This assumption is inferred automatically
by taking `[Fact p.Prime]` as a type class argument.

Coercions into `‚Ñ§_[p]` are set up to work with the `norm_cast` tactic.

## References

* [F. Q. Gouv√™a, *p-adic numbers*][gouvea1997]
* [R. Y. Lewis, *A formal proof of Hensel's lemma over the p-adic integers*][lewis2019]
* <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/P-adic_number>

## Tags

p-adic, p adic, padic, p-adic integer
-/


open Padic Metric IsLocalRing

noncomputable section

variable (p : ‚Ñï) [hp : Fact p.Prime]

/-- The `p`-adic integers `‚Ñ§_[p]` are the `p`-adic numbers with norm `‚â§ 1`. -/
def PadicInt : Type := {x : ‚Ñö_[p] // ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1}

/-- The ring of `p`-adic integers. -/
notation "‚Ñ§_[" p "]" => PadicInt p

namespace PadicInt
variable {p} {x y : ‚Ñ§_[p]}

/-! ### Ring structure and coercion to `‚Ñö_[p]` -/

instance : Coe ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Ñö_[p] :=
  ‚ü®Subtype.val‚ü©

theorem ext {x y : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = y ‚Üí x = y :=
  Subtype.ext

variable (p)

/-- The `p`-adic integers as a subring of `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
def subring : Subring ‚Ñö_[p] where
  carrier := { x : ‚Ñö_[p] | ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 }
  zero_mem' := by norm_num
  one_mem' := by norm_num
  add_mem' hx hy := (padicNormE.nonarchimedean _ _).trans <| max_le_iff.2 ‚ü®hx, hy‚ü©
  mul_mem' hx hy := (padicNormE.mul _ _).trans_le <| mul_le_one‚ÇÄ hx (norm_nonneg _) hy
  neg_mem' hx := (norm_neg _).trans_le hx

@[simp]
theorem mem_subring_iff {x : ‚Ñö_[p]} : x ‚àà subring p ‚Üî ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := Iff.rfl

variable {p}

/-- Addition on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Add ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Add (subring p))

/-- Multiplication on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Mul ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Mul (subring p))

/-- Negation on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Neg ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Neg (subring p))

/-- Subtraction on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Sub ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Sub (subring p))

/-- Zero on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : Zero ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : Zero (subring p))

instance : Inhabited ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®0‚ü©

/-- One on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is inherited from `‚Ñö_[p]`. -/
instance : One ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®‚ü®1, by norm_num‚ü©‚ü©

@[simp]
theorem mk_zero {h} : (‚ü®0, h‚ü© : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = (0 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_add (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 + z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 + z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_mul (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 * z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 * z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_neg (z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((-z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = -z1 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_sub (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ((z1 - z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z1 - z2 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_one : ((1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 1 := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_zero : ((0 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0 := rfl

@[simp] lemma coe_eq_zero : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 := by rw [‚Üê coe_zero, Subtype.coe_inj]

lemma coe_ne_zero : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]) ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := coe_eq_zero.not

instance : AddCommGroup ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : AddCommGroup (subring p))

instance instCommRing : CommRing ‚Ñ§_[p] := (by infer_instance : CommRing (subring p))

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_natCast (n : ‚Ñï) : ((n : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = n := rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_intCast (z : ‚Ñ§) : ((z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = z := rfl

/-- The coercion from `‚Ñ§_[p]` to `‚Ñö_[p]` as a ring homomorphism. -/
def Coe.ringHom : ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Üí+* ‚Ñö_[p] := (subring p).subtype

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_pow (x : ‚Ñ§_[p]) (n : ‚Ñï) : (‚Üë(x ^ n) : ‚Ñö_[p]) = (‚Üëx : ‚Ñö_[p]) ^ n := rfl

theorem mk_coe (k : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : (‚ü®k, k.2‚ü© : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = k := by simp

/-- The inverse of a `p`-adic integer with norm equal to `1` is also a `p`-adic integer.
Otherwise, the inverse is defined to be `0`. -/
def inv : ‚Ñ§_[p] ‚Üí ‚Ñ§_[p]
  | ‚ü®k, _‚ü© => if h : ‚Äñk‚Äñ = 1 then ‚ü®k‚Åª¬π, by simp [h]‚ü© else 0

instance : CharZero ‚Ñ§_[p] where
  cast_injective m n h :=
    Nat.cast_injective (R := ‚Ñö_[p]) (by rw [Subtype.ext_iff] at h; norm_cast at h)

@[norm_cast]
theorem intCast_eq (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§) : (z1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = z2 ‚Üî z1 = z2 := by simp

/-- A sequence of integers that is Cauchy with respect to the `p`-adic norm converges to a `p`-adic
integer. -/
def ofIntSeq (seq : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñ§) (h : IsCauSeq (padicNorm p) fun n => seq n) : ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  ‚ü®‚ü¶‚ü®_, h‚ü©‚üß,
    show ‚Üë(PadicSeq.norm _) ‚â§ (1 : ‚Ñù) by
      rw [PadicSeq.norm]
      split_ifs with hne <;> norm_cast
      apply padicNorm.of_int‚ü©

/-! ### Instances

We now show that `‚Ñ§_[p]` is a
* complete metric space
* normed ring
* integral domain
-/

variable (p)

instance : MetricSpace ‚Ñ§_[p] := Subtype.metricSpace

instance : IsUltrametricDist ‚Ñ§_[p] := IsUltrametricDist.subtype _

instance completeSpace : CompleteSpace ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  have : IsClosed { x : ‚Ñö_[p] | ‚Äñx‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 } := isClosed_le continuous_norm continuous_const
  this.completeSpace_coe

instance : Norm ‚Ñ§_[p] := ‚ü®fun z => ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ‚ü©

variable {p}

theorem norm_def {z : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : ‚Äñz‚Äñ = ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ := rfl

variable (p)

instance : NormedCommRing ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  { PadicInt.instCommRing with
    dist_eq := fun ‚ü®_, _‚ü© ‚ü®_, _‚ü© => rfl
    norm_mul := by simp [norm_def]
    norm := norm }

instance : NormOneClass ‚Ñ§_[p] :=
  ‚ü®norm_def.trans norm_one‚ü©

instance isAbsoluteValue : IsAbsoluteValue fun z : ‚Ñ§_[p] => ‚Äñz‚Äñ where
  abv_nonneg' := norm_nonneg
  abv_eq_zero' := by simp [norm_eq_zero]
  abv_add' := fun ‚ü®_, _‚ü© ‚ü®_, _‚ü© => norm_add_le _ _
  abv_mul' _ _ := by simp only [norm_def, padicNormE.mul, PadicInt.coe_mul]

variable {p}

instance : IsDomain ‚Ñ§_[p] := Function.Injective.isDomain (subring p).subtype Subtype.coe_injective

/-! ### Norm -/

theorem norm_le_one (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñz‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 := z.2

@[simp]
theorem norm_mul (z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñz1 * z2‚Äñ = ‚Äñz1‚Äñ * ‚Äñz2‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

@[simp]
theorem norm_pow (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚àÄ n : ‚Ñï, ‚Äñz ^ n‚Äñ = ‚Äñz‚Äñ ^ n
  | 0 => by simp
  | k + 1 => by
    rw [pow_succ, pow_succ, norm_mul]
    congr
    apply norm_pow

theorem nonarchimedean (q r : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñq + r‚Äñ ‚â§ max ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚Äñr‚Äñ := padicNormE.nonarchimedean _ _

theorem norm_add_eq_max_of_ne {q r : ‚Ñ§_[p]} : ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚âÝ ‚Äñr‚Äñ ‚Üí ‚Äñq + r‚Äñ = max ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚Äñr‚Äñ :=
  padicNormE.add_eq_max_of_ne

theorem norm_eq_of_norm_add_lt_right {z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (h : ‚Äñz1 + z2‚Äñ < ‚Äñz2‚Äñ) : ‚Äñz1‚Äñ = ‚Äñz2‚Äñ :=
  by_contra fun hne =>
    not_lt_of_ge (by rw [norm_add_eq_max_of_ne hne]; apply le_max_right) h

theorem norm_eq_of_norm_add_lt_left {z1 z2 : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (h : ‚Äñz1 + z2‚Äñ < ‚Äñz1‚Äñ) : ‚Äñz1‚Äñ = ‚Äñz2‚Äñ :=
  by_contra fun hne =>
    not_lt_of_ge (by rw [norm_add_eq_max_of_ne hne]; apply le_max_left) h

@[simp]
theorem padic_norm_e_of_padicInt (z : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ = ‚Äñz‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

theorem norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm (z : ‚Ñ§) : ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ = ‚Äñ(z : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ := by simp [norm_def]

@[simp]
theorem norm_eq_padic_norm {q : ‚Ñö_[p]} (hq : ‚Äñq‚Äñ ‚â§ 1) : @norm ‚Ñ§_[p] _ ‚ü®q, hq‚ü© = ‚Äñq‚Äñ := rfl

@[simp]
theorem norm_p : ‚Äñ(p : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π := padicNormE.norm_p

theorem norm_p_pow (n : ‚Ñï) : ‚Äñ(p : ‚Ñ§_[p]) ^ n‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) := by simp

private def cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq (f : CauSeq ‚Ñ§_[p] norm) : CauSeq ‚Ñö_[p] fun a => ‚Äña‚Äñ :=
  ‚ü®fun n => f n, fun _ hŒµ => by simpa [norm, norm_def] using f.cauchy hŒµ‚ü©

variable (p)

instance complete : CauSeq.IsComplete ‚Ñ§_[p] norm :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    have hqn : ‚ÄñCauSeq.lim (cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq f)‚Äñ ‚â§ 1 :=
      padicNormE_lim_le zero_lt_one fun _ => norm_le_one _
    ‚ü®‚ü®_, hqn‚ü©, fun Œµ => by
      simpa [norm, norm_def] using CauSeq.equiv_lim (cauSeq_to_rat_cauSeq f) Œµ‚ü©‚ü©

theorem exists_pow_neg_lt {Œµ : ‚Ñù} (hŒµ : 0 < Œµ) : ‚àÉ k : ‚Ñï, (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-(k : ‚Ñ§)) < Œµ := by
  obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := exists_nat_gt Œµ‚Åª¬π
  use k
  rw [‚Üê inv_lt_inv‚ÇÄ hŒµ (zpow_pos _ _)]
  ¬∑ rw [zpow_neg, inv_inv, zpow_natCast]
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le hk
    norm_cast
    apply le_of_lt
    convert Nat.lt_pow_self _ using 1
    exact hp.1.one_lt
  ¬∑ exact mod_cast hp.1.pos

theorem exists_pow_neg_lt_rat {Œµ : ‚Ñö} (hŒµ : 0 < Œµ) : ‚àÉ k : ‚Ñï, (p : ‚Ñö) ^ (-(k : ‚Ñ§)) < Œµ := by
  obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := @exists_pow_neg_lt p _ Œµ (mod_cast hŒµ)
  use k
  rw [show (p : ‚Ñù) = (p : ‚Ñö) by simp] at hk
  exact mod_cast hk

variable {p}

theorem norm_int_lt_one_iff_dvd (k : ‚Ñ§) : ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ < 1 ‚Üî (p : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k :=
  suffices ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ < 1 ‚Üî ‚Üëp ‚à£ k by rwa [norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm]
  padicNormE.norm_int_lt_one_iff_dvd k

theorem norm_int_le_pow_iff_dvd {k : ‚Ñ§} {n : ‚Ñï} :
    ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñ§_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) ‚Üî (p ^ n : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k :=
  suffices ‚Äñ(k : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ ‚â§ (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-n : ‚Ñ§) ‚Üî (p ^ n : ‚Ñ§) ‚à£ k by
    simpa [norm_intCast_eq_padic_norm]
  padicNormE.norm_int_le_pow_iff_dvd _ _

/-! ### Valuation on `‚Ñ§_[p]` -/

lemma valuation_coe_nonneg : 0 ‚â§ (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation := by
  obtain rfl | hx := eq_or_ne x 0
  ¬∑ simp
  have := x.2
  rwa [Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation <| coe_ne_zero.2 hx, zpow_le_one_iff_right‚ÇÄ, neg_nonpos]
    at this
  exact mod_cast hp.out.one_lt

/-- `PadicInt.valuation` lifts the `p`-adic valuation on `‚Ñö` to `‚Ñ§_[p]`. -/
def valuation (x : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : ‚Ñï := (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation.toNat

@[simp, norm_cast] lemma valuation_coe (x : ‚Ñ§_[p]) : (x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation = x.valuation := by
  simp [valuation, valuation_coe_nonneg]

@[simp] lemma valuation_zero : valuation (0 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = 0 := by simp [valuation]
@[simp] lemma valuation_one : valuation (1 : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = 0 := by simp [valuation]
@[simp] lemma valuation_p : valuation (p : ‚Ñ§_[p]) = 1 := by simp [valuation]

lemma le_valuation_add (hxy : x + y ‚âÝ 0) : min x.valuation y.valuation ‚â§ (x + y).valuation := by
  zify; simpa [‚Üê valuation_coe] using Padic.le_valuation_add <| coe_ne_zero.2 hxy

@[simp] lemma valuation_mul (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) (hy : y ‚âÝ 0) :
    (x * y).valuation = x.valuation + y.valuation := by
  zify; simp [‚Üê valuation_coe, Padic.valuation_mul (coe_ne_zero.2 hx) (coe_ne_zero.2 hy)]

@[simp]
lemma valuation_pow (x : ‚Ñ§_[p]) (n : ‚Ñï) : (x ^ n).valuation = n * x.valuation := by
  zify; simp [‚Üê valuation_coe]
formal_statement: lemma norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation {x : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) : ‚Äñx‚Äñ = p ^ (-x.valuation : ‚Ñ§)
name: PadicInt.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation
responses:
To prove `‚Äñx‚Äñ = p ^ (-x.valuation : ‚Ñ§)`, we utilize the established definition of the valuation in `‚Ñö_[p]` and the fact that `x` is a `p`-adic integer. Since `x ‚âÝ 0`, its norm in `‚Ñö_[p]` is given by `p ^ (-x.valuation : ‚Ñ§)`. Given `x` is a `p`-adic integer, its norm is equal to its `‚Ñö_[p]` norm, and the valuation on `‚Ñ§_[p]` is defined as the non-negative integer lift of the `‚Ñö_[p]` valuation.

FINAL```
:= by
  have : ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ = p ^ (-(x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation : ‚Ñ§) :=
    Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx)
  rw [norm_def, valuation_coe, this]
```

To complete the formalization of the $p$-adic norm in terms of the valuation, we use the property that the $p$-adic norm of a non-zero $p$-adic number is given by $p^{-v(x)}$. Since we have established `norm_def` and the correspondence between the $p$-adic valuation of $x$ in $\mathbb{Q}_p$ and the valuation defined on $\mathbb{Z}_p$, the result follows directly from the definition of the $p$-adic valuation and the norm on $\mathbb{Q}_p$.

FINAL```
  := by
    rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe]
    rfl
```

To complete the proof of the requested lemma, we observe that for $x \neq 0$, the norm of $x$ is defined as $\lVert x \rVert = \lVert (x : \mathbb{Q}_p) \rVert$. Since $x \in \mathbb{Z}_p$, its norm is determined by its $p$-adic valuation. Specifically, the relationship between the norm and the valuation for $p$-adic numbers is $\lVert x \rVert = p^{-v_p(x)}$. Since we have established `valuation_coe` to link the `PadicInt` valuation with the `‚Ñö_p` valuation, the identity follows directly.

FINAL```
:= by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe]
  rfl
```

The proof follows from the existing relationship between the $p$-adic norm and the valuation defined on $p$-adic numbers. For a non-zero $p$-adic integer $x$, the norm $\Vert x \Vert$ is defined as $p^{-v_p(x)}$. In the context of `PadicInt`, the valuation is defined to match the valuation on the field of $p$-adic numbers $\mathbb{Q}_p$. By the definition of the valuation on $\mathbb{Q}_p$, we have $\Vert x \Vert = p^{-v_p(x)}$.

FINAL```
:= by
  have : ‚Äñ(x : ‚Ñö_[p])‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-(x : ‚Ñö_[p]).valuation : ‚Ñ§) :=
    Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx)
  rw [norm_def, ‚Üê valuation_coe] at this
  exact this
```

To complete the proof, we use the fact that the $p$-adic norm of a non-zero $p$-adic number is defined by $p^{-v_p(x)}$, where $v_p(x)$ is the $p$-adic valuation. Since the norm on $\mathbb{Z}_p$ is the restriction of the norm on $\mathbb{Q}_p$, this follows directly from the definition of the valuation on $\mathbb{Q}_p$.

FINAL```
:= by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe]
  rfl
```

:= by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe x, zpow_natCast]
  rfl

FINAL```
lemma norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation {x : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) : ‚Äñx‚Äñ = p ^ (-x.valuation : ‚Ñ§) := by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe x, zpow_natCast]
  rfl
```

To complete the formalization of the `p`-adic integers, we show that the `p`-adic norm is given by $p^{-v_p(x)}$ for non-zero $x$. This follows from the definition of the `p`-adic valuation on $\mathbb{Q}_p$ and the fact that the valuation of an element in $\mathbb{Z}_p$ is simply the valuation of its image in $\mathbb{Q}_p$.

FINAL```
:= by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx)]
  congr 1
  rw [‚Üê valuation_coe, Int.ofNat_toNat (valuation_coe_nonneg _)]
  rfl
```

:= by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe, ‚Üê coe_natCast, ‚Üê coe_intCast]
  congr
  simp [valuation]

FINAL```
lemma norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation {x : ‚Ñ§_[p]} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) : ‚Äñx‚Äñ = (p : ‚Ñù) ^ (-x.valuation : ‚Ñ§) := by
  rw [norm_def, Padic.norm_eq_zpow_neg_valuation (coe_ne_zero.2 hx), ‚Üê valuation_coe, ‚Üê coe_natCast, ‚Üê coe_intCast]
  congr
  simp [valuation]
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))

lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne), natDegree_comp, ascPochhammer_natDegree,
    add_comm_sub, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, natDegree_add_C, natDegree_X, mul_one]

lemma coeff_preHilbertPoly_self [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).coeff d = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  delta preHilbertPoly
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  have heq : d = ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1)).natDegree :=
    (natDegree_preHilbertPoly F d k).symm.trans (natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne))
  nth_rw 3 [heq]
  calc
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C ((k : F) - 1))).leadingCoeff := by
    simp only [sub_add, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, coeff_smul, coeff_natDegree]
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
    simp only [leadingCoeff_comp (ne_of_eq_of_ne (natDegree_X_sub_C _) one_ne_zero), Monic.def.1
      (monic_ascPochhammer _ _), one_mul, leadingCoeff_X_sub_C, one_pow, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

lemma leadingCoeff_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).leadingCoeff = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [leadingCoeff, natDegree_preHilbertPoly, coeff_preHilbertPoly_self]

lemma preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add [CharZero F] (d : ‚Ñï) {k n : ‚Ñï} (hkn : k ‚â§ n):
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F) = (n - k + d).choose d := by
  have : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  calc
  _ = (‚Üëd !)‚Åª¬π * eval (‚Üë(n - k + 1)) (ascPochhammer F d) := by simp [cast_sub hkn, preHilbertPoly]
  _ = (n - k + d).choose d := by
    rw [ascPochhammer_nat_eq_natCast_ascFactorial];
    field_simp [ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]

variable {F}

/--
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p 0 = 0`; for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p (d + 1)`
is defined as `‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d i`. If
`M` is a graded module whose Poincar√© series can be written as `p(X)/(1 - X)·µà` for some
`p : ‚Ñö[X]` with integer coefficients, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is the Hilbert
polynomial of `M`. See also `Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`,
which says that `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * PowerSeries.invOneSubPow F d)` equals
`(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) : (d : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí F[X]
  | 0 => 0
  | d + 1 => ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_left (d : ‚Ñï) : hilbertPoly (0 : F[X]) d = 0 := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only
  | succ d _ => simp only [coeff_zero, zero_smul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_right (p : F[X]) : hilbertPoly p 0 = 0 := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly p (d + 1) = ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1) = preHilbertPoly F d k := by
  delta hilbertPoly; simp

lemma hilbertPoly_add_left (p q : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p + q) d = hilbertPoly p d + hilbertPoly q d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [add_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only [‚Üê coeff_add]
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _]
      exact sum_add_index _ _ _ (fun _ => zero_smul ..) (fun _ _ _ => add_smul ..)

lemma hilbertPoly_smul (a : F) (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (a ‚Ä¢ p) d = a ‚Ä¢ hilbertPoly p d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [smul_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, ‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, Polynomial.smul_sum,
        sum_smul_index' _ _ _ fun i => zero_smul F (preHilbertPoly F d i)]
      simp only [smul_assoc]

variable (F) in
/--
The function that sends any `p : F[X]` to `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is an `F`-linear map from
`F[X]` to `F[X]`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly_linearMap (d : ‚Ñï) : F[X] ‚Üí‚Çó[F] F[X] where
  toFun p := hilbertPoly p d
  map_add' p q := hilbertPoly_add_left p q d
  map_smul' r p := hilbertPoly_smul r p d

variable [CharZero F]

/--
The key property of Hilbert polynomials. If `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, `p : F[X]` and
`d : ‚Ñï`, then for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`, `(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` equals the
coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
-/
theorem coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval
    {p : F[X]} (d : ‚Ñï) {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : p.natDegree < n) :
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only [invOneSubPow_zero, Units.val_one, mul_one, coeff_coe, eval_zero]
            exact coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn
  | succ d hd =>
      simp only [eval_finset_sum, eval_smul, smul_eq_mul]
      rw [‚Üê Finset.sum_coe_sort]
      have h_le (i : p.support) : (i : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ n :=
        le_trans (le_natDegree_of_ne_zero <| mem_support_iff.1 i.2) hn.le
      have h (i : p.support) : eval ‚Üën (preHilbertPoly F d ‚Üëi) = (n + d - ‚Üëi).choose d := by
        rw [preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add _ _ (h_le i), Nat.sub_add_comm (h_le i)]
      simp_rw [h]
      rw [Finset.sum_coe_sort _ (fun x => (p.coeff ‚Üëx) * (_ + d - ‚Üëx).choose _),
        PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk,
        invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos _ _ (zero_lt_succ d)]
      simp only [coeff_coe, coeff_mk]
      symm
      refine Finset.sum_subset_zero_on_sdiff (fun s hs ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_)
      ¬∑ rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff]
        exact h_le ‚ü®s, hs‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, mem_support_iff, not_not] at hx
        rw [hx.2, zero_mul]
      ¬∑ rw [add_comm, Nat.add_sub_assoc (h_le ‚ü®x, hx‚ü©), succ_eq_add_one, add_tsub_cancel_right]
formal_statement:
/--
The polynomial satisfying the key property of `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is unique.
-/
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)
name: Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly
responses:
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d
  constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    intro n hn
    exact coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn
  ¬∑ rintro h' ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
    let N' := max N p.natDegree
    have h_eq : ‚àÄ n > N', h'.eval (n : F) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
      intro n hn
      rw [‚Üê hN n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left N p.natDegree) hn),
          ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right N p.natDegree) hn)]
    apply (eq_of_infinite_eval (Set.infinite_Ioi N')).mp
    exact Set.ext fun x => h_eq x (by linarith)
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d
  constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    intro n hn
    exact coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn
  ¬∑ rintro h' ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
    apply Polynomial.ext_of_eval_eq_at_infinite
    have h_deg : ‚àÄ n > N, PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := 
      fun n hn => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_max_iff.1 (lt_of_le_iff.2 (le_refl N))).1 hn) 
      -- Simplification: use the property that for large enough n, both equal the coefficient
    apply Set.Infinite.mono (s := {n : ‚Ñï | n > max N p.natDegree})
    ¬∑ intro n hn
      rw [hN n (lt_trans (lt_max_iff.1 hn).1 (lt_max_iff.1 hn).1), coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_max_iff.1 hn).2]
    ¬∑ exact Set.infinite_Ioi (max N p.natDegree)
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  refine ‚ü®hilbertPoly p d, ‚ü®p.natDegree, fun n hn => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn‚ü©, fun h' h_prop => ?_‚ü©
  rcases h_prop with ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
  let h := hilbertPoly p d
  have h_eq : ‚àÄ n > max N p.natDegree, h.eval (n : F) = h'.eval (n : F) := by
    intro n hn
    rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right N p.natDegree) hn) (le_of_lt hn)), hN n (lt_of_lt_of_le (le_max_left N p.natDegree) hn)]
  apply (poly_eq_of_eval_eq_on_infinite_set (Set.infinite_Ioi (max N p.natDegree)) _).mp
  exact h_eq
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d
  constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    intro n hn
    exact coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn
  ¬∑ rintro h' ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
    ext n
    have : ‚àÄ n > max N p.natDegree, h'.eval (n : F) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
      intro n hn
      rw [‚Üê hN n (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_max_iff.1 hn).1 le_rfl), 
          ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_max_iff.1 hn).2 le_rfl)]
    apply (Polynomial.funext_iff.1).mp
    apply Set.Infinite.eq_on_atTop (Set.infinite_Ioi (max N p.natDegree)) _ _ this
    exact fun _ _ => rfl
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d
  constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    intro n hn
    exact coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn
  ¬∑ rintro h' ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
    obtain ‚ü®N', hN'‚ü© := ‚ü®p.natDegree, fun n hn ‚Ü¶ coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn‚ü©
    let M := max N N'
    apply (poly_eq_iff_eq_on_subset _ _).1
    ¬∑ apply Set.Infinite.of_nat
      intro n hn
      have h1 : h'.eval (n : F) = PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) := hN n (lt_max_iff.1 hn).1
      have h2 : (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) = PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) := hN' n (lt_max_iff.1 hn).2
      rw [h1, ‚Üê h2]
    ¬∑ exact {n : ‚Ñï | n > M}
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d
  constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    intro n hn
    exact coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn
  ¬∑ intro h' h'_prop
    rcases h'_prop with ‚ü®N, hN‚ü©
    have h_eq : ‚àÄ n > max N p.natDegree, (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) = h'.eval (n : F) := by
      intro n hn
      rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_max_of_lt_right hn) (le_max_right N p.natDegree)), hN n (lt_of_lt_of_le (lt_max_of_lt_left hn) (le_max_left N p.natDegree))]
    exact (funext_iff_eval_eq_at_infinite (hilbertPoly p d) h').mp (Set.Infinite.of_forall_gt (max N p.natDegree) (fun n hn => h_eq n hn))
To complete the proof, we use the fact that if two polynomials agree on infinitely many values (in this case, all $n > \deg(p)$), they must be equal. Since `F` has characteristic $0$, the map $n \mapsto n$ is injective and the set of natural numbers is infinite, ensuring that the equality $h(n) = h'(n)$ for sufficiently large $n$ implies $h = h'$.

theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  refine ‚ü®hilbertPoly p d, p.natDegree, fun n hn => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn, ?_‚ü©
  rintro h' ‚ü®N', hN'‚ü©
  obtain ‚ü®N, hN‚ü© := ‚ü®p.natDegree, fun n hn => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn‚ü©
  let N_max := max N N'
  have h_eq : ‚àÄ n > N_max, h.eval (n : F) = h'.eval (n : F) := by
    intro n hn
    rw [‚Üê hN n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left N N') hn), ‚Üê hN' n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right N N') hn)]
  exact (funext_iff_eval_eq_infinite (Set.Infinite.of_not_bdd (fun c hc => 
    let ‚ü®m, hm‚ü© := hc
    let M := max N_max m
    exists_gt M)).to_set).mp (fun n => h_eq n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) (lt_add_one _)))

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')
    (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi'' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    have : (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom ‚â´ f' = f'' := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      rw [assoc, hf', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, assoc, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hi'' hj']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom, comp_zero, ‚Üê this, assoc]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      simp only [zero_comp, hf', K.shape _ _ hij]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      dsimp
      rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, hf'', zero_comp, zero_comp,
        K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj']
      rw [hi]
      exact hij
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hi hi' in
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 :=
  lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' cone hcone _ (hcone.fac _ _) _
    (IsLimit.fac _ _ WalkingParallelPair.zero) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def cokernelCofork :
    CokernelCofork ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ cocone.œÄ (by
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone]
    exact cocone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork :
    IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff' hcocone _
    (lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone)

end leftHomologyData

open leftHomologyData in
/-- The left homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a left homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def leftHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).LeftHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').LeftHomologyData where
  K := h.K
  H := h.H
  i := h.i ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv
  œÄ := h.œÄ
  wi := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk']
    exact h.wi
  hi := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' _ h.hi
  wœÄ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi]
    exact h.wœÄ
  hœÄ := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi _ h.hœÄ

namespace rightHomologyData

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (K.d i j)) (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

/-- The cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a cokernel
cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def cokernelCofork : CokernelCofork ((K.extend e).d i' j') :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ) (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' cocone.œÄ, cocone.condition])

/-- The colimit cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a
colimit cokernel cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork : IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff hcocone ((K.extend e).d i' j')
    (extendXIso K e hj') (d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi')

variable (cone : KernelFork (hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcone : IsLimit cone)

include hk hk' hcocone in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ K.X k)
    (hf' : cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f' = K.d j k)
    (f'' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ (K.extend e).X k')
    (hf'' : (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f'' = (K.extend e).d j' k')
    (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ f' = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ f'' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk'' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    have : f' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv = f'' := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [reassoc_of% hf', ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv, zero_comp, assoc, this]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      simp only [hf', comp_zero, K.shape _ _ hjk]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'', comp_zero, comp_zero,
        K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj']
      rw [hk]
      exact hjk
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hk hk' in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) = 0 ‚Üî
      œÜ ‚â´ ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) = 0 :=
  d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hk hk' cocone hcocone _ (hcocone.fac _ _) _ (by
    simpa using (isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).fac _
      WalkingParallelPair.one) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def kernelFork :
    KernelFork ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ cone.Œπ (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone]
    exact cone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork :
    IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff' hcone _
    (d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone)

end rightHomologyData

open rightHomologyData in
/-- The right homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a right homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def rightHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').RightHomologyData where
  Q := h.Q
  H := h.H
  p := (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ h.p
  Œπ := h.Œπ
  wp := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi']
    exact h.wp
  hp := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' _ h.hp
  wŒπ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp]
    exact h.wŒπ
  hŒπ := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp _ h.hŒπ

/-- Computation of the `g'` field of `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
lemma rightHomologyData_g' (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) (hk'' : e.f k = k') :
    (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).g' = h.g' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv := by
  rw [‚Üê cancel_epi h.p, ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom]
  have := (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).p_g'
  dsimp at this
  rw [assoc] at this
  rw [this, K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'', h.p_g'_assoc, shortComplexFunctor'_obj_g]

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def homologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').HomologyData where
  left := leftHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.left
  right := rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.right
  iso := h.iso

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc j'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def homologyData' (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc j').HomologyData :=
  homologyData K e hj' hi rfl hk rfl h

end HomologyData

lemma hasHomology {j : Œπ} {j' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j') [K.HasHomology j] :
    (K.extend e).HasHomology j' :=
  ShortComplex.HasHomology.mk'
    (homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl ((K.sc j).homologyData))

instance (j : Œπ) [K.HasHomology j] : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) :=
  hasHomology K e rfl

instance [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] (j' : Œπ') : (K.extend e).HasHomology j' := by
  by_cases h : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h
    infer_instance
  ¬∑ have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' (by tauto)
    exact ShortComplex.HasHomology.mk'
      (ShortComplex.HomologyData.ofZeros _ (hj.eq_of_tgt _ _) (hj.eq_of_src _ _))

end extend

lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' :=
  exactAt_of_isSupported _ e j' hj'

section

variable {j : Œπ} {j' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j') [K.HasHomology j] [L.HasHomology j]
  [(K.extend e).HasHomology j'] [(L.extend e).HasHomology j']

/-- The isomorphism `(K.extend e).cycles j' ‚âÖ K.cycles j` when `e.f j = j'`. -/
noncomputable def extendCyclesIso :
    (K.extend e).cycles j' ‚âÖ K.cycles j :=
  (extend.homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl (K.sc j).homologyData).left.cyclesIso ‚â™‚â´
    (K.sc j).homologyData.left.cyclesIso.symm

/-- The isomorphism `(K.extend e).opcycles j' ‚âÖ K.opcycles j` when `e.f j = j'`. -/
noncomputable def extendOpcyclesIso :
    (K.extend e).opcycles j' ‚âÖ K.opcycles j :=
  (extend.homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl (K.sc j).homologyData).right.opcyclesIso ‚â™‚â´
    (K.sc j).homologyData.right.opcyclesIso.symm

/-- The isomorphism `(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j` when `e.f j = j'`. -/
noncomputable def extendHomologyIso :
    (K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j :=
  (extend.homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl (K.sc j).homologyData).left.homologyIso ‚â™‚â´
    (K.sc j).homologyData.left.homologyIso.symm

include hj' in
lemma extend_exactAt_iff :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j := by
  simp only [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology]
  exact (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').isZero_iff

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendCyclesIso_hom_iCycles :
    (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').hom ‚â´ K.iCycles j =
      (K.extend e).iCycles j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom := by
  rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').inv, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  dsimp [extendCyclesIso, iCycles]
  rw [assoc, ShortComplex.LeftHomologyData.cyclesIso_inv_comp_iCycles_assoc]
  dsimp
  rw [assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id,
    ShortComplex.LeftHomologyData.cyclesIso_hom_comp_i]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendCyclesIso_inv_iCycles :
    (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).iCycles j' =
      K.iCycles j ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv := by
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc,
    extendCyclesIso_hom_iCycles_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma homologyœÄ_extendHomologyIso_hom :
    (K.extend e).homologyœÄ j' ‚â´ (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom =
      (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').hom ‚â´ K.homologyœÄ j := by
  dsimp [extendHomologyIso, homologyœÄ]
  rw [ShortComplex.LeftHomologyData.homologyœÄ_comp_homologyIso_hom_assoc,
    ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.sc j).homologyData.left.homologyIso.hom,
    assoc, assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id,
    ShortComplex.LeftHomologyData.homologyœÄ_comp_homologyIso_hom]
  dsimp [extendCyclesIso]
  simp only [assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma homologyœÄ_extendHomologyIso_inv :
    K.homologyœÄ j ‚â´ (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').inv =
      (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).homologyœÄ j' := by
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom,
    assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id, homologyœÄ_extendHomologyIso_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pOpcycles_extendOpcyclesIso_inv :
    K.pOpcycles j ‚â´ (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').inv =
      (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).pOpcycles j' := by
  rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').hom, assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id]
  dsimp [extendOpcyclesIso, pOpcycles]
  rw [ShortComplex.RightHomologyData.pOpcycles_comp_opcyclesIso_hom_assoc]
  dsimp
  rw [assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, ShortComplex.RightHomologyData.p_comp_opcyclesIso_inv]
  rfl

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pOpcycles_extendOpcyclesIso_hom :
    (K.extend e).pOpcycles j' ‚â´ (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').hom =
      (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ K.pOpcycles j := by
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').inv,
    assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id, pOpcycles_extendOpcyclesIso_inv, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendHomologyIso_hom_homologyŒπ :
    (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom ‚â´ K.homologyŒπ j =
      (K.extend e).homologyŒπ j' ‚â´ (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').hom := by
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_epi ((K.extend e).homologyœÄ j'),
    homologyœÄ_extendHomologyIso_hom_assoc, homology_œÄ_Œπ, extendCyclesIso_hom_iCycles_assoc,
    homology_œÄ_Œπ_assoc, pOpcycles_extendOpcyclesIso_hom]

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendHomologyIso_inv_homologyŒπ :
    (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).homologyŒπ j' =
      K.homologyŒπ j ‚â´ (K.extendOpcyclesIso e hj').inv := by
  simp only [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom,
    Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, extendHomologyIso_hom_homologyŒπ_assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]

variable {K L}

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendCyclesIso_hom_naturality :
    cyclesMap (extendMap œÜ e) j' ‚â´ (L.extendCyclesIso e hj').hom =
      (K.extendCyclesIso e hj').hom ‚â´ cyclesMap œÜ j := by
  simp [‚Üê cancel_mono (L.iCycles j), extendMap_f œÜ e hj']

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma extendHomologyIso_hom_naturality :
    homologyMap (extendMap œÜ e) j' ‚â´ (L.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom =
      (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').hom ‚â´ homologyMap œÜ j := by
  simp [‚Üê cancel_epi ((K.extend e).homologyœÄ _)]

include hj' in
lemma quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff :
    QuasiIsoAt (extendMap œÜ e) j' ‚Üî QuasiIsoAt œÜ j := by
  simp only [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
  exact (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C).arrow_mk_iso_iff
    (Arrow.isoMk (K.extendHomologyIso e hj') (L.extendHomologyIso e hj'))

end
formal_statement:
lemma quasiIso_extendMap_iff [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] [‚àÄ j, L.HasHomology j] :
    QuasiIso (extendMap œÜ e) ‚Üî QuasiIso œÜ
name: HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_extendMap_iff
responses:
To complete the proof of `quasiIso_extendMap_iff`, we use the fact that `extendMap œÜ e` is a quasi-isomorphism if and only if it is a quasi-isomorphism at every degree $j'$. For $j'$ in the image of $e.f$, this reduces to the quasi-isomorphism condition for $\phi$ at the corresponding $j$ via `quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff`. For $j'$ not in the image, the homology of the extension is zero, which is trivially isomorphic to the zero homology (the definition of a quasi-isomorphism at a zero object).

FINAL```
lemma quasiIso_extendMap_iff [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] [‚àÄ j, L.HasHomology j] :
    QuasiIso (extendMap œÜ e) ‚Üî QuasiIso œÜ := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j
    have : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := inferInstance
    have : (L.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := inferInstance
    rw [‚Üê quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e (by rfl)] at h
    exact h.quasiIsoAt j
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j'
    by_cases h' : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h'
      rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e (by rfl)]
      exact h.quasiIsoAt j
    ¬∑ have : (K.extend e).Homology j' ‚âÖ 0 :=
        (isZero_extend_homology K e j' (by tauto)).isZero.isoZero
      have : (L.extend e).Homology j' ‚âÖ 0 :=
        (isZero_extend_homology L e j' (by tauto)).isZero.isoZero
      exact ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C).arrow_mk_iso_iff
        (Arrow.isoMk this.symm this.symm)).mpr (isIso_zero _)
```

To complete the formalization of the quasi-isomorphism property for the extended complex, we utilize the previously defined `quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff`. Given that a map of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism if and only if it is a quasi-isomorphism at every degree, and given the extension property, we can relate the global quasi-isomorphism property of `extendMap œÜ e` to that of `œÜ` provided the extension preserves the homology structure.

FINAL```
  := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply QuasiIso.of_quasiIsoAt
    intro j
    have : QuasiIsoAt (extendMap œÜ e) (e.f j) := h.1 (e.f j)
    rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl] at this
    exact this
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply QuasiIso.of_quasiIsoAt
    intro j'
    by_cases h' : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h'
      rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl]
      exact h.1 j
    ¬∑ exact QuasiIsoAt.of_isZero_homology (isZero_homology_extend_X K e j' (by tauto))
        (isZero_homology_extend_X L e j' (by tauto))
```

This proof establishes that the extension functor on homological complexes preserves quasi-isomorphisms at specific degrees and globally. By using the constructed isomorphisms between the homology of the original complex and the extended complex, we show that the extension map induces an isomorphism on homology, which characterizes quasi-isomorphisms.

FINAL```
lemma quasiIso_extendMap_iff [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] [‚àÄ j, L.HasHomology j] :
    QuasiIso (extendMap œÜ e) ‚Üî QuasiIso œÜ := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j
    have : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology K e rfl
    have : (L.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology L e rfl
    rw [‚Üê quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl]
    exact h.quasiIsoAt (e.f j)
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j'
    by_cases h' : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h'
      have : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology K e rfl
      have : (L.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology L e rfl
      rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl]
      exact h.quasiIsoAt j
    ¬∑ have hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j' := by simpa
      have hK := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology K e j'
      have hL := HomologicalComplex.extend.hasHomology L e j'
      have hK0 : IsZero ((K.extend e).homology j') := (exactAt_of_isSupported K e j' hj').isZero_homology
      have hL0 : IsZero ((L.extend e).homology j') := (exactAt_of_isSupported L e j' hj').isZero_homology
      rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
      exact IsZero.isIso (hK0.homologyMap (extendMap œÜ e) j') (hL0.homologyMap (extendMap œÜ e) j')
```

To complete the formalization, we use the fact that an `extendMap` is a quasi-isomorphism if and only if it is a quasi-isomorphism at every degree `j'`. For indices in the image of `e.f`, we use `quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff`. For indices not in the image, the complex is zero, and we check the isomorphism of homology groups.

FINAL```
lemma quasiIso_extendMap_iff [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] [‚àÄ j, L.HasHomology j] :
    QuasiIso (extendMap œÜ e) ‚Üî QuasiIso œÜ := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [QuasiIso.iff_quasiIsoAt] at h ‚ä¢
    intro j
    have : (extendMap œÜ e).quasiIsoAt (e.f j) := h (e.f j)
    rwa [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl] at this
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [QuasiIso.iff_quasiIsoAt] at h ‚ä¢
    intro j'
    by_cases hj' : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := hj'
      rwa [‚Üê quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e rfl]
    ¬∑ have hK := (K.extend e).isZero_homology j' (extend_exactAt K e j' (fun _ h => hj' ‚ü®_, h‚ü©))
      have hL := (L.extend e).isZero_homology j' (extend_exactAt L e j' (fun _ h => hj' ‚ü®_, h‚ü©))
      exact (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms C).isIso_of_isZero hK hL
```
theorem
theorem

To complete the proof of `quasiIso_extendMap_iff`, we note that a map is a quasi-isomorphism if and only if it is a quasi-isomorphism at every degree. The extension `extendMap œÜ e` acts as zero in degrees not in the image of `e.f`, which are also the degrees where the homology is zero, while it behaves as `œÜ` in degrees where `e.f j = j'`. We can therefore conclude by checking the quasi-isomorphism condition at each degree `j'` using the previously defined `quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff`.

FINAL```
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j
    have : QuasiIsoAt (extendMap œÜ e) (e.f j) := h.quasiIsoAt (e.f j)
    rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e (e.f j) rfl] at this
    exact this
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j'
    by_cases h' : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h'
      rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e _ rfl]
      exact h.quasiIsoAt j
    ¬∑ have hK := isZero_extend_X K e j' (by tauto)
      have hL := isZero_extend_X L e j' (by tauto)
      have hH_K := (K.extend e).homology_isZero j' (by exact hK)
      have hH_L := (L.extend e).homology_isZero j' (by exact hL)
      rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
      have : homologyMap (extendMap œÜ e) j' = 0 := by
        rw [isZero_iff_id_eq_zero.mp (isZero_homology_of_isZero_X _ hK),
            isZero_iff_id_eq_zero.mp (isZero_homology_of_isZero_X _ hL)]
        simp
      rw [this]
      infer_instance
```

To complete the proof of `quasiIso_extendMap_iff`, we note that a morphism between homological complexes is a quasi-isomorphism if and only if it induces an isomorphism on homology at every degree. Using the result `quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff` established for the degrees in the image of `e.f` and the fact that homology is zero in other degrees, the equivalence follows naturally.

FINAL```
lemma quasiIso_extendMap_iff [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] [‚àÄ j, L.HasHomology j] :
    QuasiIso (extendMap œÜ e) ‚Üî QuasiIso œÜ := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j
    have : QuasiIsoAt (extendMap œÜ e) (e.f j) := h.quasiIsoAt (e.f j)
    rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e (rfl : e.f j = e.f j)] at this
    exact this
  ¬∑ intro h
    constructor
    intro j'
    by_cases hj : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
    ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := hj
      rw [quasiIsoAt_extendMap_iff e (rfl : e.f j = e.f j)]
      exact h.quasiIsoAt j
    ¬∑ have hK := isZero_extend_X K e j' (by tauto)
      have hL := isZero_extend_X L e j' (by tauto)
      have hH_K := ((K.extend e).homology j').isZero_of_isZero_X hK
      have hH_L := ((L.extend e).homology j').isZero_of_isZero_X hL
      rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isZero_homologyMap]
      exact isIso_of_isZero hH_K hH_L
```
