/-
Copyright (c) 2018 Kenny Lau. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Kenny Lau, Chris Hughes, Anne Baanen
-/
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Block
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Notation
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.RowCol
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.GroupAction.Ring
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.Perm.Fin
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Alternating.Basic
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.SemiringInverse

/-!
# Determinant of a matrix

This file defines the determinant of a matrix, `Matrix.det`, and its essential properties.

## Main definitions

 - `Matrix.det`: the determinant of a square matrix, as a sum over permutations
 - `Matrix.detRowAlternating`: the determinant, as an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix

## Main results

 - `det_mul`: the determinant of `A * B` is the product of determinants
 - `det_zero_of_row_eq`: the determinant is zero if there is a repeated row
 - `det_block_diagonal`: the determinant of a block diagonal matrix is a product
   of the blocks' determinants

## Implementation notes

It is possible to configure `simp` to compute determinants. See the file
`MathlibTest/matrix.lean` for some examples.

-/


universe u v w z

open Equiv Equiv.Perm Finset Function

namespace Matrix

variable {m n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] [DecidableEq m] [Fintype m]
variable {R : Type v} [CommRing R]

local notation "Œµ " œÉ:arg => ((sign œÉ : ‚Ñ§) : R)

/-- `det` is an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix. -/
def detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization ((MultilinearMap.mkPiAlgebra R n R).compLinearMap LinearMap.proj)

/-- The determinant of a matrix given by the Leibniz formula. -/
abbrev det (M : Matrix n n R) : R :=
  detRowAlternating M

theorem det_apply (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Equiv.Perm.sign œÉ ‚Ä¢ ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization_apply _ M

-- This is what the old definition was. We use it to avoid having to change the old proofs below
theorem det_apply' (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i := by
  simp [det_apply, Units.smul_def]

theorem det_eq_detp_sub_detp (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = M.detp 1 - M.detp (-1) := by
  rw [det_apply, ‚Üê Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.inv (Perm n)), ‚Üê ofSign_disjUnion, sum_disjUnion]
  simp_rw [inv_apply, sign_inv, sub_eq_add_neg, detp, ‚Üê sum_neg_distrib]
  refine congr_arg‚ÇÇ (¬∑ + ¬∑) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) <;>
    rw [mem_ofSign.mp hœÉ, ‚Üê Equiv.prod_comp œÉ] <;> simp

@[simp]
theorem det_diagonal {d : n ‚Üí R} : det (diagonal d) = ‚àè i, d i := by
  rw [det_apply']
  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single 1 ?_ ?_).trans ?_
  ¬∑ rintro œÉ - h2
    cases' not_forall.1 (mt Equiv.ext h2) with x h3
    convert mul_zero (Œµ œÉ)
    apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ x)
    exact if_neg h3
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simp

theorem det_zero (_ : Nonempty n) : det (0 : Matrix n n R) = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_zero

@[simp]
theorem det_one : det (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1 := by rw [‚Üê diagonal_one]; simp [-diagonal_one]

theorem det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] {A : Matrix n n R} : det A = 1 := by simp [det_apply]

@[simp]
theorem coe_det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] : (det : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = Function.const _ 1 := by
  ext
  exact det_isEmpty

theorem det_eq_one_of_card_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 0) : det A = 1 :=
  haveI : IsEmpty n := Fintype.card_eq_zero_iff.mp h
  det_isEmpty

/-- If `n` has only one element, the determinant of an `n` by `n` matrix is just that element.
Although `Unique` implies `DecidableEq` and `Fintype`, the instances might
not be syntactically equal. Thus, we need to fill in the args explicitly. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_unique {n : Type*} [Unique n] [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det A = A default default := by simp [det_apply, univ_unique]

theorem det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton [Subsingleton n] (A : Matrix n n R) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k := by
  have := uniqueOfSubsingleton k
  convert det_unique A

theorem det_eq_elem_of_card_eq_one {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 1) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k :=
  haveI : Subsingleton n := Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton.mp h.le
  det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton _ _

theorem det_mul_aux {M N : Matrix n n R} {p : n ‚Üí n} (H : ¬¨Bijective p) :
    (‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x) * N (p x) x) = 0 := by
  obtain ‚ü®i, j, hpij, hij‚ü© : ‚àÉ i j, p i = p j ‚àß i ‚âÝ j := by
    rw [‚Üê Finite.injective_iff_bijective, Injective] at H
    push_neg at H
    exact H
  exact
    sum_involution (fun œÉ _ => œÉ * Equiv.swap i j)
      (fun œÉ _ => by
        have : (‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x)) = ‚àè x, M ((œÉ * Equiv.swap i j) x) (p x) :=
          Fintype.prod_equiv (swap i j) _ _ (by simp [apply_swap_eq_self hpij])
        simp [this, sign_swap hij, -sign_swap', prod_mul_distrib])
      (fun œÉ _ _ => (not_congr mul_swap_eq_iff).mpr hij) (fun _ _ => mem_univ _) fun œÉ _ =>
      mul_swap_involutive i j œÉ

@[simp]
theorem det_mul (M N : Matrix n n R) : det (M * N) = det M * det N :=
  calc
    det (M * N) = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      simp only [det_apply', mul_apply, prod_univ_sum, mul_sum, Fintype.piFinset_univ]
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
    _ = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n with Bijective p, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      refine (sum_subset (filter_subset _ _) fun f _ hbij ‚Ü¶ det_mul_aux ?_).symm
      simpa only [true_and, mem_filter, mem_univ] using hbij
    _ = ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (œÑ i) * N (œÑ i) i :=
      sum_bij (fun p h ‚Ü¶ Equiv.ofBijective p (mem_filter.1 h).2) (fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ mem_univ _)
        (fun _ _ _ _ h ‚Ü¶ by injection h)
        (fun b _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®b, mem_filter.2 ‚ü®mem_univ _, b.bijective‚ü©, coe_fn_injective rfl‚ü©) fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ rfl
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * Œµ œÑ * ‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j) := by
      simp only [mul_comm, mul_left_comm, prod_mul_distrib, mul_assoc]
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * (Œµ œÉ * Œµ œÑ) * ‚àè i, M (œÑ i) i :=
      (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ _ =>
        Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.mulRight œÉ‚Åª¬π) _ _ fun œÑ => by
          have : (‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j)) = ‚àè j, M ((œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) j) j := by
            rw [‚Üê (œÉ‚Åª¬π : _ ‚âÉ _).prod_comp]
            simp only [Equiv.Perm.coe_mul, apply_inv_self, Function.comp_apply]
          have h : Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ œÑ :=
            calc
              Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π * œÉ) := by
                rw [mul_comm, sign_mul (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π)]
                simp only [Int.cast_mul, Units.val_mul]
              _ = Œµ œÑ := by simp only [inv_mul_cancel_right]

          simp_rw [Equiv.coe_mulRight, h]
          simp only [this])
    _ = det M * det N := by
      simp only [det_apply', Finset.mul_sum, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]

/-- The determinant of a matrix, as a monoid homomorphism. -/
def detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí* R where
  toFun := det
  map_one' := det_one
  map_mul' := det_mul

@[simp]
theorem coe_detMonoidHom : (detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = det :=
  rfl

/-- On square matrices, `mul_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_comm (M N : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N) = det (N * M) := by
  rw [det_mul, det_mul, mul_comm]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_left_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_left_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * (N * P)) = det (N * (M * P)) := by
  rw [‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, ‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm M N, ‚Üê det_mul]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_right_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_right_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N * P) = det (M * P * N) := by
  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc, Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm N P, ‚Üê det_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M.val * N * M‚Åª¬π.val) = det N := by
  rw [det_mul_right_comm, Units.mul_inv, one_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj' (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M‚Åª¬π.val * N * ‚ÜëM.val) = det N :=
  det_units_conj M‚Åª¬π N

/-- Transposing a matrix preserves the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_transpose (M : Matrix n n R) : M·µÄ.det = M.det := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  refine Fintype.sum_bijective _ inv_involutive.bijective _ _ ?_
  intro œÉ
  rw [sign_inv]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv œÉ
  simp

/-- Permuting the columns changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix œÉ id).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det :=
  ((detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_perm M œÉ).trans (by simp [Units.smul_def])

/-- Permuting the rows changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute' (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix id œÉ).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, transpose_submatrix, det_permute, det_transpose]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with the same equivalence does not change the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_submatrix_equiv_self (e : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    det (A.submatrix e e) = det A := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  apply Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.permCongr e)
  intro œÉ
  rw [Equiv.Perm.sign_permCongr e œÉ]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv e
  intro i
  rw [Equiv.permCongr_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply, submatrix_apply]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with two equivalences does not change the absolute value of the
determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem abs_det_submatrix_equiv_equiv {R : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommRing R]
    (e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    |(A.submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ).det| = |A.det| := by
  have hee : e‚ÇÇ = e‚ÇÅ.trans (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ) := by ext; simp
  rw [hee]
  show |((A.submatrix id (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ)).submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÅ).det| = |A.det|
  rw [Matrix.det_submatrix_equiv_self, Matrix.det_permute', abs_mul, abs_unit_intCast, one_mul]

/-- Reindexing both indices along the same equivalence preserves the determinant.

For the `simp` version of this lemma, see `det_submatrix_equiv_self`; this one is unsuitable because
`Matrix.reindex_apply` unfolds `reindex` first.
-/
theorem det_reindex_self (e : m ‚âÉ n) (A : Matrix m m R) : det (reindex e e A) = det A :=
  det_submatrix_equiv_self e.symm A

theorem det_smul (A : Matrix n n R) (c : R) : det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A :=
  calc
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = det ((diagonal fun _ => c) * A) := by rw [smul_eq_diagonal_mul]
    _ = det (diagonal fun _ => c) * det A := det_mul _ _
    _ = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by simp [card_univ]

@[simp]
theorem det_smul_of_tower {Œ±} [Monoid Œ±] [DistribMulAction Œ± R] [IsScalarTower Œ± R R]
    [SMulCommClass Œ± R R] (c : Œ±) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê smul_one_smul R c A, det_smul, smul_pow, one_pow, smul_mul_assoc, one_mul]

theorem det_neg (A : Matrix n n R) : det (-A) = (-1) ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul, neg_one_smul]

/-- A variant of `Matrix.det_neg` with scalar multiplication by `Units ‚Ñ§` instead of multiplication
by `R`. -/
theorem det_neg_eq_smul (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (-A) = (-1 : Units ‚Ñ§) ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul_of_tower, Units.neg_smul, one_smul]

/-- Multiplying each row by a fixed `v i` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_row (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  calc
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = det (A * diagonal v) :=
      congr_arg det <| by
        ext
        simp [mul_comm]
    _ = (‚àè i, v i) * det A := by rw [det_mul, det_diagonal, mul_comm]

/-- Multiplying each column by a fixed `v j` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_column (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v i * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  MultilinearMap.map_smul_univ _ v A

@[simp]
theorem det_pow (M : Matrix m m R) (n : ‚Ñï) : det (M ^ n) = det M ^ n :=
  (detMonoidHom : Matrix m m R ‚Üí* R).map_pow M n

section HomMap

variable {S : Type w} [CommRing S]

theorem _root_.RingHom.map_det (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) := by
  simp [Matrix.det_apply', map_sum f, map_prod f]

theorem _root_.RingEquiv.map_det (f : R ‚âÉ+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgHom.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T] (f : S ‚Üí‚Çê[R] T)
    (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgEquiv.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T]
    (f : S ‚âÉ‚Çê[R] T) (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toAlgHom.map_det _

end HomMap

@[simp]
theorem det_conjTranspose [StarRing R] (M : Matrix m m R) : det M·¥¥ = star (det M) :=
  ((starRingEnd R).map_det _).symm.trans <| congr_arg star M.det_transpose

section DetZero

/-!
### `det_zero` section

Prove that a matrix with a repeated column has determinant equal to zero.
-/


theorem det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (i : n) (h : ‚àÄ j, A i j = 0) : det A = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_coord_zero i (funext h)

theorem det_eq_zero_of_column_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (j : n) (h : ‚àÄ i, A i j = 0) :
    det A = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose]
  exact det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero j h

variable {M : Matrix n n R} {i j : n}

/-- If a matrix has a repeated row, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_row_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : M i = M j) : M.det = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_eq_zero_of_eq M hij i_ne_j

/-- If a matrix has a repeated column, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_column_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : ‚àÄ k, M k i = M k j) : M.det = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, det_zero_of_row_eq i_ne_j]
  exact funext hij

/-- If we repeat a row of a matrix, we get a matrix of determinant zero. -/
theorem det_updateRow_eq_zero (h : i ‚âÝ j) :
    (M.updateRow j (M i)).det = 0 := det_zero_of_row_eq h (by simp [h])

/-- If we repeat a column of a matrix, we get a matrix of determinant zero. -/
theorem det_updateCol_eq_zero (h : i ‚âÝ j) :
    (M.updateCol j (fun k ‚Ü¶ M k i)).det = 0 := det_zero_of_column_eq h (by simp [h])

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_eq_zero := det_updateCol_eq_zero

end DetZero

theorem det_updateRow_add (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (u v : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow M j <| u + v) = det (updateRow M j u) + det (updateRow M j v) :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_update_add M j u v

theorem det_updateCol_add (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (u v : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol M j <| u + v) = det (updateCol M j u) + det (updateCol M j v) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, det_updateRow_add]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_add := det_updateCol_add

theorem det_updateRow_smul (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow M j <| s ‚Ä¢ u) = s * det (updateRow M j u) :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_update_smul M j s u

theorem det_updateCol_smul (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol M j <| s ‚Ä¢ u) = s * det (updateCol M j u) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, det_updateRow_smul]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul := det_updateCol_smul

theorem det_updateRow_smul_left (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow (s ‚Ä¢ M) j u) = s ^ (Fintype.card n - 1) * det (updateRow M j u) :=
  MultilinearMap.map_update_smul_left _ M j s u

@[deprecated (since := "2024-11-03")] alias det_updateRow_smul' := det_updateRow_smul_left

theorem det_updateCol_smul_left (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol (s ‚Ä¢ M) j u) = s ^ (Fintype.card n - 1) * det (updateCol M j u) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, transpose_smul, det_updateRow_smul_left]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul' := det_updateCol_smul_left
@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul_left := det_updateCol_smul_left

theorem det_updateRow_sum_aux (M : Matrix n n R) {j : n} (s : Finset n) (hj : j ‚àâ s) (c : n ‚Üí R)
    (a : R) :
    (M.updateRow j (a ‚Ä¢ M j + ‚àë k ‚àà s, (c k) ‚Ä¢ M k)).det = a ‚Ä¢ M.det := by
  induction s using Finset.induction_on with
  | empty => rw [Finset.sum_empty, add_zero, smul_eq_mul, det_updateRow_smul, updateRow_eq_self]
  | @insert k _ hk h_ind =>
      have h : k ‚âÝ j := fun h ‚Ü¶ (h ‚ñ∏ hj) (Finset.mem_insert_self _ _)
      rw [Finset.sum_insert hk, add_comm ((c k) ‚Ä¢ M k), ‚Üê add_assoc, det_updateRow_add,
        det_updateRow_smul, det_updateRow_eq_zero h, mul_zero, add_zero, h_ind]
      exact fun h ‚Ü¶ hj (Finset.mem_insert_of_mem h)

/-- If we replace a row of a matrix by a linear combination of its rows, then the determinant is
multiplied by the coefficient of that row. -/
theorem det_updateRow_sum (A : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (c : n ‚Üí R) :
    (A.updateRow j (‚àë k, (c k) ‚Ä¢ A k)).det = (c j) ‚Ä¢ A.det := by
  convert det_updateRow_sum_aux A (Finset.univ.erase j) (Finset.univ.not_mem_erase j) c (c j)
  rw [‚Üê Finset.univ.add_sum_erase _ (Finset.mem_univ j)]

/-- If we replace a column of a matrix by a linear combination of its columns, then the determinant
is multiplied by the coefficient of that column. -/
theorem det_updateCol_sum (A : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (c : n ‚Üí R) :
    (A.updateCol j (fun k ‚Ü¶ ‚àë i, (c i) ‚Ä¢ A k i)).det = (c j) ‚Ä¢ A.det

import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Pi
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Field

/-!
# Etale algebras over fields

## Main results

Let `K` be a field, `A` be a `K`-algebra and `L` be a field extension of `K`.

- `Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable`:
    If `L` is separable over `K`, then `L` is formally √©tale over `K`.
- `Algebra.FormallyEtale.iff_isSeparable`:
    If `L` is (essentially) of finite type over `K`, then `L/K` is √©tale iff `L/K` is separable.
- `Algebra.FormallyEtale.iff_exists_algEquiv_prod`:
    If `A` is (essentially) of finite type over `K`,
    then `A/K` is √©tale iff `A` is a finite product of separable field extensions.
- `Algebra.Etale.iff_exists_algEquiv_prod`:
    `A/K` is √©tale iff `A` is a finite product of finite separable field extensions.

## References

- [B. Iversen, *Generic Local Structure of the Morphisms in Commutative Algebra*][iversen]

-/


universe u

variable (K L A : Type u) [Field K] [Field L] [CommRing A] [Algebra K L] [Algebra K A]

open Algebra Polynomial

open scoped TensorProduct

namespace Algebra.FormallyEtale

/--
This is a weaker version of `of_isSeparable` that additionally assumes `EssFiniteType K L`.
Use that instead.

This is Iversen Corollary II.5.3.
-/
theorem of_isSeparable_aux [Algebra.IsSeparable K L] [EssFiniteType K L] :
    FormallyEtale K L := by
  -- We already know that for field extensions
  -- IsSeparable + EssFiniteType => FormallyUnramified + Finite
  have := FormallyUnramified.of_isSeparable K L
  have := FormallyUnramified.finite_of_free (R := K) (S := L)
  constructor
  -- We shall show that any `f : L ‚Üí B/I` can be lifted to `L ‚Üí B` if `I^2 = ‚ä•`
  intros B _ _ I h
  refine ‚ü®FormallyUnramified.iff_comp_injective.mp (FormallyUnramified.of_isSeparable K L) I h, ?_‚ü©
  intro f
  -- By separability and finiteness, we may assume `L = K(Œ±)` with `p` the minpoly of `Œ±`.
  let pb := Field.powerBasisOfFiniteOfSeparable K L
  -- Let `x : B` such that `f(Œ±) = x` in `B / I`.
  obtain ‚ü®x, hx‚ü© := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective (f pb.gen)
  have helper : ‚àÄ x, IsScalarTower.toAlgHom K B (B ‚ß∏ I) x = Ideal.Quotient.mk I x := fun _ ‚Ü¶ rfl
  -- Then `p(x) = 0 mod I`, and the goal is to find some `Œµ ‚àà I` such that
  -- `p(x + Œµ) = p(x) + Œµ p'(x) = 0`, and we will get our lift into `B`.
  have hx' : Ideal.Quotient.mk I (aeval x (minpoly K pb.gen)) = 0 := by
    rw [‚Üê helper, ‚Üê aeval_algHom_apply, helper, hx, aeval_algHom_apply, minpoly.aeval, map_zero]
  -- Since `p` is separable, `-p'(x)` is invertible in `B ‚ß∏ I`,
  obtain ‚ü®u, hu‚ü© : ‚àÉ u, (aeval x) (derivative (minpoly K pb.gen)) * u + 1 ‚àà I := by
    have := (isUnit_iff_ne_zero.mpr ((Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable K
      pb.gen).aeval_derivative_ne_zero (minpoly.aeval K _))).map f
    rw [‚Üê aeval_algHom_apply, ‚Üê hx, ‚Üê helper, aeval_algHom_apply, helper] at this
    obtain ‚ü®u, hu‚ü© := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective (-this.unit‚Åª¬π : B ‚ß∏ I)
    use u
    rw [‚Üê Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem, map_add, map_mul, map_one, hu, mul_neg,
      IsUnit.mul_val_inv, neg_add_cancel]
  -- And `Œµ = p(x)/(-p'(x))` works.
  use pb.liftEquiv.symm ‚ü®x + u * aeval x (minpoly K pb.gen), ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ apply pb.algHom_ext
    simp [hx, hx']
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê eval_map_algebraMap, Polynomial.eval_add_of_sq_eq_zero, derivative_map,
      ‚Üê one_mul (eval x _), eval_map_algebraMap, eval_map_algebraMap, ‚Üê mul_assoc, ‚Üê add_mul,
      ‚Üê Ideal.mem_bot, ‚Üê h, pow_two, add_comm]
    ¬∑ exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul hu (Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mp hx')
    rw [‚Üê Ideal.mem_bot, ‚Üê h]
    apply Ideal.pow_mem_pow

lemma of_isSeparable [Algebra.IsSeparable K L] : FormallyEtale K L := by
  constructor
  intros B _ _ I h

  -- But we already know that there exists a unique lift for every finite subfield of `L`
  -- by `of_isSeparable_aux`, so we can glue them all together.

  intro f
  have : ‚àÄ k : L, ‚àÉ! g : K‚üÆk‚üØ ‚Üí‚Çê[K] B,
      (Ideal.Quotient.mk‚Çê K I).comp g = f.comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom K _ L) := by
    intro k
    have := IsSeparable.of_algHom _ _ (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom K (K‚üÆk‚üØ) L)
    have := IntermediateField.adjoin.finiteDimensional
      (Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable K k).isIntegral
    have := FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable_aux K (K‚üÆk‚üØ)
    have := FormallyEtale.comp_bijective (R := K) (A := K‚üÆk‚üØ) I h
    exact this.existsUnique _
  choose g hg‚ÇÅ hg‚ÇÇ using this
  have hg‚ÇÉ : ‚àÄ x y (h : x ‚àà K‚üÆy‚üØ), g y ‚ü®x, h‚ü© = g x (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen K x) := by
    intro x y h
    have e : K‚üÆx‚üØ ‚â§ K‚üÆy‚üØ := by
      rw [IntermediateField.adjoin_le_iff]
      rintro _ rfl
      exact h
    rw [‚Üê hg‚ÇÇ _ ((g _).comp (IntermediateField.inclusion e))]
    ¬∑ rfl
    apply AlgHom.ext
    intro ‚ü®a, _‚ü©
    rw [‚Üê AlgHom.comp_assoc, hg‚ÇÅ, AlgHom.comp_assoc]
    simp
  have H : ‚àÄ x y : L, ‚àÉ Œ± : L, x ‚àà K‚üÆŒ±‚üØ ‚àß y ‚àà K‚üÆŒ±‚üØ := by
    intro x y
    have : FiniteDimensional K K‚üÆx, y‚üØ := by
      apply IntermediateField.finiteDimensional_adjoin
      intro x _; exact (Algebra.IsSeparable.isSeparable K x).isIntegral
    have := IsSeparable.of_algHom _ _ (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom K (K‚üÆx, y‚üØ) L)
    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®Œ±, hŒ±‚ü©, e‚ü© := Field.exists_primitive_element K K‚üÆx,y‚üØ
    apply_fun (IntermediateField.map (IntermediateField.val _)) at e

    simp only [IntermediateField.coe_val, Set.image_singleton,
      IntermediateField.fieldRange_val] at e
    have hx : x ‚àà K‚üÆŒ±‚üØ := e ‚ñ∏ IntermediateField.subset_adjoin K {x, y} (by simp)
    have hy : y ‚àà K‚üÆŒ±‚üØ := e ‚ñ∏ IntermediateField.subset_adjoin K {x, y} (by simp)
    exact ‚ü®Œ±, hx, hy‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®fun x ‚Ü¶ g x (IntermediateField.AdjoinSimple.gen K x), ?_‚ü©, ?_‚ü©, ?_, ?_‚ü©, ?_‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ show g 1 1 = 1; rw [map_one]

    rfl
  ¬∑ show g 0 0 = 0; rw [map_zero]
  ¬∑ intros x y

    simp only [‚Üê hg‚ÇÉ _ _ hx, ‚Üê hg‚ÇÉ _ _ hy, ‚Üê map_add, ‚Üê hg‚ÇÉ _ _ (add_mem hx hy)]

theorem iff_isSeparable [EssFiniteType K L] :
    FormallyEtale K L ‚Üî Algebra.IsSeparable K L :=
  ‚ü®fun _ ‚Ü¶ FormallyUnramified.isSeparable K L, fun _ ‚Ü¶ of_isSeparable K L‚ü©

attribute [local instance] IsArtinianRing.fieldOfSubtypeIsMaximal in
/--
If `A` is an essentially of finite type algebra over a field `K`, then `A` is formally √©tale
over `K` if and only if `A` is a finite product of separable field extensions.
-/
theorem iff_exists_algEquiv_prod [EssFiniteType K A] :
    FormallyEtale K A ‚Üî
      ‚àÉ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (Ai : I ‚Üí Type u) (_ : ‚àÄ i, Field (Ai i))
        (_ : ‚àÄ i, Algebra K (Ai i)) (_ : A ‚âÉ‚Çê[K] ŒÝ i, Ai i),
        ‚àÄ i, Algebra.IsSeparable K (Ai i) := by
  classical
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro H
    have := FormallyUnramified.finite_of_free K A
    have := FormallyUnramified.isReduced_of_field K A
    have : IsArtinianRing A := isArtinian_of_tower K inferInstance
    letI : Fintype (MaximalSpectrum A) := (nonempty_fintype _).some
    let v (i : MaximalSpectrum A) : A := (IsArtinianRing.equivPi A).symm (Pi.single i 1)
    let e : A ‚âÉ‚Çê[K] _ := { __ := IsArtinianRing.equivPi A, commutes' := fun r ‚Ü¶ rfl }
    have := (FormallyEtale.iff_of_equiv e).mp inferInstance
    rw [FormallyEtale.pi_iff] at this
    exact ‚ü®_, inferInstance, _, _, _, e, fun I ‚Ü¶ (iff_isSeparable _ _).mp inferInstance‚ü©
  ¬∑ intro ‚ü®I, _, Ai, _, _, e, _‚ü©
    rw [FormallyEtale.iff_of_equiv e, FormallyEtale.pi_iff]
    have (i) : EssFiniteType K (Ai i) := by
      letI := ((Pi.evalRingHom Ai i).comp e.toRingHom).toAlgebra
      have : IsScalarTower K A (Ai i) :=
        .of_algebraMap_eq fun r ‚Ü¶ by simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
      have : Algebra.FiniteType A (Ai i) := .of_surjective inferInstance (Algebra.ofId _ _)
        (RingHomSurjective.is_surjective (œÉ := Pi.evalRingHom Ai i).comp e.surjective)
      exact EssFiniteType.comp K A (Ai i)
    exact fun I ‚Ü¶ (iff_isSeparable _ _).mpr inferInstance

end Algebra.FormallyEtale

/-
Copyright (c) 2024 Xavier Roblot. All rights reserved.
Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Xavier Roblot
-/
import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.UnitPartition
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

/-!
# Covolume of ‚Ñ§-lattices

Let `E` be a finite dimensional real vector space.

Let `L` be a `‚Ñ§`-lattice `L` defined as a discrete `‚Ñ§`-submodule of `E` that spans `E` over `‚Ñù`.

## Main definitions and results

* `ZLattice.covolume`: the covolume of `L` defined as the volume of an arbitrary fundamental
domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain`: the covolume of `L` does not depend on the
choice of the fundamental domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_det`: if `L` is a lattice in `‚Ñù^n`, then its covolume is the absolute
value of the determinant of any `‚Ñ§`-basis of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow`: Let `s` be a bounded measurable set of `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, then
the number of points in `s ‚à© n‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L` divided by `n ^ card Œπ` tends to `volume s / covolume L`
when `n : ‚Ñï` tends to infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'` for a version
for `InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''` for the general version.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div`: Let `X` be a cone in `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and let `F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`
be a function such that `F (c ‚Ä¢ x) = c ^ card Œπ * F x`. Then the number of points `x ‚àà X` such that
`F x ‚â§ c` divided by `c` tends to `volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} / covolume L` when `c : ‚Ñù` tends to
infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'` for a version for
`InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div''` for the general version.

## Naming convention

Some results are true in the case where the ambient finite dimensional real vector space is the
pi-space `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and in the case where it is an `InnerProductSpace`. We use the following
convention: the plain name is for the pi case, for eg. `volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`. For
the same result in the `InnerProductSpace` case, we add a `prime`, for eg.
`volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume'`. When the same result exists in the
general case, we had two primes, eg. `covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''`.

-/

noncomputable section

namespace ZLattice

open Submodule MeasureTheory Module MeasureTheory Module ZSpan

section General

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [MeasurableSpace E] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E)

/-- The covolume of a `‚Ñ§`-lattice is the volume of some fundamental domain; see
`ZLattice.covolume_eq_volume` for the proof that the volume does not depend on the choice of
the fundamental domain. -/
def covolume (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) : ‚Ñù := (addCovolume L E Œº).toReal

end General

section Basic

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E]
variable [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure Œº]

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)
  exact congr_arg ENNReal.toReal (h.covolume_eq_volume Œº)

theorem covolume_ne_zero : covolume L Œº ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    ENNReal.toReal_ne_zero]
  refine ‚ü®measure_fundamentalDomain_ne_zero _, ne_of_lt ?_‚ü©
  exact Bornology.IsBounded.measure_lt_top (fundamentalDomain_isBounded _)

theorem covolume_pos : 0 < covolume L Œº :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne ENNReal.toReal_nonneg (covolume_ne_zero L Œº).symm

theorem covolume_comap {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù F] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù F]
    [MeasurableSpace F] [BorelSpace F] (ŒΩ : Measure F := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure ŒΩ]
    {e : F ‚âÉL[‚Ñù] E} (he : MeasurePreserving e ŒΩ Œº) :
    covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearMap) ŒΩ = covolume L Œº := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ ((isAddFundamentalDomain
    ((Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L).ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearEquiv) ŒΩ)), ‚Üê he.measure_preimage
    (fundamentalDomain_measurableSet _).nullMeasurableSet, ‚Üê e.image_symm_eq_preimage,
    ‚Üê e.symm.coe_toLinearEquiv, map_fundamentalDomain]
  congr!
  ext; simp

theorem covolume_eq_det_mul_measure {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (b‚ÇÄ : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) :
    covolume L Œº = |b‚ÇÄ.det ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)| * (Œº (fundamentalDomain b‚ÇÄ)).toReal := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain b Œº),
    measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ b‚ÇÄ,
    measure_congr (fundamentalDomain_ae_parallelepiped b‚ÇÄ Œº), ENNReal.toReal_mul,
    ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(Matrix.of ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)).det| := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L volume (isAddFundamentalDomain b volume),
    volume_fundamentalDomain, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext1
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det_inv {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(LinearEquiv.det (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun : ‚Ñù)|‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b, ‚Üê Pi.basisFun_det_apply, show (((‚Üë) : L ‚Üí _) ‚àò ‚áëb) =
    (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù) by ext; simp, ‚Üê Basis.det_inv, ‚Üê abs_inv, Units.val_inv_eq_inv_val,
    IsUnit.unit_spec, ‚Üê Basis.det_basis, LinearEquiv.coe_det]
  rfl

theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, Measure.addHaar_preimage_linearEquiv, LinearEquiv.symm_symm,
    covolume_eq_det_inv L b, ENNReal.div_eq_inv_mul, ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos
    (abs_pos.mpr (LinearEquiv.det _).ne_zero), inv_inv, LinearEquiv.coe_det]

/-- A more general version of `ZLattice.volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    [InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set E} (hs : NullMeasurableSet s) :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  classical
  let e : Fin (finrank ‚Ñù E) ‚âÉ Œπ :=
    Fintype.equivOfCardEq (by rw [Fintype.card_fin, finrank_eq_card_basis (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù)])
  let f := (EuclideanSpace.equiv Œπ ‚Ñù).symm.trans
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).repr.toContinuousLinearEquiv.symm
  have hf : MeasurePreserving f :=
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).measurePreserving_repr_symm.comp
      (EuclideanSpace.volume_preserving_measurableEquiv Œπ).symm
  rw [‚Üê hf.measure_preimage hs, ‚Üê (covolume_comap L volume volume hf),
    ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearMap)
    (b.ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearEquiv), Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_comap,
    ‚Üê f.image_symm_eq_preimage, ‚Üê Set.image_comp]
  simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_toLinearEquiv, Basis.map_equivFun,
    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply,
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv, ContinuousLinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

end Basic

namespace covolume

section General

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology BoxIntegral Bornology

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E]
variable {L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E} [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div` for the general case;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1})) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
      Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / (c : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal)

theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1})) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
      Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / (c : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal / covolume L) := by
  let n := Fintype.card Œπ
  have hn : (n : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0 := Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (card_pos_iff.mpr ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫).ne'
  rw [tendsto_atTop_atTop_iff_inv_tendsto_zero]
  let f (c : ‚Ñù) := Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / c
  let g (t : ‚Ñù) := Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ t^n} ‚à© L : Set E) / t^n
  have h_eq : ‚àÄ t > 0, g t = f (t^n) := fun t ht ‚Ü¶ rfl
  have h_g : Tendsto (fun t : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ t^n} ‚à© L : Set E) / t^n) atTop
    (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal / covolume L) := by
    have : ‚àÄ t : ‚Ñù, 0 < t ‚Üí {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ t^n} ‚à© L = t ‚Ä¢ ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} ‚à© L) := by
      intro t ht
      rw [‚Üê Set.smul_inter, tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ ht]
    simp_rw [this, Nat.card_smul_set_eq_of_isZLattice _ _ (by positivity),
      div_self (pow_ne_zero n ht.ne'), div_one]
    convert tendsto_card_div_pow'' b h‚ÇÇ h‚ÇÉ h‚ÇÑ
    rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' L b (measurableSet_image.mp h‚ÇÉ)]
    rw [ENNReal.toReal_div, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (covolume_pos L).le]
  apply (atTop_pow_atTop (by exact Nat.card_pos_of_nonempty)).comp h_g
theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})

To complete the proof of `tendsto_card_le_div''`, we need to express the volume of the set $\{x \in X \mid F(x) \le c\}$ in terms of the volume of the unit set $\{x \in X \mid F(x) \le 1\}$ using the homogeneity property of the measure and the function $F$. 

Given $F(r \cdot x) = r^d F(x)$ where $d = \text{card } \iota$, for a target value $v$, the set $\{x \in X \mid F(x) \le v\}$ is the image of the unit set under the scalar multiplication by $v^{1/d}$. By the change of variables formula for Lebesgue measure, scaling a set in a $d$-dimensional space by $r$ scales its volume by $r^d$. Since $F(r^{1/d} \cdot x) = (r^{1/d})^d F(x) = r F(x)$, we have $F(x) \le c \iff F(c^{-1/d} \cdot x) \le 1$. Thus, the volume scales by $(c^{1/d})^d = c$.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Hom.Monoid
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Maps
import Mathlib.Tactic.TFAE

/-!

# The basics of valuation theory.

The basic theory of valuations (non-archimedean norms) on a commutative ring,
following T. Wedhorn's unpublished notes ‚ÄúAdic Spaces‚Äù ([wedhorn_adic]).

The definition of a valuation we use here is Definition 1.22 of [wedhorn_adic].
A valuation on a ring `R` is a monoid homomorphism `v` to a linearly ordered
commutative monoid with zero, that in addition satisfies the following two axioms:
 * `v 0 = 0`
 * `‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y)`

`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`is the type of valuations `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`, with a coercion to the underlying
function. If `v` is a valuation from `R` to `Œì‚ÇÄ` then the induced group
homomorphism `Units(R) ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is called `unit_map v`.

The equivalence "relation" `IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Prop` defined in 1.27 of [wedhorn_adic] is not strictly
speaking a relation, because `v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÅ` and `v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÇ` might
not have the same type. This corresponds in ZFC to the set-theoretic difficulty
that the class of all valuations (as `Œì‚ÇÄ` varies) on a ring `R` is not a set.
The "relation" is however reflexive, symmetric and transitive in the obvious
sense. Note that we use 1.27(iii) of [wedhorn_adic] as the definition of equivalence.

## Main definitions

* `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of valuations on `R` with values in `Œì‚ÇÄ`
* `Valuation.IsEquiv`, the heterogeneous equivalence relation on valuations
* `Valuation.supp`, the support of a valuation

* `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of additive valuations on `R` with values in a
  linearly ordered additive commutative group with a top element, `Œì‚ÇÄ`.

## Implementation Details

`AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` is implemented as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`.

## Notation

In the `DiscreteValuation` locale:

 * `‚Ñï‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñï)`
 * `‚Ñ§‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñ§)`

## TODO

If ever someone extends `Valuation`, we should fully comply to the `DFunLike` by migrating the
boilerplate lemmas to `ValuationClass`.
-/

open Function Ideal

noncomputable section

variable {K F R : Type*} [DivisionRing K]

section

variable (F R) (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued valuations on `R`.

When you extend this structure, make sure to extend `ValuationClass`. -/
structure Valuation extends R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max' : ‚àÄ x y, toFun (x + y) ‚â§ max (toFun x) (toFun y)

/-- `ValuationClass F Œ± Œ≤` states that `F` is a type of valuations.

You should also extend this typeclass when you extend `Valuation`. -/
class ValuationClass (F) (R Œì‚ÇÄ : outParam Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]
  [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ]
  extends MonoidWithZeroHomClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ : Prop where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max (f : F) (x y : R) : f (x + y) ‚â§ max (f x) (f y)

export ValuationClass (map_add_le_max)

instance [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ] [ValuationClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ] : CoeTC F (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    { toFun := f
      map_one' := map_one f
      map_zero' := map_zero f
      map_mul' := map_mul f
      map_add_le_max' := map_add_le_max f }‚ü©

end

namespace Valuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì''‚ÇÄ : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì''‚ÇÄ]

section Basic

variable [Ring R]

section Monoid

variable [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]

instance : FunLike (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe f := f.toFun
  coe_injective' f g h := by
    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®_,_‚ü©, _‚ü©, _‚ü© := f
    congr

instance : ValuationClass (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  map_mul f := f.map_mul'
  map_one f := f.map_one'
  map_zero f := f.map_zero'
  map_add_le_max f := f.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem coe_mk (f : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) (h) : ‚áë(Valuation.mk f h) = f := rfl

theorem toFun_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : v.toFun = v := rfl

@[simp]
theorem toMonoidWithZeroHom_coe_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    (v.toMonoidWithZeroHom : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

@[ext]
theorem ext {v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : ‚àÄ r, v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÇ r) : v‚ÇÅ = v‚ÇÇ :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ h

variable (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_coe : ‚áë(v : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

theorem map_zero : v 0 = 0 :=
  v.map_zero'

theorem map_one : v 1 = 1 :=
  v.map_one'

theorem map_mul : ‚àÄ x y, v (x * y) = v x * v y :=
  v.map_mul'

-- Porting note: LHS side simplified so created map_add'
theorem map_add : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  v.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem map_add' : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ v x ‚à® v (x + y) ‚â§ v y := by
  intro x y
  rw [‚Üê le_max_iff, ‚Üê ge_iff_le]
  apply map_add

theorem map_add_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x + y) ‚â§ g :=
  le_trans (v.map_add x y) <| max_le hx hy

theorem map_add_lt {x y g} (hx : v x < g) (hy : v y < g) : v (x + y) < g :=
  lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) <| max_lt hx hy

theorem map_sum_le {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) ‚â§ g) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) ‚â§ g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ zero_le')
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_le hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g ‚âÝ 0)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ (zero_lt_iff.2 hg))
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_lt hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt' {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : 0 < g)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g :=
  v.map_sum_lt (ne_of_gt hg) hf

theorem map_pow : ‚àÄ (x) (n : ‚Ñï), v (x ^ n) = v x ^ n :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_pow

-- The following definition is not an instance, because we have more than one `v` on a given `R`.
-- In addition, type class inference would not be able to infer `v`.
/-- A valuation gives a preorder on the underlying ring. -/
def toPreorder : Preorder R :=
  Preorder.lift v

/-- If `v` is a valuation on a division ring then `v(x) = 0` iff `x = 0`. -/
theorem zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 :=
  map_eq_zero v

theorem ne_zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 :=
  map_ne_zero v

lemma pos_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : 0 < v x ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem unit_map_eq (u : RÀ£) : (Units.map (v : R ‚Üí* Œì‚ÇÄ) u : Œì‚ÇÄ) = v u :=
  rfl

theorem ne_zero_of_unit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : KÀ£) : v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simp only [ne_eq, Valuation.zero_iff, Units.ne_zero x, not_false_iff]

theorem ne_zero_of_isUnit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) (hx : IsUnit x) :
    v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simpa [hx.choose_spec] using ne_zero_of_unit v hx.choose

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
def comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  { v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.comp f.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := v ‚àò f
    map_add_le_max' := fun x y => by simp only [comp_apply, map_add, f.map_add] }

@[simp]
theorem comap_apply {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (s : S) :
    v.comap f s = v (f s) := rfl

@[simp]
theorem comap_id : v.comap (RingHom.id R) = v :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`.
-/
def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ :=
  { MonoidWithZeroHom.comp f v.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := f ‚àò v
    map_add_le_max' := fun r s =>
      calc
        f (v (r + s)) ‚â§ f (max (v r) (v s)) := hf (v.map_add r s)
        _ = max (f (v r)) (f (v s)) := hf.map_max
         }

@[simp]
lemma map_apply (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (r : R) :
    v.map f hf r = f (v r) := rfl

/-- Two valuations on `R` are defined to be equivalent if they induce the same preorder on `R`. -/
def IsEquiv (v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ r s, v‚ÇÅ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÇ s

@[simp]
theorem map_neg (x : R) : v (-x) = v x :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_neg x

theorem map_sub_swap (x y : R) : v (x - y) = v (y - x) :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_sub_swap x y

theorem map_sub (x y : R) : v (x - y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  calc
    v (x - y) = v (x + -y) := by rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
    _ ‚â§ max (v x) (v <| -y) := v.map_add _ _
    _ = max (v x) (v y) := by rw [map_neg]

theorem map_sub_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x - y) ‚â§ g := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
  exact v.map_add_le hx (le_trans (le_of_eq (v.map_neg y)) hy)

variable {x y : R}

theorem map_add_of_distinct_val (h : v x ‚âÝ v y) : v (x + y) = max (v x) (v y) := by
  suffices ¬¨v (x + y) < max (v x) (v y) from
    or_iff_not_imp_right.1 (le_iff_eq_or_lt.1 (v.map_add x y)) this
  intro h'
  wlog vyx : v y < v x generalizing x y
  ¬∑ refine this h.symm ?_ (h.lt_or_lt.resolve_right vyx)
    rwa [add_comm, max_comm]
  rw [max_eq_left_of_lt vyx] at h'
  apply lt_irrefl (v x)
  calc
    v x = v (x + y - y) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ max (v <| x + y) (v y) := map_sub _ _ _
    _ < v x := max_lt h' vyx

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x + y) = v y :=
  (v.map_add_of_distinct_val h.ne).trans (max_eq_right_iff.mpr h.le)

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x + y) = v x := by
  rw [add_comm]; exact map_add_eq_of_lt_right _ h

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x - y) = v y := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_right, map_neg]
  rwa [map_neg]

open scoped Classical in
theorem map_sum_eq_of_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {j : Œπ}
    (hj : j ‚àà s) (h0 : v (f j) ‚âÝ 0) (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s \ {j}, v (f i) < v (f j)) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) = v (f j) := by
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton hj]
  exact map_add_eq_of_lt_left _ (map_sum_lt _ h0 hf)

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x - y) = v x := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_left]
  rwa [map_neg]

theorem map_eq_of_sub_lt (h : v (y - x) < v x) : v y = v x := by
  have := Valuation.map_add_of_distinct_val v (ne_of_gt h).symm
  rw [max_eq_right (le_of_lt h)] at this
  simpa using this

theorem map_one_add_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one] at h
  simpa only [v.map_one] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

theorem map_one_sub_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 - x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one, ‚Üê v.map_neg] at h
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg 1 x]
  simpa only [v.map_one, v.map_neg] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

/-- An ordered monoid isomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces an equivalence
`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`. -/
def congr (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := map f f.toOrderIso.monotone
  invFun := map f.symm f.toOrderIso.symm.monotone
  left_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp
  right_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp

end Monoid

section Group

variable [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : R}

theorem map_inv {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x, v x‚Åª¬π = (v x)‚Åª¬π :=
  map_inv‚ÇÄ _

theorem map_div {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x y, v (x / y) = v x / v y :=
  map_div‚ÇÄ _

theorem one_lt_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 < v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  simp [inv_lt_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem one_le_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 ‚â§ v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  simp [inv_le_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_lt_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x < 1 ‚Üî 1 < v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_lt_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_le_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî 1 ‚â§ v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_le_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_eq_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 1 ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π = 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [map_inv‚ÇÄ, inv_eq_one]
  ¬∑ simpa only [le_antisymm_iff, And.comm] using and_congr (one_le_val_iff v h) (val_le_one_iff v h)

theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, zero_lt_one, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_lt_val_iff v h, le_or_lt]

/--
This theorem is a weaker version of `Valuation.val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one`, but more symmetric
in `x` and `x‚Åª¬π`.
-/
theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_le_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_le_val_iff v h, le_total]

/-- The subgroup of elements whose valuation is less than a certain unit. -/
def ltAddSubgroup (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (Œ≥ : Œì‚ÇÄÀ£) : AddSubgroup R where
  carrier := { x | v x < Œ≥ }
  zero_mem' := by simp
  add_mem' {x y} x_in y_in := lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) (max_lt x_in y_in)
  neg_mem' x_in := by rwa [Set.mem_setOf, map_neg]

end Group

end Basic

-- end of section
namespace IsEquiv

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÉ : Valuation R Œì''‚ÇÄ}

@[refl]
theorem refl : v.IsEquiv v := fun _ _ => Iff.refl _

@[symm]
theorem symm (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ := fun _ _ => Iff.symm (h _ _)

@[trans]
theorem trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ) : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ := fun _ _ =>
  Iff.trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ _ _) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ _ _)

theorem of_eq {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : v = v') : v.IsEquiv v' := by subst h; rfl

theorem map {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (inf : Injective f)
    (h : v.IsEquiv v') : (v.map f hf).IsEquiv (v'.map f hf) :=
  let H : StrictMono f := hf.strictMono_of_injective inf
  fun r s =>
  calc
    f (v r) ‚â§ f (v s) ‚Üî v r ‚â§ v s := by rw [H.le_iff_le]
    _ ‚Üî v' r ‚â§ v' s := h r s
    _ ‚Üî f (v' r) ‚â§ f (v' s) := by rw [H.le_iff_le]

/-- `comap` preserves equivalence. -/
theorem comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) :
    (v‚ÇÅ.comap f).IsEquiv (v‚ÇÇ.comap f) := fun r s => h (f r) (f s)

theorem val_eq (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r s : R} : v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r = v‚ÇÇ s := by
  simpa only [le_antisymm_iff] using and_congr (h r s) (h s r)

theorem ne_zero (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r : R} : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ 0 := by
  have : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÅ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÇ 0 := not_congr h.val_eq
  rwa [v‚ÇÅ.map_zero, v‚ÇÇ.map_zero] at this

end IsEquiv

-- end of namespace
section

theorem isEquiv_of_map_strictMono [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] [Ring R] {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ)
    (H : StrictMono f) : IsEquiv (v.map f H.monotone) v := fun _x _y =>
  ‚ü®H.le_iff_le.mp, fun h => H.monotone h‚ü©

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x y : K}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y := by
  simp only [IsEquiv, le_iff_le_iff_lt_iff_lt]
  exact forall_comm

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_iff_lt, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val

theorem isEquiv_of_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ}
    (h : ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1) : v.IsEquiv v' := by
  intro x y
  obtain rfl | hy := eq_or_ne y 0
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê div_le_one‚ÇÄ, ‚Üê v.map_div, h, v'.map_div, div_le_one‚ÇÄ] <;>
      rwa [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1 :=
  ‚ü®fun h x => by simpa using h x 1, isEquiv_of_val_le_one‚ü©

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.le_one_iff_le_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simpa using @IsEquiv.val_eq _ _ _ _ _ _ v v' h x 1
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply isEquiv_of_val_le_one
    intro x
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v'.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v' (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v'.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [‚Üê h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [‚Üê h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.eq_one_iff_eq_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simp only [lt_iff_le_and_ne,
      and_congr h.le_one_iff_le_one h.eq_one_iff_eq_one.not]
  ¬∑ rw [isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one]
    intro h x
    by_cases hx : x = 0
    ¬∑ simp only [(zero_iff _).2 hx, zero_ne_one]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.2 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
          rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
          exact hh.not_lt (h.2 ((one_lt_val_iff v' hx).1 h_2))
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.1 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
        rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
        exact hh.not_lt (h.1 ((one_lt_val_iff v hx).1 h_2))

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_one_iff_lt_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 := by
  rw [isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one]
  exact (Equiv.subRight 1).surjective.forall

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.val_sub_one_lt_one_iff, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_tfae [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
    (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ) :
    [ v.IsEquiv v',
      ‚àÄ {x y}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 ].TFAE := by
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 2 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 3 := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 4 := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 5 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 6 := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one
  tfae_finish

end

section Supp

variable [CommRing R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

/-- The support of a valuation `v : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is the ideal of `R` where `v` vanishes. -/
def supp : Ideal R where
  carrier := { x | v x = 0 }
  zero_mem' := map_zero v
  add_mem' {x y} hx hy := le_zero_iff.mp <|
    calc
      v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) := v.map_add x y
      _ ‚â§ 0 := max_le (le_zero_iff.mpr hx) (le_zero_iff.mpr hy)
  smul_mem' c x hx :=
    calc
      v (c * x) = v c * v x := map_mul v c x
      _ = v c * 0 := congr_arg _ hx
      _ = 0 := mul_zero _

@[simp]
theorem mem_supp_iff (x : R) : x ‚àà supp v ‚Üî v x = 0 :=
  Iff.rfl

/-- The support of a valuation is a prime ideal. -/
instance [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] [NoZeroDivisors Œì‚ÇÄ] : Ideal.IsPrime (supp v) :=
  ‚ü®fun h =>
    one_ne_zero (Œ± := Œì‚ÇÄ) <|
      calc
        1 = v 1 := v.map_one.symm
        _ = 0 := by rw [‚Üê mem_supp_iff, h]; exact Submodule.mem_top,
   fun {x y} hxy => by
    simp only [mem_supp_iff] at hxy ‚ä¢
    rw [v.map_mul x y] at hxy
    exact eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero hxy‚ü©

theorem map_add_supp (a : R) {s : R} (h : s ‚àà supp v) : v (a + s) = v a := by
  have aux : ‚àÄ a s, v s = 0 ‚Üí v (a + s) ‚â§ v a := by
    intro a' s' h'
    refine le_trans (v.map_add a' s') (max_le le_rfl ?_)
    simp [h']
  apply le_antisymm (aux a s h)
  calc
    v a = v (a + s + -s) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ v (a + s) := aux (a + s) (-s) (by rwa [‚Üê Ideal.neg_mem_iff] at h)

theorem comap_supp {S : Type*} [CommRing S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) :
    supp (v.comap f) = Ideal.comap f v.supp :=
  Ideal.ext fun x => by rw [mem_supp_iff, Ideal.mem_comap, mem_supp_iff, comap_apply]

end Supp

-- end of section
end Valuation

section AddMonoid

variable (R) [Ring R] (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued additive valuations on `R`. -/
def AddValuation :=
  Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà)

end AddMonoid

namespace AddValuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*} {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}

section Basic

section Monoid

/-- A valuation is coerced to the underlying function `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
instance (R) (Œì‚ÇÄ) [Ring R] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] :
    FunLike (AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe v := v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toFun
  coe_injective' f g := by cases f; cases g; simp (config := {contextual := true})

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

section

variable (f : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) (h0 : f 0 = ‚ä§) (h1 : f 1 = 0)
variable (hadd : ‚àÄ x y, min (f x) (f y) ‚â§ f (x + y)) (hmul : ‚àÄ x y, f (x * y) = f x + f y)

/-- An alternate constructor of `AddValuation`, that doesn't reference `Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà` -/
def of : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := f
  map_one' := h1
  map_zero' := h0
  map_add_le_max' := hadd
  map_mul' := hmul

variable {h0} {h1} {hadd} {hmul} {r : R}

@[simp]
theorem of_apply : (of f h0 h1 hadd hmul) r = f r := rfl

/-- The `Valuation` associated to an `AddValuation` (useful if the latter is constructed using
`AddValuation.of`). -/
def toValuation : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) :=
  Equiv.refl _

@[deprecated (since := "2024-11-09")]
alias valuation := toValuation

/-- The `AddValuation` associated to a `Valuation`.
-/
def ofValuation : Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) ‚âÉ AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  Equiv.refl _

@[simp]
lemma ofValuation_symm_eq : ofValuation.symm = toValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toValuation_symm_eq : toValuation.symm = ofValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofValuation_toValuation : ofValuation (toValuation v) = v := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toValuation_ofValuation (v : Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà)) :
    toValuation (ofValuation v) = v := rfl

@[simp]
theorem toValuation_apply (r : R) :
    toValuation v r = Multiplicative.ofAdd (OrderDual.toDual (v r)) :=
  rfl

@[deprecated (since := "2024-11-09")]
alias valuation_apply := toValuation_apply

@[simp]
theorem ofValuation_apply (v : Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà)) (r : R) :
    ofValuation v r = OrderDual.ofDual (Multiplicative.toAdd (v r)) :=
  rfl

end

-- Porting note: Lean get confused about namespaces and instances below
@[simp]
theorem map_zero : v 0 = (‚ä§ : Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  Valuation.map_zero v

@[simp]
theorem map_one : v 1 = (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  Valuation.map_one v

/- Porting note: helper wrapper to coerce `v` to the correct function type -/
/-- A helper function for Lean to inferring types correctly -/
def asFun : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ := v

@[simp]
theorem map_mul : ‚àÄ (x y : R), v (x * y) = v x + v y :=
  Valuation.map_mul v

-- Porting note: LHS simplified so created map_add' and removed simp tag
theorem map_add : ‚àÄ (x y : R), min (v x) (v y) ‚â§ v (x + y) :=
  Valuation.map_add v

@[simp]
theorem map_add' : ‚àÄ (x y : R), v x ‚â§ v (x + y) ‚à® v y ‚â§ v (x + y) := by
  intro x y
  rw [‚Üê @min_le_iff _ _ (v x) (v y) (v (x+y)), ‚Üê ge_iff_le]
  apply map_add

theorem map_le_add {x y : R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hx : g ‚â§ v x) (hy : g ‚â§ v y) : g ‚â§ v (x + y) :=
  Valuation.map_add_le v hx hy

theorem map_lt_add {x y : R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hx : g < v x) (hy : g < v y) : g < v (x + y) :=
  Valuation.map_add_lt v hx hy

theorem map_le_sum {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, g ‚â§ v (f i)) :
    g ‚â§ v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) :=
  v.map_sum_le hf

theorem map_lt_sum {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g ‚âÝ ‚ä§)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, g < v (f i)) : g < v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) :=
  v.map_sum_lt hg hf

theorem map_lt_sum' {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g < ‚ä§)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, g < v (f i)) : g < v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) :=
  v.map_sum_lt' hg hf

@[simp]
theorem map_pow : ‚àÄ (x : R) (n : ‚Ñï), v (x ^ n) = n ‚Ä¢ (v x) :=
  Valuation.map_pow v

@[ext]
theorem ext {v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : ‚àÄ r, v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÇ r) : v‚ÇÅ = v‚ÇÇ :=
  Valuation.ext h

-- The following definition is not an instance, because we have more than one `v` on a given `R`.
-- In addition, type class inference would not be able to infer `v`.
/-- A valuation gives a preorder on the underlying ring. -/
def toPreorder : Preorder R :=
  Preorder.lift v

/-- If `v` is an additive valuation on a division ring then `v(x) = ‚ä§` iff `x = 0`. -/
@[simp]
theorem top_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : AddValuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = (‚ä§ : Œì‚ÇÄ) ‚Üî x = 0 :=
  v.zero_iff

theorem ne_top_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : AddValuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x ‚âÝ (‚ä§ : Œì‚ÇÄ) ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 :=
  v.ne_zero_iff

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí AddValuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
def comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : AddValuation S Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  Valuation.comap f v

@[simp]
theorem comap_id : v.comap (RingHom.id R) = v :=
  Valuation.comap_id v

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=
  Valuation.comap_comp v f g

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving, `‚ä§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map
  `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí AddValuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`.
-/
def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí+ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (ht : f ‚ä§ = ‚ä§) (hf : Monotone f) (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    AddValuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ :=
  @Valuation.map R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) (Multiplicative Œì'‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) _ _ _
    { toFun := f
      map_mul' := f.map_add
      map_one' := f.map_zero
      map_zero' := ht } (fun _ _ h => hf h) v

@[simp]
lemma map_apply (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí+ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (ht : f ‚ä§ = ‚ä§) (hf : Monotone f) (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (r : R) :
    v.map f ht hf r = f (v r) := rfl

/-- Two additive valuations on `R` are defined to be equivalent if they induce the same
  preorder on `R`. -/
def IsEquiv (v‚ÇÅ : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (v‚ÇÇ : AddValuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Prop :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ

@[simp]
theorem map_neg (x : R) : v (-x) = v x :=
  Valuation.map_neg v x

theorem map_sub_swap (x y : R) : v (x - y) = v (y - x) :=
  Valuation.map_sub_swap v x y

theorem map_sub (x y : R) : min (v x) (v y) ‚â§ v (x - y) :=
  Valuation.map_sub v x y

theorem map_le_sub {x y : R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hx : g ‚â§ v x) (hy : g ‚â§ v y) : g ‚â§ v (x - y) :=
  Valuation.map_sub_le v hx hy

variable {x y : R}

theorem map_add_of_distinct_val (h : v x ‚âÝ v y) : v (x + y) = @Min.min Œì‚ÇÄ _ (v x) (v y) :=
  Valuation.map_add_of_distinct_val v h

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_left {x y : R} (h : v x < v y) :
    v (x + y) = v x := by
  rw [map_add_of_distinct_val _ h.ne, min_eq_left h.le]

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_right {x y : R} (hx : v y < v x) :
    v (x + y) = v y := add_comm y x ‚ñ∏ map_add_eq_of_lt_left v hx

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_left {x y : R} (hx : v x < v y) :
    v (x - y) = v x := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
  apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
  rwa [map_neg]

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_right {x y : R} (hx : v y < v x) :
    v (x - y) = v y := map_sub_swap v x y ‚ñ∏ map_sub_eq_of_lt_left v hx

theorem map_eq_of_lt_sub (h : v x < v (y - x)) : v y = v x :=
  Valuation.map_eq_of_sub_lt v h

end Monoid

section Group

variable [LinearOrderedAddCommGroupWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R] (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : R}

@[simp]
theorem map_inv (v : AddValuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x‚Åª¬π = - (v x) :=
  map_inv‚ÇÄ (toValuation v) x

@[simp]
theorem map_div (v : AddValuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : K} : v (x / y) = v x - v y :=
  map_div‚ÇÄ (toValuation v) x y

end Group

end Basic

namespace IsEquiv

variable [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  [Ring R]
  {Œì''‚ÇÄ : Type*} [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì''‚ÇÄ]
  {v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ}
   {v‚ÇÅ : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÇ : AddValuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÉ : AddValuation R Œì''‚ÇÄ}

@[refl]
theorem refl : v.IsEquiv v :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.refl

@[symm]
theorem symm (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.symm h

@[trans]
theorem trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ) : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.trans h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ

theorem of_eq {v' : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : v = v') : v.IsEquiv v' :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.of_eq h

theorem map {v' : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí+ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (ht : f ‚ä§ = ‚ä§) (hf : Monotone f)
    (inf : Injective f) (h : v.IsEquiv v') : (v.map f ht hf).IsEquiv (v'.map f ht hf) :=
  @Valuation.IsEquiv.map R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) (Multiplicative Œì'‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) _ _ _ _ _
    { toFun := f
      map_mul' := f.map_add
      map_one' := f.map_zero
      map_zero' := ht } (fun _x _y h => hf h) inf h

/-- `comap` preserves equivalence. -/
theorem comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) :
    (v‚ÇÅ.comap f).IsEquiv (v‚ÇÇ.comap f) :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.comap f h

theorem val_eq (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r s : R} : v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r = v‚ÇÇ s :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.val_eq h

theorem ne_top (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r : R} : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ (‚ä§ : Œì‚ÇÄ) ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ (‚ä§ : Œì'‚ÇÄ) :=
  Valuation.IsEquiv.ne_zero h

end IsEquiv

section Supp

variable [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] [CommRing R] (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

/-- The support of an additive valuation `v : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is the ideal of `R` where `v x = ‚ä§` -/
def supp : Ideal R :=
  Valuation.supp v

@[simp]
theorem mem_supp_iff (x : R) : x ‚àà supp v ‚Üî v x = (‚ä§ : Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  Valuation.mem_supp_iff v x

theorem map_add_supp (a : R) {s : R} (h : s ‚àà supp v) : v (a + s) = v a :=
  Valuation.map_add_supp v a h

end Supp

-- end of section
end AddValuation

namespace Valuation

variable {K Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*} [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]

/-- The `AddValuation` associated to a `Valuation`. -/
def toAddValuation : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà :=
  .trans (congr
    { toFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ .ofAdd <| .toDual <| .toDual <| .ofMul x
      invFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ x.toAdd.ofDual.ofDual.toMul
      left_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      right_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_mul' := fun _x _y ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_le_map_iff' := .rfl }) (AddValuation.ofValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà))

/-- The `Valuation` associated to a `AddValuation`.
-/
def ofAddValuation : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  AddValuation.toValuation.trans <| congr <|
    { toFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ x.toAdd.ofDual.ofDual.toMul
      invFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ .ofAdd <| .toDual <| .toDual <| .ofMul x
      left_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      right_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_mul' := fun _x _y ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_le_map_iff' := .rfl }

@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_symm_eq : ofAddValuation.symm = toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toAddValuation_symm_eq : toAddValuation.symm = ofAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

import Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Rat
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.NatAntidiagonal
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Choose.Sum
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.Basic

/-!
# Definition of well-known power series

In this file we define the following power series:

* `PowerSeries.invUnitsSub`: given `u : RÀ£`, this is the series for `1 / (u - x)`.
  It is given by `‚àë n, x ^ n /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1)`.

* `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow`: given a commutative ring `S` and a number `d : ‚Ñï`,
  `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is the multiplicative inverse of `(1 - X) ^ d` in `S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£`.
  When `d` is `0`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will just be `1`. When `d` is positive,
  `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will be `‚àë n, Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1)`.

* `PowerSeries.sin`, `PowerSeries.cos`, `PowerSeries.exp` : power series for sin, cosine, and
  exponential functions.
-/


namespace PowerSeries

section Ring

variable {R S : Type*} [Ring R] [Ring S]

/-- The power series for `1 / (u - x)`. -/
def invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) : PowerSeries R :=
  mk fun n => 1 /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1)

@[simp]
theorem coeff_invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) (n : ‚Ñï) : coeff R n (invUnitsSub u) = 1 /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1) :=
  coeff_mk _ _

@[simp]
theorem constantCoeff_invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) : constantCoeff R (invUnitsSub u) = 1 /‚Çö u := by
  rw [‚Üê coeff_zero_eq_constantCoeff_apply, coeff_invUnitsSub, zero_add, pow_one]

@[simp]
theorem invUnitsSub_mul_X (u : RÀ£) : invUnitsSub u * X = invUnitsSub u * C R u - 1 := by
  ext (_ | n)
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simp [n.succ_ne_zero, pow_succ']

@[simp]
theorem invUnitsSub_mul_sub (u : RÀ£) : invUnitsSub u * (C R u - X) = 1 := by
  simp [mul_sub, sub_sub_cancel]

theorem map_invUnitsSub (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (u : RÀ£) :
    map f (invUnitsSub u) = invUnitsSub (Units.map (f : R ‚Üí* S) u) := by
  ext
  simp only [‚Üê map_pow, coeff_map, coeff_invUnitsSub, one_divp]
  rfl

end Ring

section invOneSubPow

variable (S : Type*) [CommRing S] (d : ‚Ñï)

/--
(1 + X + X^2 + ...) * (1 - X) = 1.

Note that the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...` is written as `mk 1` where `1` is the constant
function so that `mk 1` is the power series with all coefficients equal to one.
-/
theorem mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one : (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) * (1 - X) = 1 := by
  rw [mul_comm, PowerSeries.ext_iff]
  intro n
  cases n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n => simp [sub_mul]

/--
Note that `mk 1` is the constant function `1` so the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...`. This theorem
states that for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `(1 + X + X^2 + ... : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ (d + 1)` is equal to the power series
`mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß`.
-/
theorem mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add :
    (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ (d + 1) = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := by
  induction d with
  | zero => ext; simp
  | succ d hd =>
      ext n
      rw [pow_add, hd, pow_one, mul_comm, coeff_mul]
      simp_rw [coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, one_mul]
      norm_cast
      rw [Finset.sum_antidiagonal_choose_add, add_right_comm]

/--
Given a natural number `d : ‚Ñï` and a commutative ring `S`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is the
multiplicative inverse of `(1 - X) ^ d` in `S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£`. When `d` is `0`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d`
will just be `1`. When `d` is positive, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will be the power series
`mk fun n => Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1)`.
-/
noncomputable def invOneSubPow : ‚Ñï ‚Üí S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£
  | 0 => 1
  | d + 1 => {
    val := mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d
    inv := (1 - X) ^ (d + 1)
    val_inv := by
      rw [‚Üê mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add, ‚Üê mul_pow, mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one, one_pow]
    inv_val := by
      rw [‚Üê mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add, ‚Üê mul_pow, mul_comm, mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one, one_pow]
    }

theorem invOneSubPow_zero : invOneSubPow S 0 = 1 := by
  delta invOneSubPow
  simp only [Units.val_one]

theorem invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos (h : 0 < d) :
    (invOneSubPow S d).val = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1) : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := by
  rw [‚Üê Nat.sub_one_add_one_eq_of_pos h, invOneSubPow, add_tsub_cancel_right]

theorem invOneSubPow_val_succ_eq_mk_add_choose :
    (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).val = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := rfl

theorem invOneSubPow_val_one_eq_invUnitSub_one :
    (invOneSubPow S 1).val = invUnitsSub (1 : SÀ£) := by
  simp [invOneSubPow, invUnitsSub]

/--
The theorem `PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one` implies that `1 - X` is a unit in `S‚ü¶X‚üß`
whose inverse is the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...`. This theorem states that for any `d : ‚Ñï`,
`PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is equal to `(1 - X)‚Åª¬π ^ d`.
-/
theorem invOneSubPow_eq_inv_one_sub_pow :
    invOneSubPow S d =
      (Units.mkOfMulEqOne (1 - X) (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) <|
        Eq.trans (mul_comm _ _) (mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one S))‚Åª¬π ^ d := by
  induction d with
  | zero => exact Eq.symm <| pow_zero _
  | succ d _ =>
      rw [inv_pow]
      exact (DivisionMonoid.inv_eq_of_mul _ (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)) <| by
        rw [‚Üê Units.val_eq_one, Units.val_mul, Units.val_pow_eq_pow_val]
        exact (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).inv_val).symm

theorem invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow :
    (invOneSubPow S d).inv = (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ d := by
  induction d with
  | zero => exact Eq.symm <| pow_zero _
  | succ d => rfl

theorem invOneSubPow_inv_zero_eq_one : (invOneSubPow S 0).inv = 1 := by
  delta invOneSubPow
  simp only [Units.inv_eq_val_inv, inv_one, Units.val_one]

theorem mk_add_choose_mul_one_sub_pow_eq_one :
    (mk fun n ‚Ü¶ Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) * ((1 - X) ^ (d + 1)) = 1 :=
  (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).val_inv

theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  simp_rw [invOneSubPow_eq_inv_one_sub_pow, pow_add]

theorem one_sub_pow_mul_invOneSubPow_val_add_eq_invOneSubPow_val (e : ‚Ñï) :
    (1 - X) ^ e * (invOneSubPow S (d + e)).val = (invOneSubPow S d).val := by
  simp [invOneSubPow_add, Units.val_mul, mul_comm, mul_assoc, ‚Üê invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow]
formal_statement:
theorem one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow (e : ‚Ñï) :
    (1 - X) ^ (d + e) * (invOneSubPow S e).val = (1 - X) ^ d
name: PowerSeries.one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow

  rw [pow_add, mul_assoc, mul_comm ((1 - X) ^ e), ‚Üê mul_assoc, ‚Üê invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow, (invOneSubPow S e).inv_val, one_mul]
theorem one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow (e : ‚Ñï) :

theorem one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow (e : ‚Ñï) :
    (1 - X) ^ (d + e) * (invOneSubPow S e).val = (1 - X) ^ d := by

  rw [pow_add, mul_assoc, mul_comm ((1 - X) ^ e), ‚Üê mul_assoc, (invOneSubPow S e).inv_val, one_mul]
theorem one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow (e : ‚Ñï) :
    (1 - X) ^ (d + e) * (invOneSubPow S e).val = (1 - X) ^ d := by
  rw [pow_add, mul_assoc, mul_comm ((1 - X) ^ e), ‚Üê mul_assoc ((1 - X) ^ e) ((1 - X) ^ e)‚Åª¬π,
    invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow, Units.inv_mul_cancel, mul_one]
theorem one_sub_pow_add_mul_invOneSubPow_val_eq_one_sub_pow (e : ‚Ñï) :
