import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')
    (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi'' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    have : (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom ‚â´ f' = f'' := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      rw [assoc, hf', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, assoc, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hi'' hj']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom, comp_zero, ‚Üê this, assoc]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      simp only [zero_comp, hf', K.shape _ _ hij]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      dsimp
      rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, hf'', zero_comp, zero_comp,
        K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj']
      rw [hi]
      exact hij
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hi hi' in
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 :=
  lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' cone hcone _ (hcone.fac _ _) _
    (IsLimit.fac _ _ WalkingParallelPair.zero) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def cokernelCofork :
    CokernelCofork ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ cocone.œÄ (by
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone]
    exact cocone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork :
    IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff' hcocone _
    (lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone)

end leftHomologyData

open leftHomologyData in
/-- The left homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a left homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def leftHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).LeftHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').LeftHomologyData where
  K := h.K
  H := h.H
  i := h.i ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv
  œÄ := h.œÄ
  wi := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk']
    exact h.wi
  hi := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' _ h.hi
  wœÄ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi]
    exact h.wœÄ
  hœÄ := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi _ h.hœÄ

namespace rightHomologyData

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (K.d i j)) (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

/-- The cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a cokernel
cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def cokernelCofork : CokernelCofork ((K.extend e).d i' j') :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ) (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' cocone.œÄ, cocone.condition])

/-- The colimit cokernel cofork of `(K.extend e).d i' j'` that is deduced from a
colimit cokernel cofork of `K.d i j`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork : IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff hcocone ((K.extend e).d i' j')
    (extendXIso K e hj') (d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi')

variable (cone : KernelFork (hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcone : IsLimit cone)

include hk hk' hcocone in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ K.X k)
    (hf' : cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f' = K.d j k)
    (f'' : cocone.pt ‚ü∂ (K.extend e).X k')
    (hf'' : (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ cocone.œÄ ‚â´ f'' = (K.extend e).d j' k')
    (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ f' = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ f'' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk'' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    have : f' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv = f'' := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [reassoc_of% hf', ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv, zero_comp, assoc, this]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      simp only [hf', comp_zero, K.shape _ _ hjk]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Cofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
      rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, hf'', comp_zero, comp_zero,
        K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj']
      rw [hk]
      exact hjk
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hk hk' in
lemma d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ cocone.pt) :
    œÜ ‚â´ hcocone.desc (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ (K.d j k) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) = 0 ‚Üî
      œÜ ‚â´ ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) = 0 :=
  d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hk hk' cocone hcocone _ (hcocone.fac _ _) _ (by
    simpa using (isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).fac _
      WalkingParallelPair.one) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def kernelFork :
    KernelFork ((isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' cocone hcocone).desc
      (CokernelCofork.ofœÄ ((K.extend e).d j' k') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ cone.Œπ (by
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone]
    exact cone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork :
    IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff' hcone _
    (d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cocone hcocone)

end rightHomologyData

open rightHomologyData in
/-- The right homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a right homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def rightHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').RightHomologyData where
  Q := h.Q
  H := h.H
  p := (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ h.p
  Œπ := h.Œπ
  wp := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi']
    exact h.wp
  hp := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' _ h.hp
  wŒπ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp]
    exact h.wŒπ
  hŒπ := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hp _ h.hŒπ

/-- Computation of the `g'` field of `extend.rightHomologyData`. -/
lemma rightHomologyData_g' (h : (K.sc' i j k).RightHomologyData) (hk'' : e.f k = k') :
    (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).g' = h.g' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hk'').inv := by
  rw [‚Üê cancel_epi h.p, ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom]
  have := (rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h).p_g'
  dsimp at this
  rw [assoc] at this
  rw [this, K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk'', h.p_g'_assoc, shortComplexFunctor'_obj_g]

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def homologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').HomologyData where
  left := leftHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.left
  right := rightHomologyData K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' h.right
  iso := h.iso

/-- The homology data of `(K.extend e).sc j'` that is deduced
from a homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps!]
noncomputable def homologyData' (h : (K.sc' i j k).HomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc j').HomologyData :=
  homologyData K e hj' hi rfl hk rfl h

end HomologyData

lemma hasHomology {j : Œπ} {j' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j') [K.HasHomology j] :
    (K.extend e).HasHomology j' :=
  ShortComplex.HasHomology.mk'
    (homologyData' K e hj' rfl rfl ((K.sc j).homologyData))

instance (j : Œπ) [K.HasHomology j] : (K.extend e).HasHomology (e.f j) :=
  hasHomology K e rfl

instance [‚àÄ j, K.HasHomology j] (j' : Œπ') : (K.extend e).HasHomology j' := by
  by_cases h : ‚àÉ j, e.f j = j'
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü© := h
    infer_instance
  ¬∑ have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' (by tauto)
    exact ShortComplex.HasHomology.mk'
      (ShortComplex.HomologyData.ofZeros _ (hj.eq_of_tgt _ _) (hj.eq_of_src _ _))

end extend

lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j'
name: HomologicalComplex.extend_exactAt
responses:
To finalize the proof that the extension of a homological complex is exact at degrees not in the image of the embedding, we utilize the fact that the object `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero when `j'` is not in the image of `e.f`. Specifically, `isZero_extend_X` provides the zero object status, which implies the short complex at that degree is zero, and thus trivially exact.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  exact ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofZero _ (hj.eq_of_tgt _ _) (hj.eq_of_src _ _)
```

To formalize the proof that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` holds when `j'` is not in the image of `e.f`, we recall that `(K.extend e).X j'` is a zero object under this condition (as shown by `isZero_extend_X`). Since `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero, the homology at `j'` must be zero, which implies exactness at `j'`.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have : IsZero ((K.extend e).X j') := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  exact (ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofIsZero (K.extend e).sc' _ _ _ this).mpr
    (ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofIsZero (K.extend e).sc' _ _ _ this)
```

To complete the proof, we must show that $(K.extend \ e)$ is exact at $j'$ when $j'$ is not in the image of $e.f$. In this case, the object $(K.extend \ e).X \ j'$ is zero by the property of `extend`, and therefore the homology at $j'$ is zero, implying exactness.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  exact ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofIsZero (K.extend e).sc' _ _ _ hj
```

To prove that the extension of a complex is exact at indices not in the image of the embedding `e.f`, we use the fact that the object $(K.\text{extend } e).X j'$ is zero for such $j'$. Since the complex is zero at this degree, the homology at this degree is zero, which implies exactness.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have : IsZero ((K.extend e).X j') := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  exact ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofZero _ (this.eq_of_tgt _ _) (this.eq_of_src _ _)
```

To prove that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` when `j'` is not in the image of `e.f`, we use the fact that the object `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero, which implies that the homology at `j'` is zero. Specifically, the definition of `ExactAt` is equivalent to the homology at `j'` being zero. Since `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero, the short complex at `j'` is zero, and its homology is zero, satisfying the definition of exactness.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  apply ShortComplex.ExactAt.of_isZero_X
  exact hj
```

To prove that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` holds when `j'` is not in the image of `e.f`, we use the fact that the object `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero, which is a consequence of the definition of the extension functor `extend`. If the object at a vertex in a homological complex is zero, the homology (and thus the exactness) at that vertex is trivial.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' := by
  have hj := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  exact ShortComplex.ExactAt.of_isZero_X (K.extend e).sc' j' hj
```

To formalize the proof that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` holds when `j'` is not in the image of `e.f`, we observe that the complex `K.extend e` at degree `j'` is zero (by the definition of the extension functor). Specifically, the complex is zero, and the homology of a zero complex is zero, which satisfies the condition for being exact at that position.

```lean
  apply ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofZeros
  ¬∑ exact isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  ¬∑ exact isZero_extend_X K e (c'.prev j') (by intro j; exact hj' _ (c'.prev_eq' _))
  ¬∑ exact isZero_extend_X K e (c'.next j') (by intro j; exact hj' _ (c'.next_eq' _))

FINAL```

To complete the formalization of the exactness of the extended complex at a degree not in the image of `e.f`, we use the fact that the object `(K.extend e).X j'` is zero, as established by the `isZero_extend_X` lemma. Since the objects are zero, the short complex at this degree is a zero complex, which is trivially exact.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt (j' : Œπ') (hj' : ‚àÄ j, e.f j ‚âÝ j') :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' :=
  let h := isZero_extend_X K e j' hj'
  ShortComplex.ExactAt.ofZero _ (h.eq_of_tgt _ _) (h.eq_of_src _ _)
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Rat
import Mathlib.Algebra.BigOperators.NatAntidiagonal
import Mathlib.Data.Nat.Choose.Sum
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.Basic

/-!
# Definition of well-known power series

In this file we define the following power series:

* `PowerSeries.invUnitsSub`: given `u : RÀ£`, this is the series for `1 / (u - x)`.
  It is given by `‚àë n, x ^ n /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1)`.

* `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow`: given a commutative ring `S` and a number `d : ‚Ñï`,
  `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is the multiplicative inverse of `(1 - X) ^ d` in `S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£`.
  When `d` is `0`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will just be `1`. When `d` is positive,
  `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will be `‚àë n, Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1)`.

* `PowerSeries.sin`, `PowerSeries.cos`, `PowerSeries.exp` : power series for sin, cosine, and
  exponential functions.
-/


namespace PowerSeries

section Ring

variable {R S : Type*} [Ring R] [Ring S]

/-- The power series for `1 / (u - x)`. -/
def invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) : PowerSeries R :=
  mk fun n => 1 /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1)

@[simp]
theorem coeff_invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) (n : ‚Ñï) : coeff R n (invUnitsSub u) = 1 /‚Çö u ^ (n + 1) :=
  coeff_mk _ _

@[simp]
theorem constantCoeff_invUnitsSub (u : RÀ£) : constantCoeff R (invUnitsSub u) = 1 /‚Çö u := by
  rw [‚Üê coeff_zero_eq_constantCoeff_apply, coeff_invUnitsSub, zero_add, pow_one]

@[simp]
theorem invUnitsSub_mul_X (u : RÀ£) : invUnitsSub u * X = invUnitsSub u * C R u - 1 := by
  ext (_ | n)
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simp [n.succ_ne_zero, pow_succ']

@[simp]
theorem invUnitsSub_mul_sub (u : RÀ£) : invUnitsSub u * (C R u - X) = 1 := by
  simp [mul_sub, sub_sub_cancel]

theorem map_invUnitsSub (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (u : RÀ£) :
    map f (invUnitsSub u) = invUnitsSub (Units.map (f : R ‚Üí* S) u) := by
  ext
  simp only [‚Üê map_pow, coeff_map, coeff_invUnitsSub, one_divp]
  rfl

end Ring

section invOneSubPow

variable (S : Type*) [CommRing S] (d : ‚Ñï)

/--
(1 + X + X^2 + ...) * (1 - X) = 1.

Note that the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...` is written as `mk 1` where `1` is the constant
function so that `mk 1` is the power series with all coefficients equal to one.
-/
theorem mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one : (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) * (1 - X) = 1 := by
  rw [mul_comm, PowerSeries.ext_iff]
  intro n
  cases n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n => simp [sub_mul]

/--
Note that `mk 1` is the constant function `1` so the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...`. This theorem
states that for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `(1 + X + X^2 + ... : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ (d + 1)` is equal to the power series
`mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß`.
-/
theorem mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add :
    (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ (d + 1) = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := by
  induction d with
  | zero => ext; simp
  | succ d hd =>
      ext n
      rw [pow_add, hd, pow_one, mul_comm, coeff_mul]
      simp_rw [coeff_mk, Pi.one_apply, one_mul]
      norm_cast
      rw [Finset.sum_antidiagonal_choose_add, add_right_comm]

/--
Given a natural number `d : ‚Ñï` and a commutative ring `S`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is the
multiplicative inverse of `(1 - X) ^ d` in `S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£`. When `d` is `0`, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d`
will just be `1`. When `d` is positive, `PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` will be the power series
`mk fun n => Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1)`.
-/
noncomputable def invOneSubPow : ‚Ñï ‚Üí S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£
  | 0 => 1
  | d + 1 => {
    val := mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d
    inv := (1 - X) ^ (d + 1)
    val_inv := by
      rw [‚Üê mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add, ‚Üê mul_pow, mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one, one_pow]
    inv_val := by
      rw [‚Üê mk_one_pow_eq_mk_choose_add, ‚Üê mul_pow, mul_comm, mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one, one_pow]
    }

theorem invOneSubPow_zero : invOneSubPow S 0 = 1 := by
  delta invOneSubPow
  simp only [Units.val_one]

theorem invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos (h : 0 < d) :
    (invOneSubPow S d).val = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d - 1 + n) (d - 1) : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := by
  rw [‚Üê Nat.sub_one_add_one_eq_of_pos h, invOneSubPow, add_tsub_cancel_right]

theorem invOneSubPow_val_succ_eq_mk_add_choose :
    (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).val = (mk fun n => Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := rfl

theorem invOneSubPow_val_one_eq_invUnitSub_one :
    (invOneSubPow S 1).val = invUnitsSub (1 : SÀ£) := by
  simp [invOneSubPow, invUnitsSub]

/--
The theorem `PowerSeries.mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one` implies that `1 - X` is a unit in `S‚ü¶X‚üß`
whose inverse is the power series `1 + X + X^2 + ...`. This theorem states that for any `d : ‚Ñï`,
`PowerSeries.invOneSubPow S d` is equal to `(1 - X)‚Åª¬π ^ d`.
-/
theorem invOneSubPow_eq_inv_one_sub_pow :
    invOneSubPow S d =
      (Units.mkOfMulEqOne (1 - X) (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) <|
        Eq.trans (mul_comm _ _) (mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one S))‚Åª¬π ^ d := by
  induction d with
  | zero => exact Eq.symm <| pow_zero _
  | succ d _ =>
      rw [inv_pow]
      exact (DivisionMonoid.inv_eq_of_mul _ (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)) <| by
        rw [‚Üê Units.val_eq_one, Units.val_mul, Units.val_pow_eq_pow_val]
        exact (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).inv_val).symm

theorem invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow :
    (invOneSubPow S d).inv = (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ d := by
  induction d with
  | zero => exact Eq.symm <| pow_zero _
  | succ d => rfl

theorem invOneSubPow_inv_zero_eq_one : (invOneSubPow S 0).inv = 1 := by
  delta invOneSubPow
  simp only [Units.inv_eq_val_inv, inv_one, Units.val_one]

theorem mk_add_choose_mul_one_sub_pow_eq_one :
    (mk fun n ‚Ü¶ Nat.choose (d + n) d : S‚ü¶X‚üß) * ((1 - X) ^ (d + 1)) = 1 :=
  (invOneSubPow S (d + 1)).val_inv

theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e
name: PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_add
responses:
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  have h1 : (invOneSubPow S (d + e)).inv = (1 - X) ^ (d + e) := invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow S (d + e)
  have h2 : (invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e).inv = (invOneSubPow S d).inv * (invOneSubPow S e).inv := Units.inv_mul _ _
  rw [h1, h2, invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow, invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow, pow_add]
  exact inv_eq_inv_of_inv_eq_inv (by rw [h1, h2, pow_add])
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  have h : (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£) = Units.mkOfMulEqOne (1 - X) (mk 1) (by rw [mul_comm, mk_one_mul_one_sub_eq_one]) := by rfl
  have hinv : (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£)‚Åª¬π = (mk 1 : S‚ü¶X‚üß) := rfl
  rw [invOneSubPow, invOneSubPow, invOneSubPow]
  show (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£)‚Åª¬π ^ (d + e) = (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£)‚Åª¬π ^ d * (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üßÀ£)‚Åª¬π ^ e
  rw [pow_add]
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  induction d with
  | zero =>
      simp [invOneSubPow_zero]
  | succ d ih =>
      rw [add_assoc, invOneSubPow, invOneSubPow, ‚Üê ih, mul_assoc, ‚Üê Units.mul_pow]
      congr
      rfl
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  have h (n : ‚Ñï) : invOneSubPow S n = (invOneSubPow S 1) ^ n := by
    induction n with
    | zero => exact Eq.symm (pow_zero _)
    | succ k hk =>
      rw [pow_succ, ‚Üê hk]
      delta invOneSubPow
      split_ifs with h1 h2
      ¬∑ simp at h1; subst h1; rfl
      ¬∑ rfl
  rw [h (d + e), h d, h e, pow_add]
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  show (Units.inv (1 - X)^d) * (Units.inv (1 - X)^e) = Units.inv (1 - X)^(d + e)
  rw [pow_add]
  rfl
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  have h (n : ‚Ñï) : (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ n * (invOneSubPow S n).val = 1 := (invOneSubPow S n).inv_val
  have h' (n : ‚Ñï) : (invOneSubPow S n).val * (1 - X : S‚ü¶X‚üß) ^ n = 1 := (invOneSubPow S n).val_inv
  rw [invOneSubPow, invOneSubPow, invOneSubPow]
  cases d with
  | zero => simp [invOneSubPow_zero]
  | succ d =>
    cases e with
    | zero => simp [invOneSubPow_zero]
    | succ e =>
      apply Units.ext
      simp only [Units.val_mul, pow_add]
      rfl
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  have h (n : ‚Ñï) : (invOneSubPow S n).inv = (1 - X) ^ n := invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow S n
  apply Units.ext
  rw [Units.val_mul, ‚Üê inv_inv (invOneSubPow S (d + e)), ‚Üê inv_inv (invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e)]
  simp only [Units.val_inv, h, pow_add]
  rfl
theorem invOneSubPow_add (e : ‚Ñï) :
    invOneSubPow S (d + e) = invOneSubPow S d * invOneSubPow S e := by
  apply Units.ext
  rw [Units.val_mul, ‚Üê Units.inv_eq_inv_iff]
  simp only [invOneSubPow_inv_eq_one_sub_pow, ‚Üê pow_add, add_comm d e]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Basic
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.HomologicalComplex

/-!
# Boundary of an embedding of complex shapes

In the file `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Basic`, given `p : ‚Ñ§`, we have defined
an embedding `embeddingUpIntGE p` of `ComplexShape.up ‚Ñï` in `ComplexShape.up ‚Ñ§`
which sends `n : ‚Ñï` to `p + n`. The (canonical) truncation (`‚â• p`) of
`K : CochainComplex C ‚Ñ§` shall be defined as the extension to `‚Ñ§`
(see `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend`) of
a certain cochain complex indexed by `‚Ñï`:

`Q ‚ü∂ K.X (p + 1) ‚ü∂ K.X (p + 2) ‚ü∂ K.X (p + 3) ‚ü∂ ...`

where in degree `0`, the object `Q` identifies to the cokernel
of `K.X (p - 1) ‚ü∂ K.X p` (this is `K.opcycles p`). In this case,
we see that the degree `0 : ‚Ñï` needs a particular attention when
constructing the truncation.

In this file, more generally, for `e : Embedding c c'`, we define
a predicate `Œπ ‚Üí Prop` named `e.BoundaryGE` which shall be relevant
when constructing the truncation `K.truncGE e` when `e.IsTruncGE`.
In the case of `embeddingUpIntGE p`, we show that `0 : ‚Ñï` is the
only element in this lower boundary. Similarly, we define
`Embedding.BoundaryLE`.

-/

namespace ComplexShape

namespace Embedding

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'} (e : Embedding c c')

/-- The lower boundary of an embedding `e : Embedding c c'`, as a predicate on `Œπ`.
It is satisfied by `j : Œπ` when there exists `i' : Œπ'` not in the image of `e.f`
such that `c'.Rel i' (e.f j)`. -/
def BoundaryGE (j : Œπ) : Prop :=
  c'.Rel (c'.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)

lemma boundaryGE {i' : Œπ'} {j : Œπ} (hj : c'.Rel i' (e.f j)) (hi' : ‚àÄ i, e.f i ‚âÝ i') :
    e.BoundaryGE j := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ simpa only [c'.prev_eq' hj] using hj
  ¬∑ intro i hi
    apply hi' i
    rw [‚Üê c'.prev_eq' hj, c'.prev_eq' hi]

lemma not_boundaryGE_next [e.IsRelIff] {j k : Œπ} (hk : c.Rel j k) :
    ¬¨ e.BoundaryGE k := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE]
  simp only [not_and, not_forall, not_not]
  intro
  exact ‚ü®j, by simpa only [e.rel_iff] using hk‚ü©

lemma not_boundaryGE_next' [e.IsRelIff] {j k : Œπ} (hj : ¬¨ e.BoundaryGE j) (hk : c.next j = k) :
    ¬¨ e.BoundaryGE k := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ exact e.not_boundaryGE_next hjk
  ¬∑ subst hk
    simpa only [c.next_eq_self j hjk] using hj

variable {e} in
lemma BoundaryGE.not_mem {j : Œπ} (hj : e.BoundaryGE j) {i' : Œπ'} (hi' : c'.Rel i' (e.f j))
    (a : Œπ) : e.f a ‚âÝ i' := fun ha =>
  hj.2 a (by simpa only [ha] using hi')

lemma prev_f_of_not_boundaryGE [e.IsRelIff] {i j : Œπ} (hij : c.prev j = i)
    (hj : ¬¨ e.BoundaryGE j) :
    c'.prev (e.f j) = e.f i := by
  by_cases hij' : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ exact c'.prev_eq' (by simpa only [e.rel_iff] using hij')
  ¬∑ obtain rfl : j = i := by
      simpa only [c.prev_eq_self j (by simpa only [hij] using hij')] using hij
    apply c'.prev_eq_self
    intro hj'
    simp only [BoundaryGE, not_and, not_forall, not_not] at hj
    obtain ‚ü®i, hi‚ü© := hj hj'
    rw [e.rel_iff] at hi
    rw [c.prev_eq' hi] at hij
    exact hij' (by simpa only [hij] using hi)

variable {e} in
lemma BoundaryGE.false_of_isTruncLE {j : Œπ} (hj : e.BoundaryGE j) [e.IsTruncLE] : False := by
  obtain ‚ü®i, hi‚ü© := e.mem_prev hj.1
  exact hj.2 i (by simpa only [hi] using hj.1)

/-- The upper boundary of an embedding `e : Embedding c c'`, as a predicate on `Œπ`.
It is satisfied by `j : Œπ` when there exists `k' : Œπ'` not in the image of `e.f`
such that `c'.Rel (e.f j) k'`. -/
def BoundaryLE (j : Œπ) : Prop :=
  c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j)) ‚àß ‚àÄ k, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f k)

lemma boundaryLE {k' : Œπ'} {j : Œπ} (hj : c'.Rel (e.f j) k') (hk' : ‚àÄ i, e.f i ‚âÝ k') :
    e.BoundaryLE j := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ simpa only [c'.next_eq' hj] using hj
  ¬∑ intro k hk
    apply hk' k
    rw [‚Üê c'.next_eq' hj, c'.next_eq' hk]

lemma not_boundaryLE_prev [e.IsRelIff] {i j : Œπ} (hi : c.Rel i j) :
    ¬¨ e.BoundaryLE i := by
  dsimp [BoundaryLE]
  simp only [not_and, not_forall, not_not]
  intro
  exact ‚ü®j, by simpa only [e.rel_iff] using hi‚ü©

lemma not_boundaryLE_prev' [e.IsRelIff] {i j : Œπ} (hj : ¬¨ e.BoundaryLE j) (hk : c.prev j = i) :
    ¬¨ e.BoundaryLE i := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ exact e.not_boundaryLE_prev hij
  ¬∑ subst hk
    simpa only [c.prev_eq_self j hij] using hj

variable {e} in
lemma BoundaryLE.not_mem {j : Œπ} (hj : e.BoundaryLE j) {k' : Œπ'} (hk' : c'.Rel (e.f j) k')
    (a : Œπ) : e.f a ‚âÝ k' := fun ha =>
  hj.2 a (by simpa only [ha] using hk')

lemma next_f_of_not_boundaryLE [e.IsRelIff] {j k : Œπ} (hjk : c.next j = k)
    (hj : ¬¨ e.BoundaryLE j) :
    c'.next (e.f j) = e.f k := by
  by_cases hjk' : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ exact c'.next_eq' (by simpa only [e.rel_iff] using hjk')
  ¬∑ obtain rfl : j = k := by
      simpa only [c.next_eq_self j (by simpa only [hjk] using hjk')] using hjk
    apply c'.next_eq_self
    intro hj'
    simp only [BoundaryLE, not_and, not_forall, not_not] at hj
    obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := hj hj'
    rw [e.rel_iff] at hk
    rw [c.next_eq' hk] at hjk
    exact hjk' (by simpa only [hjk] using hk)

lemma next_f [e.IsTruncGE] {j k : Œπ} (hjk : c.next j = k) : c'.next (e.f j) = e.f k := by
  by_cases hj : c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j))
  ¬∑ obtain ‚ü®k', hk'‚ü© := e.mem_next hj
    rw [‚Üê hk', e.rel_iff] at hj
    rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê c.next_eq' hj, hjk]
  ¬∑ rw [c'.next_eq_self _ hj, ‚Üê hjk, c.next_eq_self j]
    intro hj'
    apply hj
    rw [‚Üê e.rel_iff] at hj'
    simpa only [c'.next_eq' hj'] using hj'

lemma prev_f [e.IsTruncLE] {i j : Œπ} (hij : c.prev j = i) : c'.prev (e.f j) = e.f i :=
  e.op.next_f hij

variable {e} in
lemma BoundaryLE.false_of_isTruncGE {j : Œπ} (hj : e.BoundaryLE j) [e.IsTruncGE] : False := by
  obtain ‚ü®k, hk‚ü© := e.mem_next hj.1
  exact hj.2 k (by simpa only [hk] using hj.1)

@[simp] lemma op_boundaryLE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryLE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryGE j := by rfl
formal_statement: @[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j
name: ComplexShape.Embedding.op_boundaryGE_iff
responses:
To formalize the requested lemma `op_boundaryGE_iff`, we recall the definitions:
- `BoundaryGE j` is defined as `c'.Rel (c'.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)`.
- `BoundaryLE j` is defined as `c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j)) ‚àß ‚àÄ k, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f k)`.
- The `op` operation on `ComplexShape` swaps `Rel`, `prev`, and `next`. Specifically, `(c.op).Rel i j` is `c.Rel j i`, `(c.op).prev` is `c.next`, and `(c.op).next` is `c.prev`.

Given `e.op : Embedding c.op c'.op`, we have `e.op.f = e.f`.
Evaluating `e.op.BoundaryGE j`:
`c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.op.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)`
Substitute the op definitions:
`c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j)) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f i)`
This is exactly `e.BoundaryLE j`.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, Embedding.op]
  simp only [ComplexShape.op_rel, ComplexShape.op_prev, ComplexShape.op_next]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    exact ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    exact ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
```

To prove the final lemma, we recall the definition of `op` for `ComplexShape`. The opposite of an embedding `e : Embedding c c'` involves the opposite relations `c.op` and `c'.op`. By definition, `c'.op.Rel i' j' ‚Üî c'.Rel j' i'`. 

The predicate `BoundaryGE` for `e.op` on the shape `c.op` checks for `i' : Œπ'` such that `c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j)`. Since `c'.op.prev` corresponds to `c'.next` in the original shape, and the conditions are symmetric, `e.op.BoundaryGE` is definitionally equivalent to `e.BoundaryLE`. We provide the formal proof below.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, op, Embedding.op]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa only [c'.op_rel_iff] at h1 
    ¬∑ intro k
      specialize h2 k
      simp only [c'.op_rel_iff]
      exact h2
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa only [‚Üê c'.op_rel_iff] at h1
    ¬∑ intro i
      specialize h2 i
      simp only [‚Üê c'.op_rel_iff]
      exact h2
```

To prove the lemma `op_boundaryGE_iff`, we recall the definitions of `BoundaryGE` and `BoundaryLE` for the opposite embedding `e.op`. By the definition of the opposite complex shape `c.op`, we have `c.op.Rel i j ‚Üî c.Rel j i`, `c.op.prev = c.next`, and `c.op.next = c.prev`.

The property `e.op.BoundaryGE j` is defined as:
`c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.op.f j)) (e.op.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨ c'.op.Rel (e.op.f i) (e.op.f j)`

Using the opposite properties:
1. `c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j)` becomes `c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j))`.
2. `‚àÄ i, ¬¨ c'.op.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)` becomes `‚àÄ i, ¬¨ c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f i)`.

This is exactly the definition of `e.BoundaryLE j`.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, Embedding.op]
  simp only [ComplexShape.op_rel, ComplexShape.op_prev, ComplexShape.op_next]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro k hk
      exact h2 k hk
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro i hi
      exact h2 i hi
```

To prove `e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j`, we refer to the definitions provided in the module.

The definition of `BoundaryGE` for an embedding `e : Embedding c c'` is:
`BoundaryGE j := c'.Rel (c'.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)`

The definition of `BoundaryLE` is:
`BoundaryLE j := c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j)) ‚àß ‚àÄ k, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f k)`

Since `e.op` is the embedding with the opposite complex shapes `c.op` and `c'.op`, the function `f` remains the same. The opposite relation `c'.op.Rel x y` is defined as `c'.Rel y x`. 
Thus, `c'.op.prev` is `c'.next` and `c'.op.next` is `c'.prev`. 
Expanding `e.op.BoundaryGE j`:
`c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.op.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)`
`c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j)) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f i)`
This is exactly the definition of `e.BoundaryLE j`.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, Embedding.op]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro k hk
      apply h2 k
      simp only [ComplexShape.op_rel] at hk
      exact hk
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro i hi
      apply h2 i
      simp only [ComplexShape.op_rel]
      exact hi
```

To complete the proof, we simply show that the `op` operation on the embedding `e` swaps the definitions of `BoundaryGE` and `BoundaryLE`. Since `e.op` is defined such that the underlying relations are reversed, the condition for `BoundaryGE` in `e` maps directly to the condition for `BoundaryLE` in `e.op`.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, Embedding.op]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro k
      specialize h2 k
      rw [not_imp_not]
      intro h
      rw [‚Üê e.op.f_op_f] at h
      exact h
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ exact h1
    ¬∑ intro i
      specialize h2 i
      rw [not_imp_not]
      intro h
      rw [‚Üê e.op.f_op_f] at h
      exact h
```

To prove the lemma `op_boundaryGE_iff`, we recall that `e.op` is the dual embedding where the relation `c.Rel` is replaced by `c.Rel_op`. By the definition of the dual embedding and the definitions of `BoundaryGE` and `BoundaryLE`, we see that:

`e.op.BoundaryGE j` is defined as `c'.Rel_op (c'.prev_op (e.op.f j)) (e.op.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel_op (e.op.f i) (e.op.f j)`.
Since `c'.Rel_op x y ‚Üî c'.Rel y x`, `c'.prev_op` is `c'.next`, and `e.op.f` is `e.f`, this translates directly to the definition of `e.BoundaryLE j`.

FINAL```
lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, op]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa only [c'.Rel_op, c'.next_eq_prev_op] using h1
    ¬∑ simpa only [c'.Rel_op] using h2
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa only [c'.Rel_op, c'.next_eq_prev_op] at h1 ‚ä¢
      exact h1
    ¬∑ simpa only [c'.Rel_op] at h2 ‚ä¢
      exact h2
```

To prove the lemma `op_boundaryGE_iff`, we recall the definitions of `BoundaryGE` and `BoundaryLE` and their relationship under the opposite complex shape `c.op`.

1. `e.op` is defined such that `e.op.f = e.f`.
2. In `ComplexShape.op`, the relation `Rel` is defined as `c.op.Rel j i := c.Rel i j`.
3. Consequently, `c.op.next j` is `c.prev j` and `c.op.prev j` is `c.next j`.
4. `BoundaryGE` for `e.op` involves `c'.op.Rel (c'.op.prev (e.f j)) (e.f j)`, which translates to `c'.Rel (e.f j) (c'.next (e.f j))` in the original shape `c'`.
5. The universal quantifier `‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.op.Rel (e.f i) (e.f j)` translates to `‚àÄ i, ¬¨c'.Rel (e.f j) (e.f i)`, which is exactly the condition for `BoundaryLE`.

Thus, `e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j` holds by definition.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, ComplexShape.op]
  constructor
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa using h1
    ¬∑ intro k
      simpa using h2 k
  ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
    constructor
    ¬∑ simpa using h1
    ¬∑ intro i
      simpa using h2 i
```

The proof of `op_boundaryGE_iff` follows immediately from the definition of `BoundaryGE` and `BoundaryLE` in terms of the opposite complex shape `c.op` and the opposite embedding `e.op`. Specifically, for `j : Œπ`, `e.op.BoundaryGE j` is defined as `c.op.Rel (c.op.prev (e.op.f j)) (e.op.f j) ‚àß ‚àÄ i, ¬¨c.op.Rel (e.op.f i) (e.op.f j)`. Since `c.op.Rel x y` is `c.Rel y x`, `c.op.prev x` is `c.next x`, and `e.op.f` is `e.f`, this simplifies exactly to the definition of `BoundaryLE`.

FINAL```
@[simp] lemma op_boundaryGE_iff {j : Œπ} : e.op.BoundaryGE j ‚Üî e.BoundaryLE j := by
  dsimp [BoundaryGE, BoundaryLE, op]
  simp only [op_rel, op_prev, op_next, f_op]
  rfl
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Hom.Monoid
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Maps
import Mathlib.Tactic.TFAE

/-!

# The basics of valuation theory.

The basic theory of valuations (non-archimedean norms) on a commutative ring,
following T. Wedhorn's unpublished notes ‚ÄúAdic Spaces‚Äù ([wedhorn_adic]).

The definition of a valuation we use here is Definition 1.22 of [wedhorn_adic].
A valuation on a ring `R` is a monoid homomorphism `v` to a linearly ordered
commutative monoid with zero, that in addition satisfies the following two axioms:
 * `v 0 = 0`
 * `‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y)`

`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`is the type of valuations `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`, with a coercion to the underlying
function. If `v` is a valuation from `R` to `Œì‚ÇÄ` then the induced group
homomorphism `Units(R) ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is called `unit_map v`.

The equivalence "relation" `IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Prop` defined in 1.27 of [wedhorn_adic] is not strictly
speaking a relation, because `v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÅ` and `v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÇ` might
not have the same type. This corresponds in ZFC to the set-theoretic difficulty
that the class of all valuations (as `Œì‚ÇÄ` varies) on a ring `R` is not a set.
The "relation" is however reflexive, symmetric and transitive in the obvious
sense. Note that we use 1.27(iii) of [wedhorn_adic] as the definition of equivalence.

## Main definitions

* `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of valuations on `R` with values in `Œì‚ÇÄ`
* `Valuation.IsEquiv`, the heterogeneous equivalence relation on valuations
* `Valuation.supp`, the support of a valuation

* `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of additive valuations on `R` with values in a
  linearly ordered additive commutative group with a top element, `Œì‚ÇÄ`.

## Implementation Details

`AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` is implemented as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`.

## Notation

In the `DiscreteValuation` locale:

 * `‚Ñï‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñï)`
 * `‚Ñ§‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñ§)`

## TODO

If ever someone extends `Valuation`, we should fully comply to the `DFunLike` by migrating the
boilerplate lemmas to `ValuationClass`.
-/

open Function Ideal

noncomputable section

variable {K F R : Type*} [DivisionRing K]

section

variable (F R) (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued valuations on `R`.

When you extend this structure, make sure to extend `ValuationClass`. -/
structure Valuation extends R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max' : ‚àÄ x y, toFun (x + y) ‚â§ max (toFun x) (toFun y)

/-- `ValuationClass F Œ± Œ≤` states that `F` is a type of valuations.

You should also extend this typeclass when you extend `Valuation`. -/
class ValuationClass (F) (R Œì‚ÇÄ : outParam Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]
  [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ]
  extends MonoidWithZeroHomClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ : Prop where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max (f : F) (x y : R) : f (x + y) ‚â§ max (f x) (f y)

export ValuationClass (map_add_le_max)

instance [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ] [ValuationClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ] : CoeTC F (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    { toFun := f
      map_one' := map_one f
      map_zero' := map_zero f
      map_mul' := map_mul f
      map_add_le_max' := map_add_le_max f }‚ü©

end

namespace Valuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì''‚ÇÄ : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì''‚ÇÄ]

section Basic

variable [Ring R]

section Monoid

variable [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]

instance : FunLike (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe f := f.toFun
  coe_injective' f g h := by
    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®_,_‚ü©, _‚ü©, _‚ü© := f
    congr

instance : ValuationClass (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  map_mul f := f.map_mul'
  map_one f := f.map_one'
  map_zero f := f.map_zero'
  map_add_le_max f := f.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem coe_mk (f : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) (h) : ‚áë(Valuation.mk f h) = f := rfl

theorem toFun_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : v.toFun = v := rfl

@[simp]
theorem toMonoidWithZeroHom_coe_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    (v.toMonoidWithZeroHom : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

@[ext]
theorem ext {v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : ‚àÄ r, v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÇ r) : v‚ÇÅ = v‚ÇÇ :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ h

variable (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_coe : ‚áë(v : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

theorem map_zero : v 0 = 0 :=
  v.map_zero'

theorem map_one : v 1 = 1 :=
  v.map_one'

theorem map_mul : ‚àÄ x y, v (x * y) = v x * v y :=
  v.map_mul'

-- Porting note: LHS side simplified so created map_add'
theorem map_add : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  v.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem map_add' : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ v x ‚à® v (x + y) ‚â§ v y := by
  intro x y
  rw [‚Üê le_max_iff, ‚Üê ge_iff_le]
  apply map_add

theorem map_add_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x + y) ‚â§ g :=
  le_trans (v.map_add x y) <| max_le hx hy

theorem map_add_lt {x y g} (hx : v x < g) (hy : v y < g) : v (x + y) < g :=
  lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) <| max_lt hx hy

theorem map_sum_le {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) ‚â§ g) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) ‚â§ g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ zero_le')
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_le hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g ‚âÝ 0)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ (zero_lt_iff.2 hg))
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_lt hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt' {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : 0 < g)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g :=
  v.map_sum_lt (ne_of_gt hg) hf

theorem map_pow : ‚àÄ (x) (n : ‚Ñï), v (x ^ n) = v x ^ n :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_pow

-- The following definition is not an instance, because we have more than one `v` on a given `R`.
-- In addition, type class inference would not be able to infer `v`.
/-- A valuation gives a preorder on the underlying ring. -/
def toPreorder : Preorder R :=
  Preorder.lift v

/-- If `v` is a valuation on a division ring then `v(x) = 0` iff `x = 0`. -/
theorem zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 :=
  map_eq_zero v

theorem ne_zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 :=
  map_ne_zero v

lemma pos_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : 0 < v x ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem unit_map_eq (u : RÀ£) : (Units.map (v : R ‚Üí* Œì‚ÇÄ) u : Œì‚ÇÄ) = v u :=
  rfl

theorem ne_zero_of_unit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : KÀ£) : v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simp only [ne_eq, Valuation.zero_iff, Units.ne_zero x, not_false_iff]

theorem ne_zero_of_isUnit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) (hx : IsUnit x) :
    v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simpa [hx.choose_spec] using ne_zero_of_unit v hx.choose

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
def comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  { v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.comp f.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := v ‚àò f
    map_add_le_max' := fun x y => by simp only [comp_apply, map_add, f.map_add] }

@[simp]
theorem comap_apply {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (s : S) :
    v.comap f s = v (f s) := rfl

@[simp]
theorem comap_id : v.comap (RingHom.id R) = v :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`.
-/
def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ :=
  { MonoidWithZeroHom.comp f v.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := f ‚àò v
    map_add_le_max' := fun r s =>
      calc
        f (v (r + s)) ‚â§ f (max (v r) (v s)) := hf (v.map_add r s)
        _ = max (f (v r)) (f (v s)) := hf.map_max
         }

@[simp]
lemma map_apply (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (r : R) :
    v.map f hf r = f (v r) := rfl

/-- Two valuations on `R` are defined to be equivalent if they induce the same preorder on `R`. -/
def IsEquiv (v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ r s, v‚ÇÅ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÇ s

@[simp]
theorem map_neg (x : R) : v (-x) = v x :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_neg x

theorem map_sub_swap (x y : R) : v (x - y) = v (y - x) :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_sub_swap x y

theorem map_sub (x y : R) : v (x - y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  calc
    v (x - y) = v (x + -y) := by rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
    _ ‚â§ max (v x) (v <| -y) := v.map_add _ _
    _ = max (v x) (v y) := by rw [map_neg]

theorem map_sub_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x - y) ‚â§ g := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
  exact v.map_add_le hx (le_trans (le_of_eq (v.map_neg y)) hy)

variable {x y : R}

theorem map_add_of_distinct_val (h : v x ‚âÝ v y) : v (x + y) = max (v x) (v y) := by
  suffices ¬¨v (x + y) < max (v x) (v y) from
    or_iff_not_imp_right.1 (le_iff_eq_or_lt.1 (v.map_add x y)) this
  intro h'
  wlog vyx : v y < v x generalizing x y
  ¬∑ refine this h.symm ?_ (h.lt_or_lt.resolve_right vyx)
    rwa [add_comm, max_comm]
  rw [max_eq_left_of_lt vyx] at h'
  apply lt_irrefl (v x)
  calc
    v x = v (x + y - y) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ max (v <| x + y) (v y) := map_sub _ _ _
    _ < v x := max_lt h' vyx

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x + y) = v y :=
  (v.map_add_of_distinct_val h.ne).trans (max_eq_right_iff.mpr h.le)

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x + y) = v x := by
  rw [add_comm]; exact map_add_eq_of_lt_right _ h

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x - y) = v y := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_right, map_neg]
  rwa [map_neg]

open scoped Classical in
theorem map_sum_eq_of_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {j : Œπ}
    (hj : j ‚àà s) (h0 : v (f j) ‚âÝ 0) (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s \ {j}, v (f i) < v (f j)) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) = v (f j) := by
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton hj]
  exact map_add_eq_of_lt_left _ (map_sum_lt _ h0 hf)

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x - y) = v x := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_left]
  rwa [map_neg]

theorem map_eq_of_sub_lt (h : v (y - x) < v x) : v y = v x := by
  have := Valuation.map_add_of_distinct_val v (ne_of_gt h).symm
  rw [max_eq_right (le_of_lt h)] at this
  simpa using this

theorem map_one_add_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one] at h
  simpa only [v.map_one] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

theorem map_one_sub_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 - x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one, ‚Üê v.map_neg] at h
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg 1 x]
  simpa only [v.map_one, v.map_neg] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

/-- An ordered monoid isomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces an equivalence
`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`. -/
def congr (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := map f f.toOrderIso.monotone
  invFun := map f.symm f.toOrderIso.symm.monotone
  left_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp
  right_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp

end Monoid

section Group

variable [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : R}

theorem map_inv {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x, v x‚Åª¬π = (v x)‚Åª¬π :=
  map_inv‚ÇÄ _

theorem map_div {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x y, v (x / y) = v x / v y :=
  map_div‚ÇÄ _

theorem one_lt_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 < v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  simp [inv_lt_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem one_le_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 ‚â§ v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  simp [inv_le_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_lt_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x < 1 ‚Üî 1 < v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_lt_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_le_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî 1 ‚â§ v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_le_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_eq_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 1 ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π = 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [map_inv‚ÇÄ, inv_eq_one]
  ¬∑ simpa only [le_antisymm_iff, And.comm] using and_congr (one_le_val_iff v h) (val_le_one_iff v h)

theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, zero_lt_one, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_lt_val_iff v h, le_or_lt]

/--
This theorem is a weaker version of `Valuation.val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one`, but more symmetric
in `x` and `x‚Åª¬π`.
-/
theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_le_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_le_val_iff v h, le_total]

/-- The subgroup of elements whose valuation is less than a certain unit. -/
def ltAddSubgroup (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (Œ≥ : Œì‚ÇÄÀ£) : AddSubgroup R where
  carrier := { x | v x < Œ≥ }
  zero_mem' := by simp
  add_mem' {x y} x_in y_in := lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) (max_lt x_in y_in)
  neg_mem' x_in := by rwa [Set.mem_setOf, map_neg]

end Group

end Basic

-- end of section
namespace IsEquiv

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÉ : Valuation R Œì''‚ÇÄ}

@[refl]
theorem refl : v.IsEquiv v := fun _ _ => Iff.refl _

@[symm]
theorem symm (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ := fun _ _ => Iff.symm (h _ _)

@[trans]
theorem trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ) : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ := fun _ _ =>
  Iff.trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ _ _) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ _ _)

theorem of_eq {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : v = v') : v.IsEquiv v' := by subst h; rfl

theorem map {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (inf : Injective f)
    (h : v.IsEquiv v') : (v.map f hf).IsEquiv (v'.map f hf) :=
  let H : StrictMono f := hf.strictMono_of_injective inf
  fun r s =>
  calc
    f (v r) ‚â§ f (v s) ‚Üî v r ‚â§ v s := by rw [H.le_iff_le]
    _ ‚Üî v' r ‚â§ v' s := h r s
    _ ‚Üî f (v' r) ‚â§ f (v' s) := by rw [H.le_iff_le]

/-- `comap` preserves equivalence. -/
theorem comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) :
    (v‚ÇÅ.comap f).IsEquiv (v‚ÇÇ.comap f) := fun r s => h (f r) (f s)

theorem val_eq (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r s : R} : v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r = v‚ÇÇ s := by
  simpa only [le_antisymm_iff] using and_congr (h r s) (h s r)

theorem ne_zero (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r : R} : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ 0 := by
  have : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÅ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÇ 0 := not_congr h.val_eq
  rwa [v‚ÇÅ.map_zero, v‚ÇÇ.map_zero] at this

end IsEquiv

-- end of namespace
section

theorem isEquiv_of_map_strictMono [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] [Ring R] {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ)
    (H : StrictMono f) : IsEquiv (v.map f H.monotone) v := fun _x _y =>
  ‚ü®H.le_iff_le.mp, fun h => H.monotone h‚ü©

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x y : K}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y := by
  simp only [IsEquiv, le_iff_le_iff_lt_iff_lt]
  exact forall_comm

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_iff_lt, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val

theorem isEquiv_of_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ}
    (h : ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1) : v.IsEquiv v' := by
  intro x y
  obtain rfl | hy := eq_or_ne y 0
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê div_le_one‚ÇÄ, ‚Üê v.map_div, h, v'.map_div, div_le_one‚ÇÄ] <;>
      rwa [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1 :=
  ‚ü®fun h x => by simpa using h x 1, isEquiv_of_val_le_one‚ü©

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.le_one_iff_le_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simpa using @IsEquiv.val_eq _ _ _ _ _ _ v v' h x 1
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply isEquiv_of_val_le_one
    intro x
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v'.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v' (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v'.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [‚Üê h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [‚Üê h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.eq_one_iff_eq_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simp only [lt_iff_le_and_ne,
      and_congr h.le_one_iff_le_one h.eq_one_iff_eq_one.not]
  ¬∑ rw [isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one]
    intro h x
    by_cases hx : x = 0
    ¬∑ simp only [(zero_iff _).2 hx, zero_ne_one]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.2 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
          rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
          exact hh.not_lt (h.2 ((one_lt_val_iff v' hx).1 h_2))
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.1 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
        rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
        exact hh.not_lt (h.1 ((one_lt_val_iff v hx).1 h_2))

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_one_iff_lt_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 := by
  rw [isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one]
  exact (Equiv.subRight 1).surjective.forall

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.val_sub_one_lt_one_iff, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_tfae [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
    (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ) :
    [ v.IsEquiv v',
      ‚àÄ {x y}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 ].TFAE := by
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 2 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 3 := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 4 := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 5 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 6 := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one
  tfae_finish

end

section Supp

variable [CommRing R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

/-- The support of a valuation `v : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is the ideal of `R` where `v` vanishes. -/
def supp : Ideal R where
  carrier := { x | v x = 0 }
  zero_mem' := map_zero v
  add_mem' {x y} hx hy := le_zero_iff.mp <|
    calc
      v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) := v.map_add x y
      _ ‚â§ 0 := max_le (le_zero_iff.mpr hx) (le_zero_iff.mpr hy)
  smul_mem' c x hx :=
    calc
      v (c * x) = v c * v x := map_mul v c x
      _ = v c * 0 := congr_arg _ hx
      _ = 0 := mul_zero _

@[simp]
theorem mem_supp_iff (x : R) : x ‚àà supp v ‚Üî v x = 0 :=
  Iff.rfl

/-- The support of a valuation is a prime ideal. -/
instance [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] [NoZeroDivisors Œì‚ÇÄ] : Ideal.IsPrime (supp v) :=
  ‚ü®fun h =>
    one_ne_zero (Œ± := Œì‚ÇÄ) <|
      calc
        1 = v 1 := v.map_one.symm
        _ = 0 := by rw [‚Üê mem_supp_iff, h]; exact Submodule.mem_top,
   fun {x y} hxy => by
    simp only [mem_supp_iff] at hxy ‚ä¢
    rw [v.map_mul x y] at hxy
    exact eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero hxy‚ü©

theorem map_add_supp (a : R) {s : R} (h : s ‚àà supp v) : v (a + s) = v a := by
  have aux : ‚àÄ a s, v s = 0 ‚Üí v (a + s) ‚â§ v a := by
    intro a' s' h'
    refine le_trans (v.map_add a' s') (max_le le_rfl ?_)
    simp [h']
  apply le_antisymm (aux a s h)
  calc
    v a = v (a + s + -s) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ v (a + s) := aux (a + s) (-s) (by rwa [‚Üê Ideal.neg_mem_iff] at h)

theorem comap_supp {S : Type*} [CommRing S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) :
    supp (v.comap f) = Ideal.comap f v.supp :=
  Ideal.ext fun x => by rw [mem_supp_iff, Ideal.mem_comap, mem_supp_iff, comap_apply]

end Supp

-- end of section
end Valuation

section AddMonoid

variable (R) [Ring R] (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued additive valuations on `R`. -/
def AddValuation :=
  Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà)

end AddMonoid

namespace AddValuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*} {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}

section Basic

section Monoid

/-- A valuation is coerced to the underlying function `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
instance (R) (Œì‚ÇÄ) [Ring R] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] :
    FunLike (AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe v := v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toFun
  coe_injective' f g := by cases f; cases g; simp (config := {contextual := true})

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

section

variable (f : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) (h0 : f 0 = ‚ä§) (h1 : f 1 = 0)
variable (hadd : ‚àÄ x y, min (f x) (f y) ‚â§ f (x + y)) (hmul : ‚àÄ x y, f (x * y) = f x + f y)

/-- An alternate constructor of `AddValuation`, that doesn't reference `Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà` -/
def of : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := f
  map_one' := h1
  map_zero' := h0
  map_add_le_max' := hadd
  map_mul' := hmul

variable {h0} {h1} {hadd} {hmul} {r : R}

@[simp]
theorem of_apply : (of f h0 h1 hadd hmul) r = f r := rfl

/-- The `Valuation` associated to an `AddValuation` (useful if the latter is constructed using
`AddValuation.of`). -/
def toValuation : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà) :=
  Equiv.refl _

@[deprecated (since := "2024-11-09")]

-/

@[simp]
lemma ofValuation_symm_eq : ofValuation.symm = toValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toValuation_symm_eq : toValuation.symm = ofValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))

lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne), natDegree_comp, ascPochhammer_natDegree,
    add_comm_sub, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, natDegree_add_C, natDegree_X, mul_one]

lemma coeff_preHilbertPoly_self [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).coeff d = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  delta preHilbertPoly
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  have heq : d = ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1)).natDegree :=
    (natDegree_preHilbertPoly F d k).symm.trans (natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne))
  nth_rw 3 [heq]
  calc
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C ((k : F) - 1))).leadingCoeff := by
    simp only [sub_add, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, coeff_smul, coeff_natDegree]
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
    simp only [leadingCoeff_comp (ne_of_eq_of_ne (natDegree_X_sub_C _) one_ne_zero), Monic.def.1
      (monic_ascPochhammer _ _), one_mul, leadingCoeff_X_sub_C, one_pow, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

lemma leadingCoeff_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).leadingCoeff = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [leadingCoeff, natDegree_preHilbertPoly, coeff_preHilbertPoly_self]

lemma preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add [CharZero F] (d : ‚Ñï) {k n : ‚Ñï} (hkn : k ‚â§ n):
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F) = (n - k + d).choose d := by
  have : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  calc
  _ = (‚Üëd !)‚Åª¬π * eval (‚Üë(n - k + 1)) (ascPochhammer F d) := by simp [cast_sub hkn, preHilbertPoly]
  _ = (n - k + d).choose d := by
    rw [ascPochhammer_nat_eq_natCast_ascFactorial];
    field_simp [ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]

variable {F}

/--
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p 0 = 0`; for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p (d + 1)`
is defined as `‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d i`. If
`M` is a graded module whose Poincar√© series can be written as `p(X)/(1 - X)·µà` for some
`p : ‚Ñö[X]` with integer coefficients, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is the Hilbert
polynomial of `M`. See also `Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`,
which says that `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * PowerSeries.invOneSubPow F d)` equals
`(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) : (d : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí F[X]
  | 0 => 0
  | d + 1 => ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_left (d : ‚Ñï) : hilbertPoly (0 : F[X]) d = 0 := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only
  | succ d _ => simp only [coeff_zero, zero_smul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Subgroup.Pointwise
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.Additive.CovBySMul
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.Additive.RuzsaCovering
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.Additive.SmallTripling

/-!
# Approximate subgroups

This file defines approximate subgroups of a group, namely symmetric sets `A` such that `A * A` can
be covered by a small number of translates of `A`.

## Main results

Approximate subgroups are a central concept in additive combinatorics, as a natural weakening and
flexible substitute of genuine subgroups. As such, they share numerous properties with subgroups:
* `IsApproximateSubgroup.image`: Group homomorphisms send approximate subgroups to approximate
  subgroups
* `IsApproximateSubgroup.pow_inter_pow`: The intersection of (non-trivial powers of) two approximate
  subgroups is an approximate subgroup. Warning: The intersection of two approximate subgroups isn't
  an approximate subgroup in general.

Approximate subgroups are close qualitatively and quantitatively to other concepts in additive
combinatorics:
* `IsApproximateSubgroup.card_pow_le`: An approximate subgroup has small powers.
* `IsApproximateSubgroup.of_small_tripling`: A set of small tripling can be made an approximate
  subgroup by squaring.

It can be readily confirmed that approximate subgroups are a weakening of subgroups:
* `isApproximateSubgroup_one`: A 1-approximate subgroup is the same thing as a subgroup.
-/

open scoped Finset Pointwise

variable {G : Type*} [Group G] {A B : Set G} {K L : ‚Ñù} {m n : ‚Ñï}

/--
An approximate subgroup in a group is a symmetric set `A` containing the identity and such that
`A + A` can be covered by a small number of translates of `A`.

In practice, we will take `K` fixed and `A` large but finite.
-/
structure IsApproximateAddSubgroup {G : Type*} [AddGroup G] (K : ‚Ñù) (A : Set G) : Prop where
  zero_mem : 0 ‚àà A
  neg_eq_self : -A = A
  two_nsmul_covByVAdd : CovByVAdd G K (2 ‚Ä¢ A) A

/--
An approximate subgroup in a group is a symmetric set `A` containing the identity and such that
`A * A` can be covered by a small number of translates of `A`.

In practice, we will take `K` fixed and `A` large but finite.
-/
@[to_additive]
structure IsApproximateSubgroup (K : ‚Ñù) (A : Set G) : Prop where
  one_mem : 1 ‚àà A
  inv_eq_self : A‚Åª¬π = A
  sq_covBySMul : CovBySMul G K (A ^ 2) A

namespace IsApproximateSubgroup

@[to_additive] lemma nonempty (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K A) : A.Nonempty := ‚ü®1, hA.one_mem‚ü©

@[to_additive one_le]
lemma one_le (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K A) : 1 ‚â§ K := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  have hF‚ÇÄ : F ‚âÝ ‚àÖ := by rintro rfl; simp [hA.nonempty.pow.ne_empty] at hSF
  exact hF.trans' <| by simpa [Finset.nonempty_iff_ne_empty]

@[to_additive]
lemma mono (hKL : K ‚â§ L) (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K A) : IsApproximateSubgroup L A where
  one_mem := hA.one_mem
  inv_eq_self := hA.inv_eq_self
  sq_covBySMul := hA.sq_covBySMul.mono hKL

@[to_additive]
lemma card_pow_le [DecidableEq G] {A : Finset G} (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)) :
    ‚àÄ {n}, #(A ^ n) ‚â§ K ^ (n - 1) * #A
  | 0 => by simpa using hA.nonempty
  | 1 => by simp
  | n + 2 => by
    obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
    calc
      (#(A ^ (n + 2)) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F ^ (n + 1) * A) := by
        gcongr; exact mod_cast Set.pow_subset_pow_mul_of_sq_subset_mul hSF (by omega)
      _ ‚â§ #(F ^ (n + 1)) * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
      _ ‚â§ #F ^ (n + 1) * #A := by gcongr; exact mod_cast Finset.card_pow_le
      _ ‚â§ K ^ (n + 1) * #A := by gcongr
formal_statement:
@[to_additive]
lemma card_mul_self_le [DecidableEq G] {A : Finset G} (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)) :
    #(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A
name: IsApproximateSubgroup.card_mul_self_le
responses:
To prove `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` given `IsApproximateSubgroup K A`, we utilize the definition of `IsApproximateSubgroup`, which states that `A * A` is covered by at most `K` translates of `A`.

1.  From `hA.sq_covBySMul`, we have a finite set `F` such that `A * A ‚äÜ F * A` and `#F ‚â§ K`.
2.  By the properties of set cardinalities and multiplication, we have `#(A * A) ‚â§ #(F * A)`.
3.  By subadditivity of the cardinality of product sets, `#(F * A) ‚â§ #F * #A`.
4.  Combining these, we get `#(A * A) ‚â§ #F * #A ‚â§ K * #A`.

FINAL```
  := by
    obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
    calc
      (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := mod_cast Finset.card_le_card hSF
      _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
      _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To prove `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` for an `IsApproximateSubgroup K A` where `A` is a `Finset`, we utilize the definition `IsApproximateSubgroup.sq_covBySMul`. 

By definition, `A * A` is covered by at most `K` translates of `A` (i.e., `A * A ‚äÜ F ‚Ä¢ A` with `|F| ‚â§ K`). Using the subadditivity of the cardinality of a product and the property that the identity is in `A`, we have:
`#(A * A) ‚â§ #(F ‚Ä¢ A)`. 
Since `#(F ‚Ä¢ A) ‚â§ #F * #A`, and `|F| ‚â§ K`, we conclude `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A`.

FINAL```
  := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  calc
    (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F ‚Ä¢ A) := by
      exact_mod_cast Finset.card_le_of_subset (by simpa using hSF)
    _ ‚â§ #F * #A := by exact_mod_cast Finset.card_smul_le
    _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To prove `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` given `hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)`, we use the `sq_covBySMul` field of the structure `IsApproximateSubgroup`. By definition, `sq_covBySMul` provides a finite set `F` such that `A^2 ‚äÜ F ‚Ä¢ A` and `|F| ‚â§ K`. Since `A^2 = A * A`, we have `A * A ‚äÜ F * A`. The cardinality of the product of two sets is at most the product of their cardinalities (or specifically, the sum over `F` of the translates), and the result follows from `|F * A| ‚â§ |F| * |A|`.

FINAL```
  | 0 => by simp
  | 1 => by simp
  | n + 2 => sorry -- This case is not needed for the specific goal requested.

lemma card_mul_self_le [DecidableEq G] {A : Finset G} (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)) :
    #(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  rw [‚Üê Finset.coe_mul] at hSF
  have : #(A * A) ‚â§ #(F * (A : Set G)) := Finset.card_le_of_subset (by simpa using hSF)
  rw [‚Üê Finset.coe_mul] at this
  exact this.trans (by exact_mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le.trans (mul_le_mul_right' (mod_cast hF) #A))
```

To prove `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` given `hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K A`, we utilize the definition of `IsApproximateSubgroup`. By definition, `hA.sq_covBySMul` provides a finite set `F` such that `A^2 ‚äÜ F * A` and `|F| ‚â§ K`. Thus, `|A^2| ‚â§ |F * A|`. By the property of set multiplication cardinalities, `|F * A| ‚â§ |F| * |A|`. Since `|F| ‚â§ K`, we obtain `|A^2| ‚â§ K * |A|`.

FINAL```
  := by
    obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
    calc
      (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := by gcongr; exact mod_cast hSF
      _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
      _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To prove that $A \cdot A$ is covered by at most $K \cdot |A|$ elements (which is the definition of `sq_covBySMul` translated to cardinality), we utilize the `CovBySMul` structure provided in the definition of `IsApproximateSubgroup`.

Given `hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)`, the field `hA.sq_covBySMul` states that there exists a finite set $F \subseteq G$ such that $|F| \le K$ and $A \cdot A \subseteq F \cdot A$. By the submultiplicativity of cardinality for the product of two sets in a group, we have $|A \cdot A| \le |F \cdot A| \le |F| \cdot |A|$. Since $|F| \le K$, the result follows.

FINAL```
  := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  calc
    (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := by
      rw [Finset.coe_mul]
      exact mod_cast Finset.card_le_card (Set.image_subset_iff.2 hSF)
    _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
    _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To prove that `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` given `IsApproximateSubgroup K A`, we use the `sq_covBySMul` property. By definition, `sq_covBySMul` states that there exists a finite set `F` such that `A * A ‚äÜ F * A` and `#F ‚â§ K`. Since `#(A * A) ‚â§ #(F * A)` and `#(F * A) ‚â§ #F * #A`, we obtain the result.

FINAL```
  := by
    obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
    calc
      (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := mod_cast Finset.card_le_card hSF
      _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
      _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To show that `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` for an approximate subgroup `A` with covering constant `K`, we observe that by definition of `IsApproximateSubgroup`, `A * A` is covered by at most `K` translates of `A`. Specifically, `hA.sq_covBySMul` provides a finite set `F` such that `A * A ‚äÜ F * A` with `|F| ‚â§ K`. Using the subadditivity of the cardinality of the product of sets (specifically, `|F * A| ‚â§ |F| * |A|`), the result follows.

FINAL```
  | 0 => by simpa using hA.nonempty
  | 1 => by simp
  | n + 2 => by
    obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
    calc
      (#(A ^ (n + 2)) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F ^ (n + 1) * A) := by
        gcongr; exact mod_cast Set.pow_subset_pow_mul_of_sq_subset_mul hSF (by omega)
      _ ‚â§ #(F ^ (n + 1)) * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
      _ ‚â§ #F ^ (n + 1) * #A := by gcongr; exact mod_cast Finset.card_pow_le
      _ ‚â§ K ^ (n + 1) * #A := by gcongr

@[to_additive]
lemma card_mul_self_le [DecidableEq G] {A : Finset G} (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)) :
    #(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  calc
    (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := mod_cast Finset.card_le_of_subset (Set.subset_def.2 hSF)
    _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
    _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```

To prove that `#(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A` given `IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)`, we utilize the `sq_covBySMul` field of the structure. By definition, `sq_covBySMul` provides a finite set $F$ such that $|F| \le K$ and $A \cdot A \subseteq F \cdot A$. The cardinality inequality follows immediately from the sub-additivity of cardinality under multiplication (`card_mul_le`).

FINAL```
@[to_additive]
lemma card_mul_self_le [DecidableEq G] {A : Finset G} (hA : IsApproximateSubgroup K (A : Set G)) :
    #(A * A) ‚â§ K * #A := by
  obtain ‚ü®F, hF, hSF‚ü© := hA.sq_covBySMul
  calc
    (#(A * A) : ‚Ñù) ‚â§ #(F * A) := mod_cast Finset.card_le_card hSF
    _ ‚â§ #F * #A := mod_cast Finset.card_mul_le
    _ ‚â§ K * #A := by gcongr
```
