import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Continuity
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Complex.LogDeriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Extend
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Prod
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Deriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Trigonometric.Deriv

/-!
# Derivatives of power function on `‚ÑÇ`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñù‚â•0`, and `‚Ñù‚â•0‚àû`

We also prove differentiability and provide derivatives for the power functions `x ^ y`.
-/


noncomputable section

open scoped Real Topology NNReal ENNReal
open Filter

namespace Complex

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p := by
  have A : p.1 ‚âÝ 0 := slitPlane_ne_zero hp
  have : (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    ((isOpen_ne.preimage continuous_fst).eventually_mem A).mono fun p hp =>
      cpow_def_of_ne_zero hp _
  rw [cpow_sub _ _ A, cpow_one, mul_div_left_comm, mul_smul, mul_smul]
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero A, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_smul, add_comm, smul_add] using
    ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.clog hp).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).cexp

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow' {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (hp : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((y * x ^ (y - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (x ^ y * log x) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) (x, y) :=
  @hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow (x, y) hp

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® y ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun y => x ^ y) (x ^ y * log x) y := by
  rcases em (x = 0) with (rfl | hx)
  ¬∑ replace h := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
    rw [log_zero, mul_zero]
    refine (hasStrictDerivAt_const y 0).congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
    exact (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem h).mono fun y hy => (zero_cpow hy).symm
  ¬∑ simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero hx, mul_one] using
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id y).const_mul (log x)).cexp

theorem hasFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p :=
  (hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow hp).hasFDerivAt

end Complex

section fderiv

open Complex

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚ÑÇ E] {f g : E ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ}
  {x : E} {s : Set E} {c : ‚ÑÇ}

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).comp_hasStrictFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by
  convert
    (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hg : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ g x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivAt h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (hg : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ g s x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableWithinAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s) (hg : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ g s)
    (h0 : Set.MapsTo f s slitPlane) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).cpow (hg x hx) (h0 hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableOn_const c) h0

theorem Differentiable.cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f) (hg : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ g)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x, f x ‚àà slitPlane) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).cpow (hg x) (h0 x)

theorem Differentiable.const_cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x, f x ‚âÝ 0) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiable_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] :
    Differentiable ‚ÑÇ fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s :=
  differentiable_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiableAt_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] (t : ‚ÑÇ) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s) t :=
  differentiableAt_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

end fderiv

section deriv

open Complex

variable {f g : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {s : Set ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' x c : ‚ÑÇ}

/-- A private lemma that rewrites the output of lemmas like `HasFDerivAt.cpow` to the form
expected by lemmas like `HasDerivAt.cpow`. -/
private theorem aux : ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight f' +
    (f x ^ g x * log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight g') 1 =
      g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * log (f x) * g' := by
  simp only [Algebra.id.smul_eq_mul, one_mul, ContinuousLinearMap.one_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.smulRight_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul']

nonrec theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (h : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=

  simpa only [mul_zero, add_zero, mul_one] using
    (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).cpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x c) h

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)

    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasDerivAt g g' x)

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)

      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') s x := by

    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') s x :=

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') s x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

/-- Although `fun x => x ^ r` for fixed `r` is *not* complex-differentiable along the negative real
line, it is still real-differentiable, and the derivative is what one would formally expect.
See `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` for an alternate formulation. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ -1) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x := by
  rw [Ne, ‚Üê add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ‚Üê Ne] at hr
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- easy case : `0 < x`
    -- Porting note: proof used to be
    -- convert (((hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)).cpow_const _).div_const (r + 1)).comp_ofReal using 1
    -- ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel, id.def, mul_one, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel _ hr]
    -- ¬∑ rw [id.def, ofReal_re]; exact Or.inl hx
    apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal (e := fun y => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1))
    convert HasDerivAt.div_const (ùïú := ‚ÑÇ) ?_ (r + 1) using 1
    ¬∑ exact (mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr).symm
    ¬∑ convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
      ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, mul_comm]; exact (mul_one _).symm
      ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)
      ¬∑ simp [hx]
  ¬∑ -- harder case : `x < 0`
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x,
        (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1) = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) * exp (œÄ * I * (r + 1)) / (r + 1) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-‚Üëy) ^ (r + 1) * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * (r + 1)))
        ((r + 1) * (-‚Üëx) ^ r * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * r)) x by
      convert this.div_const (r + 1) using 1
      conv_rhs => rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr]
    rw [mul_add ((œÄ : ‚ÑÇ) * _), mul_one, exp_add, exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm (_ : ‚ÑÇ) (-1 : ‚ÑÇ),
      neg_one_mul]
    simp_rw [mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (‚Üë(-y) : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) (-(r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) x by
      convert this.neg.mul_const _ using 1; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) (-x) by
      convert @HasDerivAt.scomp ‚Ñù _ ‚ÑÇ _ _ x ‚Ñù _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ this (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      rw [real_smul, ofReal_neg 1, ofReal_one]; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) ‚Üë(-x) by
      exact this.comp_ofReal
    conv in ‚Üë_ ^ _ => rw [(by ring : r = r + 1 - 1)]
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
    ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, add_sub_cancel_right]; exact (mul_one _).symm
    ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id ((-x : ‚Ñù) : ‚ÑÇ)
    ¬∑ simp [hx]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-15")] alias hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow := hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'
formal_statement:
/-- An alternate formulation of `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'`. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x
name: hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const
responses:
theorem states the derivative of $x^r$ (viewed as a function from $\mathbb{R}$ to $\mathbb{C}$) at $x \neq 0$. 

The derivative of $y \mapsto y^r$ at $x$ is $r x^{r-1}$. We prove this by cases:
1. When $x > 0$, the function is complex-differentiable (specifically, its restriction to the positive real axis), so we use the derivative of 
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  have hr' : r + 1 ‚âÝ 1 := by rw [add_left_inj]; exact hr
  have hr'' : r + 1 ‚âÝ 0 := by
    by_contra! h
    have : r = -1 := by simpa using h
    rw [this] at hr'
    exact hr' rfl
  have : HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x :=
    hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' hx hr''
  convert this.hasDerivAt_mul_const (r + 1) using 1
  ¬∑ ext y
    rw [mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr'']
  ¬∑ rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm (r + 1), mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr'']
    rw [cpow_sub _ _ (ofReal_ne_zero.mpr hx), cpow_one, mul_comm]
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)) (Or.inl hx) using 1
    rw [mul_one, mul_comm, mul_comm (r - 1)]
  ¬∑ have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x, (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r * exp (œÄ * I * r) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => ((-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r)) (-(r * (-x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1))) x := by
      suffices HasDerivAt (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ r) (r * (-x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1)) (-x : ‚ÑÇ) by
        convert this.comp (fun y => (-y : ‚ÑÇ)) (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
        rw [show (fun y => (-y : ‚ÑÇ)) x = (-x : ‚ÑÇ) by rfl]
        rw [show (deriv (fun y => (-y : ‚ÑÇ)) x) = -1 by norm_num]
        simp
      exact HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hasDerivAt_id _) (Or.inl (by simp [hx]))
    convert h1.mul_const (exp (œÄ * I * r)) using 1
    rw [mul_assoc, ‚Üê mul_assoc r, mul_comm (exp _), ‚Üê mul_assoc, ‚Üê exp_add, ‚Üê add_assoc,
      show (r - 1 + 1 = r) by ring]
    rw [‚Üê ofReal_neg, ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    ring
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- Case x < 0
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x, (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r * exp (œÄ * I * r) := by
      filter_upwards [Iio_mem_nhds hx] with y hy
      exact ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-(y : ‚ÑÇ)) ^ r) (-r * (-x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1)) x by
      convert this.mul_const (exp (œÄ * I * r)) using 1
      rw [exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm, mul_assoc, ‚Üê mul_assoc r, mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê exp_add, ‚Üê mul_add]
      simp
    convert (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (by simp [hx])).hasDerivAt.comp x (hasDerivAt_neg x).comp_ofReal using 1
    simp [neg_sub]
  ¬∑ -- Case x > 0
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x, (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r := eventually_of_forall fun _ => rfl
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    convert (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (by simp [hx])).hasDerivAt.comp_ofReal using 1
    simp
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  have hr' : r - 1 ‚âÝ -1 := by simpa using hr
  have h := hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' hx hr'
  have : (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) =·∂Ý[ùìù x] fun y : ‚Ñù => deriv (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) y := by
    apply eventually_of_mem (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem hx)
    intro y hy
    suffices deriv (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) y = (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r by
      rw [this]
    rw [deriv_div_const _ (r + 1), deriv_cpow_const (Complex.slitPlane_of_ne_zero (ofReal_ne_zero.mpr hy)), add_sub_cancel_right, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr']
  refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq (deriv_hasDerivAt ?_) this
  apply DifferentiableAt.div_const
  apply DifferentiableAt.cpow_const
  ¬∑ exact differentiableAt_id
  ¬∑ exact Complex.slitPlane_of_ne_zero (ofReal_ne_zero.mpr hx)
  ¬∑ exact hr'
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ refine HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal ?_
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)) (Or.inl hx) using 1
    rw [mul_one]
  ¬∑ have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x, (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r * exp (œÄ * I * r) :=
      (Iio_mem_nhds hx).mono fun y hy => by rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    have h1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (-r * (-x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1)) x := by
      have : HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ r) (r * (-x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r - 1)) (-x) :=
        HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hasDerivAt_id (-x : ‚ÑÇ)) (Or.inl (by simp [hx]))
      convert this.comp_ofReal.comp (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      simp only [Function.comp_apply, neg_neg]
    convert h1.mul_const (exp (œÄ * I * r)) using 1
    rw [mul_assoc, ‚Üê mul_assoc r, ‚Üê exp_pi_mul_I, mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    simp only [neg_mul, ‚Üê mul_assoc]
    rfl
The proof for `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` proceeds by differentiating the power function. Using the identity `(y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = exp(r * log(y : ‚ÑÇ))`, we can relate the derivative to the derivative of the exponential function. The case distinction handles whether the real variable $x$ is positive or negative (using the branches of the logarithm in the complex plane). The `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'` lemma already established the result for the antiderivative; the derivative follows from standard calculus rules for power functions in the complex plane adapted to the real line via `ofReal`.

To prove `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const`, we can use the derivative of `y ^ r` with respect to `y`. For $y > 0$, this follows from the composition of the complex power function and the inclusion map $‚Ñù \hookrightarrow ‚ÑÇ$. For $y < 0$, we use the identity `(y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r * exp(œÄ * I * r)` for $y < 0$. We differentiate this expression and relate it to the formula $r * x^{r-1}$.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')

lemma extend_exactAt_iff :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j
name: HomologicalComplex.extend_exactAt_iff
responses:
The proof follows by comparing the `HomologyData` constructed for the extended complex `(K.extend e).sc j'` with the `HomologyData` for the original complex `K.sc j`. Since the homology of a complex is defined by the `homologyData`, and we have constructed an isomorphism `(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`, the exactness at `j'` corresponds directly to the vanishing of the homology at `j`.

FINAL```
  (extendHomologyIso K e hj').iso.isZero ‚Üî (K.sc j).homologyData.left.homology.isZero := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [‚Üê isZero_iff_of_iso (extendHomologyIso K e hj').symm] at h
    exact h
  ¬∑ intro h
    rw [‚Üê isZero_iff_of_iso (extendHomologyIso K e hj')]
    exact h

end
end HomologicalComplex
```

To prove the equivalence `(K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j` for a homological complex extension, we use the fact that the homology of the extended complex at an image point `j' = e.f j` is isomorphic to the homology of the original complex at `j`. Specifically, since `(K.extend e).homology j'` is defined via the `homologyData'` which uses an isomorphism to `K.homology j`, the vanishing of one is equivalent to the vanishing of the other.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt_iff :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j := by
  have := extendHomologyIso K e hj'
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    have : IsZero ((K.extend e).homology j') := (K.extend e).exactAt_iff_homology_isZero j'
    have : IsZero (K.homology j) := (IsZero.iff_of_iso this.symm).mp this
    exact K.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero j |>.mpr this
  ¬∑ intro h
    have : IsZero (K.homology j) := K.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero j |>.mp h
    have : IsZero ((K.extend e).homology j') := (IsZero.iff_of_iso this).mp this
    exact (K.extend e).exactAt_iff_homology_isZero j' |>.mpr this
```

To complete the proof of `extend_exactAt_iff`, we use the fact that the homology of the extended complex at an image index $j' = e.f(j)$ is isomorphic to the homology of the original complex at $j$. A complex is exact at a position if and only if its homology at that position is zero. Since the isomorphism `extendHomologyIso` preserves zero-objects (as an isomorphism), the result follows immediately.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt_iff :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j := by
  have h := extendHomologyIso K e hj'
  rw [ExactAt.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero, K.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero]
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro hK
    rw [‚Üê IsZero.iff_isZero_of_iso h.symm]
    exact hK
  ¬∑ intro hK
    rw [‚Üê IsZero.iff_isZero_of_iso h]
    exact hK
```

To complete the proof, we relate the exactness of the extended complex at $j'$ to the exactness of the original complex at $j$. Since the homology of the extended complex at $j'$ is isomorphic to the homology of the original complex at $j$ via `extendHomologyIso`, the condition of being exact (homology is zero) is equivalent.

FINAL```
  (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j := by
  have h := extendHomologyIso K e hj'
  rw [ExactAt.iff_homology_isZero, ExactAt.iff_homology_isZero]
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro H
    have : IsZero ((K.extend e).homology j') := H
    have : IsZero (K.homology j) := IsZero.of_iso (IsZero.of_iso this h.symm)
    exact this
  ¬∑ intro H
    have : IsZero (K.homology j) := H
    have : IsZero ((K.extend e).homology j') := IsZero.of_iso (IsZero.of_iso this h)
    exact this
```

To complete the proof of `extend_exactAt_iff`, we note that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` is defined by the homology at `j'` being zero. Since we have the isomorphism `(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`, this is equivalent to `K.homology j ‚âÖ 0`, which is the definition of `K.ExactAt j`.

FINAL```
  refine ‚ü®fun h => ?_, fun h => ?_‚ü©
  ¬∑ have := (K.extend e).homologyIso j'
    rw [‚Üê (K.extend e).homology_eq_zero_iff_exactAt] at h
    rw [‚Üê K.homology_eq_zero_iff_exactAt]
    apply (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').symm.isZero_iff.mp
    exact h
  ¬∑ have := K.homologyIso j
    rw [‚Üê K.homology_eq_zero_iff_exactAt] at h
    rw [‚Üê (K.extend e).homology_eq_zero_iff_exactAt]
    apply (K.extendHomologyIso e hj').isZero_iff.mp
    exact h
``````

To complete the formalization of `extend_exactAt_iff`, we utilize the established isomorphisms between the homology data of the extended complex and the original complex. Specifically, `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` is equivalent to the homology at `j'` being zero. Since `(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`, the vanishing of the former is equivalent to the vanishing of the latter, which is precisely `K.ExactAt j`.

FINAL```
lemma extend_exactAt_iff :
    (K.extend e).ExactAt j' ‚Üî K.ExactAt j := by
  rw [K.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero, (K.extend e).exactAt_iff_homology_isZero]
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h
    have := (extendHomologyIso K e hj').symm

    rwa [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply (IsZero.iso_iff (extendHomologyIso K e hj')).mpr
    rwa [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero] at h
```

To complete the proof of `extend_exactAt_iff`, we note that `(K.extend e).ExactAt j'` is defined by the homology at `j'` being zero, and `K.ExactAt j` is defined by the homology at `j` being zero. The isomorphism `extendHomologyIso` provides a natural identification between these two homology objects when `e.f j = j'`. Specifically, if one is zero, the other must be zero.

FINAL```

    rw [‚Üê ShortComplex.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero, ‚Üê ShortComplex.exactAt_iff_homology_isZero]

end

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Continuity
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Complex.LogDeriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Extend
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Prod
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Deriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Trigonometric.Deriv

/-!
# Derivatives of power function on `‚ÑÇ`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñù‚â•0`, and `‚Ñù‚â•0‚àû`

We also prove differentiability and provide derivatives for the power functions `x ^ y`.
-/


noncomputable section

open scoped Real Topology NNReal ENNReal
open Filter

namespace Complex

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p := by
  have A : p.1 ‚âÝ 0 := slitPlane_ne_zero hp
  have : (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    ((isOpen_ne.preimage continuous_fst).eventually_mem A).mono fun p hp =>
      cpow_def_of_ne_zero hp _
  rw [cpow_sub _ _ A, cpow_one, mul_div_left_comm, mul_smul, mul_smul]
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero A, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_smul, add_comm, smul_add] using
    ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.clog hp).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).cexp

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow' {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (hp : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((y * x ^ (y - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (x ^ y * log x) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) (x, y) :=
  @hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow (x, y) hp

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® y ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun y => x ^ y) (x ^ y * log x) y := by
  rcases em (x = 0) with (rfl | hx)
  ¬∑ replace h := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
    rw [log_zero, mul_zero]
    refine (hasStrictDerivAt_const y 0).congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
    exact (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem h).mono fun y hy => (zero_cpow hy).symm
  ¬∑ simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero hx, mul_one] using
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id y).const_mul (log x)).cexp

theorem hasFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p :=
  (hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow hp).hasFDerivAt

end Complex

section fderiv

open Complex

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚ÑÇ E] {f g : E ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ}
  {x : E} {s : Set E} {c : ‚ÑÇ}

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).comp_hasStrictFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by
  convert
    (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hg : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ g x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivAt h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (hg : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ g s x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableWithinAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s) (hg : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ g s)
    (h0 : Set.MapsTo f s slitPlane) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).cpow (hg x hx) (h0 hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableOn_const c) h0

theorem Differentiable.cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f) (hg : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ g)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x, f x ‚àà slitPlane) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).cpow (hg x) (h0 x)

theorem Differentiable.const_cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x, f x ‚âÝ 0) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiable_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] :
    Differentiable ‚ÑÇ fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s :=
  differentiable_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiableAt_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] (t : ‚ÑÇ) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s) t :=
  differentiableAt_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

end fderiv

section deriv

open Complex

variable {f g : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {s : Set ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' x c : ‚ÑÇ}

/-- A private lemma that rewrites the output of lemmas like `HasFDerivAt.cpow` to the form
expected by lemmas like `HasDerivAt.cpow`. -/
private theorem aux : ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight f' +
    (f x ^ g x * log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight g') 1 =
      g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * log (f x) * g' := by
  simp only [Algebra.id.smul_eq_mul, one_mul, ContinuousLinearMap.one_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.smulRight_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul']

nonrec theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa using (hf.cpow hg h0).hasStrictDerivAt

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (h : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h).comp x hf

theorem Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (h : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ c) (c * x ^ (c - 1)) x := by
  simpa only [mul_zero, add_zero, mul_one] using
    (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).cpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x c) h

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivAt

theorem HasDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') s x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivWithinAt

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') s x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

/-- Although `fun x => x ^ r` for fixed `r` is *not* complex-differentiable along the negative real
line, it is still real-differentiable, and the derivative is what one would formally expect.
See `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` for an alternate formulation. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ -1) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x := by
  rw [Ne, ‚Üê add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ‚Üê Ne] at hr
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- easy case : `0 < x`
    -- Porting note: proof used to be
    -- convert (((hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)).cpow_const _).div_const (r + 1)).comp_ofReal using 1
    -- ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel, id.def, mul_one, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel _ hr]
    -- ¬∑ rw [id.def, ofReal_re]; exact Or.inl hx
    apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal (e := fun y => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1))
    convert HasDerivAt.div_const (ùïú := ‚ÑÇ) ?_ (r + 1) using 1
    ¬∑ exact (mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr).symm
    ¬∑ convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
      ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, mul_comm]; exact (mul_one _).symm
      ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)
      ¬∑ simp [hx]
  ¬∑ -- harder case : `x < 0`
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x,
        (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1) = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) * exp (œÄ * I * (r + 1)) / (r + 1) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-‚Üëy) ^ (r + 1) * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * (r + 1)))
        ((r + 1) * (-‚Üëx) ^ r * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * r)) x by
      convert this.div_const (r + 1) using 1
      conv_rhs => rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr]
    rw [mul_add ((œÄ : ‚ÑÇ) * _), mul_one, exp_add, exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm (_ : ‚ÑÇ) (-1 : ‚ÑÇ),
      neg_one_mul]
    simp_rw [mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (‚Üë(-y) : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) (-(r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) x by
      convert this.neg.mul_const _ using 1; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) (-x) by
      convert @HasDerivAt.scomp ‚Ñù _ ‚ÑÇ _ _ x ‚Ñù _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ this (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      rw [real_smul, ofReal_neg 1, ofReal_one]; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) ‚Üë(-x) by
      exact this.comp_ofReal
    conv in ‚Üë_ ^ _ => rw [(by ring : r = r + 1 - 1)]
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
    ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, add_sub_cancel_right]; exact (mul_one _).symm
    ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id ((-x : ‚Ñù) : ‚ÑÇ)
    ¬∑ simp [hx]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-15")] alias hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow := hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'

/-- An alternate formulation of `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'`. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  have := HasDerivAt.const_mul r <| hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' hx
    (by rwa [ne_eq, sub_eq_neg_self])
  simpa [sub_add_cancel, mul_div_cancel‚ÇÄ _ hr] using this

/-- A version of `DifferentiableAt.cpow_const` for a real function. -/
theorem DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const {f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {x : ‚Ñù} (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x)
    (h0 : f x ‚âÝ 0) (h1 : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun (y : ‚Ñù) => (f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x :=
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).differentiableAt.comp x hf

theorem Complex.deriv_cpow_const (hx : x ‚àà Complex.slitPlane) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ü¶ x ^ c) x = c * x ^ (c - 1) :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const hx).hasDerivAt.deriv

/-- A version of `Complex.deriv_cpow_const` for a real variable. -/
theorem Complex.deriv_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) (hc : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    deriv (fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x = c * x ^ (c - 1) :=
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const hx hc).deriv

theorem deriv_cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hx : f x ‚àà Complex.slitPlane) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ü¶ f x ^ c) x = c * f x ^ (c - 1) * deriv f x :=
  (hf.hasDerivAt.cpow_const hx).deriv

theorem isTheta_deriv_ofReal_cpow_const_atTop {c : ‚ÑÇ} (hc : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚Ñù) => (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) =Œò[atTop] fun x => x ^ (c.re - 1) := by
  calc
    _ =·∂Ý[atTop] fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ c * x ^ (c - 1) := by
      filter_upwards [eventually_ne_atTop 0] with x hx using by rw [deriv_ofReal_cpow_const hx hc]
    _ =Œò[atTop] fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ ‚Äñ(x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (c - 1)‚Äñ :=
      (Asymptotics.IsTheta.of_norm_eventuallyEq EventuallyEq.rfl).const_mul_left hc
    _ =·∂Ý[atTop] fun x ‚Ü¶ x ^ (c.re - 1) := by
      filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with x hx
      rw [norm_eq_abs, abs_cpow_eq_rpow_re_of_pos hx, sub_re, one_re]

theorem isBigO_deriv_ofReal_cpow_const_atTop (c : ‚ÑÇ) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚Ñù) => (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) =O[atTop] fun x => x ^ (c.re - 1) := by
  obtain rfl | hc := eq_or_ne c 0
  ¬∑ simp_rw [cpow_zero, deriv_const', Asymptotics.isBigO_zero]
  ¬∑ exact (isTheta_deriv_ofReal_cpow_const_atTop hc).1

end deriv

namespace Real

variable {x y z : ‚Ñù}

/-- `(x, y) ‚Ü¶ x ^ y` is strictly differentiable at `p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù` such that `0 < p.fst`. -/
theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_rpow_of_pos (p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù) (hp : 0 < p.1) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚Ñù ‚Ñù ‚Ñù +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚Ñù ‚Ñù ‚Ñù) p := by
  have : (fun x : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    (continuousAt_fst.eventually (lt_mem_nhds hp)).mono fun p hp => rpow_def_of_pos hp _
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  convert ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.log hp.ne').mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).exp using 1
  rw [rpow_sub_one hp.ne', ‚Üê rpow_def_of_pos hp, smul_add, smul_smul, mul_div_left_comm,
    div_eq_mul_inv, smul_smul, smul_smul, mul_assoc, add_comm]

/-- `(x, y) ‚Ü¶ x ^ y` is strictly differentiable at `p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù` such that `p.fst < 0`. -/
theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_rpow_of_neg (p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù) (hp : p.1 < 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚Ñù ‚Ñù ‚Ñù +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1 - exp (log p.1 * p.2) * sin (p.2 * œÄ) * œÄ) ‚Ä¢
          ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚Ñù ‚Ñù ‚Ñù) p := by
  have : (fun x : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) * cos (x.2 * œÄ) :=
    (continuousAt_fst.eventually (gt_mem_nhds hp)).mono fun p hp => rpow_def_of_neg hp _
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  convert ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.log hp.ne).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).exp.mul
    (hasStrictFDerivAt_snd.mul_const œÄ).cos using 1
  simp_rw [rpow_sub_one hp.ne, smul_add, ‚Üê add_assoc, smul_smul, ‚Üê add_smul, ‚Üê mul_assoc,
    mul_comm (cos _), ‚Üê rpow_def_of_neg hp]
  rw [div_eq_mul_inv, add_comm]; congr 2 <;> ring

/-- The function `fun (x, y) => x ^ y` is infinitely smooth at `(x, y)` unless `x = 0`. -/
theorem contDiffAt_rpow_of_ne (p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù) (hp : p.1 ‚âÝ 0) {n : WithTop ‚Ñï‚àû} :
    ContDiffAt ‚Ñù n (fun p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => p.1 ^ p.2) p := by
  cases' hp.lt_or_lt with hneg hpos
  exacts
    [(((contDiffAt_fst.log hneg.ne).mul contDiffAt_snd).exp.mul
          (contDiffAt_snd.mul contDiffAt_const).cos).congr_of_eventuallyEq
      ((continuousAt_fst.eventually (gt_mem_nhds hneg)).mono fun p hp => rpow_def_of_neg hp _),
    ((contDiffAt_fst.log hpos.ne').mul contDiffAt_snd).exp.congr_of_eventuallyEq
      ((continuousAt_fst.eventually (lt_mem_nhds hpos)).mono fun p hp => rpow_def_of_pos hp _)]

theorem differentiableAt_rpow_of_ne (p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù) (hp : p.1 ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun p : ‚Ñù √ó ‚Ñù => p.1 ^ p.2) p :=
  (contDiffAt_rpow_of_ne p hp).differentiableAt le_rfl

theorem _root_.HasStrictDerivAt.rpow {f g : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {f' g' : ‚Ñù} (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)
    (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x) (h : 0 < f x) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (f' * g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) + g' * f x ^ g x * Real.log (f x)) x := by
  convert (hasStrictFDerivAt_rpow_of_pos ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h).comp_hasStrictDerivAt x
    (hf.prod hg) using 1
  simp [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_rpow_const_of_ne {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) (p : ‚Ñù) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => x ^ p) (p * x ^ (p - 1)) x := by
  cases' hx.lt_or_lt with hx hx
  ¬∑ have := (hasStrictFDerivAt_rpow_of_neg (x, p) hx).comp_hasStrictDerivAt x
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id x).prod (hasStrictDerivAt_const x p))
    convert this using 1; simp
  ¬∑ simpa using (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).rpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x p) hx

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_rpow {a : ‚Ñù} (ha : 0 < a) (x : ‚Ñù) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => a ^ x) (a ^ x * log a) x := by
  simpa using (hasStrictDerivAt_const _ _).rpow (hasStrictDerivAt_id x) ha

lemma differentiableAt_rpow_const_of_ne (p : ‚Ñù) {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun x => x ^ p) x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_rpow_const_of_ne hx p).differentiableAt

lemma differentiableOn_rpow_const (p : ‚Ñù) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚Ñù (fun x => (x : ‚Ñù) ^ p) {0}·∂ú :=
  fun _ hx => (Real.differentiableAt_rpow_const_of_ne p hx).differentiableWithinAt

/-- This lemma says that `fun x => a ^ x` is strictly differentiable for `a < 0`. Note that these
values of `a` are outside of the "official" domain of `a ^ x`, and we may redefine `a ^ x`
for negative `a` if some other definition will be more convenient. -/
theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_rpow_of_neg {a x : ‚Ñù} (ha : a < 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => a ^ x) (a ^ x * log a - exp (log a * x) * sin (x * œÄ) * œÄ) x := by
  simpa using (hasStrictFDerivAt_rpow_of_neg (a, x) ha).comp_hasStrictDerivAt x
    ((hasStrictDerivAt_const _ _).prod (hasStrictDerivAt_id _))

end Real

namespace Real

variable {z x y : ‚Ñù}

theorem hasDerivAt_rpow_const {x p : ‚Ñù} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => x ^ p) (p * x ^ (p - 1)) x := by
  rcases ne_or_eq x 0 with (hx | rfl)
  ¬∑ exact (hasStrictDerivAt_rpow_const_of_ne hx _).hasDerivAt
  replace h : 1 ‚â§ p := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
  apply hasDerivAt_of_hasDerivAt_of_ne fun x hx =>
    (hasStrictDerivAt_rpow_const_of_ne hx p).hasDerivAt
  exacts [continuousAt_id.rpow_const (Or.inr (zero_le_one.trans h)),
    continuousAt_const.mul (continuousAt_id.rpow_const (Or.inr (sub_nonneg.2 h)))]

theorem differentiable_rpow_const {p : ‚Ñù} (hp : 1 ‚â§ p) : Differentiable ‚Ñù fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p :=
  fun _ => (hasDerivAt_rpow_const (Or.inr hp)).differentiableAt

theorem deriv_rpow_const {x p : ‚Ñù} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    deriv (fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p) x = p * x ^ (p - 1) :=
  (hasDerivAt_rpow_const h).deriv

theorem deriv_rpow_const' {p : ‚Ñù} (h : 1 ‚â§ p) :
    (deriv fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p) = fun x => p * x ^ (p - 1) :=
  funext fun _ => deriv_rpow_const (Or.inr h)

theorem contDiffAt_rpow_const_of_ne {x p : ‚Ñù} {n : WithTop ‚Ñï‚àû} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) :
    ContDiffAt ‚Ñù n (fun x => x ^ p) x :=
  (contDiffAt_rpow_of_ne (x, p) h).comp x (contDiffAt_id.prod contDiffAt_const)

theorem contDiff_rpow_const_of_le {p : ‚Ñù} {n : ‚Ñï} (h : ‚Üën ‚â§ p) :
    ContDiff ‚Ñù n fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p := by
  induction' n with n ihn generalizing p
  ¬∑ exact contDiff_zero.2 (continuous_id.rpow_const fun x => Or.inr <| by simpa using h)
  ¬∑ have h1 : 1 ‚â§ p := le_trans (by simp) h
    rw [Nat.cast_succ, ‚Üê le_sub_iff_add_le] at h
    rw [show ((n + 1 : ‚Ñï) : WithTop ‚Ñï‚àû) = n + 1 from rfl,
      contDiff_succ_iff_deriv, deriv_rpow_const' h1]
    simp only [WithTop.natCast_ne_top, analyticOn_univ, IsEmpty.forall_iff, true_and]
    exact ‚ü®differentiable_rpow_const h1, contDiff_const.mul (ihn h)‚ü©

theorem contDiffAt_rpow_const_of_le {x p : ‚Ñù} {n : ‚Ñï} (h : ‚Üën ‚â§ p) :
    ContDiffAt ‚Ñù n (fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p) x :=
  (contDiff_rpow_const_of_le h).contDiffAt

theorem contDiffAt_rpow_const {x p : ‚Ñù} {n : ‚Ñï} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚Üën ‚â§ p) :
    ContDiffAt ‚Ñù n (fun x : ‚Ñù => x ^ p) x :=
  h.elim contDiffAt_rpow_const_of_ne contDiffAt_rpow_const_of_le

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_rpow_const {x p : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => x ^ p) (p * x ^ (p - 1)) x :=
  ContDiffAt.hasStrictDerivAt' (contDiffAt_rpow_const (by rwa [‚Üê Nat.cast_one] at hx))
    (hasDerivAt_rpow_const hx) le_rfl

end Real

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.rpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)

      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Real.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by

    Differentiable ‚Ñù fun x => f x ^ p := fun x => (hf x).rpow_const (h x)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_rpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hc : 0 < c) :

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.rpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)

  convert (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.rpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h).hasDerivWithinAt using 1

      (f' * g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) + g' * f x ^ g x * Real.log (f x)) x := by

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.rpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hx : f x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun y => f y ^ p) (f' * p * f x ^ (p - 1)) s x := by
  convert (hasDerivAt_rpow_const hx).comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf using 1
  ring

theorem HasDerivAt.rpow_const (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hx : f x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y => f y ^ p) (f' * p * f x ^ (p - 1)) x := by
  rw [‚Üê hasDerivWithinAt_univ] at *
  exact hf.rpow_const hx

theorem derivWithin_rpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚Ñù f s x) (hx : f x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p)
    (hxs : UniqueDiffWithinAt ‚Ñù s x) :
    derivWithin (fun x => f x ^ p) s x = derivWithin f s x * p * f x ^ (p - 1) :=
  (hf.hasDerivWithinAt.rpow_const hx).derivWithin hxs

@[simp]
theorem deriv_rpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x) (hx : f x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® 1 ‚â§ p) :
    deriv (fun x => f x ^ p) x = deriv f x * p * f x ^ (p - 1) :=

formal_statement:
theorem deriv_norm_ofReal_cpow (c : ‚ÑÇ) {t : ‚Ñù} (ht : 0 < t) :
    (deriv fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ ‚Äñ(x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c‚Äñ) t = c.re * t ^ (c.re - 1)
name: deriv_norm_ofReal_cpow
responses:
To complete the proof of `deriv_norm_ofReal_cpow`, we need to express the norm of the power function `(x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c` for `x > 0` as a real power function and then compute its derivative. 

Given $x > 0$, we have $(x : ‚ÑÇ)^c = \exp(c \cdot \log(x : ‚ÑÇ))$. Using the property $\log(x : ‚ÑÇ) = \ln(x) + i \cdot 0 = \ln(x)$, we have $(x : ‚ÑÇ)^c = \exp((c_{re} + i \cdot c_{im}) \cdot \ln(x)) = \exp(c_{re} \ln(x)) \cdot \exp(i \cdot c_{im} \ln(x))$. 
Taking the norm, we get $|(x : ‚ÑÇ)^c| = |\exp(c_{re} \ln(x))| \cdot |\exp(i \cdot c_{im} \ln(x))| = x^{c_{re}} \cdot 1 = x^{c_{re}}$.
Thus, we are differentiating $t \mapsto t^{c_{re}}$.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))

lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne), natDegree_comp, ascPochhammer_natDegree,
    add_comm_sub, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, natDegree_add_C, natDegree_X, mul_one]

lemma coeff_preHilbertPoly_self [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).coeff d = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  delta preHilbertPoly
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  have heq : d = ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1)).natDegree :=
    (natDegree_preHilbertPoly F d k).symm.trans (natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne))
  nth_rw 3 [heq]
  calc
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C ((k : F) - 1))).leadingCoeff := by
    simp only [sub_add, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, coeff_smul, coeff_natDegree]
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
    simp only [leadingCoeff_comp (ne_of_eq_of_ne (natDegree_X_sub_C _) one_ne_zero), Monic.def.1
      (monic_ascPochhammer _ _), one_mul, leadingCoeff_X_sub_C, one_pow, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

lemma leadingCoeff_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).leadingCoeff = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [leadingCoeff, natDegree_preHilbertPoly, coeff_preHilbertPoly_self]

lemma preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add [CharZero F] (d : ‚Ñï) {k n : ‚Ñï} (hkn : k ‚â§ n):
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F) = (n - k + d).choose d := by
  have : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  calc
  _ = (‚Üëd !)‚Åª¬π * eval (‚Üë(n - k + 1)) (ascPochhammer F d) := by simp [cast_sub hkn, preHilbertPoly]
  _ = (n - k + d).choose d := by
    rw [ascPochhammer_nat_eq_natCast_ascFactorial];
    field_simp [ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]

variable {F}

/--
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p 0 = 0`; for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p (d + 1)`
is defined as `‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d i`. If
`M` is a graded module whose Poincar√© series can be written as `p(X)/(1 - X)·µà` for some
`p : ‚Ñö[X]` with integer coefficients, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is the Hilbert
polynomial of `M`. See also `Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`,
which says that `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * PowerSeries.invOneSubPow F d)` equals
`(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) : (d : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí F[X]
  | 0 => 0
  | d + 1 => ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_left (d : ‚Ñï) : hilbertPoly (0 : F[X]) d = 0 := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only
  | succ d _ => simp only [coeff_zero, zero_smul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_right (p : F[X]) : hilbertPoly p 0 = 0 := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly p (d + 1) = ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1) = preHilbertPoly F d k := by
  delta hilbertPoly; simp

lemma hilbertPoly_add_left (p q : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p + q) d = hilbertPoly p d + hilbertPoly q d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [add_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only [‚Üê coeff_add]
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _]
      exact sum_add_index _ _ _ (fun _ => zero_smul ..) (fun _ _ _ => add_smul ..)

lemma hilbertPoly_smul (a : F) (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (a ‚Ä¢ p) d = a ‚Ä¢ hilbertPoly p d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [smul_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, ‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, Polynomial.smul_sum,
        sum_smul_index' _ _ _ fun i => zero_smul F (preHilbertPoly F d i)]
      simp only [smul_assoc]

variable (F) in
/--
The function that sends any `p : F[X]` to `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is an `F`-linear map from
`F[X]` to `F[X]`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly_linearMap (d : ‚Ñï) : F[X] ‚Üí‚Çó[F] F[X] where
  toFun p := hilbertPoly p d
  map_add' p q := hilbertPoly_add_left p q d
  map_smul' r p := hilbertPoly_smul r p d

variable [CharZero F]

/--
The key property of Hilbert polynomials. If `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, `p : F[X]` and
`d : ‚Ñï`, then for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`, `(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` equals the
coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
-/
theorem coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval
    {p : F[X]} (d : ‚Ñï) {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : p.natDegree < n) :
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only [invOneSubPow_zero, Units.val_one, mul_one, coeff_coe, eval_zero]
            exact coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn
  | succ d hd =>
      simp only [eval_finset_sum, eval_smul, smul_eq_mul]
      rw [‚Üê Finset.sum_coe_sort]
      have h_le (i : p.support) : (i : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ n :=
        le_trans (le_natDegree_of_ne_zero <| mem_support_iff.1 i.2) hn.le
      have h (i : p.support) : eval ‚Üën (preHilbertPoly F d ‚Üëi) = (n + d - ‚Üëi).choose d := by
        rw [preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add _ _ (h_le i), Nat.sub_add_comm (h_le i)]
      simp_rw [h]
      rw [Finset.sum_coe_sort _ (fun x => (p.coeff ‚Üëx) * (_ + d - ‚Üëx).choose _),
        PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk,
        invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos _ _ (zero_lt_succ d)]
      simp only [coeff_coe, coeff_mk]
      symm
      refine Finset.sum_subset_zero_on_sdiff (fun s hs ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_)
      ¬∑ rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff]
        exact h_le ‚ü®s, hs‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, mem_support_iff, not_not] at hx
        rw [hx.2, zero_mul]
      ¬∑ rw [add_comm, Nat.add_sub_assoc (h_le ‚ü®x, hx‚ü©), succ_eq_add_one, add_tsub_cancel_right]

/--
The polynomial satisfying the key property of `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is unique.
-/
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d; constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    exact fun n => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
  ¬∑ rintro h ‚ü®N, hhN‚ü©
    apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq h (hilbertPoly p d)
    apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N p.natDegree)).image cast_injective.injOn).mono
    rintro x ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
    simp only [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn ‚ä¢
    rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn.2, hhN n hn.1]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-17")] alias exists_unique_hilbertPoly := existsUnique_hilbertPoly
formal_statement:
/--
If `h : F[X]` and there exists some `N : ‚Ñï` such that for any number `n : ‚Ñï` bigger than `N`
we have `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)`, then `h` is exactly
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.
-/
theorem eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval
    {p h : F[X]} {d : ‚Ñï} (N : ‚Ñï) (hhN : ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)) :
    h = hilbertPoly p d
name: Polynomial.eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval
responses:
To prove that $h = \text{hilbertPoly } p \, d$ given the condition that their evaluations agree for all large enough $n$, we utilize the uniqueness result `existsUnique_hilbertPoly`. Specifically, we know that $\text{hilbertPoly } p \, d$ satisfies the property for $n > p.\text{natDegree}$. Given the hypothesis that $h$ also satisfies this property for $n > N$, we can conclude they agree on an infinite set of values, implying the equality of the polynomials in $F[X]$ because $F$ is a field of characteristic $0$.

FINAL```
    := by
  apply (existsUnique_hilbertPoly p d).unique
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    exact fun n => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
  ¬∑ use N
    exact hhN
```

To prove that `h = hilbertPoly p d` under the given condition, we use the uniqueness result established in `existsUnique_hilbertPoly`. We already know that `hilbertPoly p d` satisfies the property for all `n > p.natDegree`. By the hypothesis, `h` satisfies the same property for all `n > N`. Therefore, both `h` and `hilbertPoly p d` agree on the set of integers strictly greater than `max N p.natDegree`. Since this set is infinite and `h` and `hilbertPoly p d` are polynomials, they must be equal.

FINAL```
    := by
  apply (existsUnique_hilbertPoly p d).unique
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    exact fun n => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
  ¬∑ use N
    exact hhN
```

To prove that `h = hilbertPoly p d` under the given condition, we use the fact that `h` and `hilbertPoly p d` agree for all `n > max N p.natDegree`. Since the set of such `n` is infinite and a polynomial in `F[X]` over a field `F` of characteristic `0` is uniquely determined by its values on an infinite set, the two polynomials must be equal.

FINAL```
    := by
  apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq h (hilbertPoly p d)
  apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N p.natDegree)).image cast_injective.injOn).mono
  rintro _ ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
  simp only [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn ‚ä¢
  rw [‚Üê hhN n hn.1, ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn.2]
```
