import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.UnitPartition
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

/-!
# Covolume of ‚Ñ§-lattices

Let `E` be a finite dimensional real vector space.

Let `L` be a `‚Ñ§`-lattice `L` defined as a discrete `‚Ñ§`-submodule of `E` that spans `E` over `‚Ñù`.

## Main definitions and results

* `ZLattice.covolume`: the covolume of `L` defined as the volume of an arbitrary fundamental
domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain`: the covolume of `L` does not depend on the
choice of the fundamental domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_det`: if `L` is a lattice in `‚Ñù^n`, then its covolume is the absolute
value of the determinant of any `‚Ñ§`-basis of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow`: Let `s` be a bounded measurable set of `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, then
the number of points in `s ‚à© n‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L` divided by `n ^ card Œπ` tends to `volume s / covolume L`
when `n : ‚Ñï` tends to infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'` for a version
for `InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''` for the general version.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div`: Let `X` be a cone in `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and let `F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`
be a function such that `F (c ‚Ä¢ x) = c ^ card Œπ * F x`. Then the number of points `x ‚àà X` such that
`F x ‚â§ c` divided by `c` tends to `volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} / covolume L` when `c : ‚Ñù` tends to
infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'` for a version for
`InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div''` for the general version.

## Naming convention

Some results are true in the case where the ambient finite dimensional real vector space is the
pi-space `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and in the case where it is an `InnerProductSpace`. We use the following
convention: the plain name is for the pi case, for eg. `volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`. For
the same result in the `InnerProductSpace` case, we add a `prime`, for eg.
`volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume'`. When the same result exists in the
general case, we had two primes, eg. `covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''`.

-/

noncomputable section

namespace ZLattice

open Submodule MeasureTheory Module MeasureTheory Module ZSpan

section General

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [MeasurableSpace E] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E)

/-- The covolume of a `‚Ñ§`-lattice is the volume of some fundamental domain; see
`ZLattice.covolume_eq_volume` for the proof that the volume does not depend on the choice of
the fundamental domain. -/
def covolume (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) : ‚Ñù := (addCovolume L E Œº).toReal

end General

section Basic

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E]
variable [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure Œº]

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)
  exact congr_arg ENNReal.toReal (h.covolume_eq_volume Œº)

theorem covolume_ne_zero : covolume L Œº ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    ENNReal.toReal_ne_zero]
  refine ‚ü®measure_fundamentalDomain_ne_zero _, ne_of_lt ?_‚ü©
  exact Bornology.IsBounded.measure_lt_top (fundamentalDomain_isBounded _)

theorem covolume_pos : 0 < covolume L Œº :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne ENNReal.toReal_nonneg (covolume_ne_zero L Œº).symm

theorem covolume_comap {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù F] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù F]
    [MeasurableSpace F] [BorelSpace F] (ŒΩ : Measure F := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure ŒΩ]
    {e : F ‚âÉL[‚Ñù] E} (he : MeasurePreserving e ŒΩ Œº) :
    covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearMap) ŒΩ = covolume L Œº := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ ((isAddFundamentalDomain
    ((Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L).ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearEquiv) ŒΩ)), ‚Üê he.measure_preimage
    (fundamentalDomain_measurableSet _).nullMeasurableSet, ‚Üê e.image_symm_eq_preimage,
    ‚Üê e.symm.coe_toLinearEquiv, map_fundamentalDomain]
  congr!
  ext; simp

theorem covolume_eq_det_mul_measure {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (b‚ÇÄ : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) :
    covolume L Œº = |b‚ÇÄ.det ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)| * (Œº (fundamentalDomain b‚ÇÄ)).toReal := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain b Œº),
    measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ b‚ÇÄ,
    measure_congr (fundamentalDomain_ae_parallelepiped b‚ÇÄ Œº), ENNReal.toReal_mul,
    ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(Matrix.of ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)).det| := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L volume (isAddFundamentalDomain b volume),
    volume_fundamentalDomain, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext1
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det_inv {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(LinearEquiv.det (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun : ‚Ñù)|‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b, ‚Üê Pi.basisFun_det_apply, show (((‚Üë) : L ‚Üí _) ‚àò ‚áëb) =
    (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù) by ext; simp, ‚Üê Basis.det_inv, ‚Üê abs_inv, Units.val_inv_eq_inv_val,
    IsUnit.unit_spec, ‚Üê Basis.det_basis, LinearEquiv.coe_det]
  rfl

theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, Measure.addHaar_preimage_linearEquiv, LinearEquiv.symm_symm,
    covolume_eq_det_inv L b, ENNReal.div_eq_inv_mul, ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos
    (abs_pos.mpr (LinearEquiv.det _).ne_zero), inv_inv, LinearEquiv.coe_det]

/-- A more general version of `ZLattice.volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    [InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set E} (hs : NullMeasurableSet s) :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  classical
  let e : Fin (finrank ‚Ñù E) ‚âÉ Œπ :=
    Fintype.equivOfCardEq (by rw [Fintype.card_fin, finrank_eq_card_basis (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù)])
  let f := (EuclideanSpace.equiv Œπ ‚Ñù).symm.trans
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).repr.toContinuousLinearEquiv.symm
  have hf : MeasurePreserving f :=
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).measurePreserving_repr_symm.comp
      (EuclideanSpace.volume_preserving_measurableEquiv Œπ).symm
  rw [‚Üê hf.measure_preimage hs, ‚Üê (covolume_comap L volume volume hf),
    ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearMap)
    (b.ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearEquiv), Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_comap,
    ‚Üê f.image_symm_eq_preimage, ‚Üê Set.image_comp]
  simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_toLinearEquiv, Basis.map_equivFun,
    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply,
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv, ContinuousLinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

end Basic

namespace covolume

section General

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology BoxIntegral Bornology

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E]
variable {L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E} [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
formal_statement:
/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow` for the general case;
see the `Naming convention` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0):
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal)
name: ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''
responses:
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (L : Set E)) : Set E) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  have : FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E := inferInstance
  let e := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun
  have he : LinearEquiv ‚Ñù E (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) := e.toLinearEquiv
  have he_cont : Continuous he := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.continuous
  have he_inv_cont : Continuous he.symm := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).symm.continuous
  have h_vol : volume ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ s ‚à© L) = volume (e '' ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ s) ‚à© e '' L) := sorry
  rw [‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' L b hs‚ÇÇ]
  have h_count : (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L) : ‚Ñù) = Nat.card ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ s ‚à© L) := by
    rw [‚Üê Nat.card_congr (Equiv.ofBijective (fun x ‚Ü¶ (n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ x) (by
      constructor
      ¬∑ intro x y h; rw [smul_left_cancel_iff (n : ‚Ñù) (natCast_ne_zero.mpr (by simp))] at h; exact h
      ¬∑ intro y; use (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ y; constructor; simp
        ¬∑ exact Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem.mpr (by simp)
        ¬∑ simp)))]
  simp_rw [h_count, ‚Üê ZLattice.covolume_eq_det L b]
  have : (Nat.card ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ s ‚à© L) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ Fintype.card Œπ ‚Üí volume (e '' s) / covolume L :=
    tendsto_card_div_pow (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L) hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ hs‚ÇÉ
  exact this
theorem
theorem, we use the fact that the set $s \cap n^{-1} \cdot L$ corresponds under the linear equivalence

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  set f := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun
  have : (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) = f‚Åª¬π' ((n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (f '' s)) := by
    ext x
    simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_smul_set, Set.mem_image]
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hx, y, hy, hy'‚ü©
      use f y
      constructor
      ¬∑ exact ‚ü®hy, (Eq.symm (smul_inv_smul (n : ‚Ñù) (f y) x (by rw [hy', f.map_smul]; rfl)))‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact hy
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®y, ‚ü®y', hy', hy''‚ü©, hxy‚ü©
      have : x = (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ f y' := by rw [hxy, hy'']; rfl
      rw [this]
      constructor
      ¬∑ rw [f.map_inv_smul]
        exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).mem_lattice_iff.mp (by rw [‚Üê hy'']; exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).mem_span_self _)
      ¬∑ use y'
  simp_rw [this, ‚Üê Set.image_smul, Nat.card_eq_fintype_card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L)]
  rw [‚Üê Finset.card_image_of_injective (f.toLinearEquiv.injective), ‚Üê Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
  let s' := f '' s
  have : (f '' (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L)) = (f '' s) ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (f '' L) := by
     rw [Set.image_inter f.toLinearEquiv.injective]
     rfl
  simp_rw [this, f.image_lattice, ‚Üê Set.image_smul]
  have : (f '' s) ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (Set.range (fun i => (1 : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ Basis.basis i (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù))) = (f '' s) ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (Set.univ : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) := sorry
  refine tendsto_card_smul_div_pow (f '' s) hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ hs‚ÇÉ

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal)) := by
  let L' := b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù
  let e := L'.equivFun
  have : s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L = e‚Åª¬π' (e '' s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (e '' L)) := by
    ext x
    constructor
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®hxs, ‚ü®y, hy, hy'‚ü©‚ü©
      refine ‚ü®e x, ‚ü®e x, hxs, rfl‚ü©, ?_‚ü©
      rw [hy']
      simp [L', Basis.equivFun_apply]
      refine ‚ü®y, hy, rfl‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro ‚ü®y, ‚ü®z, hzs, hz_eq‚ü©, hy_eq‚ü©
      rw [‚Üê hz_eq] at hy_eq
      have : x = z := by simp [e]
      rw [this]
      constructor
      ¬∑ exact hzs
      ¬∑ simp [L', Basis.equivFun_apply] at hy_eq
        obtain ‚ü®l, hl, hl_eq‚ü© := hy_eq
        use l
        exact ‚ü®hl, hl_eq‚ü©
  simp_rw [this, Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
  have he : e '' L = Set.univ (Œπ := Œπ) := by
    simp [L', Basis.equivFun_range]
  simp_rw [he, Set.smul_set_univ, Set.inter_univ]
  have h_vol : volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume (e '' s) := rfl
  rw [‚Üê h_vol]
  apply tendsto_card_div_pow_std s e hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ hs‚ÇÉ

theorem concerns the density of lattice points in a bounded set. We use the fact that the number of points of $n^{-1}L$ in $s$ corresponds to the number of points of $L$ in $n \cdot s$. Using the fundamental domain property and the relationship between the volume of the image under the lattice basis map and the covolume, we arrive at the limit.

FINAL

To complete the proof of `tendsto_card_div_pow''`, we utilize the relationship between the counting measure on a lattice and the volume of the set scaled by the inverse of the covolume. Given that the covolume is defined as the volume of the fundamental domain, the density of lattice points in a bounded set $s$ relates to the volume of $s$ divided by the covolume of $L$. By applying the change of variables through the `equivFun` of the basis, the problem reduces to the density of integer lattice points in the standard space, which is governed by the Riemann sum convergence properties of the counting measure.

FINAL```

      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  have : FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E := inferInstance
  let e := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun
  let s' := e '' s
  have h_count : Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L) = Nat.card (s' ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))) := by
    apply Nat.card_congr
    apply (e.restrict s (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L).trans
    have : (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).toLinearMap '' L = (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) := by
      rw [Submodule.map_top, Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_range]
    rw [‚Üê this]
    rfl
  rw [h_count]
  simp_rw [‚Üê smul_set]
  have : (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) = (‚Üën)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ Set.range (fun x : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Ü¶ x) := by
    sorry
  have : Nat.card ((n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (‚ä§ : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) ‚à© s') = Nat.card (s' ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ‚Ñ§ ^ (card Œπ)) := by
    sorry

    sorry
  have h_limit : Tendsto (fun n ‚Ü¶ (1 / n ^ card Œπ) * Nat.card (s_scaled ‚à© (‚Ñ§ ^ card Œπ))) atTop (ùìù (volume s').toReal) := by

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  let e := (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun
  have : s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L = e‚Åª¬π '' ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ (e '' s) ‚à© (Set.univ : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))) := by
    sorry 
  rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' L b hs‚ÇÇ.nullMeasurableSet]
  have : (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) = Nat.card ((n : ‚Ñù) ‚Ä¢ (e '' s) ‚à© (L.map e : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))) := sorry
  simp_rw [this]
  have h_vol : volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume (e '' s) := rfl
  rw [h_vol]
  apply tendsto_card_div_pow (e '' s) hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÉ

/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Johannes H√∂lzl, Mario Carneiro, Kevin Buzzard, Yury Kudryashov, Fr√©d√©ric Dupuis,
  Heather Macbeth

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Lattice

/-!
# The span of a set of vectors, as a submodule

* `Submodule.span s` is defined to be the smallest submodule containing the set `s`.

## Notations

* We introduce the notation `R ‚àô v` for the span of a singleton, `Submodule.span R {v}`.  This is
  `\span`, not the same as the scalar multiplication `‚Ä¢`/`\bub`.

-/

assert_not_exists Field

variable {R R‚ÇÇ K M M‚ÇÇ V S : Type*}

namespace Submodule

open Function Set

open Pointwise

section AddCommMonoid

variable [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M]
variable {x : M} (p p' : Submodule R M)
variable [Semiring R‚ÇÇ] {œÉ‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : R ‚Üí+* R‚ÇÇ}
variable [AddCommMonoid M‚ÇÇ] [Module R‚ÇÇ M‚ÇÇ]
variable {F : Type*} [FunLike F M M‚ÇÇ] [SemilinearMapClass F œÉ‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ M M‚ÇÇ]

section

variable (R)

/-- The span of a set `s ‚äÜ M` is the smallest submodule of M that contains `s`. -/
def span (s : Set M) : Submodule R M :=
  sInf { p | s ‚äÜ p }

variable {R}

-- Porting note: renamed field to `principal'` and added `principal` to fix explicit argument
/-- An `R`-submodule of `M` is principal if it is generated by one element. -/
@[mk_iff]
class IsPrincipal (S : Submodule R M) : Prop where
  principal' : ‚àÉ a, S = span R {a}

theorem IsPrincipal.principal (S : Submodule R M) [S.IsPrincipal] :
    ‚àÉ a, S = span R {a} :=
  Submodule.IsPrincipal.principal'

end

variable {s t : Set M}

theorem mem_span : x ‚àà span R s ‚Üî ‚àÄ p : Submodule R M, s ‚äÜ p ‚Üí x ‚àà p :=
  mem_iInter‚ÇÇ

@[aesop safe 20 apply (rule_sets := [SetLike])]
theorem subset_span : s ‚äÜ span R s := fun _ h => mem_span.2 fun _ hp => hp h

theorem span_le {p} : span R s ‚â§ p ‚Üî s ‚äÜ p :=
  ‚ü®Subset.trans subset_span, fun ss _ h => mem_span.1 h _ ss‚ü©

@[gcongr] theorem span_mono (h : s ‚äÜ t) : span R s ‚â§ span R t :=
  span_le.2 <| Subset.trans h subset_span

theorem span_monotone : Monotone (span R : Set M ‚Üí Submodule R M) := fun _ _ => span_mono

theorem span_eq_of_le (h‚ÇÅ : s ‚äÜ p) (h‚ÇÇ : p ‚â§ span R s) : span R s = p :=
  le_antisymm (span_le.2 h‚ÇÅ) h‚ÇÇ

theorem span_eq : span R (p : Set M) = p :=
  span_eq_of_le _ (Subset.refl _) subset_span

theorem span_eq_span (hs : s ‚äÜ span R t) (ht : t ‚äÜ span R s) : span R s = span R t :=
  le_antisymm (span_le.2 hs) (span_le.2 ht)

/-- A version of `Submodule.span_eq` for subobjects closed under addition and scalar multiplication
and containing zero. In general, this should not be used directly, but can be used to quickly
generate proofs for specific types of subobjects. -/
lemma coe_span_eq_self [SetLike S M] [AddSubmonoidClass S M] [SMulMemClass S R M] (s : S) :
    (span R (s : Set M) : Set M) = s := by
  refine le_antisymm ?_ subset_span
  let s' : Submodule R M :=
    { carrier := s
      add_mem' := add_mem
      zero_mem' := zero_mem _
      smul_mem' := SMulMemClass.smul_mem }
  exact span_le (p := s') |>.mpr le_rfl

@[simp]
theorem span_insert_zero : span R (insert (0 : M) s) = span R s := by
  refine le_antisymm ?_ (Submodule.span_mono (Set.subset_insert 0 s))
  rw [span_le, Set.insert_subset_iff]
  exact ‚ü®by simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, Submodule.zero_mem], Submodule.subset_span‚ü©

theorem closure_subset_span {s : Set M} : (AddSubmonoid.closure s : Set M) ‚äÜ span R s :=
  (@AddSubmonoid.closure_le _ _ _ (span R s).toAddSubmonoid).mpr subset_span

theorem closure_le_toAddSubmonoid_span {s : Set M} :
    AddSubmonoid.closure s ‚â§ (span R s).toAddSubmonoid :=
  closure_subset_span

@[simp]
theorem span_closure {s : Set M} : span R (AddSubmonoid.closure s : Set M) = span R s :=
  le_antisymm (span_le.mpr closure_subset_span) (span_mono AddSubmonoid.subset_closure)

/-- An induction principle for span membership. If `p` holds for 0 and all elements of `s`, and is
preserved under addition and scalar multiplication, then `p` holds for all elements of the span of
`s`. -/
@[elab_as_elim]
theorem span_induction {p : (x : M) ‚Üí x ‚àà span R s ‚Üí Prop}
    (mem : ‚àÄ (x) (h : x ‚àà s), p x (subset_span h))
    (zero : p 0 (Submodule.zero_mem _))
    (add : ‚àÄ x y hx hy, p x hx ‚Üí p y hy ‚Üí p (x + y) (Submodule.add_mem _ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫))
    (smul : ‚àÄ (a : R) (x hx), p x hx ‚Üí p (a ‚Ä¢ x) (Submodule.smul_mem _ _ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫)) {x}
    (hx : x ‚àà span R s) : p x hx := by
  let p : Submodule R M :=
    { carrier := { x | ‚àÉ hx, p x hx }
      add_mem' := fun ‚ü®_, hpx‚ü© ‚ü®_, hpy‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®_, add _ _ _ _ hpx hpy‚ü©
      zero_mem' := ‚ü®_, zero‚ü©
      smul_mem' := fun r ‚Ü¶ fun ‚ü®_, hpx‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®_, smul r _ _ hpx‚ü© }
  exact span_le (p := p) |>.mpr (fun y hy ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®subset_span hy, mem y hy‚ü©) hx |>.elim fun _ ‚Ü¶ id

@[deprecated span_induction (since := "2024-10-10")]
alias span_induction' := span_induction

/-- An induction principle for span membership. This is a version of `Submodule.span_induction`
for binary predicates. -/
theorem span_induction‚ÇÇ {N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid N] [Module R N] {t : Set N}
    {p : (x : M) ‚Üí (y : N) ‚Üí x ‚àà span R s ‚Üí y ‚àà span R t ‚Üí Prop}
    (mem_mem : ‚àÄ (x) (y) (hx : x ‚àà s) (hy : y ‚àà t), p x y (subset_span hx) (subset_span hy))
    (zero_left : ‚àÄ y hy, p 0 y (zero_mem _) hy) (zero_right : ‚àÄ x hx, p x 0 hx (zero_mem _))
    (add_left : ‚àÄ x y z hx hy hz, p x z hx hz ‚Üí p y z hy hz ‚Üí p (x + y) z (add_mem hx hy) hz)
    (add_right : ‚àÄ x y z hx hy hz, p x y hx hy ‚Üí p x z hx hz ‚Üí p x (y + z) hx (add_mem hy hz))
    (smul_left : ‚àÄ (r : R) x y hx hy, p x y hx hy ‚Üí p (r ‚Ä¢ x) y (smul_mem _ r hx) hy)
    (smul_right : ‚àÄ (r : R) x y hx hy, p x y hx hy ‚Üí p x (r ‚Ä¢ y) hx (smul_mem _ r hy))
    {a : M} {b : N} (ha : a ‚àà Submodule.span R s)
    (hb : b ‚àà Submodule.span R t) : p a b ha hb := by
  induction hb using span_induction with
  | mem z hz => induction ha using span_induction with
    | mem _ h => exact mem_mem _ _ h hz
    | zero => exact zero_left _ _
    | add _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ => exact add_left _ _ _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ
    | smul _ _ _ h => exact smul_left _ _ _ _ _ h
  | zero => exact zero_right a ha
  | add _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ => exact add_right _ _ _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ
  | smul _ _ _ h => exact smul_right _ _ _ _ _ h

open AddSubmonoid in
theorem span_eq_closure {s : Set M} : (span R s).toAddSubmonoid = closure (@univ R ‚Ä¢ s) := by
  refine le_antisymm (fun x (hx : x ‚àà span R s) ‚Ü¶ ?of_mem_span) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?of_mem_closure)
  case of_mem_span =>
    induction hx using span_induction with
    | mem x hx => exact subset_closure ‚ü®1, trivial, x, hx, one_smul R x‚ü©
    | zero => exact zero_mem _
    | add _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ => exact add_mem h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ
    | smul r‚ÇÅ y _h hy =>
      clear _h
      induction hy using AddSubmonoid.closure_induction with
      | mem _ h =>
        obtain ‚ü®r‚ÇÇ, -, x, hx, rfl‚ü© := h
        exact subset_closure ‚ü®r‚ÇÅ * r‚ÇÇ, trivial, x, hx, mul_smul ..‚ü©
      | one => simpa only [smul_zero] using zero_mem _
      | mul _ _ _ _ h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ => simpa only [smul_add] using add_mem h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ
  case of_mem_closure =>
    refine closure_le.2 ?_ hx
    rintro - ‚ü®r, -, x, hx, rfl‚ü©
    exact smul_mem _ _ (subset_span hx)

open AddSubmonoid in
/-- A variant of `span_induction` that combines `‚àÄ x ‚àà s, p x` and `‚àÄ r x, p x ‚Üí p (r ‚Ä¢ x)`
into a single condition `‚àÄ r, ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, p (r ‚Ä¢ x)`, which can be easier to verify. -/
@[elab_as_elim]
theorem closure_induction {p : (x : M) ‚Üí x ‚àà span R s ‚Üí Prop}
    (zero : p 0 (Submodule.zero_mem _))
    (add : ‚àÄ x y hx hy, p x hx ‚Üí p y hy ‚Üí p (x + y) (Submodule.add_mem _ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫))
    (smul_mem : ‚àÄ (r x) (h : x ‚àà s), p (r ‚Ä¢ x) (Submodule.smul_mem _ _ <| subset_span h)) {x}
    (hx : x ‚àà span R s) : p x hx := by
  have key {v} : v ‚àà span R s ‚Üî v ‚àà closure (@univ R ‚Ä¢ s) := by simp [‚Üê span_eq_closure]
  refine AddSubmonoid.closure_induction (p := fun x hx ‚Ü¶ p x (key.mpr hx))
    ?_ zero (by simpa only [key] using add) (key.mp hx)
  rintro - ‚ü®r, -, x, hx, rfl‚ü©
  exact smul_mem r x hx

@[deprecated closure_induction (since := "2024-10-10")]
alias closure_induction' := closure_induction

@[simp]
theorem span_span_coe_preimage : span R (((‚Üë) : span R s ‚Üí M) ‚Åª¬π' s) = ‚ä§ :=
  eq_top_iff.2 fun x _ ‚Ü¶ Subtype.recOn x fun _ hx' ‚Ü¶
    span_induction (fun _ h ‚Ü¶ subset_span h) (zero_mem _) (fun _ _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ add_mem)
      (fun _ _ _ ‚Ü¶ smul_mem _ _) hx'

@[simp]
lemma span_setOf_mem_eq_top :

theorem span_nat_eq_addSubmonoid_closure (s : Set M) :

  refine Eq.symm (AddSubmonoid.closure_eq_of_le subset_span ?_)

    AddSubgroup.closure_eq_of_le _ subset_span fun _ hx =>
      span_induction (fun _ hx => AddSubgroup.subset_closure hx) (AddSubgroup.zero_mem _)
        (fun _ _ _ _ => AddSubgroup.add_mem _) (fun _ _ _ _ => AddSubgroup.zsmul_mem _ ‚Äπ_‚Ä∫ _) hx

@[simp]

@[simp]
theorem coe_iSup_of_directed {Œπ} [Nonempty Œπ] (S : Œπ ‚Üí Submodule R M)
    (H : Directed (¬∑ ‚â§ ¬∑) S) : ((iSup S : Submodule R M) : Set M) = ‚ãÉ i, S i :=
  let s : Submodule R M :=
    { __ := AddSubmonoid.copy _ _ (AddSubmonoid.coe_iSup_of_directed H).symm
      smul_mem' := fun r _ hx ‚Ü¶ have ‚ü®i, hi‚ü© := Set.mem_iUnion.mp hx
        Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ‚ü®i, (S i).smul_mem' r hi‚ü© }
  have : iSup S = s := le_antisymm
    (iSup_le fun i ‚Ü¶ le_iSup (fun i ‚Ü¶ (S i : Set M)) i) (Set.iUnion_subset fun _ ‚Ü¶ le_iSup S _)
  this.symm ‚ñ∏ rfl

variable {p p'}

theorem mem_sup : x ‚àà p ‚äî p' ‚Üî ‚àÉ y ‚àà p, ‚àÉ z ‚àà p', y + z = x :=
  ‚ü®fun h => by
    rw [‚Üê span_eq p, ‚Üê span_eq p', ‚Üê span_union] at h
    refine span_induction ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ h
    ¬∑ rintro y (h | h)
      ¬∑ exact ‚ü®y, h, 0, by simp, by simp‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact ‚ü®0, by simp, y, h, by simp‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®0, by simp, 0, by simp‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro _ _ - - ‚ü®y‚ÇÅ, hy‚ÇÅ, z‚ÇÅ, hz‚ÇÅ, rfl‚ü© ‚ü®y‚ÇÇ, hy‚ÇÇ, z‚ÇÇ, hz‚ÇÇ, rfl‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®_, add_mem hy‚ÇÅ hy‚ÇÇ, _, add_mem hz‚ÇÅ hz‚ÇÇ, by
        rw [add_assoc, add_assoc, ‚Üê add_assoc y‚ÇÇ, ‚Üê add_assoc z‚ÇÅ, add_comm y‚ÇÇ]‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro a - _ ‚ü®y, hy, z, hz, rfl‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®_, smul_mem _ a hy, _, smul_mem _ a hz, by simp [smul_add]‚ü©, by
    rintro ‚ü®y, hy, z, hz, rfl‚ü©
    exact add_mem ((le_sup_left : p ‚â§ p ‚äî p') hy) ((le_sup_right : p' ‚â§ p ‚äî p') hz)‚ü©

theorem mem_sup' : x ‚àà p ‚äî p' ‚Üî ‚àÉ (y : p) (z : p'), (y : M) + z = x :=
  mem_sup.trans <| by simp only [Subtype.exists, exists_prop]

lemma exists_add_eq_of_codisjoint (h : Codisjoint p p') (x : M) :
    ‚àÉ y ‚àà p, ‚àÉ z ‚àà p', y + z = x := by

theorem nontrivial_span_singleton {x : M} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : Nontrivial (R ‚àô x) :=

    use 0, ‚ü®x, Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x‚ü©

theorem mem_span_singleton {y : M} : (x ‚àà R ‚àô y) ‚Üî ‚àÉ a : R, a ‚Ä¢ y = x :=
  ‚ü®fun h => by
    refine span_induction ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_ h
    ¬∑ rintro y (rfl | ‚ü®‚ü®_‚ü©‚ü©)
      exact ‚ü®1, by simp‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®0, by simp‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro _ _ - - ‚ü®a, rfl‚ü© ‚ü®b, rfl‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®a + b, by simp [add_smul]‚ü©
    ¬∑ rintro a _ - ‚ü®b, rfl‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®a * b, by simp [smul_smul]‚ü©, by
    rintro ‚ü®a, y, rfl‚ü©; exact smul_mem _ _ (subset_span <| by simp)‚ü©

    (s ‚â§ R ‚àô v‚ÇÄ) ‚Üî ‚àÄ v ‚àà s, ‚àÉ r : R, r ‚Ä¢ v‚ÇÄ = v := by simp_rw [SetLike.le_def, mem_span_singleton]

theorem span_singleton_smul_le {S} [Monoid S] [SMul S R] [MulAction S M] [IsScalarTower S R M]
    (r : S) (x : M) : (R ‚àô r ‚Ä¢ x) ‚â§ R ‚àô x := by

    (g : G) (x : M) : (R ‚àô g ‚Ä¢ x) = R ‚àô x := by

/-- A submodule is equal to the supremum of the spans of the submodule's nonzero elements. -/
theorem submodule_eq_sSup_le_nonzero_spans (p : Submodule R M) :
    p = sSup { T : Submodule R M | ‚àÉ m ‚àà p, m ‚âÝ 0 ‚àß T = span R {m} } := by
  let S := { T : Submodule R M | ‚àÉ m ‚àà p, m ‚âÝ 0 ‚àß T = span R {m} }
  apply le_antisymm
  ¬∑ intro m hm
    by_cases h : m = 0
    ¬∑ rw [h]
      simp
    ¬∑ exact @le_sSup _ _ S _ ‚ü®m, ‚ü®hm, ‚ü®h, rfl‚ü©‚ü©‚ü© m (mem_span_singleton_self m)
  ¬∑ rw [sSup_le_iff]
    rintro S ‚ü®_, ‚ü®_, ‚ü®_, rfl‚ü©‚ü©‚ü©
    rwa [span_singleton_le_iff_mem]

/-- For every element in the span of a set, there exists a finite subset of the set
such that the element is contained in the span of the subset. -/
theorem mem_span_finite_of_mem_span {S : Set M} {x : M} (hx : x ‚àà span R S) :
    ‚àÉ T : Finset M, ‚ÜëT ‚äÜ S ‚àß x ‚àà span R (T : Set M) := by
  classical
  refine span_induction (fun x hx => ?_) ?_ ?_ ?_ hx
  ¬∑ refine ‚ü®{x}, ?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ rwa [Finset.coe_singleton, Set.singleton_subset_iff]
    ¬∑ rw [Finset.coe_singleton]
      exact Submodule.mem_span_singleton_self x
  ¬∑ use ‚àÖ
    simp
  ¬∑ rintro x y - - ‚ü®X, hX, hxX‚ü© ‚ü®Y, hY, hyY‚ü©
    refine ‚ü®X ‚à™ Y, ?_, ?_‚ü©
    ¬∑ rw [Finset.coe_union]
      exact Set.union_subset hX hY
    rw [Finset.coe_union, span_union, mem_sup]
    exact ‚ü®x, hxX, y, hyY, rfl‚ü©
  ¬∑ rintro a x - ‚ü®T, hT, h2‚ü©
    exact ‚ü®T, hT, smul_mem _ _ h2‚ü©

end

theorem mem_span_insert' {x y} {s : Set M} :
    x ‚àà span R (insert y s) ‚Üî ‚àÉ a : R, x + a ‚Ä¢ y ‚àà span R s := by

lemma span_range_update_add_smul (hij : i ‚âÝ j) (v : Œπ ‚Üí M) (r : R) :
    span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j + r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v) := by
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_ <;> simp only [span_le, Set.range_subset_iff, SetLike.mem_coe] <;>
    intro k <;> obtain rfl | hjk := eq_or_ne j k
  ¬∑ rw [update_self]
    exact add_mem (subset_span ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü©) <| smul_mem _ _ <| subset_span ‚ü®i, rfl‚ü©
  ¬∑ exact subset_span ‚ü®k, (update_of_ne hjk.symm ..).symm‚ü©
  ¬∑ nth_rw 2 [‚Üê add_sub_cancel_right (v j) (r ‚Ä¢ v i)]
    exact sub_mem (subset_span ‚ü®j, update_self ..‚ü©)
      (smul_mem _ _ (subset_span ‚ü®i, update_of_ne hij ..‚ü©))
  ¬∑ exact subset_span ‚ü®k, update_of_ne hjk.symm ..‚ü©

lemma span_range_update_sub_smul (hij : i ‚âÝ j) (v : Œπ ‚Üí M) (r : R) :
    span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v)
name: Submodule.span_range_update_sub_smul
responses:
To prove that `span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v)` for `i ‚âÝ j`, we can utilize the previously proved `span_range_update_add_smul`. 

Specifically, note that `v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i` can be written as `v j + (-r) ‚Ä¢ v i`. Thus, applying `span_range_update_add_smul` with the scalar `-r` yields the desired result.

FINAL```
lemma span_range_update_sub_smul (hij : i ‚âÝ j) (v : Œπ ‚Üí M) (r : R) :
    span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v) := by
  simpa only [sub_eq_add_neg, neg_smul] using span_range_update_add_smul hij v (-r)
```

To complete the proof of `span_range_update_sub_smul`, we follow the structure established in `span_range_update_add_smul`. We demonstrate that the span of the updated set is equal to the span of the original set by showing containment in both directions. The key is to note that subtracting `r ‚Ä¢ v i` from `v j` preserves the span because `v j` can be recovered by adding `r ‚Ä¢ v i` back, and `v i` is already in the span.

FINAL```
lemma span_range_update_sub_smul (hij : i ‚âÝ j) (v : Œπ ‚Üí M) (r : R) :
    span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v) := by
  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
  ¬∑ simp only [span_le, Set.range_subset_iff, SetLike.mem_coe]
    intro k
    obtain rfl | hjk := eq_or_ne j k
    ¬∑ rw [update_self]
      exact sub_mem (subset_span ‚ü®j, rfl‚ü©) (smul_mem _ _ (subset_span ‚ü®i, rfl‚ü©))
    ¬∑ exact subset_span ‚ü®k, (update_of_ne hjk.symm ..).symm‚ü©
  ¬∑ simp only [span_le, Set.range_subset_iff, SetLike.mem_coe]
    intro k
    obtain rfl | hjk := eq_or_ne j k
    ¬∑ nth_rw 2 [‚Üê sub_add_cancel (v j) (r ‚Ä¢ v i)]
      exact add_mem (subset_span ‚ü®j, update_self ..‚ü©) (smul_mem _ _ (subset_span ‚ü®i, update_of_ne hij ..‚ü©))
    ¬∑ exact subset_span ‚ü®k, update_of_ne hjk.symm ..‚ü©
```

To prove the lemma, we use the property that if a submodule contains the generators of another, they generate the same span. Specifically, we show that the set of vectors `range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))` and `range v` span the same submodule by expressing each as an `R`-linear combination of the other.

lemma span_range_update_sub_smul (hij : i ‚âÝ j) (v : Œπ ‚Üí M) (r : R) :
    span R (Set.range (Function.update v j (v j - r ‚Ä¢ v i))) = span R (Set.range v) := by

import Mathlib.RingTheory.NonUnitalSubsemiring.Defs

/-!
# Bundled subsemirings

We define bundled subsemirings and some standard constructions: `subtype` and `inclusion`
ring homomorphisms.
-/


universe u v w

section AddSubmonoidWithOneClass

/-- `AddSubmonoidWithOneClass S R` says `S` is a type of subsets `s ‚â§ R` that contain `0`, `1`,
and are closed under `(+)` -/
class AddSubmonoidWithOneClass (S : Type*) (R : outParam Type*) [AddMonoidWithOne R]
  [SetLike S R] extends AddSubmonoidClass S R, OneMemClass S R : Prop

variable {S R : Type*} [AddMonoidWithOne R] [SetLike S R] (s : S)

@[aesop safe apply (rule_sets := [SetLike])]
theorem natCast_mem [AddSubmonoidWithOneClass S R] (n : ‚Ñï) : (n : R) ‚àà s := by
  induction n <;> simp [zero_mem, add_mem, one_mem, *]

@[aesop safe apply (rule_sets := [SetLike])]
lemma ofNat_mem [AddSubmonoidWithOneClass S R] (s : S) (n : ‚Ñï) [n.AtLeastTwo] :
    ofNat(n) ‚àà s := by
  rw [‚Üê Nat.cast_ofNat]; exact natCast_mem s n

instance (priority := 74) AddSubmonoidWithOneClass.toAddMonoidWithOne
    [AddSubmonoidWithOneClass S R] : AddMonoidWithOne s :=
  { AddSubmonoidClass.toAddMonoid s with
    one := ‚ü®_, one_mem s‚ü©
    natCast := fun n => ‚ü®n, natCast_mem s n‚ü©
    natCast_zero := Subtype.ext Nat.cast_zero
    natCast_succ := fun _ => Subtype.ext (Nat.cast_succ _) }

end AddSubmonoidWithOneClass

variable {R : Type u} {S : Type v} [NonAssocSemiring R]

section SubsemiringClass

/-- `SubsemiringClass S R` states that `S` is a type of subsets `s ‚äÜ R` that
are both a multiplicative and an additive submonoid. -/
class SubsemiringClass (S : Type*) (R : outParam (Type u)) [NonAssocSemiring R]
  [SetLike S R] extends SubmonoidClass S R, AddSubmonoidClass S R : Prop

-- See note [lower instance priority]
instance (priority := 100) SubsemiringClass.addSubmonoidWithOneClass (S : Type*)
    (R : Type u) {_ : NonAssocSemiring R} [SetLike S R] [h : SubsemiringClass S R] :
    AddSubmonoidWithOneClass S R :=
  { h with }

instance (priority := 100) SubsemiringClass.nonUnitalSubsemiringClass (S : Type*)
    (R : Type u) [NonAssocSemiring R] [SetLike S R] [SubsemiringClass S R] :
    NonUnitalSubsemiringClass S R where
  mul_mem := mul_mem

variable [SetLike S R] [hSR : SubsemiringClass S R] (s : S)

namespace SubsemiringClass

-- Prefer subclasses of `NonAssocSemiring` over subclasses of `SubsemiringClass`.
/-- A subsemiring of a `NonAssocSemiring` inherits a `NonAssocSemiring` structure -/
instance (priority := 75) toNonAssocSemiring : NonAssocSemiring s := fast_instance%
  Subtype.coe_injective.nonAssocSemiring Subtype.val rfl rfl (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ => rfl)
    (fun _ _ => rfl) fun _ => rfl

instance nontrivial [Nontrivial R] : Nontrivial s :=
  nontrivial_of_ne 0 1 fun H => zero_ne_one (congr_arg Subtype.val H)

instance noZeroDivisors [NoZeroDivisors R] : NoZeroDivisors s :=
  Subtype.coe_injective.noZeroDivisors _ rfl fun _ _ => rfl

/-- The natural ring hom from a subsemiring of semiring `R` to `R`. -/
def subtype : s ‚Üí+* R :=
  { SubmonoidClass.subtype s, AddSubmonoidClass.subtype s with toFun := (‚Üë) }

@[simp]
theorem coe_subtype : (subtype s : s ‚Üí R) = ((‚Üë) : s ‚Üí R) :=
  rfl

-- Prefer subclasses of `Semiring` over subclasses of `SubsemiringClass`.
/-- A subsemiring of a `Semiring` is a `Semiring`. -/
instance (priority := 75) toSemiring {R} [Semiring R] [SetLike S R] [SubsemiringClass S R] :
    Semiring s := fast_instance%
  Subtype.coe_injective.semiring Subtype.val rfl rfl (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ => rfl)
    (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ => rfl) fun _ => rfl

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_pow {R} [Semiring R] [SetLike S R] [SubsemiringClass S R] (x : s) (n : ‚Ñï) :
    ((x ^ n : s) : R) = (x : R) ^ n := by
  induction n with
  | zero => simp
  | succ n ih => simp [pow_succ, ih]

/-- A subsemiring of a `CommSemiring` is a `CommSemiring`. -/
instance toCommSemiring {R} [CommSemiring R] [SetLike S R] [SubsemiringClass S R] :
    CommSemiring s := fast_instance%
  Subtype.coe_injective.commSemiring Subtype.val rfl rfl (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ => rfl)
    (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ => rfl) fun _ => rfl

end SubsemiringClass

end SubsemiringClass

variable [NonAssocSemiring S]

/-- A subsemiring of a semiring `R` is a subset `s` that is both a multiplicative and an additive
submonoid. -/
structure Subsemiring (R : Type u) [NonAssocSemiring R] extends Submonoid R, AddSubmonoid R

/-- Reinterpret a `Subsemiring` as a `Submonoid`. -/
add_decl_doc Subsemiring.toSubmonoid

/-- Reinterpret a `Subsemiring` as an `AddSubmonoid`. -/
add_decl_doc Subsemiring.toAddSubmonoid

namespace Subsemiring

instance : SetLike (Subsemiring R) R where
  coe s := s.carrier
  coe_injective' p q h := by cases p; cases q; congr; exact SetLike.coe_injective' h

instance : SubsemiringClass (Subsemiring R) R where
  zero_mem := zero_mem'
  add_mem {s} := AddSubsemigroup.add_mem' s.toAddSubmonoid.toAddSubsemigroup
  one_mem {s} := Submonoid.one_mem' s.toSubmonoid
  mul_mem {s} := Subsemigroup.mul_mem' s.toSubmonoid.toSubsemigroup

initialize_simps_projections Subsemiring (carrier ‚Üí coe, as_prefix coe)

/-- Turn a `Subsemiring` into a `NonUnitalSubsemiring` by forgetting that it contains `1`. -/
def toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : NonUnitalSubsemiring R where __ := S

@[simp]
theorem mem_toSubmonoid {s : Subsemiring R} {x : R} : x ‚àà s.toSubmonoid ‚Üî x ‚àà s :=
  Iff.rfl

@[simp]
lemma mem_toNonUnitalSubsemiring {S : Subsemiring R} {x : R} :
    x ‚àà S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring ‚Üî x ‚àà S := .rfl

theorem mem_carrier {s : Subsemiring R} {x : R} : x ‚àà s.carrier ‚Üî x ‚àà s :=
  Iff.rfl
formal_statement:
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S
name: Subsemiring.coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring
responses:
To show that `(S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S`, we observe that the `SetLike` instance for `Subsemiring` maps a subsemiring to its carrier set. By the definition of the `Subsemiring` structure, the carrier is the underlying set for the bundled object. The `toNonUnitalSubsemiring` function preserves the carrier, and since `SetLike` for `NonUnitalSubsemiring` also identifies the term with its carrier, the equality holds by definition.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To complete the formalization, we need to prove that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to its `NonUnitalSubsemiring` representation corresponds to the same set of elements. Since the `NonUnitalSubsemiring` structure is defined to share the same carrier as the `Subsemiring`, this is trivial by definition.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To complete the provided code, we need to show that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to a `Set` via its `NonUnitalSubsemiring` conversion is equal to the original `Subsemiring` coerced to a `Set`. By the definition of `NonUnitalSubsemiring` as a structure extending `Subsemiring` (or specifically carrying the same carrier), this is a direct identity.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To complete the proof for the lemma `coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring`, we note that `toNonUnitalSubsemiring` is defined by structure copying (`__ := S`), so the underlying set of the `NonUnitalSubsemiring` is identical to the carrier of the original `Subsemiring`. In Lean, this is a definitional equality by the way the structure is constructed.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To prove that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to a `NonUnitalSubsemiring` is equal to the set of elements of the `Subsemiring`, we look at the definitions. Since `toNonUnitalSubsemiring` is defined using the carrier of the original `Subsemiring` via structure inheritance (where `NonUnitalSubsemiring` expects a `carrier`), and the `SetLike` instance for `Subsemiring` is defined as the carrier, the equality follows immediately from `rfl`.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To complete the definition of `Subsemiring`, we need to show that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to a set is equal to the carrier of its underlying `NonUnitalSubsemiring`. Given the existing structure where `NonUnitalSubsemiring` simply shares the same carrier as the `Subsemiring`, this is definitionally true.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To finalize the definition of `Subsemiring`, we need to show that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to a `NonUnitalSubsemiring` is equal to the underlying set of the `Subsemiring`. Since `Subsemiring` is defined as a structure wrapping `Submonoid` and `AddSubmonoid`, and its `SetLike` instance projects to the carrier, this is immediate by definition.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

To complete the requested formalization, we need to show that the coercion of a `Subsemiring` to a `NonUnitalSubsemiring` corresponds to the same set in `R`. Since the `NonUnitalSubsemiring` is defined via the `carrier` of the `Subsemiring` (in the `toNonUnitalSubsemiring` structure), this follows directly from the definition.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma coe_toNonUnitalSubsemiring (S : Subsemiring R) : (S.toNonUnitalSubsemiring : Set R) = S :=
  rfl
```

import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.UnitPartition
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

/-!
# Covolume of ‚Ñ§-lattices

Let `E` be a finite dimensional real vector space.

Let `L` be a `‚Ñ§`-lattice `L` defined as a discrete `‚Ñ§`-submodule of `E` that spans `E` over `‚Ñù`.

## Main definitions and results

* `ZLattice.covolume`: the covolume of `L` defined as the volume of an arbitrary fundamental
domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain`: the covolume of `L` does not depend on the
choice of the fundamental domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_det`: if `L` is a lattice in `‚Ñù^n`, then its covolume is the absolute
value of the determinant of any `‚Ñ§`-basis of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow`: Let `s` be a bounded measurable set of `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, then
the number of points in `s ‚à© n‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L` divided by `n ^ card Œπ` tends to `volume s / covolume L`
when `n : ‚Ñï` tends to infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'` for a version
for `InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''` for the general version.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div`: Let `X` be a cone in `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and let `F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`
be a function such that `F (c ‚Ä¢ x) = c ^ card Œπ * F x`. Then the number of points `x ‚àà X` such that
`F x ‚â§ c` divided by `c` tends to `volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} / covolume L` when `c : ‚Ñù` tends to
infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'` for a version for
`InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div''` for the general version.

## Naming convention

Some results are true in the case where the ambient finite dimensional real vector space is the
pi-space `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and in the case where it is an `InnerProductSpace`. We use the following
convention: the plain name is for the pi case, for eg. `volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`. For
the same result in the `InnerProductSpace` case, we add a `prime`, for eg.
`volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume'`. When the same result exists in the
general case, we had two primes, eg. `covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''`.

-/

noncomputable section

namespace ZLattice

open Submodule MeasureTheory Module MeasureTheory Module ZSpan

section General

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [MeasurableSpace E] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E)

/-- The covolume of a `‚Ñ§`-lattice is the volume of some fundamental domain; see
`ZLattice.covolume_eq_volume` for the proof that the volume does not depend on the choice of
the fundamental domain. -/
def covolume (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) : ‚Ñù := (addCovolume L E Œº).toReal

end General

section Basic

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E]
variable [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure Œº]

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)
  exact congr_arg ENNReal.toReal (h.covolume_eq_volume Œº)

theorem covolume_ne_zero : covolume L Œº ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    ENNReal.toReal_ne_zero]
  refine ‚ü®measure_fundamentalDomain_ne_zero _, ne_of_lt ?_‚ü©
  exact Bornology.IsBounded.measure_lt_top (fundamentalDomain_isBounded _)

theorem covolume_pos : 0 < covolume L Œº :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne ENNReal.toReal_nonneg (covolume_ne_zero L Œº).symm

theorem covolume_comap {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù F] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù F]
    [MeasurableSpace F] [BorelSpace F] (ŒΩ : Measure F := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure ŒΩ]
    {e : F ‚âÉL[‚Ñù] E} (he : MeasurePreserving e ŒΩ Œº) :
    covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearMap) ŒΩ = covolume L Œº := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ ((isAddFundamentalDomain
    ((Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L).ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearEquiv) ŒΩ)), ‚Üê he.measure_preimage
    (fundamentalDomain_measurableSet _).nullMeasurableSet, ‚Üê e.image_symm_eq_preimage,
    ‚Üê e.symm.coe_toLinearEquiv, map_fundamentalDomain]
  congr!
  ext; simp

theorem covolume_eq_det_mul_measure {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (b‚ÇÄ : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) :
    covolume L Œº = |b‚ÇÄ.det ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)| * (Œº (fundamentalDomain b‚ÇÄ)).toReal := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain b Œº),
    measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ b‚ÇÄ,
    measure_congr (fundamentalDomain_ae_parallelepiped b‚ÇÄ Œº), ENNReal.toReal_mul,
    ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(Matrix.of ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)).det| := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L volume (isAddFundamentalDomain b volume),
    volume_fundamentalDomain, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext1
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det_inv {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(LinearEquiv.det (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun : ‚Ñù)|‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b, ‚Üê Pi.basisFun_det_apply, show (((‚Üë) : L ‚Üí _) ‚àò ‚áëb) =
    (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù) by ext; simp, ‚Üê Basis.det_inv, ‚Üê abs_inv, Units.val_inv_eq_inv_val,
    IsUnit.unit_spec, ‚Üê Basis.det_basis, LinearEquiv.coe_det]
  rfl

theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, Measure.addHaar_preimage_linearEquiv, LinearEquiv.symm_symm,
    covolume_eq_det_inv L b, ENNReal.div_eq_inv_mul, ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos
    (abs_pos.mpr (LinearEquiv.det _).ne_zero), inv_inv, LinearEquiv.coe_det]

/-- A more general version of `ZLattice.volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    [InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set E} (hs : NullMeasurableSet s) :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  classical
  let e : Fin (finrank ‚Ñù E) ‚âÉ Œπ :=
    Fintype.equivOfCardEq (by rw [Fintype.card_fin, finrank_eq_card_basis (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù)])
  let f := (EuclideanSpace.equiv Œπ ‚Ñù).symm.trans
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).repr.toContinuousLinearEquiv.symm
  have hf : MeasurePreserving f :=
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).measurePreserving_repr_symm.comp
      (EuclideanSpace.volume_preserving_measurableEquiv Œπ).symm
  rw [‚Üê hf.measure_preimage hs, ‚Üê (covolume_comap L volume volume hf),
    ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearMap)
    (b.ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearEquiv), Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_comap,
    ‚Üê f.image_symm_eq_preimage, ‚Üê Set.image_comp]
  simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_toLinearEquiv, Basis.map_equivFun,
    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply,
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv, ContinuousLinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

end Basic

namespace covolume

section General

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology BoxIntegral Bornology

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E]
variable {L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E} [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow` for the general case;
see the `Naming convention` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0):
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_
    (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) ?_ ?_ hs‚ÇÉ)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with n hn
    congr
    refine Nat.card_congr <| ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.toEquiv.subtypeEquiv fun x ‚Ü¶ ?_).symm
    simp_rw [Set.mem_inter_iff, ‚Üê b.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, LinearEquiv.coe_toEquiv,
      Basis.equivFun_apply, Set.mem_image, DFunLike.coe_fn_eq, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
      exists_eq_right, and_congr_right_iff, Set.mem_inv_smul_set_iff‚ÇÄ
      (mod_cast hn.ne' : (n : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0), ‚Üê Finsupp.coe_smul, ‚Üê LinearEquiv.map_smul, SetLike.mem_coe,
      Basis.mem_span_iff_repr_mem, Pi.basisFun_repr, implies_true]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê NormedSpace.isVonNBounded_iff ‚Ñù] at hs‚ÇÅ ‚ä¢
    exact Bornology.IsVonNBounded.image hs‚ÇÅ ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL : E ‚ÜíL[‚Ñù] Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
  ¬∑ exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image.mpr hs‚ÇÇ

private theorem tendsto_card_le_div''_aux {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (hF‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x)) {c : ‚Ñù} (hc : 0 < c) :
    c ‚Ä¢ {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} = {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c ^ card Œπ} := by
  ext x
  simp_rw [Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem‚ÇÄ hc.ne', Set.mem_setOf_eq, hF‚ÇÅ _
    (inv_pos_of_pos hc).le, inv_pow, inv_mul_le_iff‚ÇÄ (pow_pos hc _), mul_one, and_congr_left_iff]
  exact fun _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ (smul_inv_smul‚ÇÄ hc.ne' x) ‚ñ∏ hX h hc, fun h ‚Ü¶ hX h (inv_pos_of_pos hc)‚ü©

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div` for the general case;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1})) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
      Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / (c : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal) := by

  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_ <| (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume'
      ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) ?_ ?_ h‚ÇÑ fun x y hx hy ‚Ü¶ ?_).comp
        (tendsto_rpow_atTop <| inv_pos.mpr
          (Nat.cast_pos.mpr card_pos) : Tendsto (fun x ‚Ü¶ x ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) atTop atTop)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with c hc
    have aux‚ÇÅ : (card Œπ : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0 := Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr card_ne_zero
    have aux‚ÇÇ : 0 < c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π := Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _
    have aux‚ÇÉ : (c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π)‚Åª¬π ‚âÝ 0 := inv_ne_zero aux‚ÇÇ.ne'
    have aux‚ÇÑ : c ^ (-(card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) ‚âÝ 0 := (Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _).ne'
    obtain ‚ü®hc‚ÇÅ, hc‚ÇÇ‚ü© := lt_iff_le_and_ne.mp hc
    rw [Function.comp_apply, ‚Üê Real.rpow_natCast, Real.rpow_inv_rpow hc‚ÇÅ aux‚ÇÅ, eq_comm]
    congr
    refine Nat.card_congr <| Equiv.subtypeEquiv ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.toEquiv.trans
          (Equiv.smulRight aux‚ÇÑ)) fun _ ‚Ü¶ ?_
    rw [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_inter_iff, Equiv.trans_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_toEquiv,
      Equiv.smulRight_apply, Real.rpow_neg hc‚ÇÅ, Set.smul_mem_smul_set_iff‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÉ,
      ‚Üê Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÇ.ne', ‚Üê image_smul_set,
      tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ aux‚ÇÇ, ‚Üê Real.rpow_natCast, ‚Üê Real.rpow_mul hc‚ÇÅ,
      inv_mul_cancel‚ÇÄ aux‚ÇÅ, Real.rpow_one]
    simp_rw [SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_image, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq, exists_eq_right,
      and_congr_right_iff, ‚Üê b.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, Basis.mem_span_iff_repr_mem,
      Pi.basisFun_repr, Basis.equivFun_apply, implies_true]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê NormedSpace.isVonNBounded_iff ‚Ñù] at h‚ÇÇ ‚ä¢
    exact Bornology.IsVonNBounded.image h‚ÇÇ ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL : E ‚ÜíL[‚Ñù] Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
  ¬∑ exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image.mpr h‚ÇÉ
  ¬∑ simp_rw [‚Üê image_smul_set]
    apply Set.image_mono
    rw [tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ hx,
      tendsto_card_le_div''_aux hX h‚ÇÅ (lt_of_lt_of_le hx hy)]
    exact fun a ‚ü®ha‚ÇÅ, ha‚ÇÇ‚ü© ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®ha‚ÇÅ, le_trans ha‚ÇÇ <| pow_le_pow_left‚ÇÄ (le_of_lt hx) hy _‚ü©

end General

section Pi

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology Bornology

private theorem frontier_equivFun {E : Type*} [AddCommGroup E] [Module ‚Ñù E] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    [TopologicalSpace E] [TopologicalAddGroup E] [ContinuousSMul ‚Ñù E] [T2Space E]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) (s : Set E) :
    frontier (b.equivFun '' s) = b.equivFun '' (frontier s) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage]
  exact (Homeomorph.preimage_frontier b.equivFunL.toHomeomorph.symm s).symm

variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
formal_statement:
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s)
    (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s) (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume s).toReal / covolume L))
name: ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow
responses:
theorem tendsto_card_le_div [Nonempty Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {X : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)}
    (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1}) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) / c)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}).toReal / covolume L)) := by
  rw [‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b]
  have h_vol : (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal =
    (volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}).toReal / covolume L := by
    rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b, ENNReal.toReal_div, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  rw [‚Üê h_vol]
  apply covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'' b hX h‚ÇÅ h‚ÇÇ h‚ÇÉ
  rw [frontier_equivFun (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFunL.toLinearEquiv,
      Measure.measure_image_eq_zero_of_null _ (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFunL.toLinearEquiv.toHomeomorph.symm, h‚ÇÑ]
  simp

end Pi

end ZLattice

FINAL

theorem tendsto_card_div_pow (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s)
    (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s) (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier s) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù ((volume s).toReal / covolume L)) := by
  have hf : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s)) = 0 := by
    rw [frontier_equivFun (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L), ‚Üê Measure.map_apply (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFunL.toMeasurableEquiv (frontier_measurableSet s)]
    suffices volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFunL '' (frontier s)) = 0 by simpa
    rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b (s := frontier s), hs‚ÇÉ]
    simp
  rw [cov.tendsto_card_div_pow'' b hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ hf, volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b,
    ENNReal.toReal_div, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b]
  simp
theorem

:= by
  let b' := b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L
  rw [volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume L b, ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_div, ‚Üê ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  apply (covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'' b hs‚ÇÅ hs‚ÇÇ ?_).congr'
  ¬∑ rw [frontier_equivFun b'.equivFun, ‚Üê Measure.map_apply (MeasurableEquiv.measurable b'.equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv) hs‚ÇÇ,
    Measure.map_volume_preserves_measure, hs‚ÇÉ, measure_empty]
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with n hn
    congr 1
    apply congr_arg ((‚Üë) : ‚Ñï ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
    exact Nat.card_congr (b'.equivFun.toEquiv.subtypeEquiv fun x ‚Ü¶ by

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)

theorem tendsto_card_le_div {X : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')
    (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi'' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    have : (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom ‚â´ f' = f'' := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      rw [assoc, hf', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, assoc, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hi'' hj']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom, comp_zero, ‚Üê this, assoc]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      simp only [zero_comp, hf', K.shape _ _ hij]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      dsimp
      rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, hf'', zero_comp, zero_comp,
        K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj']
      rw [hi]
      exact hij
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hi hi' in
formal_statement:
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0
name: HomologicalComplex.extend.leftHomologyData.lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff
responses:
To compute the homology of the extension, we leverage the fact that for indices in the image of the embedding, the `extend` functor effectively behaves as an isomorphism of complexes. We relate the limit/colimit diagrams defining the homology at $j$ and $j'$ using the established `extendXIso`. 

The core of the proof relies on `Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext` and the correspondence between the kernels and cokernels mediated by the natural isomorphism `extendXIso`. By showing that the lifting maps defining the homology components commute with the isomorphism, we establish the required equivalence.

FINAL```
    := by
  set f' := hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d _ _ _))
  set f'' := (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
    (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))
  have hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j := hcone.fac _ WalkingParallelPair.zero
  have hf'' : f'' ‚â´ (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).Œπ = (K.extend e).d i' j' :=
    (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).fac _ WalkingParallelPair.zero
  have hf''' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j' := by
    rw [‚Üê hf'', ‚Üê assoc, (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).Œπ_eq, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]
  apply Iff.intro
  ¬∑ intro h
    have : f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := h
    rw [lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone f' hf' f'' hf''' œÜ] at this
    exact this
  ¬∑ intro h
    have : f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := h
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone f' hf' f'' hf''' œÜ] at this
    exact this

end leftHomologyData

end extend
end HomologicalComplex```

To formalize the requested proof, we utilize the property that `K.extend` is constructed via isomorphisms between the original complex and the extended one at the support of the embedding. We need to show that the homology at `j'` in `K.extend e` is isomorphic to the homology at `j` in `K`. Given the `IsLimit` and `IsColimit` data for the homology of `K`, we can construct the corresponding `IsLimit` and `IsColimit` data for `K.extend e`.

The provided goal is to finish the lemma `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`, which relates the cokernel of the differential map in the original complex to that of the extended complex.

FINAL```
    := by
  let cone1 := KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)
  let cone2 := kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone
  let hcone1 := hcone.isLimitOfFork (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))
  let hcone2 := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone
  let lift1 := hcone.lift cone1
  let lift2 := hcone2.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d (K.extend e) i' j' k'))
  have h_eq : (K.extendXIso e hi').hom ‚â´ lift1 = lift2 ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom := by
    apply hcone2.hom_ext
    intro j
    simp only [assoc, IsLimit.fac, IsLimit.fac_assoc, KernelFork.ofŒπ_Œπ, ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hj').inv, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, assoc, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, ‚Üê K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj']
    simp only [KernelFork.ofŒπ_Œπ, ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hj').inv, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
    apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
    simp only [KernelFork.ofŒπ_Œπ, ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hj').inv, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc, ‚Üê K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj']
  rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, zero_comp, ‚Üê assoc, h_eq, assoc, cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hj').hom, comp_zero]
```

To prove the lemma, we use the property of limits and the relationship between the morphisms in the original complex `K` and the extended complex `K.extend e`. Specifically, we leverage `Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext` and the isomorphisms `extendXIso` to relate the lifts.

FINAL```
    := by
  let l := hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))
  let l' := (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
    (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))
  have hl : l ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j := hcone.fac _ WalkingParallelPair.zero
  have hl' : l' ‚â´ (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone).Œπ = (K.extend e).d i' j' :=
    (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).fac _ WalkingParallelPair.zero
  dsimp [kernelFork] at hl'
  rw [lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone l hl l' hl' œÜ]
```

To complete the proof of `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`, we relate the lift of the kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` to the lift of the kernel fork of `K.d j k` using the naturality and properties of the `extendXIso`.

FINAL```
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  let K' := K.extend e
  let i_iso := K.extendXIso e hj'
  let lift := hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))
  let lift' := (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
    (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K'.d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))
  have h1 : lift ‚â´ i_iso.inv = lift' := by
    apply (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).hom_ext
    intro j''
    simp only [isLimitKernelFork, KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff_lift,
      KernelFork.ofŒπ_Œπ, assoc, i_iso.symm_hom, i_iso.inv_hom_id, comp_id]
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi i_iso.hom, assoc, assoc, i_iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono i_iso.inv, assoc, i_iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]
    by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
    ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
      simp only [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', ‚Üê assoc, i_iso.hom_inv_id, id_comp]
      rfl
    ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, K'.shape _ _ (e.rel_not hij), zero_comp]
  rw [‚Üê cancel_mono i_iso.inv, assoc, h1, ‚Üê zero_comp i_iso.inv]
  exact ‚ü®fun h => by rw [h], fun h => by rw [‚Üê cancel_mono i_iso.hom, assoc, h, comp_zero]‚ü©
```

The proof proceeds by utilizing the isomorphism `K.extendXIso e` to transport the kernel and cokernel diagrams between the original complex `K` and the extended complex `K.extend e`. The lemma `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff` relies on the naturality and the explicit construction of the lifts in the `HomologicalComplex.extend` module, effectively showing that the kernel-cokernel factorization (which defines homology) is preserved under the embedding.

FINAL```
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  let K_fork := KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)
  let E_fork := KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _)
  let K_limit := hcone.lift K_fork
  let E_limit := (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift E_fork
  have hK : K_limit ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j := hcone.fac K_fork WalkingParallelPair.zero
  have hE : E_limit ‚â´ (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).Œπ = (K.extend e).d i' j' :=
    (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).fac E_fork WalkingParallelPair.zero
  exact lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone K_limit hK E_limit hE œÜ

end leftHomologyData
end extend
end HomologicalComplex
```

To prove `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`, we relate the limit kernels of the differential at `j` and `j'`. Using the isomorphism provided by `extendXIso`, we show that the induced maps to the cokernel of the previous differential are compatible, allowing us to equate the conditions `f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0` and `f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0`.

FINAL```
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  let L := hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))
  let L' := (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
    (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))
  have hL : L ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j := hcone.fac _ (WalkingParallelPair.zero)
  have hL' : L' ‚â´ (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).Œπ = (K.extend e).d i' j' :=
    (isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).fac _ (WalkingParallelPair.zero)
  dsimp [isLimitKernelFork, kernelFork] at hL'
  apply lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone L hL L' hL'
```

To prove the provided lemma, we utilize the relationship between the limit of the differentials in the original complex `K` and the extended complex `K.extend e`. By using the previously established isomorphism `extendXIso`, we can transport the limit data across the extension. Specifically, the isomorphism ensures that the kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` is induced by the kernel fork of `K.d j k`, and we use the `IsLimit` properties to demonstrate that the lifts are equivalent up to this isomorphism.

FINAL```
  := by
  let fork1 := KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)
  let fork2 := KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d (K.extend e) i' j' k')
  let lim1 := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone
  let lift1 := hcone.lift fork1
  let lift2 := lim1.lift fork2
  suffices lift1 = lift2 ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hi').hom by
    rw [this, assoc, ‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hi').hom, comp_zero, comp_zero]
    exact Iff.rfl
  apply hcone.hom_ext
  intro T g hg
  rw [assoc, hcone.fac, lift2]
  have H : lim1.lift fork2 ‚â´ lim1.Œπ = (K.extend e).d i' j' := lim1.fac _ walkingParallelPair.one
  rw [assoc, H, ‚Üê d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' ((K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)]
  dsimp [lim1, isLimitKernelFork, kernelFork]
  rw [assoc, ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]
  rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]
  rw [‚Üê K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id, comp_id]
  exact hg
```

To prove the lemma, we use the fact that the limit fork of the extended complex is obtained by post-composing the original limit fork with the inverse of the isomorphism at the index $j'$. The lift of a morphism through a kernel is determined by its composition with the inclusion. By using the uniqueness properties of the `IsLimit` fork, we can verify that the two lifts are compatible with the isomorphism `extendXIso`, reducing the assertion to checking equality of the compositions, which follows from the definition of the extended differential.
