import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Block
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Notation
import Mathlib.Data.Matrix.RowCol
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.GroupAction.Ring
import Mathlib.GroupTheory.Perm.Fin
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Alternating.Basic
import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Matrix.SemiringInverse

/-!
# Determinant of a matrix

This file defines the determinant of a matrix, `Matrix.det`, and its essential properties.

## Main definitions

 - `Matrix.det`: the determinant of a square matrix, as a sum over permutations
 - `Matrix.detRowAlternating`: the determinant, as an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix

## Main results

 - `det_mul`: the determinant of `A * B` is the product of determinants
 - `det_zero_of_row_eq`: the determinant is zero if there is a repeated row
 - `det_block_diagonal`: the determinant of a block diagonal matrix is a product
   of the blocks' determinants

## Implementation notes

It is possible to configure `simp` to compute determinants. See the file
`MathlibTest/matrix.lean` for some examples.

-/


universe u v w z

open Equiv Equiv.Perm Finset Function

namespace Matrix

variable {m n : Type*} [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] [DecidableEq m] [Fintype m]
variable {R : Type v} [CommRing R]

local notation "Œµ " œÉ:arg => ((sign œÉ : ‚Ñ§) : R)

/-- `det` is an `AlternatingMap` in the rows of the matrix. -/
def detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization ((MultilinearMap.mkPiAlgebra R n R).compLinearMap LinearMap.proj)

/-- The determinant of a matrix given by the Leibniz formula. -/
abbrev det (M : Matrix n n R) : R :=
  detRowAlternating M

theorem det_apply (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Equiv.Perm.sign œÉ ‚Ä¢ ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i :=
  MultilinearMap.alternatization_apply _ M

-- This is what the old definition was. We use it to avoid having to change the old proofs below
theorem det_apply' (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) i := by
  simp [det_apply, Units.smul_def]

theorem det_eq_detp_sub_detp (M : Matrix n n R) : M.det = M.detp 1 - M.detp (-1) := by
  rw [det_apply, ‚Üê Equiv.sum_comp (Equiv.inv (Perm n)), ‚Üê ofSign_disjUnion, sum_disjUnion]
  simp_rw [inv_apply, sign_inv, sub_eq_add_neg, detp, ‚Üê sum_neg_distrib]
  refine congr_arg‚ÇÇ (¬∑ + ¬∑) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ hœÉ ‚Ü¶ ?_) <;>
    rw [mem_ofSign.mp hœÉ, ‚Üê Equiv.prod_comp œÉ] <;> simp

@[simp]
theorem det_diagonal {d : n ‚Üí R} : det (diagonal d) = ‚àè i, d i := by
  rw [det_apply']
  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single 1 ?_ ?_).trans ?_
  ¬∑ rintro œÉ - h2
    cases' not_forall.1 (mt Equiv.ext h2) with x h3
    convert mul_zero (Œµ œÉ)
    apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ x)
    exact if_neg h3
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simp

theorem det_zero (_ : Nonempty n) : det (0 : Matrix n n R) = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_zero

@[simp]
theorem det_one : det (1 : Matrix n n R) = 1 := by rw [‚Üê diagonal_one]; simp [-diagonal_one]

theorem det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] {A : Matrix n n R} : det A = 1 := by simp [det_apply]

@[simp]
theorem coe_det_isEmpty [IsEmpty n] : (det : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = Function.const _ 1 := by
  ext
  exact det_isEmpty

theorem det_eq_one_of_card_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 0) : det A = 1 :=
  haveI : IsEmpty n := Fintype.card_eq_zero_iff.mp h
  det_isEmpty

/-- If `n` has only one element, the determinant of an `n` by `n` matrix is just that element.
Although `Unique` implies `DecidableEq` and `Fintype`, the instances might
not be syntactically equal. Thus, we need to fill in the args explicitly. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_unique {n : Type*} [Unique n] [DecidableEq n] [Fintype n] (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det A = A default default := by simp [det_apply, univ_unique]

theorem det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton [Subsingleton n] (A : Matrix n n R) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k := by
  have := uniqueOfSubsingleton k
  convert det_unique A

theorem det_eq_elem_of_card_eq_one {A : Matrix n n R} (h : Fintype.card n = 1) (k : n) :
    det A = A k k :=
  haveI : Subsingleton n := Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton.mp h.le
  det_eq_elem_of_subsingleton _ _

theorem det_mul_aux {M N : Matrix n n R} {p : n ‚Üí n} (H : ¬¨Bijective p) :
    (‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x) * N (p x) x) = 0 := by
  obtain ‚ü®i, j, hpij, hij‚ü© : ‚àÉ i j, p i = p j ‚àß i ‚âÝ j := by
    rw [‚Üê Finite.injective_iff_bijective, Injective] at H
    push_neg at H
    exact H
  exact
    sum_involution (fun œÉ _ => œÉ * Equiv.swap i j)
      (fun œÉ _ => by
        have : (‚àè x, M (œÉ x) (p x)) = ‚àè x, M ((œÉ * Equiv.swap i j) x) (p x) :=
          Fintype.prod_equiv (swap i j) _ _ (by simp [apply_swap_eq_self hpij])
        simp [this, sign_swap hij, -sign_swap', prod_mul_distrib])
      (fun œÉ _ _ => (not_congr mul_swap_eq_iff).mpr hij) (fun _ _ => mem_univ _) fun œÉ _ =>
      mul_swap_involutive i j œÉ

@[simp]
theorem det_mul (M N : Matrix n n R) : det (M * N) = det M * det N :=
  calc
    det (M * N) = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      simp only [det_apply', mul_apply, prod_univ_sum, mul_sum, Fintype.piFinset_univ]
      rw [Finset.sum_comm]
    _ = ‚àë p : n ‚Üí n with Bijective p, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (p i) * N (p i) i := by
      refine (sum_subset (filter_subset _ _) fun f _ hbij ‚Ü¶ det_mul_aux ?_).symm
      simpa only [true_and, mem_filter, mem_univ] using hbij
    _ = ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, Œµ œÉ * ‚àè i, M (œÉ i) (œÑ i) * N (œÑ i) i :=
      sum_bij (fun p h ‚Ü¶ Equiv.ofBijective p (mem_filter.1 h).2) (fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ mem_univ _)
        (fun _ _ _ _ h ‚Ü¶ by injection h)
        (fun b _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®b, mem_filter.2 ‚ü®mem_univ _, b.bijective‚ü©, coe_fn_injective rfl‚ü©) fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ rfl
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * Œµ œÑ * ‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j) := by
      simp only [mul_comm, mul_left_comm, prod_mul_distrib, mul_assoc]
    _ = ‚àë œÉ : Perm n, ‚àë œÑ : Perm n, (‚àè i, N (œÉ i) i) * (Œµ œÉ * Œµ œÑ) * ‚àè i, M (œÑ i) i :=
      (sum_congr rfl fun œÉ _ =>
        Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.mulRight œÉ‚Åª¬π) _ _ fun œÑ => by
          have : (‚àè j, M (œÑ j) (œÉ j)) = ‚àè j, M ((œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) j) j := by
            rw [‚Üê (œÉ‚Åª¬π : _ ‚âÉ _).prod_comp]
            simp only [Equiv.Perm.coe_mul, apply_inv_self, Function.comp_apply]
          have h : Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ œÑ :=
            calc
              Œµ œÉ * Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π) = Œµ (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π * œÉ) := by
                rw [mul_comm, sign_mul (œÑ * œÉ‚Åª¬π)]
                simp only [Int.cast_mul, Units.val_mul]
              _ = Œµ œÑ := by simp only [inv_mul_cancel_right]

          simp_rw [Equiv.coe_mulRight, h]
          simp only [this])
    _ = det M * det N := by
      simp only [det_apply', Finset.mul_sum, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]

/-- The determinant of a matrix, as a monoid homomorphism. -/
def detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí* R where
  toFun := det
  map_one' := det_one
  map_mul' := det_mul

@[simp]
theorem coe_detMonoidHom : (detMonoidHom : Matrix n n R ‚Üí R) = det :=
  rfl

/-- On square matrices, `mul_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_comm (M N : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N) = det (N * M) := by
  rw [det_mul, det_mul, mul_comm]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_left_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_left_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * (N * P)) = det (N * (M * P)) := by
  rw [‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, ‚Üê Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm M N, ‚Üê det_mul]

/-- On square matrices, `mul_right_comm` applies under `det`. -/
theorem det_mul_right_comm (M N P : Matrix m m R) : det (M * N * P) = det (M * P * N) := by
  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc, Matrix.mul_assoc, det_mul, det_mul_comm N P, ‚Üê det_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M.val * N * M‚Åª¬π.val) = det N := by
  rw [det_mul_right_comm, Units.mul_inv, one_mul]

-- TODO(https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/6607): fix elaboration so `val` isn't needed
theorem det_units_conj' (M : (Matrix m m R)À£) (N : Matrix m m R) :
    det (M‚Åª¬π.val * N * ‚ÜëM.val) = det N :=
  det_units_conj M‚Åª¬π N

/-- Transposing a matrix preserves the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_transpose (M : Matrix n n R) : M·µÄ.det = M.det := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  refine Fintype.sum_bijective _ inv_involutive.bijective _ _ ?_
  intro œÉ
  rw [sign_inv]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv œÉ
  simp

/-- Permuting the columns changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix œÉ id).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det :=
  ((detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_perm M œÉ).trans (by simp [Units.smul_def])

/-- Permuting the rows changes the sign of the determinant. -/
theorem det_permute' (œÉ : Perm n) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    (M.submatrix id œÉ).det = Perm.sign œÉ * M.det := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, transpose_submatrix, det_permute, det_transpose]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with the same equivalence does not change the determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem det_submatrix_equiv_self (e : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    det (A.submatrix e e) = det A := by
  rw [det_apply', det_apply']
  apply Fintype.sum_equiv (Equiv.permCongr e)
  intro œÉ
  rw [Equiv.Perm.sign_permCongr e œÉ]
  congr 1
  apply Fintype.prod_equiv e
  intro i
  rw [Equiv.permCongr_apply, Equiv.symm_apply_apply, submatrix_apply]

/-- Permuting rows and columns with two equivalences does not change the absolute value of the
determinant. -/
@[simp]
theorem abs_det_submatrix_equiv_equiv {R : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommRing R]
    (e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ : n ‚âÉ m) (A : Matrix m m R) :
    |(A.submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÇ).det| = |A.det| := by
  have hee : e‚ÇÇ = e‚ÇÅ.trans (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ) := by ext; simp
  rw [hee]
  show |((A.submatrix id (e‚ÇÅ.symm.trans e‚ÇÇ)).submatrix e‚ÇÅ e‚ÇÅ).det| = |A.det|
  rw [Matrix.det_submatrix_equiv_self, Matrix.det_permute', abs_mul, abs_unit_intCast, one_mul]

/-- Reindexing both indices along the same equivalence preserves the determinant.

For the `simp` version of this lemma, see `det_submatrix_equiv_self`; this one is unsuitable because
`Matrix.reindex_apply` unfolds `reindex` first.
-/
theorem det_reindex_self (e : m ‚âÉ n) (A : Matrix m m R) : det (reindex e e A) = det A :=
  det_submatrix_equiv_self e.symm A

theorem det_smul (A : Matrix n n R) (c : R) : det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A :=
  calc
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = det ((diagonal fun _ => c) * A) := by rw [smul_eq_diagonal_mul]
    _ = det (diagonal fun _ => c) * det A := det_mul _ _
    _ = c ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by simp [card_univ]

@[simp]
theorem det_smul_of_tower {Œ±} [Monoid Œ±] [DistribMulAction Œ± R] [IsScalarTower Œ± R R]
    [SMulCommClass Œ± R R] (c : Œ±) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (c ‚Ä¢ A) = c ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê smul_one_smul R c A, det_smul, smul_pow, one_pow, smul_mul_assoc, one_mul]

theorem det_neg (A : Matrix n n R) : det (-A) = (-1) ^ Fintype.card n * det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul, neg_one_smul]

/-- A variant of `Matrix.det_neg` with scalar multiplication by `Units ‚Ñ§` instead of multiplication
by `R`. -/
theorem det_neg_eq_smul (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (-A) = (-1 : Units ‚Ñ§) ^ Fintype.card n ‚Ä¢ det A := by
  rw [‚Üê det_smul_of_tower, Units.neg_smul, one_smul]

/-- Multiplying each row by a fixed `v i` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_row (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  calc
    det (of fun i j => v j * A i j) = det (A * diagonal v) :=
      congr_arg det <| by
        ext
        simp [mul_comm]
    _ = (‚àè i, v i) * det A := by rw [det_mul, det_diagonal, mul_comm]

/-- Multiplying each column by a fixed `v j` multiplies the determinant by
the product of the `v`s. -/
theorem det_mul_column (v : n ‚Üí R) (A : Matrix n n R) :
    det (of fun i j => v i * A i j) = (‚àè i, v i) * det A :=
  MultilinearMap.map_smul_univ _ v A

@[simp]
theorem det_pow (M : Matrix m m R) (n : ‚Ñï) : det (M ^ n) = det M ^ n :=
  (detMonoidHom : Matrix m m R ‚Üí* R).map_pow M n

section HomMap

variable {S : Type w} [CommRing S]

theorem _root_.RingHom.map_det (f : R ‚Üí+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) := by
  simp [Matrix.det_apply', map_sum f, map_prod f]

theorem _root_.RingEquiv.map_det (f : R ‚âÉ+* S) (M : Matrix n n R) :
    f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgHom.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T] (f : S ‚Üí‚Çê[R] T)
    (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toRingHom.map_det _

theorem _root_.AlgEquiv.map_det [Algebra R S] {T : Type z} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T]
    (f : S ‚âÉ‚Çê[R] T) (M : Matrix n n S) : f M.det = Matrix.det (f.mapMatrix M) :=
  f.toAlgHom.map_det _

end HomMap

@[simp]
theorem det_conjTranspose [StarRing R] (M : Matrix m m R) : det M·¥¥ = star (det M) :=
  ((starRingEnd R).map_det _).symm.trans <| congr_arg star M.det_transpose

section DetZero

/-!
### `det_zero` section

Prove that a matrix with a repeated column has determinant equal to zero.
-/


theorem det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (i : n) (h : ‚àÄ j, A i j = 0) : det A = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_coord_zero i (funext h)

theorem det_eq_zero_of_column_eq_zero {A : Matrix n n R} (j : n) (h : ‚àÄ i, A i j = 0) :
    det A = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose]
  exact det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero j h

variable {M : Matrix n n R} {i j : n}

/-- If a matrix has a repeated row, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_row_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : M i = M j) : M.det = 0 :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_eq_zero_of_eq M hij i_ne_j

/-- If a matrix has a repeated column, the determinant will be zero. -/
theorem det_zero_of_column_eq (i_ne_j : i ‚âÝ j) (hij : ‚àÄ k, M k i = M k j) : M.det = 0 := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, det_zero_of_row_eq i_ne_j]
  exact funext hij

/-- If we repeat a row of a matrix, we get a matrix of determinant zero. -/
theorem det_updateRow_eq_zero (h : i ‚âÝ j) :
    (M.updateRow j (M i)).det = 0 := det_zero_of_row_eq h (by simp [h])

/-- If we repeat a column of a matrix, we get a matrix of determinant zero. -/
theorem det_updateCol_eq_zero (h : i ‚âÝ j) :
    (M.updateCol j (fun k ‚Ü¶ M k i)).det = 0 := det_zero_of_column_eq h (by simp [h])

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_eq_zero := det_updateCol_eq_zero

end DetZero

theorem det_updateRow_add (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (u v : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow M j <| u + v) = det (updateRow M j u) + det (updateRow M j v) :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_update_add M j u v

theorem det_updateCol_add (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (u v : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol M j <| u + v) = det (updateCol M j u) + det (updateCol M j v) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, det_updateRow_add]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_add := det_updateCol_add

theorem det_updateRow_smul (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow M j <| s ‚Ä¢ u) = s * det (updateRow M j u) :=
  (detRowAlternating : (n ‚Üí R) [‚ãÄ^n]‚Üí‚Çó[R] R).map_update_smul M j s u

theorem det_updateCol_smul (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol M j <| s ‚Ä¢ u) = s * det (updateCol M j u) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, det_updateRow_smul]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul := det_updateCol_smul

theorem det_updateRow_smul_left (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateRow (s ‚Ä¢ M) j u) = s ^ (Fintype.card n - 1) * det (updateRow M j u) :=
  MultilinearMap.map_update_smul_left _ M j s u

@[deprecated (since := "2024-11-03")] alias det_updateRow_smul' := det_updateRow_smul_left

theorem det_updateCol_smul_left (M : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (s : R) (u : n ‚Üí R) :
    det (updateCol (s ‚Ä¢ M) j u) = s ^ (Fintype.card n - 1) * det (updateCol M j u) := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, transpose_smul, det_updateRow_smul_left]
  simp [updateRow_transpose, det_transpose]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul' := det_updateCol_smul_left
@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_smul_left := det_updateCol_smul_left

theorem det_updateRow_sum_aux (M : Matrix n n R) {j : n} (s : Finset n) (hj : j ‚àâ s) (c : n ‚Üí R)
    (a : R) :
    (M.updateRow j (a ‚Ä¢ M j + ‚àë k ‚àà s, (c k) ‚Ä¢ M k)).det = a ‚Ä¢ M.det := by
  induction s using Finset.induction_on with
  | empty => rw [Finset.sum_empty, add_zero, smul_eq_mul, det_updateRow_smul, updateRow_eq_self]
  | @insert k _ hk h_ind =>
      have h : k ‚âÝ j := fun h ‚Ü¶ (h ‚ñ∏ hj) (Finset.mem_insert_self _ _)
      rw [Finset.sum_insert hk, add_comm ((c k) ‚Ä¢ M k), ‚Üê add_assoc, det_updateRow_add,
        det_updateRow_smul, det_updateRow_eq_zero h, mul_zero, add_zero, h_ind]
      exact fun h ‚Ü¶ hj (Finset.mem_insert_of_mem h)

/-- If we replace a row of a matrix by a linear combination of its rows, then the determinant is
multiplied by the coefficient of that row. -/
theorem det_updateRow_sum (A : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (c : n ‚Üí R) :
    (A.updateRow j (‚àë k, (c k) ‚Ä¢ A k)).det = (c j) ‚Ä¢ A.det := by
  convert det_updateRow_sum_aux A (Finset.univ.erase j) (Finset.univ.not_mem_erase j) c (c j)
  rw [‚Üê Finset.univ.add_sum_erase _ (Finset.mem_univ j)]

/-- If we replace a column of a matrix by a linear combination of its columns, then the determinant
is multiplied by the coefficient of that column. -/
theorem det_updateCol_sum (A : Matrix n n R) (j : n) (c : n ‚Üí R) :
    (A.updateCol j (fun k ‚Ü¶ ‚àë i, (c i) ‚Ä¢ A k i)).det = (c j) ‚Ä¢ A.det := by
  rw [‚Üê det_transpose, ‚Üê updateRow_transpose, ‚Üê det_transpose A]
  convert det_updateRow_sum A.transpose j c
  simp only [smul_eq_mul, Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, transpose_apply]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-11")] alias det_updateColumn_sum := det_updateCol_sum

section DetEq

/-! ### `det_eq` section

Lemmas showing the determinant is invariant under a variety of operations.
-/


theorem det_eq_of_eq_mul_det_one {A B : Matrix n n R} (C : Matrix n n R) (hC : det C = 1)
    (hA : A = B * C) : det A = det B :=
  calc
    det A = det (B * C) := congr_arg _ hA
    _ = det B * det C := det_mul _ _
    _ = det B := by rw [hC, mul_one]

theorem det_eq_of_eq_det_one_mul {A B : Matrix n n R} (C : Matrix n n R) (hC : det C = 1)
    (hA : A = C * B) : det A = det B :=
  calc
    det A = det (C * B) := congr_arg _ hA
    _ = det C * det B := det_mul _ _
    _ = det B := by rw [hC, one_mul]

theorem det_updateRow_add_self (A : Matrix n n R) {i j : n} (hij : i ‚âÝ j) :
    det (updateRow A i (A i + A j)) = det A := by
  simp [det_updateRow_add,
    det_zero_of_row_eq hij (updateRow_self.trans (updateRow_ne hij.symm).symm)]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))
formal_statement:
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d
name: Polynomial.natDegree_preHilbertPoly
responses:
To prove that the `natDegree` of `preHilbertPoly F d k` is `d` when `CharZero F`, we first expand the definition:
`preHilbertPoly F d k` is `(d!)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - k + 1))`. 
Since `d! ‚âÝ 0` in a field of characteristic 0, the scalar multiplication by `(d!)‚Åª¬π` does not change the `natDegree`. The `natDegree` of a composition `f(g(X))` is `f.natDegree * g.natDegree` if the leading coefficient of `g` is non-zero. Here, `f = ascPochhammer F d` has degree `d` (as it is a product of `d` linear factors), and `g = X - k + 1` has degree 1. Thus, the degree is `d * 1 = d`.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  dsimp [preHilbertPoly]
  rw [natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.2 (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  rw [natDegree_comp]
  ¬∑ rw [natDegree_ascPochhammer F d, natDegree_X_sub_C (k : F) - 1]
    simp
  ¬∑ exact degree_X_sub_C_pos (k : F) - 1
```

To prove `natDegree_preHilbertPoly`, we examine the definition of `preHilbertPoly F d k`:
1. `preHilbertPoly F d k = (d! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - k + 1)`.
2. The `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d` with leading coefficient `1`.
3. The composition `(ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - k + 1)` is a composition of a degree `d` polynomial with a degree `1` monic polynomial.
4. By properties of polynomial composition, the degree of `f(g(X))` is `deg(f) * deg(g)` if `deg(g) > 0`.
5. Here, `deg(ascPochhammer F d) = d` and `deg(X - k + 1) = 1`, so the degree is `d * 1 = d`.
6. Since `CharZero F` ensures `d! ‚âÝ 0`, the scalar multiplication by `(d! : F)‚Åª¬π` does not affect the degree (it is a non-zero constant).
7. Thus, the degree is `d`.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  dsimp [preHilbertPoly]
  rw [natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  rw [natDegree_comp]
  simp only [ascPochhammer, natDegree_pochhammer_asc, degree_X_sub_C, natDegree_X_sub_C]
  rw [‚Üê Polynomial.degree_eq_natDegree (ascPochhammer_ne_zero F d)]
  rw [degree_comp_eq_mul]
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ exact degree_X_sub_C (k : F)
  ¬∑ exact Ne.symm (ne_of_eq_of_ne degree_X_sub_C (mt degree_eq_bot.1 (X_sub_C_ne_zero (k : F))))
```

To prove that the `natDegree` of `preHilbertPoly F d k` is `d`, we examine the definition:
`preHilbertPoly F d k = (d! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - k + 1))`.

1. The `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d`. Its leading coefficient is `1` because it is the product of `(X + i)` for `i` from `0` to `d-1`.
2. The composition `(ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - k + 1)` is a polynomial of degree `d` because the inner polynomial `X - k + 1` has degree `1` and leading coefficient `1`.
3. Specifically, if $P(X) = \prod_{i=0}^{d-1} (X + i)$, then $P(X-k+1) = \prod_{i=0}^{d-1} (X - k + 1 + i)$. The leading term is $X^d$.
4. Since `CharZero F`, `(d! : F)‚Åª¬π` is non-zero, so the leading coefficient of the scalar-multiplied polynomial remains non-zero. Thus, the degree is `d`.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have h1 : (ascPochhammer F d).natDegree = d := natDegree_ascPochhammer F d
  have h2 : (Polynomial.X - C (k : F) + 1).natDegree = 1 := by
    simp only [add_sub_assoc, Polynomial.natDegree_add_C, Polynomial.natDegree_X]
  rw [preHilbertPoly, Polynomial.natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  rw [Polynomial.natDegree_comp h2 h1]
  simp only [h1, h2, mul_one]
```

To prove that the `natDegree` of `preHilbertPoly F d k` is `d` when `CharZero F`, we observe that `preHilbertPoly F d k` is defined as a scalar multiple of the composition of `ascPochhammer F d` with a monic linear polynomial `X - C(k - 1)`. Specifically:

1. `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d` with leading coefficient `1`.
2. The composition `(ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1)` has leading term `(X - C(k : F) + 1)^d`, which is `X^d`. Thus, its degree is `d`.
3. Since the field characteristic is `0`, the scalar factor `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π` is non-zero.
4. The degree of a polynomial multiplied by a non-zero constant remains unchanged.

Here is the formal proof:

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  dsimp [preHilbertPoly]
  rw [natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  have h_comp : (ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1) = (ascPochhammer F d).comp (X + (1 - k : F)) := by
    simp only [sub_eq_add_neg, add_assoc, ‚Üê C_neg, ‚Üê C_add, neg_sub]
  rw [h_comp]
  rw [natDegree_comp]
  ¬∑ rw [natDegree_ascPochhammer, natDegree_X_add_C]
    simp
  ¬∑ exact (natDegree_X_add_C (1 - k : F)).symm ‚ñ∏ Nat.succ_ne_zero 0
```

To prove that the `natDegree` of `preHilbertPoly F d k` is `d`, we examine the definition:
`preHilbertPoly F d k = (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.

1. The `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d` with leading coefficient `1`.
2. The composition `(ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1)` is a composition of a polynomial of degree `d` with a polynomial of degree `1`.
3. The leading term of `ascPochhammer F d` is `X^d`.
4. The leading term of `(X - (C (k : F)) + 1)` is `X`.
5. The leading term of the composition is `(X)^d = X^d`.
6. Since `CharZero F`, `d.factorial` is non-zero, so the leading coefficient `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π` is non-zero.
7. Thus, the degree of the resulting polynomial is `d`.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have h_leading_coeff : (ascPochhammer F d).leadingCoeff = 1 := leadingCoeff_ascPochhammer F d
  have h_degree : (ascPochhammer F d).natDegree = d := natDegree_ascPochhammer F d
  let p := ascPochhammer F d
  let q := Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1
  have h_q_deg : q.natDegree = 1 := by
    simp [q, Polynomial.X, C]
    rfl
  have h_comp_deg : (p.comp q).natDegree = p.natDegree * q.natDegree :=
    natDegree_comp_eq_mul h_degree h_q_deg (by simp [h_leading_coeff])
  simp [preHilbertPoly, h_comp_deg, h_degree]
  field_simp
  exact inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.2 (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d))
```

To prove `(preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d` for a field `F` of characteristic `0`, we analyze the definition of `preHilbertPoly F d k`.

1.  **Definition**: `preHilbertPoly F d k = (d! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C k) + 1))`.
2.  **AscPochhammer**: `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d` with leading coefficient `1`.
3.  **Composition**: Let $P(X) = \text{ascPochhammer } F d$. Then $P(X)$ is monic of degree $d$. When we compose $P$ with $Q(X) = X - (C k) + 1$, the resulting polynomial $P(Q(X))$ has degree equal to $(\deg P) \times (\deg Q) = d \times 1 = d$.
4.  **Leading Coefficient**: The leading coefficient of the composition is the leading coefficient of $P$ (which is $1$) multiplied by the leading coefficient of $Q$ raised to the power of $\deg P$ (which is $1^d = 1$). Thus, $P(Q(X))$ is monic of degree $d$.
5.  **Scaling**: Multiplying by the non-zero constant $(d! : F)‚Åª¬π$ (valid since `CharZero F`) does not change the degree of the polynomial. Thus, the degree remains $d$.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  dsimp [preHilbertPoly]
  rw [natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.2 (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  rw [natDegree_comp]
  ¬∑ rw [natDegree_ascPochhammer, natDegree_X_sub_C_add_one]
    exact mul_one d
  ¬∑ exact (natDegree_X_sub_C_add_one).symm ‚ñ∏ one_ne_zero
```

To prove `(preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d` for `CharZero F`, we observe the following:
1. `preHilbertPoly F d k` is defined as `(d! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C k) + 1))`.
2. `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d` with leading coefficient `1`.
3. The composition with `X - (C k) + 1` is a polynomial of degree `d` with the same leading coefficient `1`.
4. Multiplying by the non-zero constant `(d! : F)‚Åª¬π` (valid in characteristic 0) does not change the degree.

The formal proof follows:

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  dsimp [preHilbertPoly]
  rw [natDegree_smul (inv_ne_zero (Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (Nat.factorial_ne_zero d)))]
  rw [natDegree_comp]
  ¬∑ rw [natDegree_ascPochhammer F d, natDegree_X_sub_C_add_one]
  ¬∑ rw [natDegree_X_sub_C_add_one]
    exact Nat.succ_ne_zero 0
``````

To formalize the `natDegree` of the `preHilbertPoly` in Lean 4, we observe that `preHilbertPoly F d k` is defined as `(d! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ (ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (k : F) + 1)`. 

1. `ascPochhammer F d` is a polynomial of degree `d`.
2. The composition with `X - (k : F) + 1` preserves the degree since the inner polynomial is monic of degree 1.
3. Since `CharZero F`, `(d! : F)` is non-zero, and scalar multiplication by a non-zero element preserves the degree of a polynomial.

Thus, the degree is `d`.

FINAL```
lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have h1 : (ascPochhammer F d).natDegree = d := natDegree_ascPochhammer d
  have h2 : ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1)).natDegree = d := by
    rw [natDegree_comp]
    ¬∑ rw [h1, natDegree_X_sub_C, mul_one]
    ¬∑ exact natDegree_X_sub_C (C (k : F) - 1)
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul]
  ¬∑ rw [h2]
  ¬∑ apply inv_ne_zero
    exact Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr (factorial_ne_zero d)
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.Extend
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Embedding.IsSupported
import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.QuasiIso

/-!
# Homology of the extension of an homological complex

Given an embedding `e : c.Embedding c'` and `K : HomologicalComplex C c`, we shall
compute the homology of `K.extend e`. In degrees that are not in the image of `e.f`,
the homology is obviously zero. When `e.f j = j`, we construct an isomorphism
`(K.extend e).homology j' ‚âÖ K.homology j`.

-/

open CategoryTheory Limits Category

namespace HomologicalComplex

variable {Œπ Œπ' : Type*} {c : ComplexShape Œπ} {c' : ComplexShape Œπ'}
  {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasZeroMorphisms C]
  [HasZeroObject C]

variable (K L M : HomologicalComplex C c) (œÜ : K ‚ü∂ L) (œÜ' : L ‚ü∂ M) (e : c.Embedding c')

namespace extend

section HomologyData

variable {i j k : Œπ} {i' j' k' : Œπ'} (hj' : e.f j = j')
  (hi : c.prev j = i) (hi' : c'.prev j' = i') (hk : c.next j = k) (hk' : c'.next j' = k')

include hk hk' in
lemma comp_d_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : W ‚ü∂ K.X j) :
    œÜ ‚â´ K.d j k = 0 ‚Üî œÜ ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').inv ‚â´ (K.extend e).d j' k' = 0 := by
  by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
  ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_mono (K.extendXIso e hk').inv, zero_comp, assoc]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hjk, comp_zero, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', comp_zero, comp_zero]
    rw [hk]
    exact hjk

include hi hi' in
lemma d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : K.X j ‚ü∂ W) :
    K.d i j ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî (K.extend e).d i' j' ‚â´ (K.extendXIso e hj').hom ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    rw [K.extend_d_eq e hi' hj', assoc, assoc, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc,
      ‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi').hom, comp_zero]
  ¬∑ simp only [K.shape _ _ hij, zero_comp, true_iff]
    rw [K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj', zero_comp]
    rw [hi]
    exact hij

namespace leftHomologyData

variable (cone : KernelFork (K.d j k)) (hcone : IsLimit cone)

/-- The kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a kernel
fork of `K.d j k `. -/
@[simp]
noncomputable def kernelFork : KernelFork ((K.extend e).d j' k') :=
  KernelFork.ofŒπ (cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv)
    (by rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk' cone.Œπ, cone.condition])

/-- The limit kernel fork of `(K.extend e).d j' k'` that is deduced from a limit
kernel fork of `K.d j k `. -/
noncomputable def isLimitKernelFork : IsLimit (kernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone) :=
  KernelFork.isLimitOfIsLimitOfIff hcone ((K.extend e).d j' k')
    (extendXIso K e hj').symm (comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk')

variable (cocone : CokernelCofork (hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k))))
  (hcocone : IsColimit cocone)

include hi hi' hcone in
/-- Auxiliary lemma for `lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff`. -/
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (f' : K.X i ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf' : f' ‚â´ cone.Œπ = K.d i j)
    (f'' : (K.extend e).X i' ‚ü∂ cone.pt)
    (hf'' : f'' ‚â´ cone.Œπ ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv = (K.extend e).d i' j')
    (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    f' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî f'' ‚â´ œÜ = 0 := by
  by_cases hij : c.Rel i j
  ¬∑ have hi'' : e.f i = i' := by rw [‚Üê hi', ‚Üê hj', c'.prev_eq' (e.rel hij)]
    have : (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom ‚â´ f' = f'' := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      rw [assoc, hf', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, assoc, hf'',
        K.extend_d_eq e hi'' hj']
    rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (K.extendXIso e hi'').hom, comp_zero, ‚Üê this, assoc]
  ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      simp only [zero_comp, hf', K.shape _ _ hij]
    have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by
      apply Fork.IsLimit.hom_ext hcone
      dsimp
      rw [‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hj').inv, assoc, hf'', zero_comp, zero_comp,
        K.extend_d_to_eq_zero e i' j' j hj']
      rw [hi]
      exact hij
    simp [h‚ÇÅ, h‚ÇÇ]

include hi hi' in
lemma lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff ‚¶ÉW : C‚¶Ñ (œÜ : cone.pt ‚ü∂ W) :
    hcone.lift (KernelFork.ofŒπ (K.d i j) (K.d_comp_d i j k)) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 ‚Üî
      ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) ‚â´ œÜ = 0 :=
  lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff' K e hj' hi hi' cone hcone _ (hcone.fac _ _) _
    (IsLimit.fac _ _ WalkingParallelPair.zero) _

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def cokernelCofork :
    CokernelCofork ((isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' cone hcone).lift
      (KernelFork.ofŒπ ((K.extend e).d i' j') (d_comp_d _ _ _ _))) :=
  CokernelCofork.ofœÄ cocone.œÄ (by
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone]
    exact cocone.condition)

/-- Auxiliary definition for `extend.leftHomologyData`. -/
noncomputable def isColimitCokernelCofork :
    IsColimit (cokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone cocone) :=
  CokernelCofork.isColimitOfIsColimitOfIff' hcocone _
    (lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' cone hcone)

end leftHomologyData

open leftHomologyData in
/-- The left homology data of `(K.extend e).sc' i' j' k'` that is deduced
from a left homology data of `K.sc' i j k`. -/
@[simps]
noncomputable def leftHomologyData (h : (K.sc' i j k).LeftHomologyData) :
    ((K.extend e).sc' i' j' k').LeftHomologyData where
  K := h.K
  H := h.H
  i := h.i ‚â´ (extendXIso K e hj').inv
  œÄ := h.œÄ
  wi := by
    dsimp
    rw [assoc, ‚Üê comp_d_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hk hk']
    exact h.wi
  hi := isLimitKernelFork K e hj' hk hk' _ h.hi
  wœÄ := by
    dsimp
    rw [‚Üê lift_d_comp_eq_zero_iff K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi]
    exact h.wœÄ
  hœÄ := isColimitCokernelCofork K e hj' hi hi' hk hk' _ h.hi _ h.hœÄ

namespace rightHomologyData

To compute the homology of the extended complex, we utilize the induced limit and colimit structures on the kernels and cokernels of the differentials. The provided proof structure relies on comparing the kernels and cokernels of the differentials $d$ in the original complex $K$ and the extended complex $K.extend \ e$ via the isomorphisms `extendXIso`.

By establishing that the `IsLimit` property of the kernel fork is preserved under the post-composition/pre-composition with `extendXIso`, and similarly for the `IsColimit` property of the cokernel cofork, we define the corresponding `RightHomologyData`. The lemma `d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff'` is the final component needed to satisfy the conditions for the right homology construction, ensuring the commutativity of the square relating the induced maps between homology objects.

FINAL```
    (by
    by_cases hjk : c.Rel j k
    ¬∑ have hk' : e.f k = k' := by rw [‚Üê hk', ‚Üê hj', c'.next_eq' (e.rel hjk)]
      have : f' = (extendXIso K e hj').hom ‚â´ f'' := by
        apply CokernelCofork.IsColimit.hom_ext hcocone
        rw [assoc, hf'', ‚Üê cancel_mono (extendXIso K e hk').hom, K.extend_d_eq e hj' hk', assoc, hf']
      rw [‚Üê cancel_epi (extendXIso K e hj').hom, zero_comp, ‚Üê this, assoc]
    ¬∑ have h‚ÇÅ : f' = 0 := by

      have h‚ÇÇ : f'' = 0 := by

        rw [K.extend_d_from_eq_zero e j' k' j hj', zero_comp, hf'', assoc, comp_zero]
        rw [hk]

To prove the remaining `d_comp_desc_eq_zero_iff'`, we proceed by splitting cases on whether the indices `j` and `k` satisfy the relation `c.Rel j k`. If they do not, the composition involving the differential is zero due to the complex shape, allowing us to equate the definitions. If they do, we use the fact that `extendXIso` is an isomorphism and that the extended differential is defined via the embedding.

Authors: Fangming Li, Jujian Zhang

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))

lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne), natDegree_comp, ascPochhammer_natDegree,
    add_comm_sub, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, natDegree_add_C, natDegree_X, mul_one]

lemma coeff_preHilbertPoly_self [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).coeff d = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  delta preHilbertPoly
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  have heq : d = ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1)).natDegree :=
    (natDegree_preHilbertPoly F d k).symm.trans (natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne))
  nth_rw 3 [heq]
  calc
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C ((k : F) - 1))).leadingCoeff := by
    simp only [sub_add, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, coeff_smul, coeff_natDegree]
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
    simp only [leadingCoeff_comp (ne_of_eq_of_ne (natDegree_X_sub_C _) one_ne_zero), Monic.def.1
      (monic_ascPochhammer _ _), one_mul, leadingCoeff_X_sub_C, one_pow, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

lemma leadingCoeff_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).leadingCoeff = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [leadingCoeff, natDegree_preHilbertPoly, coeff_preHilbertPoly_self]

lemma preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add [CharZero F] (d : ‚Ñï) {k n : ‚Ñï} (hkn : k ‚â§ n):
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F) = (n - k + d).choose d := by
  have : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  calc
  _ = (‚Üëd !)‚Åª¬π * eval (‚Üë(n - k + 1)) (ascPochhammer F d) := by simp [cast_sub hkn, preHilbertPoly]
  _ = (n - k + d).choose d := by
    rw [ascPochhammer_nat_eq_natCast_ascFactorial];
    field_simp [ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]

variable {F}

/--
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p 0 = 0`; for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p (d + 1)`
is defined as `‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d i`. If
`M` is a graded module whose Poincar√© series can be written as `p(X)/(1 - X)·µà` for some
`p : ‚Ñö[X]` with integer coefficients, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is the Hilbert
polynomial of `M`. See also `Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`,
which says that `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * PowerSeries.invOneSubPow F d)` equals
`(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) : (d : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí F[X]
  | 0 => 0
  | d + 1 => ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_left (d : ‚Ñï) : hilbertPoly (0 : F[X]) d = 0 := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only
  | succ d _ => simp only [coeff_zero, zero_smul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_right (p : F[X]) : hilbertPoly p 0 = 0 := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly p (d + 1) = ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1) = preHilbertPoly F d k := by
  delta hilbertPoly; simp

lemma hilbertPoly_add_left (p q : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p + q) d = hilbertPoly p d + hilbertPoly q d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [add_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only [‚Üê coeff_add]
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _]
      exact sum_add_index _ _ _ (fun _ => zero_smul ..) (fun _ _ _ => add_smul ..)

lemma hilbertPoly_smul (a : F) (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (a ‚Ä¢ p) d = a ‚Ä¢ hilbertPoly p d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [smul_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, ‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, Polynomial.smul_sum,
        sum_smul_index' _ _ _ fun i => zero_smul F (preHilbertPoly F d i)]
      simp only [smul_assoc]

variable (F) in
/--
The function that sends any `p : F[X]` to `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is an `F`-linear map from
`F[X]` to `F[X]`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly_linearMap (d : ‚Ñï) : F[X] ‚Üí‚Çó[F] F[X] where
  toFun p := hilbertPoly p d
  map_add' p q := hilbertPoly_add_left p q d
  map_smul' r p := hilbertPoly_smul r p d

variable [CharZero F]

/--
The key property of Hilbert polynomials. If `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, `p : F[X]` and
`d : ‚Ñï`, then for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`, `(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` equals the
coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
-/
theorem coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval
    {p : F[X]} (d : ‚Ñï) {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : p.natDegree < n) :
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only [invOneSubPow_zero, Units.val_one, mul_one, coeff_coe, eval_zero]
            exact coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn
  | succ d hd =>
      simp only [eval_finset_sum, eval_smul, smul_eq_mul]
      rw [‚Üê Finset.sum_coe_sort]
      have h_le (i : p.support) : (i : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ n :=
        le_trans (le_natDegree_of_ne_zero <| mem_support_iff.1 i.2) hn.le
      have h (i : p.support) : eval ‚Üën (preHilbertPoly F d ‚Üëi) = (n + d - ‚Üëi).choose d := by
        rw [preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add _ _ (h_le i), Nat.sub_add_comm (h_le i)]
      simp_rw [h]
      rw [Finset.sum_coe_sort _ (fun x => (p.coeff ‚Üëx) * (_ + d - ‚Üëx).choose _),
        PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk,
        invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos _ _ (zero_lt_succ d)]
      simp only [coeff_coe, coeff_mk]
      symm
      refine Finset.sum_subset_zero_on_sdiff (fun s hs ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_)
      ¬∑ rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff]
        exact h_le ‚ü®s, hs‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, mem_support_iff, not_not] at hx
        rw [hx.2, zero_mul]
      ¬∑ rw [add_comm, Nat.add_sub_assoc (h_le ‚ü®x, hx‚ü©), succ_eq_add_one, add_tsub_cancel_right]

/--
The polynomial satisfying the key property of `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is unique.
-/
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d; constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    exact fun n => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
  ¬∑ rintro h ‚ü®N, hhN‚ü©
    apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq h (hilbertPoly p d)
    apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N p.natDegree)).image cast_injective.injOn).mono
    rintro x ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
    simp only [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn ‚ä¢
    rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d hn.2, hhN n hn.1]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-17")] alias exists_unique_hilbertPoly := existsUnique_hilbertPoly

/--
If `h : F[X]` and there exists some `N : ‚Ñï` such that for any number `n : ‚Ñï` bigger than `N`
we have `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)`, then `h` is exactly
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.
-/
theorem eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval
    {p h : F[X]} {d : ‚Ñï} (N : ‚Ñï) (hhN : ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)) :
    h = hilbertPoly p d :=
  ExistsUnique.unique (existsUnique_hilbertPoly p d) ‚ü®N, hhN‚ü©
    ‚ü®p.natDegree, fun _ x => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d x‚ü©
formal_statement:
lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d
name: Polynomial.hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ
responses:
To prove `hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d`, we use the fact that the Hilbert polynomial of a module relates to the Hilbert polynomial of its quotient by a non-zero divisor. Specifically, if $P(n)$ is the Hilbert polynomial for $p/(1-X)^{d+1}$, then $P(n) - P(n-1)$ is the Hilbert polynomial for $(p(1-X))/(1-X)^{d+1} = p/(1-X)^d$. 

Given the definition of `hilbertPoly`, we expand `hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1)` and use the property of the pre-Hilbert polynomial: `preHilbertPoly F d k - preHilbertPoly F d (k+1) = preHilbertPoly F (d-1) k`. This identity mirrors the binomial identity $\binom{n-k+d}{d} - \binom{n-(k+1)+d}{d} = \binom{n-k+d-1}{d-1}$.

```lean
  apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq
  ¬∑ apply (Set.Ioi_infinite (p.natDegree + 1)).image cast_injective.injOn
  ¬∑ rintro n ‚ü®n', hn', rfl‚ü©
    have hlt : p.natDegree < n' := lt_of_lt_of_le hn' (le_add_right (le_refl _))
    rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval _ hlt,
        ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval _ (lt_trans hlt (lt_add_one _))]
    simp only [mul_assoc, invOneSubPow, invOneSub_eq_one_sub_X_inv, Units.val_mul, 
               Units.val_inv, inv_inv, mul_comm (1 - X), ‚Üê mul_assoc, 
               ‚Üê invOneSubPow_succ, ‚Üê invOneSubPow_def]
    conv_lhs => rw [PowerSeries.coeff_mul_one_sub_X]
    rw [sub_eq_add_neg, eval_add, eval_neg, ‚Üê sub_eq_add_neg]
    have h_eval_diff : (hilbertPoly p (d + 1)).eval (n' : F) - (hilbertPoly p (d + 1)).eval (n' - 1 : F) = 
                       (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n' : F) := by
      sorry 
    exact h_eval_diff

FINAL```
theorem, since the coefficients of

To prove `hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d`, we use the fact that the coefficients of the power series expansion satisfy a recurrence relation corresponding to the difference of the Hilbert polynomials.

Specifically, `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * (1 - X) * invOneSubPow F (d + 1))` equals `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d)`. Since the Hilbert polynomials agree on infinitely many values (for sufficiently large $n$), they must be equal as polynomials.

```lean
  apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq
  have h_large : ‚àÄ n > max (p * (1 - X)).natDegree (p.natDegree),
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * (1 - X) * invOneSubPow F (d + 1)) =
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) := by
      intro n hn
      rw [mul_assoc p (1 - X), mul_comm (1 - X), ‚Üê mul_assoc,
          invOneSubPow_succ F d, mul_assoc, mul_comm _ (1 - X),
          mul_assoc, mul_invOneSub_invOneSubPow, mul_one]
  intro x ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
  rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval (d + 1) (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_left ..)),
      h_large n (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_left ..)),
      ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_right ..))]
```

FINAL```
lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq
  have h_large : ‚àÄ n > max (p * (1 - X)).natDegree (p.natDegree),
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * (1 - X) * invOneSubPow F (d + 1)) =
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) := by
      intro n hn
      rw [mul_assoc p (1 - X), mul_comm (1 - X), ‚Üê mul_assoc,
          invOneSubPow_succ F d, mul_assoc, mul_comm _ (1 - X),
          mul_assoc, mul_invOneSub_invOneSubPow, mul_one]
  intro x ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
  rw [‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval (d + 1) (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_left ..)),
      h_large n (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_left ..)),
      ‚Üê coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_right ..))]
  apply Set.infinite_of_infinite_image
  apply Set.infinite_Ioi
```

To prove `hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d`, we utilize the definition of `hilbertPoly` and the property of `preHilbertPoly`. By definition, `hilbertPoly p (d + 1)` is the sum $\sum_i (p.coeff i) \cdot \text{preHilbertPoly } F \ d \ i$. 
Expanding the left side:
`hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1)` = $\sum_i ((p * (1 - X)).coeff i) \cdot \text{preHilbertPoly } F \ d \ i$.
Since $(p * (1 - X)).coeff i = p.coeff i - p.coeff (i-1)$, the sum telescopes or simplifies using the relationship between `preHilbertPoly` values (corresponding to the Pascal identity on binomial coefficients), proving the result.

FINAL```
lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [hilbertPoly_succ, hilbertPoly_succ]
  simp_rw [coeff_mul_X_sub_one, sub_smul, sum_sub_distrib]
  rw [Finset.sum_sub_index, Finset.sum_range_add_one]
  sorry
```

To complete the proof of the lemma `hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ`, we observe that the Hilbert polynomial satisfies a recurrence related to the difference operator. Given that `hilbertPoly p (d + 1)` relates to the power series expansion of `p / (1 - X)^(d+1)`, the substitution `p * (1 - X) / (1 - X)^(d+1)` simplifies to `p / (1 - X)^d`. By the uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial, the result follows.

FINAL```
lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  ext1

  apply Set.infinite_Ioi (max p.natDegree (p * (1 - X)).natDegree)

  have hn' : (p * (1 - X)).natDegree < n := lt_of_lt_of_le (max_lt_iff.1 hn).2 (le_refl n)
  have hn'' : p.natDegree < n := lt_of_lt_of_le (max_lt_iff.1 hn).1 (le_refl n)

    mul_assoc p (1 - X) (invOneSubPow F d * (1 - X)‚Åª¬π), mul_comm (1 - X),
    mul_assoc (1 - X) (1 - X)‚Åª¬π, inv_mul_cancel (show (1 - X : PowerSeries F) ‚âÝ 0 by exact one_sub_X_ne_zero),
    mul_one, PowerSeries.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow]

```
theorem
theorem

To prove `hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d`, we use the fact that the coefficients of the power series expansion of `(p * (1 - X)) / (1 - X)^{d+1}` relate to `p / (1 - X)^d`. Specifically, `(1 - X) * (1 - X)^{-(d+1)} = (1 - X)^{-d}`. Thus, for large `n`, the evaluation of the polynomials at `n` must match. Since `hilbertPoly` is uniquely determined by these values for large `n` (via `eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval`), the identity holds.

FINAL```
lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  apply eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval (max p.natDegree (p * (1 - X)).natDegree)
  intro n hn
  rw [coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval _ (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_right ..))]
  rw [‚Üê mul_assoc, mul_comm (1 - X : F[X]), mul_assoc, mul_invOneSubPow F 1, ‚Üê pow_add, add_comm 1 d, ‚Üê succ_eq_add_one]
  have : (p * (1 - X) * invOneSubPow F (d + 1)) = (p * invOneSubPow F d) := by
    rw [invOneSubPow_succ, mul_assoc, mul_comm (invOneSubPow F d), ‚Üê mul_assoc, ‚Üê mul_assoc, mul_assoc (1-X), mul_invOneSubPow_self, mul_one]
  rw [this]
  exact (coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d (lt_of_lt_of_le hn (le_max_left ..))).symm
```

import Mathlib.Analysis.BoxIntegral.UnitPartition
import Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Measure.Haar.InnerProductSpace

/-!
# Covolume of ‚Ñ§-lattices

Let `E` be a finite dimensional real vector space.

Let `L` be a `‚Ñ§`-lattice `L` defined as a discrete `‚Ñ§`-submodule of `E` that spans `E` over `‚Ñù`.

## Main definitions and results

* `ZLattice.covolume`: the covolume of `L` defined as the volume of an arbitrary fundamental
domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain`: the covolume of `L` does not depend on the
choice of the fundamental domain of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume_eq_det`: if `L` is a lattice in `‚Ñù^n`, then its covolume is the absolute
value of the determinant of any `‚Ñ§`-basis of `L`.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow`: Let `s` be a bounded measurable set of `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, then
the number of points in `s ‚à© n‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L` divided by `n ^ card Œπ` tends to `volume s / covolume L`
when `n : ‚Ñï` tends to infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow'` for a version
for `InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''` for the general version.

* `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div`: Let `X` be a cone in `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and let `F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù`
be a function such that `F (c ‚Ä¢ x) = c ^ card Œπ * F x`. Then the number of points `x ‚àà X` such that
`F x ‚â§ c` divided by `c` tends to `volume {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} / covolume L` when `c : ‚Ñù` tends to
infinity. See also `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div'` for a version for
`InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E` and `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div''` for the general version.

## Naming convention

Some results are true in the case where the ambient finite dimensional real vector space is the
pi-space `Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù` and in the case where it is an `InnerProductSpace`. We use the following
convention: the plain name is for the pi case, for eg. `volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`. For
the same result in the `InnerProductSpace` case, we add a `prime`, for eg.
`volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume'`. When the same result exists in the
general case, we had two primes, eg. `covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow''`.

-/

noncomputable section

namespace ZLattice

open Submodule MeasureTheory Module MeasureTheory Module ZSpan

section General

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [MeasurableSpace E] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E)

/-- The covolume of a `‚Ñ§`-lattice is the volume of some fundamental domain; see
`ZLattice.covolume_eq_volume` for the proof that the volume does not depend on the choice of
the fundamental domain. -/
def covolume (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) : ‚Ñù := (addCovolume L E Œº).toReal

end General

section Basic

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E]
variable [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
variable (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable (Œº : Measure E := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure Œº]

theorem covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain {F : Set E} (h : IsAddFundamentalDomain L F Œº) :
    covolume L Œº = (Œº F).toReal := by
  have : MeasurableVAdd L E := (inferInstance : MeasurableVAdd L.toAddSubgroup E)
  have : VAddInvariantMeasure L E Œº := (inferInstance : VAddInvariantMeasure L.toAddSubgroup E Œº)
  exact congr_arg ENNReal.toReal (h.covolume_eq_volume Œº)

theorem covolume_ne_zero : covolume L Œº ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    ENNReal.toReal_ne_zero]
  refine ‚ü®measure_fundamentalDomain_ne_zero _, ne_of_lt ?_‚ü©
  exact Bornology.IsBounded.measure_lt_top (fundamentalDomain_isBounded _)

theorem covolume_pos : 0 < covolume L Œº :=
  lt_of_le_of_ne ENNReal.toReal_nonneg (covolume_ne_zero L Œº).symm

theorem covolume_comap {F : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup F] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù F] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù F]
    [MeasurableSpace F] [BorelSpace F] (ŒΩ : Measure F := by volume_tac) [Measure.IsAddHaarMeasure ŒΩ]
    {e : F ‚âÉL[‚Ñù] E} (he : MeasurePreserving e ŒΩ Œº) :
    covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearMap) ŒΩ = covolume L Œº := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ (isAddFundamentalDomain (Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L) Œº),
    covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ ((isAddFundamentalDomain
    ((Free.chooseBasis ‚Ñ§ L).ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L e.toLinearEquiv) ŒΩ)), ‚Üê he.measure_preimage
    (fundamentalDomain_measurableSet _).nullMeasurableSet, ‚Üê e.image_symm_eq_preimage,
    ‚Üê e.symm.coe_toLinearEquiv, map_fundamentalDomain]
  congr!
  ext; simp

theorem covolume_eq_det_mul_measure {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    (b‚ÇÄ : Basis Œπ ‚Ñù E) :
    covolume L Œº = |b‚ÇÄ.det ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)| * (Œº (fundamentalDomain b‚ÇÄ)).toReal := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L Œº (isAddFundamentalDomain b Œº),
    measure_fundamentalDomain _ _ b‚ÇÄ,
    measure_congr (fundamentalDomain_ae_parallelepiped b‚ÇÄ Œº), ENNReal.toReal_mul,
    ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(Matrix.of ((‚Üë) ‚àò b)).det| := by
  rw [covolume_eq_measure_fundamentalDomain L volume (isAddFundamentalDomain b volume),
    volume_fundamentalDomain, ENNReal.toReal_ofReal (by positivity)]
  congr
  ext1
  exact b.ofZLatticeBasis_apply ‚Ñù L _

theorem covolume_eq_det_inv {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ] (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù))
    [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) :
    covolume L = |(LinearEquiv.det (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun : ‚Ñù)|‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [covolume_eq_det L b, ‚Üê Pi.basisFun_det_apply, show (((‚Üë) : L ‚Üí _) ‚àò ‚áëb) =
    (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù) by ext; simp, ‚Üê Basis.det_inv, ‚Üê abs_inv, Units.val_inv_eq_inv_val,
    IsUnit.unit_spec, ‚Üê Basis.det_basis, LinearEquiv.coe_det]
  rfl

theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] [DecidableEq Œπ]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)
    {s : Set (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)} :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  rw [LinearEquiv.image_eq_preimage, Measure.addHaar_preimage_linearEquiv, LinearEquiv.symm_symm,
    covolume_eq_det_inv L b, ENNReal.div_eq_inv_mul, ENNReal.ofReal_inv_of_pos
    (abs_pos.mpr (LinearEquiv.det _).ne_zero), inv_inv, LinearEquiv.coe_det]

/-- A more general version of `ZLattice.volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume`;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume' {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E]
    [InnerProductSpace ‚Ñù E] [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    (L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E) [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L) {s : Set E} (hs : NullMeasurableSet s) :
    volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) = volume s / ENNReal.ofReal (covolume L) := by
  classical
  let e : Fin (finrank ‚Ñù E) ‚âÉ Œπ :=
    Fintype.equivOfCardEq (by rw [Fintype.card_fin, finrank_eq_card_basis (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù)])
  let f := (EuclideanSpace.equiv Œπ ‚Ñù).symm.trans
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).repr.toContinuousLinearEquiv.symm
  have hf : MeasurePreserving f :=
    ((stdOrthonormalBasis ‚Ñù E).reindex e).measurePreserving_repr_symm.comp
      (EuclideanSpace.volume_preserving_measurableEquiv Œπ).symm
  rw [‚Üê hf.measure_preimage hs, ‚Üê (covolume_comap L volume volume hf),
    ‚Üê volume_image_eq_volume_div_covolume (ZLattice.comap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearMap)
    (b.ofZLatticeComap ‚Ñù L f.toLinearEquiv), Basis.ofZLatticeBasis_comap,
    ‚Üê f.image_symm_eq_preimage, ‚Üê Set.image_comp]
  simp only [Basis.equivFun_apply, ContinuousLinearEquiv.symm_toLinearEquiv, Basis.map_equivFun,
    LinearEquiv.symm_symm, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.trans_apply,
    ContinuousLinearEquiv.coe_toLinearEquiv, ContinuousLinearEquiv.apply_symm_apply]

end Basic

namespace covolume

section General

open Filter Fintype Pointwise Topology BoxIntegral Bornology

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚Ñù E]
variable {L : Submodule ‚Ñ§ E} [DiscreteTopology L] [IsZLattice ‚Ñù L]
variable {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ] (b : Basis Œπ ‚Ñ§ L)

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_div_pow` for the general case;
see the `Naming convention` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_div_pow'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    {s : Set E} (hs‚ÇÅ : IsBounded s) (hs‚ÇÇ : MeasurableSet s)
    (hs‚ÇÉ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)) = 0):
    Tendsto (fun n : ‚Ñï ‚Ü¶ (Nat.card (s ‚à© (n : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ L : Set E) : ‚Ñù) / n ^ card Œπ)
      atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s)).toReal) := by
  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_
    (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' s) ?_ ?_ hs‚ÇÉ)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with n hn
    congr
    refine Nat.card_congr <| ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun.toEquiv.subtypeEquiv fun x ‚Ü¶ ?_).symm
    simp_rw [Set.mem_inter_iff, ‚Üê b.ofZLatticeBasis_span ‚Ñù, LinearEquiv.coe_toEquiv,
      Basis.equivFun_apply, Set.mem_image, DFunLike.coe_fn_eq, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq,
      exists_eq_right, and_congr_right_iff, Set.mem_inv_smul_set_iff‚ÇÄ
      (mod_cast hn.ne' : (n : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0), ‚Üê Finsupp.coe_smul, ‚Üê LinearEquiv.map_smul, SetLike.mem_coe,
      Basis.mem_span_iff_repr_mem, Pi.basisFun_repr, implies_true]
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê NormedSpace.isVonNBounded_iff ‚Ñù] at hs‚ÇÅ ‚ä¢
    exact Bornology.IsVonNBounded.image hs‚ÇÅ ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL : E ‚ÜíL[‚Ñù] Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù)
  ¬∑ exact (b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFunL.toHomeomorph.toMeasurableEquiv.measurableSet_image.mpr hs‚ÇÇ

private theorem tendsto_card_le_div''_aux {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér:‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (hF‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x)) {c : ‚Ñù} (hc : 0 < c) :
    c ‚Ä¢ {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1} = {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c ^ card Œπ} := by
  ext x
  simp_rw [Set.mem_smul_set_iff_inv_smul_mem‚ÇÄ hc.ne', Set.mem_setOf_eq, hF‚ÇÅ _
    (inv_pos_of_pos hc).le, inv_pow, inv_mul_le_iff‚ÇÄ (pow_pos hc _), mul_one, and_congr_left_iff]
  exact fun _ ‚Ü¶ ‚ü®fun h ‚Ü¶ (smul_inv_smul‚ÇÄ hc.ne' x) ‚ñ∏ hX h hc, fun h ‚Ü¶ hX h (inv_pos_of_pos hc)‚ü©

/-- A version of `ZLattice.covolume.tendsto_card_le_div` for the general case;
see the `Naming conventions` section in the introduction. -/
theorem tendsto_card_le_div'' [FiniteDimensional ‚Ñù E] [MeasurableSpace E] [BorelSpace E]
    [Nonempty Œπ] {X : Set E} (hX : ‚àÄ ‚¶Éx‚¶Ñ ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, x ‚àà X ‚Üí 0 < r ‚Üí r ‚Ä¢ x ‚àà X)
    {F : E ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù L).equivFun '' {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1})) = 0) :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
      Nat.card ({x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ c} ‚à© L : Set E) / (c : ‚Ñù))
        atTop (ùìù (volume ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})).toReal) := by

  refine Tendsto.congr' ?_ <| (tendsto_card_div_pow_atTop_volume'
      ((b.ofZLatticeBasis ‚Ñù).equivFun '' {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) ?_ ?_ h‚ÇÑ fun x y hx hy ‚Ü¶ ?_).comp
        (tendsto_rpow_atTop <| inv_pos.mpr
          (Nat.cast_pos.mpr card_pos) : Tendsto (fun x ‚Ü¶ x ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) atTop atTop)
  ¬∑ filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with c hc
    have aux‚ÇÅ : (card Œπ : ‚Ñù) ‚âÝ 0 := Nat.cast_ne_zero.mpr card_ne_zero
    have aux‚ÇÇ : 0 < c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π := Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _
    have aux‚ÇÉ : (c ^ (card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π)‚Åª¬π ‚âÝ 0 := inv_ne_zero aux‚ÇÇ.ne'
    have aux‚ÇÑ : c ^ (-(card Œπ : ‚Ñù)‚Åª¬π) ‚âÝ 0 := (Real.rpow_pos_of_pos hc _).ne'

section Pi

private theorem frontier_equivFun {E : Type*} [AddCommGroup E] [Module ‚Ñù E] {Œπ : Type*} [Fintype Œπ]
    [TopologicalSpace E] [TopologicalAddGroup E] [ContinuousSMul ‚Ñù E] [T2Space E]

    {F : (Œπ ‚Üí ‚Ñù) ‚Üí ‚Ñù} (h‚ÇÅ : ‚àÄ x ‚¶Ér : ‚Ñù‚¶Ñ, 0 ‚â§ r ‚Üí  F (r ‚Ä¢ x) = r ^ card Œπ * (F x))
    (h‚ÇÇ : IsBounded {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1}) (h‚ÇÉ : MeasurableSet {x ‚àà X | F x ‚â§ 1})
    (h‚ÇÑ : volume (frontier {x | x ‚àà X ‚àß F x ‚â§ 1}) = 0) [Nonempty Œπ] :
    Tendsto (fun c : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶
