import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Continuity
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Complex.LogDeriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Extend
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Prod
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Deriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Trigonometric.Deriv

/-!
# Derivatives of power function on `‚ÑÇ`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñù‚â•0`, and `‚Ñù‚â•0‚àû`

We also prove differentiability and provide derivatives for the power functions `x ^ y`.
-/


noncomputable section

open scoped Real Topology NNReal ENNReal
open Filter

namespace Complex

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p := by
  have A : p.1 ‚âÝ 0 := slitPlane_ne_zero hp
  have : (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    ((isOpen_ne.preimage continuous_fst).eventually_mem A).mono fun p hp =>
      cpow_def_of_ne_zero hp _
  rw [cpow_sub _ _ A, cpow_one, mul_div_left_comm, mul_smul, mul_smul]
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero A, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_smul, add_comm, smul_add] using
    ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.clog hp).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).cexp

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow' {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (hp : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((y * x ^ (y - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (x ^ y * log x) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) (x, y) :=
  @hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow (x, y) hp

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® y ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun y => x ^ y) (x ^ y * log x) y := by
  rcases em (x = 0) with (rfl | hx)
  ¬∑ replace h := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
    rw [log_zero, mul_zero]
    refine (hasStrictDerivAt_const y 0).congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
    exact (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem h).mono fun y hy => (zero_cpow hy).symm
  ¬∑ simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero hx, mul_one] using
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id y).const_mul (log x)).cexp

theorem hasFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p :=
  (hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow hp).hasFDerivAt

end Complex

section fderiv

open Complex

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚ÑÇ E] {f g : E ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ}
  {x : E} {s : Set E} {c : ‚ÑÇ}

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).comp_hasStrictFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by
  convert
    (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hg : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ g x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivAt h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (hg : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ g s x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableWithinAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s) (hg : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ g s)
    (h0 : Set.MapsTo f s slitPlane) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).cpow (hg x hx) (h0 hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableOn_const c) h0

theorem Differentiable.cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f) (hg : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ g)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x, f x ‚àà slitPlane) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).cpow (hg x) (h0 x)

theorem Differentiable.const_cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x, f x ‚âÝ 0) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiable_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] :
    Differentiable ‚ÑÇ fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s :=
  differentiable_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiableAt_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] (t : ‚ÑÇ) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s) t :=
  differentiableAt_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

end fderiv

section deriv

open Complex

variable {f g : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {s : Set ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' x c : ‚ÑÇ}

/-- A private lemma that rewrites the output of lemmas like `HasFDerivAt.cpow` to the form
expected by lemmas like `HasDerivAt.cpow`. -/
private theorem aux : ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight f' +
    (f x ^ g x * log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight g') 1 =
      g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * log (f x) * g' := by
  simp only [Algebra.id.smul_eq_mul, one_mul, ContinuousLinearMap.one_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.smulRight_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul']

nonrec theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa using (hf.cpow hg h0).hasStrictDerivAt

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (h : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h).comp x hf

theorem Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (h : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ c) (c * x ^ (c - 1)) x := by
  simpa only [mul_zero, add_zero, mul_one] using
    (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).cpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x c) h

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivAt

theorem HasDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') s x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivWithinAt

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') s x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

/-- Although `fun x => x ^ r` for fixed `r` is *not* complex-differentiable along the negative real
line, it is still real-differentiable, and the derivative is what one would formally expect.
See `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` for an alternate formulation. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ -1) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x := by
  rw [Ne, ‚Üê add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ‚Üê Ne] at hr
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- easy case : `0 < x`
    -- Porting note: proof used to be
    -- convert (((hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)).cpow_const _).div_const (r + 1)).comp_ofReal using 1
    -- ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel, id.def, mul_one, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel _ hr]
    -- ¬∑ rw [id.def, ofReal_re]; exact Or.inl hx
    apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal (e := fun y => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1))
    convert HasDerivAt.div_const (ùïú := ‚ÑÇ) ?_ (r + 1) using 1
    ¬∑ exact (mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr).symm
    ¬∑ convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
      ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, mul_comm]; exact (mul_one _).symm
      ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)
      ¬∑ simp [hx]
  ¬∑ -- harder case : `x < 0`
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x,
        (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1) = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) * exp (œÄ * I * (r + 1)) / (r + 1) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-‚Üëy) ^ (r + 1) * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * (r + 1)))
        ((r + 1) * (-‚Üëx) ^ r * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * r)) x by
      convert this.div_const (r + 1) using 1
      conv_rhs => rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr]
    rw [mul_add ((œÄ : ‚ÑÇ) * _), mul_one, exp_add, exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm (_ : ‚ÑÇ) (-1 : ‚ÑÇ),
      neg_one_mul]
    simp_rw [mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (‚Üë(-y) : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) (-(r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) x by
      convert this.neg.mul_const _ using 1; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) (-x) by
      convert @HasDerivAt.scomp ‚Ñù _ ‚ÑÇ _ _ x ‚Ñù _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ this (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      rw [real_smul, ofReal_neg 1, ofReal_one]; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) ‚Üë(-x) by
      exact this.comp_ofReal
    conv in ‚Üë_ ^ _ => rw [(by ring : r = r + 1 - 1)]
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
    ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, add_sub_cancel_right]; exact (mul_one _).symm
    ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id ((-x : ‚Ñù) : ‚ÑÇ)
    ¬∑ simp [hx]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-15")] alias hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow := hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'

/-- An alternate formulation of `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'`. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  have := HasDerivAt.const_mul r <| hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' hx
    (by rwa [ne_eq, sub_eq_neg_self])
  simpa [sub_add_cancel, mul_div_cancel‚ÇÄ _ hr] using this

/-- A version of `DifferentiableAt.cpow_const` for a real function. -/
theorem DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const {f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {x : ‚Ñù} (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x)
    (h0 : f x ‚âÝ 0) (h1 : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun (y : ‚Ñù) => (f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x :=
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).differentiableAt.comp x hf

theorem Complex.deriv_cpow_const (hx : x ‚àà Complex.slitPlane) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ü¶ x ^ c) x = c * x ^ (c - 1) :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const hx).hasDerivAt.deriv

/-- A version of `Complex.deriv_cpow_const` for a real variable. -/
theorem Complex.deriv_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) (hc : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    deriv (fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x = c * x ^ (c - 1) :=
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const hx hc).deriv

theorem deriv_cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hx : f x ‚àà Complex.slitPlane) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚ÑÇ) ‚Ü¶ f x ^ c) x = c * f x ^ (c - 1) * deriv f x :=
  (hf.hasDerivAt.cpow_const hx).deriv

theorem isTheta_deriv_ofReal_cpow_const_atTop {c : ‚ÑÇ} (hc : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    deriv (fun (x : ‚Ñù) => (x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) =Œò[atTop] fun x => x ^ (c.re - 1) := by
  calc
    _ =·∂Ý[atTop] fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ c * x ^ (c - 1) := by
      filter_upwards [eventually_ne_atTop 0] with x hx using by rw [deriv_ofReal_cpow_const hx hc]
    _ =Œò[atTop] fun x : ‚Ñù ‚Ü¶ ‚Äñ(x : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (c - 1)‚Äñ :=
      (Asymptotics.IsTheta.of_norm_eventuallyEq EventuallyEq.rfl).const_mul_left hc
    _ =·∂Ý[atTop] fun x ‚Ü¶ x ^ (c.re - 1) := by
      filter_upwards [eventually_gt_atTop 0] with x hx
      rw [norm_eq_abs, abs_cpow_eq_rpow_re_of_pos hx, sub_re, one_re]

import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Pow.Continuity
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Complex.LogDeriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.FDeriv.Extend
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Prod
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Log.Deriv
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Trigonometric.Deriv

/-!
# Derivatives of power function on `‚ÑÇ`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñù‚â•0`, and `‚Ñù‚â•0‚àû`

We also prove differentiability and provide derivatives for the power functions `x ^ y`.
-/


noncomputable section

open scoped Real Topology NNReal ENNReal
open Filter

namespace Complex

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p := by
  have A : p.1 ‚âÝ 0 := slitPlane_ne_zero hp
  have : (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2) =·∂Ý[ùìù p] fun x => exp (log x.1 * x.2) :=
    ((isOpen_ne.preimage continuous_fst).eventually_mem A).mono fun p hp =>
      cpow_def_of_ne_zero hp _
  rw [cpow_sub _ _ A, cpow_one, mul_div_left_comm, mul_smul, mul_smul]
  refine HasStrictFDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this.symm
  simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero A, div_eq_mul_inv, mul_smul, add_comm, smul_add] using
    ((hasStrictFDerivAt_fst.clog hp).mul hasStrictFDerivAt_snd).cexp

theorem hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow' {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (hp : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((y * x ^ (y - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (x ^ y * log x) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) (x, y) :=
  @hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow (x, y) hp

theorem hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow {x y : ‚ÑÇ} (h : x ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® y ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun y => x ^ y) (x ^ y * log x) y := by
  rcases em (x = 0) with (rfl | hx)
  ¬∑ replace h := h.neg_resolve_left rfl
    rw [log_zero, mul_zero]
    refine (hasStrictDerivAt_const y 0).congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_
    exact (isOpen_ne.eventually_mem h).mono fun y hy => (zero_cpow hy).symm
  ¬∑ simpa only [cpow_def_of_ne_zero hx, mul_one] using
      ((hasStrictDerivAt_id y).const_mul (log x)).cexp

theorem hasFDerivAt_cpow {p : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ} (hp : p.1 ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x : ‚ÑÇ √ó ‚ÑÇ => x.1 ^ x.2)
      ((p.2 * p.1 ^ (p.2 - 1)) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.fst ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ +
        (p.1 ^ p.2 * log p.1) ‚Ä¢ ContinuousLinearMap.snd ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ ‚ÑÇ) p :=
  (hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow hp).hasFDerivAt

end Complex

section fderiv

open Complex

variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [NormedSpace ‚ÑÇ E] {f g : E ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' : E ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ}
  {x : E} {s : Set E} {c : ‚ÑÇ}

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@hasStrictFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasStrictFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).comp_hasStrictFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasFDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') x := by
  convert (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivAt x hf

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasFDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ f' + (f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ g') s x := by
  convert
    (@Complex.hasFDerivAt_cpow ((fun x => (f x, g x)) x) h0).comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x (hf.prod hg)

theorem HasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasFDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasFDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) ((c ^ f x * Complex.log c) ‚Ä¢ f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasFDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (hg : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ g x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivAt h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableAt

theorem DifferentiableAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ f x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (hg : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ g s x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ g x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg.hasFDerivWithinAt h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => c ^ f x) s x :=
  (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.const_cpow h0).differentiableWithinAt

theorem DifferentiableWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ f s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableWithinAt ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s x :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableWithinAt_const c) h0

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s) (hg : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ g s)
    (h0 : Set.MapsTo f s slitPlane) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).cpow (hg x hx) (h0 hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.const_cpow (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚âÝ 0) : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) s :=
  fun x hx ‚Ü¶ (hf x hx).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x hx)

theorem DifferentiableOn.cpow_const (hf : DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ f s)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x ‚àà s, f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    DifferentiableOn ‚ÑÇ (fun x => f x ^ c) s :=
  hf.cpow (differentiableOn_const c) h0

theorem Differentiable.cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f) (hg : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ g)
    (h0 : ‚àÄ x, f x ‚àà slitPlane) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ f x ^ g x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).cpow (hg x) (h0 x)

theorem Differentiable.const_cpow (hf : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ f)
    (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® ‚àÄ x, f x ‚âÝ 0) : Differentiable ‚ÑÇ (fun x ‚Ü¶ c ^ f x) :=
  fun x ‚Ü¶ (hf x).const_cpow (h0.imp_right fun h ‚Ü¶ h x)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiable_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] :
    Differentiable ‚ÑÇ fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s :=
  differentiable_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

@[fun_prop]
lemma differentiableAt_const_cpow_of_neZero (z : ‚ÑÇ) [NeZero z] (t : ‚ÑÇ) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚ÑÇ (fun s : ‚ÑÇ ‚Ü¶ z ^ s) t :=
  differentiableAt_id.const_cpow (.inl <| NeZero.ne z)

end fderiv

section deriv

open Complex

variable {f g : ‚ÑÇ ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ} {s : Set ‚ÑÇ} {f' g' x c : ‚ÑÇ}

/-- A private lemma that rewrites the output of lemmas like `HasFDerivAt.cpow` to the form
expected by lemmas like `HasDerivAt.cpow`. -/
private theorem aux : ((g x * f x ^ (g x - 1)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight f' +
    (f x ^ g x * log (f x)) ‚Ä¢ (1 : ‚ÑÇ ‚ÜíL[‚ÑÇ] ‚ÑÇ).smulRight g') 1 =
      g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * log (f x) * g' := by
  simp only [Algebra.id.smul_eq_mul, one_mul, ContinuousLinearMap.one_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.smulRight_apply, ContinuousLinearMap.add_apply, Pi.smul_apply,
    ContinuousLinearMap.coe_smul']

nonrec theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasStrictDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa using (hf.cpow hg h0).hasStrictDerivAt

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x) (h : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h).comp x hf

theorem Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (h : x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun z : ‚ÑÇ => z ^ c) (c * x ^ (c - 1)) x := by
  simpa only [mul_zero, add_zero, mul_one] using
    (hasStrictDerivAt_id x).cpow (hasStrictDerivAt_const x c) h

theorem HasStrictDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasStrictDerivAt f f' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasStrictDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (hg : HasDerivAt g g' x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivAt

theorem HasDerivAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivAt f f' x) (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (hg : HasDerivWithinAt g g' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) : HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ g x)
      (g x * f x ^ (g x - 1) * f' + f x ^ g x * Complex.log (f x) * g') s x := by
  simpa only [aux] using (hf.hasFDerivWithinAt.cpow hg h0).hasDerivWithinAt

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.const_cpow (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x) (h0 : c ‚âÝ 0 ‚à® f x ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => c ^ f x) (c ^ f x * Complex.log c * f') s x :=
  (hasStrictDerivAt_const_cpow h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

theorem HasDerivWithinAt.cpow_const (hf : HasDerivWithinAt f f' s x)
    (h0 : f x ‚àà slitPlane) :
    HasDerivWithinAt (fun x => f x ^ c) (c * f x ^ (c - 1) * f') s x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const h0).hasDerivAt.comp_hasDerivWithinAt x hf

/-- Although `fun x => x ^ r` for fixed `r` is *not* complex-differentiable along the negative real
line, it is still real-differentiable, and the derivative is what one would formally expect.
See `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const` for an alternate formulation. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ -1) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1)) (x ^ r) x := by
  rw [Ne, ‚Üê add_eq_zero_iff_eq_neg, ‚Üê Ne] at hr
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hx.symm with (hx | hx)
  ¬∑ -- easy case : `0 < x`
    -- Porting note: proof used to be
    -- convert (((hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)).cpow_const _).div_const (r + 1)).comp_ofReal using 1
    -- ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel, id.def, mul_one, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel _ hr]
    -- ¬∑ rw [id.def, ofReal_re]; exact Or.inl hx
    apply HasDerivAt.comp_ofReal (e := fun y => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1))
    convert HasDerivAt.div_const (ùïú := ‚ÑÇ) ?_ (r + 1) using 1
    ¬∑ exact (mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr).symm
    ¬∑ convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
      ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, mul_comm]; exact (mul_one _).symm
      ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id (x : ‚ÑÇ)
      ¬∑ simp [hx]
  ¬∑ -- harder case : `x < 0`
    have : ‚àÄ·∂Ý y : ‚Ñù in ùìù x,
        (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) / (r + 1) = (-y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1) * exp (œÄ * I * (r + 1)) / (r + 1) := by
      refine Filter.eventually_of_mem (Iio_mem_nhds hx) fun y hy => ?_
      rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hy)]
    refine HasDerivAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq ?_ this
    rw [ofReal_cpow_of_nonpos (le_of_lt hx)]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (-‚Üëy) ^ (r + 1) * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * (r + 1)))
        ((r + 1) * (-‚Üëx) ^ r * exp (‚ÜëœÄ * I * r)) x by
      convert this.div_const (r + 1) using 1
      conv_rhs => rw [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_div_cancel_right‚ÇÄ _ hr]
    rw [mul_add ((œÄ : ‚ÑÇ) * _), mul_one, exp_add, exp_pi_mul_I, mul_comm (_ : ‚ÑÇ) (-1 : ‚ÑÇ),
      neg_one_mul]
    simp_rw [mul_neg, ‚Üê neg_mul, ‚Üê ofReal_neg]
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (‚Üë(-y) : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) (-(r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) x by
      convert this.neg.mul_const _ using 1; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) (-x) by
      convert @HasDerivAt.scomp ‚Ñù _ ‚ÑÇ _ _ x ‚Ñù _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ this (hasDerivAt_neg x) using 1
      rw [real_smul, ofReal_neg 1, ofReal_one]; ring
    suffices HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚ÑÇ => y ^ (r + 1)) ((r + 1) * ‚Üë(-x) ^ r) ‚Üë(-x) by
      exact this.comp_ofReal
    conv in ‚Üë_ ^ _ => rw [(by ring : r = r + 1 - 1)]
    convert HasDerivAt.cpow_const ?_ ?_ using 1
    ¬∑ rw [add_sub_cancel_right, add_sub_cancel_right]; exact (mul_one _).symm
    ¬∑ exact hasDerivAt_id ((-x : ‚Ñù) : ‚ÑÇ)
    ¬∑ simp [hx]

@[deprecated (since := "2024-12-15")] alias hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow := hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'

/-- An alternate formulation of `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const'`. -/
theorem hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const {x : ‚Ñù} (hx : x ‚âÝ 0) {r : ‚ÑÇ} (hr : r ‚âÝ 0) :
    HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ r) (r * x ^ (r - 1)) x := by
  have := HasDerivAt.const_mul r <| hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const' hx
    (by rwa [ne_eq, sub_eq_neg_self])
  simpa [sub_add_cancel, mul_div_cancel‚ÇÄ _ hr] using this
formal_statement:
/-- A version of `DifferentiableAt.cpow_const` for a real function. -/
theorem DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const {f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {x : ‚Ñù} (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x)
    (h0 : f x ‚âÝ 0) (h1 : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun (y : ‚Ñù) => (f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x
name: DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const
responses:
To complete the formalization, we use the previously defined `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const`. Given that `f` is differentiable at `x` as a function `‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù`, we can leverage the chain rule (via `HasDerivAt.comp`) to compute the derivative of `(f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c`. 

The function `(f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c` is the composition of the differentiable complex-valued function `z ‚Ü¶ z ^ c` and the differentiable real-valued function `f`. Since `f` is differentiable at `x`, the complex-valued function `f` is also differentiable at `x` (in the sense of `HasDerivAt` with scalar `‚Ñù`). Specifically, `HasDerivAt (fun y ‚Ü¶ (f y : ‚ÑÇ)) (f' : ‚ÑÇ) x`. Then, applying the chain rule with the complex power function derivative `c * (f x) ^ (c - 1)` completes the proof.

FINAL```
  := by
  let f_comp := (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf.hasDerivAt
  exact f_comp.differentiableAt
```

To complete the proof of `DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const`, we utilize the chain rule for real functions. Given a differentiable function `f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù` at `x` and a constant `c : ‚ÑÇ`, the composition `(f ¬∑)^c` is differentiable at `x` if `f(x) ‚âÝ 0` and `c ‚âÝ 0`, using the derivative formula provided by `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const`.

Specifically, since `hf` is `DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x`, we have `HasDerivAt f (deriv f x) x`. Composing this with the `HasDerivAt` property of the complex power function (which is differentiable at any non-zero real point), we obtain the result.

FINAL```
    := (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf.hasDerivAt |> HasDerivAt.differentiableAt
```

To complete the proof, we need to show that the composition of the complex-differentiable power function with the real-differentiable function `f` is real-differentiable. Since `f x ‚âÝ 0` and the power function $z \mapsto z^c$ is complex-differentiable on the slit plane (or specifically at any non-zero point), we can use the chain rule for derivatives.

The derivative is given by the chain rule: $\frac{d}{dx} (f(x)^c) = c \cdot f(x)^{c-1} \cdot f'(x)$, where $f'(x)$ is the derivative of the real function `f`. We express the derivative in terms of `HasDerivAt` and then apply the property of differentiability.

FINAL```
  := by
  apply HasDerivAt.differentiableAt
  have hf' : HasDerivAt (fun y : ‚Ñù => (f y : ‚ÑÇ)) (‚Üë(deriv f x)) x :=
    (hf.hasDerivAt).comp_ofReal
  have h_comp := HasDerivAt.cpow_const hf' (x := f x) (by
    rw [mem_slitPlane, ‚Üê Complex.ofReal_ne_zero, Complex.ofReal_re]
    exact h0)
  simp only [mul_assoc] at h_comp
  exact h_comp
```

theorem DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const {f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {x : ‚Ñù} (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x)
    (h0 : f x ‚âÝ 0) (h1 : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun (y : ‚Ñù) => (f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x :=
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf

To complete the provided proof for `DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const`, we rely on the previously proven `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const`. We apply the chain rule for derivatives, noting that if $f$ is differentiable at $x$ and $f(x) \neq 0$, the function $y \mapsto f(y)^c$ can be viewed as the composition of $f$ with the function $z \mapsto z^c$, which is differentiable at $f(x)$ for $f(x) \neq 0$.

FINAL```
    := (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf.hasDerivAt
```

theorem DifferentiableAt.ofReal_cpow_const {f : ‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚Ñù} {x : ‚Ñù} (hf : DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù f x)
    (h0 : f x ‚âÝ 0) (h1 : c ‚âÝ 0) :
    DifferentiableAt ‚Ñù (fun (y : ‚Ñù) => (f y : ‚ÑÇ) ^ c) x :=
  (Complex.hasStrictDerivAt_cpow_const (Complex.slitPlane_ne_zero_iff.2 h0)).hasDerivAt.comp x
    (hf.hasDerivAt.comp_ofReal)

This proof follows from applying the chain rule to the composition of the function $f$ and the power function $z \mapsto z^c$, utilizing the previously proven `hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const`. 

Specifically, since $f$ is differentiable at $x$ and $f(x) \neq 0$, the composition with the complex power function is differentiable by the chain rule for derivatives in the `‚Ñù ‚Üí ‚ÑÇ` setting.

FINAL```
  := (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf.hasDerivAt
```

The proof follows from applying the chain rule to the composition of the function $f$ and the power function $z \mapsto z^c$, utilizing the previously established differentiability of the complex power function and the provided hypotheses.

FINAL```
  (hasDerivAt_ofReal_cpow_const h0 h1).comp x hf
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Hom.Monoid
import Mathlib.Algebra.Order.Ring.Basic
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Maps
import Mathlib.Tactic.TFAE

/-!

# The basics of valuation theory.

The basic theory of valuations (non-archimedean norms) on a commutative ring,
following T. Wedhorn's unpublished notes ‚ÄúAdic Spaces‚Äù ([wedhorn_adic]).

The definition of a valuation we use here is Definition 1.22 of [wedhorn_adic].
A valuation on a ring `R` is a monoid homomorphism `v` to a linearly ordered
commutative monoid with zero, that in addition satisfies the following two axioms:
 * `v 0 = 0`
 * `‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y)`

`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`is the type of valuations `R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ`, with a coercion to the underlying
function. If `v` is a valuation from `R` to `Œì‚ÇÄ` then the induced group
homomorphism `Units(R) ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is called `unit_map v`.

The equivalence "relation" `IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Prop` defined in 1.27 of [wedhorn_adic] is not strictly
speaking a relation, because `v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÅ` and `v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÇ` might
not have the same type. This corresponds in ZFC to the set-theoretic difficulty
that the class of all valuations (as `Œì‚ÇÄ` varies) on a ring `R` is not a set.
The "relation" is however reflexive, symmetric and transitive in the obvious
sense. Note that we use 1.27(iii) of [wedhorn_adic] as the definition of equivalence.

## Main definitions

* `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of valuations on `R` with values in `Œì‚ÇÄ`
* `Valuation.IsEquiv`, the heterogeneous equivalence relation on valuations
* `Valuation.supp`, the support of a valuation

* `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ`, the type of additive valuations on `R` with values in a
  linearly ordered additive commutative group with a top element, `Œì‚ÇÄ`.

## Implementation Details

`AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` is implemented as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`.

## Notation

In the `DiscreteValuation` locale:

 * `‚Ñï‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñï)`
 * `‚Ñ§‚Çò‚ÇÄ` is a shorthand for `WithZero (Multiplicative ‚Ñ§)`

## TODO

If ever someone extends `Valuation`, we should fully comply to the `DFunLike` by migrating the
boilerplate lemmas to `ValuationClass`.
-/

open Function Ideal

noncomputable section

variable {K F R : Type*} [DivisionRing K]

section

variable (F R) (Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]

/-- The type of `Œì‚ÇÄ`-valued valuations on `R`.

When you extend this structure, make sure to extend `ValuationClass`. -/
structure Valuation extends R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max' : ‚àÄ x y, toFun (x + y) ‚â§ max (toFun x) (toFun y)

/-- `ValuationClass F Œ± Œ≤` states that `F` is a type of valuations.

You should also extend this typeclass when you extend `Valuation`. -/
class ValuationClass (F) (R Œì‚ÇÄ : outParam Type*) [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [Ring R]
  [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ]
  extends MonoidWithZeroHomClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ : Prop where
  /-- The valuation of a a sum is less that the sum of the valuations -/
  map_add_le_max (f : F) (x y : R) : f (x + y) ‚â§ max (f x) (f y)

export ValuationClass (map_add_le_max)

instance [FunLike F R Œì‚ÇÄ] [ValuationClass F R Œì‚ÇÄ] : CoeTC F (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :=
  ‚ü®fun f =>
    { toFun := f
      map_one' := map_one f
      map_zero' := map_zero f
      map_mul' := map_mul f
      map_add_le_max' := map_add_le_max f }‚ü©

end

namespace Valuation

variable {Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì'‚ÇÄ : Type*}
variable {Œì''‚ÇÄ : Type*} [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì''‚ÇÄ]

section Basic

variable [Ring R]

section Monoid

variable [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]

instance : FunLike (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  coe f := f.toFun
  coe_injective' f g h := by
    obtain ‚ü®‚ü®‚ü®_,_‚ü©, _‚ü©, _‚ü© := f
    congr

instance : ValuationClass (Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) R Œì‚ÇÄ where
  map_mul f := f.map_mul'
  map_one f := f.map_one'
  map_zero f := f.map_zero'
  map_add_le_max f := f.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem coe_mk (f : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) (h) : ‚áë(Valuation.mk f h) = f := rfl

theorem toFun_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : v.toFun = v := rfl

@[simp]
theorem toMonoidWithZeroHom_coe_eq_coe (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    (v.toMonoidWithZeroHom : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

@[ext]
theorem ext {v‚ÇÅ v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : ‚àÄ r, v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÇ r) : v‚ÇÅ = v‚ÇÇ :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ h

variable (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

@[simp, norm_cast]
theorem coe_coe : ‚áë(v : R ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì‚ÇÄ) = v := rfl

theorem map_zero : v 0 = 0 :=
  v.map_zero'

theorem map_one : v 1 = 1 :=
  v.map_one'

theorem map_mul : ‚àÄ x y, v (x * y) = v x * v y :=
  v.map_mul'

-- Porting note: LHS side simplified so created map_add'
theorem map_add : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  v.map_add_le_max'

@[simp]
theorem map_add' : ‚àÄ x y, v (x + y) ‚â§ v x ‚à® v (x + y) ‚â§ v y := by
  intro x y
  rw [‚Üê le_max_iff, ‚Üê ge_iff_le]
  apply map_add

theorem map_add_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x + y) ‚â§ g :=
  le_trans (v.map_add x y) <| max_le hx hy

theorem map_add_lt {x y g} (hx : v x < g) (hy : v y < g) : v (x + y) < g :=
  lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) <| max_lt hx hy

theorem map_sum_le {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) ‚â§ g) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) ‚â§ g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ zero_le')
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_le hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : g ‚âÝ 0)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g := by
  classical
  refine
    Finset.induction_on s (fun _ => v.map_zero ‚ñ∏ (zero_lt_iff.2 hg))
      (fun a s has ih hf => ?_) hf
  rw [Finset.forall_mem_insert] at hf; rw [Finset.sum_insert has]
  exact v.map_add_lt hf.1 (ih hf.2)

theorem map_sum_lt' {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hg : 0 < g)
    (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, v (f i) < g) : v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) < g :=
  v.map_sum_lt (ne_of_gt hg) hf

theorem map_pow : ‚àÄ (x) (n : ‚Ñï), v (x ^ n) = v x ^ n :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_pow

-- The following definition is not an instance, because we have more than one `v` on a given `R`.
-- In addition, type class inference would not be able to infer `v`.
/-- A valuation gives a preorder on the underlying ring. -/
def toPreorder : Preorder R :=
  Preorder.lift v

/-- If `v` is a valuation on a division ring then `v(x) = 0` iff `x = 0`. -/
theorem zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 0 ‚Üî x = 0 :=
  map_eq_zero v

theorem ne_zero_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 :=
  map_ne_zero v

lemma pos_iff [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : 0 < v x ‚Üî x ‚âÝ 0 := by
  rw [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem unit_map_eq (u : RÀ£) : (Units.map (v : R ‚Üí* Œì‚ÇÄ) u : Œì‚ÇÄ) = v u :=
  rfl

theorem ne_zero_of_unit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : KÀ£) : v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simp only [ne_eq, Valuation.zero_iff, Units.ne_zero x, not_false_iff]

theorem ne_zero_of_isUnit [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) (hx : IsUnit x) :
    v x ‚âÝ (0 : Œì‚ÇÄ) := by
  simpa [hx.choose_spec] using ne_zero_of_unit v hx.choose

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/
def comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation S Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  { v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.comp f.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := v ‚àò f
    map_add_le_max' := fun x y => by simp only [comp_apply, map_add, f.map_add] }

@[simp]
theorem comap_apply {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (s : S) :
    v.comap f s = v (f s) := rfl

@[simp]
theorem comap_id : v.comap (RingHom.id R) = v :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=
  ext fun _r => rfl

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`.
-/
def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ :=
  { MonoidWithZeroHom.comp f v.toMonoidWithZeroHom with
    toFun := f ‚àò v
    map_add_le_max' := fun r s =>
      calc
        f (v (r + s)) ‚â§ f (max (v r) (v s)) := hf (v.map_add r s)
        _ = max (f (v r)) (f (v s)) := hf.map_max
         }

@[simp]
lemma map_apply (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (r : R) :
    v.map f hf r = f (v r) := rfl

/-- Two valuations on `R` are defined to be equivalent if they induce the same preorder on `R`. -/
def IsEquiv (v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ r s, v‚ÇÅ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚â§ v‚ÇÇ s

@[simp]
theorem map_neg (x : R) : v (-x) = v x :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_neg x

theorem map_sub_swap (x y : R) : v (x - y) = v (y - x) :=
  v.toMonoidWithZeroHom.toMonoidHom.map_sub_swap x y

theorem map_sub (x y : R) : v (x - y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) :=
  calc
    v (x - y) = v (x + -y) := by rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
    _ ‚â§ max (v x) (v <| -y) := v.map_add _ _
    _ = max (v x) (v y) := by rw [map_neg]

theorem map_sub_le {x y g} (hx : v x ‚â§ g) (hy : v y ‚â§ g) : v (x - y) ‚â§ g := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg]
  exact v.map_add_le hx (le_trans (le_of_eq (v.map_neg y)) hy)

variable {x y : R}

theorem map_add_of_distinct_val (h : v x ‚âÝ v y) : v (x + y) = max (v x) (v y) := by
  suffices ¬¨v (x + y) < max (v x) (v y) from
    or_iff_not_imp_right.1 (le_iff_eq_or_lt.1 (v.map_add x y)) this
  intro h'
  wlog vyx : v y < v x generalizing x y
  ¬∑ refine this h.symm ?_ (h.lt_or_lt.resolve_right vyx)
    rwa [add_comm, max_comm]
  rw [max_eq_left_of_lt vyx] at h'
  apply lt_irrefl (v x)
  calc
    v x = v (x + y - y) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ max (v <| x + y) (v y) := map_sub _ _ _
    _ < v x := max_lt h' vyx

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x + y) = v y :=
  (v.map_add_of_distinct_val h.ne).trans (max_eq_right_iff.mpr h.le)

theorem map_add_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x + y) = v x := by
  rw [add_comm]; exact map_add_eq_of_lt_right _ h

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_right (h : v x < v y) : v (x - y) = v y := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_right, map_neg]
  rwa [map_neg]

open scoped Classical in
theorem map_sum_eq_of_lt {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {j : Œπ}
    (hj : j ‚àà s) (h0 : v (f j) ‚âÝ 0) (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s \ {j}, v (f i) < v (f j)) :
    v (‚àë i ‚àà s, f i) = v (f j) := by
  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton hj]
  exact map_add_eq_of_lt_left _ (map_sum_lt _ h0 hf)

theorem map_sub_eq_of_lt_left (h : v y < v x) : v (x - y) = v x := by
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg, map_add_eq_of_lt_left]
  rwa [map_neg]

theorem map_eq_of_sub_lt (h : v (y - x) < v x) : v y = v x := by
  have := Valuation.map_add_of_distinct_val v (ne_of_gt h).symm
  rw [max_eq_right (le_of_lt h)] at this
  simpa using this

theorem map_one_add_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one] at h
  simpa only [v.map_one] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

theorem map_one_sub_of_lt (h : v x < 1) : v (1 - x) = 1 := by
  rw [‚Üê v.map_one, ‚Üê v.map_neg] at h
  rw [sub_eq_add_neg 1 x]
  simpa only [v.map_one, v.map_neg] using v.map_add_eq_of_lt_left h

/-- An ordered monoid isomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces an equivalence
`Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ`. -/
def congr (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o Œì'‚ÇÄ) : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ where
  toFun := map f f.toOrderIso.monotone
  invFun := map f.symm f.toOrderIso.symm.monotone
  left_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp
  right_inv ŒΩ := by ext; simp

end Monoid

section Group

variable [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) {x y : R}

theorem map_inv {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x, v x‚Åª¬π = (v x)‚Åª¬π :=
  map_inv‚ÇÄ _

theorem map_div {R : Type*} [DivisionRing R] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) : ‚àÄ x y, v (x / y) = v x / v y :=
  map_div‚ÇÄ _

theorem one_lt_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 < v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  simp [inv_lt_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem one_le_val_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : 1 ‚â§ v x ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  simp [inv_le_one‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_lt_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x < 1 ‚Üî 1 < v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_lt_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_le_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} (h : x ‚âÝ 0) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî 1 ‚â§ v x‚Åª¬π := by
  simp [one_le_inv‚ÇÄ (v.pos_iff.2 h)]

theorem val_eq_one_iff (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) {x : K} : v x = 1 ‚Üî v x‚Åª¬π = 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [map_inv‚ÇÄ, inv_eq_one]
  ¬∑ simpa only [le_antisymm_iff, And.comm] using and_congr (one_le_val_iff v h) (val_le_one_iff v h)

theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π < 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, zero_lt_one, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_lt_val_iff v h, le_or_lt]

/--
This theorem is a weaker version of `Valuation.val_le_one_or_val_inv_lt_one`, but more symmetric
in `x` and `x‚Åª¬π`.
-/
theorem val_le_one_or_val_inv_le_one (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (x : K) : v x ‚â§ 1 ‚à® v x‚Åª¬π ‚â§ 1 := by
  by_cases h : x = 0
  ¬∑ simp only [h, _root_.map_zero, zero_le', inv_zero, or_self]
  ¬∑ simp only [‚Üê one_le_val_iff v h, le_total]

/-- The subgroup of elements whose valuation is less than a certain unit. -/
def ltAddSubgroup (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) (Œ≥ : Œì‚ÇÄÀ£) : AddSubgroup R where
  carrier := { x | v x < Œ≥ }
  zero_mem' := by simp
  add_mem' {x y} x_in y_in := lt_of_le_of_lt (v.map_add x y) (max_lt x_in y_in)
  neg_mem' x_in := by rwa [Set.mem_setOf, map_neg]

end Group

end Basic

-- end of section
namespace IsEquiv

variable [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
  {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÅ : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÇ : Valuation R Œì'‚ÇÄ} {v‚ÇÉ : Valuation R Œì''‚ÇÄ}

@[refl]
theorem refl : v.IsEquiv v := fun _ _ => Iff.refl _

@[symm]
theorem symm (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÅ := fun _ _ => Iff.symm (h _ _)

@[trans]
theorem trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ : v‚ÇÇ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ) : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÉ := fun _ _ =>
  Iff.trans (h‚ÇÅ‚ÇÇ _ _) (h‚ÇÇ‚ÇÉ _ _)

theorem of_eq {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (h : v = v') : v.IsEquiv v' := by subst h; rfl

theorem map {v' : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (hf : Monotone f) (inf : Injective f)
    (h : v.IsEquiv v') : (v.map f hf).IsEquiv (v'.map f hf) :=
  let H : StrictMono f := hf.strictMono_of_injective inf
  fun r s =>
  calc
    f (v r) ‚â§ f (v s) ‚Üî v r ‚â§ v s := by rw [H.le_iff_le]
    _ ‚Üî v' r ‚â§ v' s := h r s
    _ ‚Üî f (v' r) ‚â§ f (v' s) := by rw [H.le_iff_le]

/-- `comap` preserves equivalence. -/
theorem comap {S : Type*} [Ring S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) :
    (v‚ÇÅ.comap f).IsEquiv (v‚ÇÇ.comap f) := fun r s => h (f r) (f s)

theorem val_eq (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r s : R} : v‚ÇÅ r = v‚ÇÅ s ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r = v‚ÇÇ s := by
  simpa only [le_antisymm_iff] using and_congr (h r s) (h s r)

theorem ne_zero (h : v‚ÇÅ.IsEquiv v‚ÇÇ) {r : R} : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ 0 := by
  have : v‚ÇÅ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÅ 0 ‚Üî v‚ÇÇ r ‚âÝ v‚ÇÇ 0 := not_congr h.val_eq
  rwa [v‚ÇÅ.map_zero, v‚ÇÇ.map_zero] at this

end IsEquiv

-- end of namespace
section

theorem isEquiv_of_map_strictMono [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] [Ring R] {v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí*‚ÇÄ Œì'‚ÇÄ)
    (H : StrictMono f) : IsEquiv (v.map f H.monotone) v := fun _x _y =>
  ‚ü®H.le_iff_le.mp, fun h => H.monotone h‚ü©

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x y : K}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y := by
  simp only [IsEquiv, le_iff_le_iff_lt_iff_lt]
  exact forall_comm

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_iff_lt, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val

theorem isEquiv_of_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ}
    (h : ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1) : v.IsEquiv v' := by
  intro x y
  obtain rfl | hy := eq_or_ne y 0
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ rw [‚Üê div_le_one‚ÇÄ, ‚Üê v.map_div, h, v'.map_div, div_le_one‚ÇÄ] <;>
      rwa [zero_lt_iff, ne_zero_iff]

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_le_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1 :=
  ‚ü®fun h x => by simpa using h x 1, isEquiv_of_val_le_one‚ü©

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.le_one_iff_le_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simpa using @IsEquiv.val_eq _ _ _ _ _ _ v v' h x 1
  ¬∑ intro h
    apply isEquiv_of_val_le_one
    intro x
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v'.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'
    ¬∑ intro hx
      rcases lt_or_eq_of_le hx with hx' | hx'
      ¬∑ have : v' (1 + x) = 1 := by
          rw [‚Üê v'.map_one]
          apply map_add_eq_of_lt_left
          simpa
        rw [‚Üê h] at this
        rw [show x = -1 + (1 + x) by simp]
        refine le_trans (v.map_add _ _) ?_
        simp [this]
      ¬∑ rw [‚Üê h] at hx'
        exact le_of_eq hx'

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.eq_one_iff_eq_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1 := by
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro h x
    simp only [lt_iff_le_and_ne,
      and_congr h.le_one_iff_le_one h.eq_one_iff_eq_one.not]
  ¬∑ rw [isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one]
    intro h x
    by_cases hx : x = 0
    ¬∑ simp only [(zero_iff _).2 hx, zero_ne_one]
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.2 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
          rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
          exact hh.not_lt (h.2 ((one_lt_val_iff v' hx).1 h_2))
    ¬∑ intro hh
      by_contra h_1
      cases ne_iff_lt_or_gt.1 h_1 with
      | inl h_2 => simpa [hh, lt_self_iff_false] using h.1 h_2
      | inr h_2 =>
        rw [‚Üê inv_one, ‚Üê inv_eq_iff_eq_inv, ‚Üê map_inv‚ÇÄ] at hh
        exact hh.not_lt (h.1 ((one_lt_val_iff v hx).1 h_2))

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.lt_one_iff_lt_one, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]
    [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ] {v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ} {v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ} :
    v.IsEquiv v' ‚Üî ‚àÄ {x : K}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 := by
  rw [isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one]
  exact (Equiv.subRight 1).surjective.forall

alias ‚ü®IsEquiv.val_sub_one_lt_one_iff, _‚ü© := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one

theorem isEquiv_tfae [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] [LinearOrderedCommGroupWithZero Œì'‚ÇÄ]
    (v : Valuation K Œì‚ÇÄ) (v' : Valuation K Œì'‚ÇÄ) :
    [ v.IsEquiv v',
      ‚àÄ {x y}, v x < v y ‚Üî v' x < v' y,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x ‚â§ 1 ‚Üî v' x ‚â§ 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x = 1 ‚Üî v' x = 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v x < 1 ‚Üî v' x < 1,
      ‚àÄ {x}, v (x - 1) < 1 ‚Üî v' (x - 1) < 1 ].TFAE := by
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 2 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_val
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 3 := isEquiv_iff_val_le_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 4 := isEquiv_iff_val_eq_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 5 := isEquiv_iff_val_lt_one
  tfae_have 1 ‚Üî 6 := isEquiv_iff_val_sub_one_lt_one
  tfae_finish

end

section Supp

variable [CommRing R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

/-- The support of a valuation `v : R ‚Üí Œì‚ÇÄ` is the ideal of `R` where `v` vanishes. -/
def supp : Ideal R where
  carrier := { x | v x = 0 }
  zero_mem' := map_zero v
  add_mem' {x y} hx hy := le_zero_iff.mp <|
    calc
      v (x + y) ‚â§ max (v x) (v y) := v.map_add x y
      _ ‚â§ 0 := max_le (le_zero_iff.mpr hx) (le_zero_iff.mpr hy)
  smul_mem' c x hx :=
    calc
      v (c * x) = v c * v x := map_mul v c x
      _ = v c * 0 := congr_arg _ hx
      _ = 0 := mul_zero _

@[simp]
theorem mem_supp_iff (x : R) : x ‚àà supp v ‚Üî v x = 0 :=
  Iff.rfl

/-- The support of a valuation is a prime ideal. -/
instance [Nontrivial Œì‚ÇÄ] [NoZeroDivisors Œì‚ÇÄ] : Ideal.IsPrime (supp v) :=
  ‚ü®fun h =>
    one_ne_zero (Œ± := Œì‚ÇÄ) <|
      calc
        1 = v 1 := v.map_one.symm
        _ = 0 := by rw [‚Üê mem_supp_iff, h]; exact Submodule.mem_top,
   fun {x y} hxy => by
    simp only [mem_supp_iff] at hxy ‚ä¢
    rw [v.map_mul x y] at hxy
    exact eq_zero_or_eq_zero_of_mul_eq_zero hxy‚ü©

theorem map_add_supp (a : R) {s : R} (h : s ‚àà supp v) : v (a + s) = v a := by
  have aux : ‚àÄ a s, v s = 0 ‚Üí v (a + s) ‚â§ v a := by
    intro a' s' h'
    refine le_trans (v.map_add a' s') (max_le le_rfl ?_)
    simp [h']
  apply le_antisymm (aux a s h)
  calc
    v a = v (a + s + -s) := by simp
    _ ‚â§ v (a + s) := aux (a + s) (-s) (by rwa [‚Üê Ideal.neg_mem_iff] at h)

theorem comap_supp {S : Type*} [CommRing S] (f : S ‚Üí+* R) :
    supp (v.comap f) = Ideal.comap f v.supp :=
  Ideal.ext fun x => by rw [mem_supp_iff, Ideal.mem_comap, mem_supp_iff, comap_apply]

end Supp

-- end of section
end Valuation

  intro x y

theorem map_le_add {x y : R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hx : g ‚â§ v x) (hy : g ‚â§ v y) : g ‚â§ v (x + y) :=

theorem map_le_sum {Œπ : Type*} {s : Finset Œπ} {f : Œπ ‚Üí R} {g : Œì‚ÇÄ} (hf : ‚àÄ i ‚àà s, g ‚â§ v (f i)) :

/-- A ring homomorphism `S ‚Üí R` induces a map `AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí AddValuation S Œì‚ÇÄ`. -/

  Valuation.comap f v

theorem comap_comp {S‚ÇÅ : Type*} {S‚ÇÇ : Type*} [Ring S‚ÇÅ] [Ring S‚ÇÇ] (f : S‚ÇÅ ‚Üí+* S‚ÇÇ) (g : S‚ÇÇ ‚Üí+* R) :
    v.comap (g.comp f) = (v.comap g).comap f :=

/-- A `‚â§`-preserving, `‚ä§`-preserving group homomorphism `Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí Œì'‚ÇÄ` induces a map

def map (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí+ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (ht : f ‚ä§ = ‚ä§) (hf : Monotone f) (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :

      map_mul' := f.map_add

    v.map f ht hf r = f (v r) := rfl

theorem map {v' : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ} (f : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚Üí+ Œì'‚ÇÄ) (ht : f ‚ä§ = ‚ä§) (hf : Monotone f)

namespace Valuation

variable {K Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*} [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]

/-- The `AddValuation` associated to a `Valuation`. -/
def toAddValuation : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà :=
  .trans (congr
    { toFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ .ofAdd <| .toDual <| .toDual <| .ofMul x
      invFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ x.toAdd.ofDual.ofDual.toMul
      left_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      right_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_mul' := fun _x _y ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_le_map_iff' := .rfl }) (AddValuation.ofValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà))

/-- The `Valuation` associated to a `AddValuation`.
-/
def ofAddValuation : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  AddValuation.toValuation.trans <| congr <|
    { toFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ x.toAdd.ofDual.ofDual.toMul
      invFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ .ofAdd <| .toDual <| .toDual <| .ofMul x
      left_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      right_inv := fun _x ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_mul' := fun _x _y ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_le_map_iff' := .rfl }
formal_statement:
@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_symm_eq : ofAddValuation.symm = toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ)
name: Valuation.ofAddValuation_symm_eq
responses:
To prove that an additive valuation $v$ on a ring $R$ satisfies the property that the support $supp(v) = \{x \in R \mid v(x) = \top\}$ is an ideal, we observe the following:

1.  **Additive Valuations**: These are valuations into the multiplicative monoid with zero `(Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`. The additive valuation $v$ maps to a linearly ordered additive commutative monoid with a top element $\top$.
2.  **Support Definition**: The support is defined as $\{x \mid v(x) = \top\}$.
3.  **Ideal Axioms**:
    *   **Zero**: $v(0) = \top$ by definition of an additive valuation (coinciding with $v(0)=0$ in the multiplicative definition).
    *   **Addition**: If $v(x) = \top$ and $v(y) = \top$, then $\min(v(x), v(y)) = \top$. Since $\min(v(x), v(y)) \le v(x+y)$, we have $\top \le v(x+y)$, implying $v(x+y) = \top$.
    *   **Scalar Multiplication**: If $v(x) = \top$, then $v(c \cdot x) = v(c) + v(x) = v(c) + \top = \top$.

Since `AddValuation` is defined as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ·µí·µà)`, the existing proof for `Valuation.supp` carries over naturally.

FINAL```
namespace AddValuation

variable [CommRing R] [LinearOrderedAddCommMonoidWithTop Œì‚ÇÄ] (v : AddValuation R Œì‚ÇÄ)

def supp : Ideal R where
  carrier := { x | v x = ‚ä§ }
  zero_mem' := v.map_zero
  add_mem' {x y} hx hy :=
    le_top.antisymm <|
      calc
        v (x + y) ‚â• min (v x) (v y) := v.map_add x y
        _ = min ‚ä§ ‚ä§ := by rw [hx, hy]
        _ = ‚ä§ := min_top_left ‚ä§
  smul_mem' c x hx :=
    calc
      v (c * x) = v c + v x := v.map_mul c x
      _ = v c + ‚ä§ := by rw [hx]
      _ = ‚ä§ := add_top _

end AddValuation
```

To complete the formalization, we define the `toAddValuation` and `ofAddValuation` equivalences by establishing the isomorphism between the multiplicative monoid with zero `Œì‚ÇÄ` and the additive monoid with top `(Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`. 

The isomorphism is mediated by the conversion functions:
1. `Multiplicative.ofAdd` and `Multiplicative.toAdd` relate additive/multiplicative structures.
2. `OrderDual` handles the inversion of the order required for `Valuation` (where `v(0)=0`) and `AddValuation` (where `v(0)=‚ä§`).

The proof uses `Valuation.congr` to transport the valuation through these order isomorphisms.

FINAL```
def toAddValuation : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà :=
  let iso : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà := 
    { toFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ (OrderDual.toDual (Additive.ofMul x))
      invFun := fun x ‚Ü¶ Multiplicative.toMul (Additive.toAdd (OrderDual.ofDual x))
      left_inv := fun x ‚Ü¶ by simp
      right_inv := fun x ‚Ü¶ by simp
      map_mul' := fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ rfl
      map_le_map_iff' := fun _ _ ‚Ü¶ Iff.rfl }
  Valuation.congr iso

def ofAddValuation : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  (toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ)).symm
```

To complete the requested LEAN 4 formalization for the `Valuation` type, we finalize the missing `toAddValuation` and `ofAddValuation` isomorphisms and ensure the snippet correctly terminates.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_symm_eq : ofAddValuation.symm = toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toAddValuation_symm_eq : toAddValuation.symm = ofAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma toAddValuation_ofAddValuation (v : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà) :
    toAddValuation (ofAddValuation v) = v := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_toAddValuation (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    ofAddValuation (toAddValuation v) = v := rfl
```

To formalize the definition of an additive valuation as a `Valuation` mapping into the dual multiplicative group of a linearly ordered additive commutative monoid with a top element, we must ensure the `Valuation` structure is correctly instantiated.

The relationship between `AddValuation` and `Valuation` is defined such that an additive valuation $v: R \to \Gamma_0$ (where $\Gamma_0$ has a top element $\top$) corresponds to a multiplicative valuation $v': R \to \Gamma_0^{\text{mult}, \text{op}}$, where $v'(x) = \text{Multiplicative.ofAdd}(v(x))$. In Lean's library, this is handled via the `Multiplicative` and `OrderDual` types.

Given the existing library definitions, we define the missing isomorphism between `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` and `AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`.

FINAL```
namespace Valuation

variable {K Œì‚ÇÄ : Type*} [Ring R] [LinearOrderedCommMonoidWithZero Œì‚ÇÄ]

/-- The `AddValuation` associated to a `Valuation`. -/
def toAddValuation : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà :=
  let iso : Œì‚ÇÄ ‚âÉ*o (Multiplicative (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà)·µí·µà := 
    { toFun := fun x => (Multiplicative.ofAdd (Additive.ofAdd x)).toDual
      invFun := fun x => Additive.toAdd (Multiplicative.toAdd x.ofDual)
      left_inv := fun x => by simp
      right_inv := fun x => by simp
      map_mul' := fun x y => by simp [map_mul]
      map_le_map_iff' := fun x y => by simp }
  congr iso

/-- The `Valuation` associated to a `AddValuation`. -/
def ofAddValuation : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  (toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ)).symm

end Valuation
```

To complete the definition of `ofAddValuation` and `toAddValuation` and satisfy the request, we define the isomorphism between `Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ` and `AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`. 

The structure of `AddValuation` is defined as `Valuation R (Multiplicative Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà`. Therefore, an additive valuation taking values in `Additive Œì‚ÇÄ` is a valuation taking values in `(Multiplicative (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ))·µí·µà`. Since `Multiplicative (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ) ‚âÉ Œì‚ÇÄ`, this provides the equivalence.

FINAL```
def ofAddValuation : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà ‚âÉ Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ :=
  AddValuation.toValuation.trans <| congr <|
    { toFun := fun x => (Multiplicative.toAdd x).toDual.ofDual
      invFun := fun x => Multiplicative.ofAdd (OrderDual.toDual x)
      left_inv := fun x => by simp
      right_inv := fun x => by simp
      map_mul' := fun x y => by simp [map_mul]
      map_le_map_iff' := fun x y => by simp }
```

To complete the formalization of the equivalence between `Valuation` and `AddValuation`, we finalize the definitions of `toAddValuation` and `ofAddValuation`. The provided snippet confirms the bijection using the `Additive` and `Multiplicative` type synonyms to bridge the multiplicative and additive structures.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma toAddValuation_symm_eq : toAddValuation.symm = ofAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_toAddValuation (v : Valuation R Œì‚ÇÄ) :
    ofAddValuation (toAddValuation v) = v :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ (fun _ => rfl)

@[simp]
lemma toAddValuation_ofAddValuation (v : AddValuation R (Additive Œì‚ÇÄ)·µí·µà) :
    toAddValuation (ofAddValuation v) = v :=
  DFunLike.ext _ _ (fun _ => rfl)
```

To prove `ofAddValuation_symm_eq`, we rely on the definitions provided in the context. The `ofAddValuation` and `toAddValuation` functions are defined as compositions of equivalences that map valuations to additive valuations and back. By inspecting the definitions:

`toAddValuation` is defined as `(congr ...) ‚àò AddValuation.ofValuation.symm` (conceptually), and `ofAddValuation` is defined as `AddValuation.toValuation ‚àò (congr ...)`. Given that these are constructed as `Equiv` compositions, their symmetry `symm` is definitionally aligned with the inverse composition. In the provided snippet, the expression `ofAddValuation.symm = toAddValuation` is a standard consequence of how these equivalences are bundled.

FINAL```
@[simp]
lemma ofAddValuation_symm_eq : ofAddValuation.symm = toAddValuation (R := R) (Œì‚ÇÄ := Œì‚ÇÄ) := rfl
```

import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.AlgebraMap
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Eval.SMul
import Mathlib.Algebra.Polynomial.Roots
import Mathlib.Order.Interval.Set.Infinite
import Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.Pochhammer
import Mathlib.RingTheory.PowerSeries.WellKnown
import Mathlib.Tactic.FieldSimp

/-!
# Hilbert polynomials

In this file, we formalise the following statement: if `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, then
given any `p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï`, there exists some `h : F[X]` such that for any large enough
`n : ‚Ñï`, `h(n)` is equal to the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
This `h` is unique and is denoted as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.

For example, given `d : ‚Ñï`, the power series expansion of `1/(1 - X)·µà‚Å∫¬π` in `F[X]`
is `Œ£‚Çô ((d + n).choose d)X‚Åø`, which equals `Œ£‚Çô ((n + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(n + d)/d!)X‚Åø` and hence
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly (1 : F[X]) (d + 1)` is the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!`. Note that
if `d! = 0` in `F`, then the polynomial `(X + 1)¬∑¬∑¬∑(X + d)/d!` no longer works, so we do not want
the characteristic of `F` to be divisible by `d!`. As `Polynomial.hilbertPoly` may take any
`p : F[X]` and `d : ‚Ñï` as its inputs, it is necessary for us to assume that `CharZero F`.

## Main definitions

* `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`. Given a field `F`, a polynomial `p : F[X]` and a natural number `d`,
  if `F` is of characteristic `0`, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d : F[X]` is the polynomial whose
  value at `n` equals the coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.

## TODO

* Hilbert polynomials of finitely generated graded modules over Noetherian rings.
-/

open Nat PowerSeries

variable (F : Type*) [Field F]

namespace Polynomial

/--
For any field `F` and natural numbers `d` and `k`, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k`
is defined as `(d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - (C (k : F)) + 1))`.
This is the most basic form of Hilbert polynomials. `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly ‚Ñö d 0`
is exactly the Hilbert polynomial of the polynomial ring `‚Ñö[X_0,...,X_d]` viewed as
a graded module over itself. In fact, `Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k` is the
same as `Polynomial.hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1)` for any field `F` and
`d k : ‚Ñï` (see the lemma `Polynomial.hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ`). See also the lemma
`Polynomial.preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add`, which states that if `CharZero F`,
then for any `d k n : ‚Ñï` with `k ‚â§ n`, `(Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F)`
equals `(n - k + d).choose d`.
-/
noncomputable def preHilbertPoly (d k : ‚Ñï) : F[X] :=
  (d.factorial : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (Polynomial.X - (C (k : F)) + 1))

lemma natDegree_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).natDegree = d := by
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  rw [preHilbertPoly, natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne), natDegree_comp, ascPochhammer_natDegree,
    add_comm_sub, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, natDegree_add_C, natDegree_X, mul_one]

lemma coeff_preHilbertPoly_self [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).coeff d = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  delta preHilbertPoly
  have hne : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  have heq : d = ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C (k : F) + 1)).natDegree :=
    (natDegree_preHilbertPoly F d k).symm.trans (natDegree_smul _ (inv_ne_zero hne))
  nth_rw 3 [heq]
  calc
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π ‚Ä¢ ((ascPochhammer F d).comp (X - C ((k : F) - 1))).leadingCoeff := by
    simp only [sub_add, ‚Üê C_1, ‚Üê map_sub, coeff_smul, coeff_natDegree]
  _ = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
    simp only [leadingCoeff_comp (ne_of_eq_of_ne (natDegree_X_sub_C _) one_ne_zero), Monic.def.1
      (monic_ascPochhammer _ _), one_mul, leadingCoeff_X_sub_C, one_pow, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]

lemma leadingCoeff_preHilbertPoly [CharZero F] (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).leadingCoeff = (d ! : F)‚Åª¬π := by
  rw [leadingCoeff, natDegree_preHilbertPoly, coeff_preHilbertPoly_self]

lemma preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add [CharZero F] (d : ‚Ñï) {k n : ‚Ñï} (hkn : k ‚â§ n):
    (preHilbertPoly F d k).eval (n : F) = (n - k + d).choose d := by
  have : (d ! : F) ‚âÝ 0 := by norm_cast; positivity
  calc
  _ = (‚Üëd !)‚Åª¬π * eval (‚Üë(n - k + 1)) (ascPochhammer F d) := by simp [cast_sub hkn, preHilbertPoly]
  _ = (n - k + d).choose d := by
    rw [ascPochhammer_nat_eq_natCast_ascFactorial];
    field_simp [ascFactorial_eq_factorial_mul_choose]

variable {F}

/--
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p 0 = 0`; for any `d : ‚Ñï`, `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p (d + 1)`
is defined as `‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ Polynomial.preHilbertPoly F d i`. If
`M` is a graded module whose Poincar√© series can be written as `p(X)/(1 - X)·µà` for some
`p : ‚Ñö[X]` with integer coefficients, then `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is the Hilbert
polynomial of `M`. See also `Polynomial.coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval`,
which says that `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * PowerSeries.invOneSubPow F d)` equals
`(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) : (d : ‚Ñï) ‚Üí F[X]
  | 0 => 0
  | d + 1 => ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_left (d : ‚Ñï) : hilbertPoly (0 : F[X]) d = 0 := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only
  | succ d _ => simp only [coeff_zero, zero_smul, Finset.sum_const_zero]

lemma hilbertPoly_zero_right (p : F[X]) : hilbertPoly p 0 = 0 := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly p (d + 1) = ‚àë i ‚àà p.support, (p.coeff i) ‚Ä¢ preHilbertPoly F d i := rfl

lemma hilbertPoly_X_pow_succ (d k : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly ((X : F[X]) ^ k) (d + 1) = preHilbertPoly F d k := by
  delta hilbertPoly; simp

lemma hilbertPoly_add_left (p q : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p + q) d = hilbertPoly p d + hilbertPoly q d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [add_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only [‚Üê coeff_add]
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _]
      exact sum_add_index _ _ _ (fun _ => zero_smul ..) (fun _ _ _ => add_smul ..)

lemma hilbertPoly_smul (a : F) (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (a ‚Ä¢ p) d = a ‚Ä¢ hilbertPoly p d := by
  delta hilbertPoly
  induction d with
  | zero => simp only [smul_zero]
  | succ d _ =>
      simp only
      rw [‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, ‚Üê sum_def _ fun _ r => r ‚Ä¢ _, Polynomial.smul_sum,
        sum_smul_index' _ _ _ fun i => zero_smul F (preHilbertPoly F d i)]
      simp only [smul_assoc]

variable (F) in
/--
The function that sends any `p : F[X]` to `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is an `F`-linear map from
`F[X]` to `F[X]`.
-/
noncomputable def hilbertPoly_linearMap (d : ‚Ñï) : F[X] ‚Üí‚Çó[F] F[X] where
  toFun p := hilbertPoly p d
  map_add' p q := hilbertPoly_add_left p q d
  map_smul' r p := hilbertPoly_smul r p d

variable [CharZero F]

/--
The key property of Hilbert polynomials. If `F` is a field with characteristic `0`, `p : F[X]` and
`d : ‚Ñï`, then for any large enough `n : ‚Ñï`, `(Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F)` equals the
coefficient of `X‚Åø` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)·µà`.
-/
theorem coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval
    {p : F[X]} (d : ‚Ñï) {n : ‚Ñï} (hn : p.natDegree < n) :
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = (hilbertPoly p d).eval (n : F) := by
  delta hilbertPoly; induction d with
  | zero => simp only [invOneSubPow_zero, Units.val_one, mul_one, coeff_coe, eval_zero]
            exact coeff_eq_zero_of_natDegree_lt hn
  | succ d hd =>
      simp only [eval_finset_sum, eval_smul, smul_eq_mul]
      rw [‚Üê Finset.sum_coe_sort]
      have h_le (i : p.support) : (i : ‚Ñï) ‚â§ n :=
        le_trans (le_natDegree_of_ne_zero <| mem_support_iff.1 i.2) hn.le
      have h (i : p.support) : eval ‚Üën (preHilbertPoly F d ‚Üëi) = (n + d - ‚Üëi).choose d := by
        rw [preHilbertPoly_eq_choose_sub_add _ _ (h_le i), Nat.sub_add_comm (h_le i)]
      simp_rw [h]
      rw [Finset.sum_coe_sort _ (fun x => (p.coeff ‚Üëx) * (_ + d - ‚Üëx).choose _),
        PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk,
        invOneSubPow_val_eq_mk_sub_one_add_choose_of_pos _ _ (zero_lt_succ d)]
      simp only [coeff_coe, coeff_mk]
      symm
      refine Finset.sum_subset_zero_on_sdiff (fun s hs ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_) (fun x hx ‚Ü¶ ?_)
      ¬∑ rw [Finset.mem_range_succ_iff]
        exact h_le ‚ü®s, hs‚ü©
      ¬∑ simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, mem_support_iff, not_not] at hx
        rw [hx.2, zero_mul]
      ¬∑ rw [add_comm, Nat.add_sub_assoc (h_le ‚ü®x, hx‚ü©), succ_eq_add_one, add_tsub_cancel_right]

/--
The polynomial satisfying the key property of `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` is unique.
-/
theorem existsUnique_hilbertPoly (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    ‚àÉ! h : F[X], ‚àÉ N : ‚Ñï, ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F) := by
  use hilbertPoly p d; constructor
  ¬∑ use p.natDegree
    exact fun n => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d
  ¬∑ rintro h ‚ü®N, hhN‚ü©
    apply eq_of_infinite_eval_eq h (hilbertPoly p d)
    apply ((Set.Ioi_infinite (max N p.natDegree)).image cast_injective.injOn).mono
    rintro x ‚ü®n, hn, rfl‚ü©
    simp only [Set.mem_Ioi, sup_lt_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hn ‚ä¢

/--
If `h : F[X]` and there exists some `N : ‚Ñï` such that for any number `n : ‚Ñï` bigger than `N`
we have `PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)`, then `h` is exactly
`Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d`.
-/
theorem eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval
    {p h : F[X]} {d : ‚Ñï} (N : ‚Ñï) (hhN : ‚àÄ n > N,
    PowerSeries.coeff F n (p * invOneSubPow F d) = h.eval (n : F)) :
    h = hilbertPoly p d :=
  ExistsUnique.unique (existsUnique_hilbertPoly p d) ‚ü®N, hhN‚ü©
    ‚ü®p.natDegree, fun _ x => coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval d x‚ü©

lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ (p : F[X]) (d : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X)) (d + 1) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  apply eq_hilbertPoly_of_forall_coeff_eq_eval (p * (1 - X)).natDegree
  intro n hn
  have heq : 1 - PowerSeries.X = ((1 - X : F[X]) : F‚ü¶X‚üß) := by simp only [coe_sub, coe_one, coe_X]
  rw [‚Üê one_sub_pow_mul_invOneSubPow_val_add_eq_invOneSubPow_val F d 1, pow_one, ‚Üê mul_assoc, heq,
    ‚Üê coe_mul, coeff_mul_invOneSubPow_eq_hilbertPoly_eval (d + 1) hn]

lemma hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_pow_add (p : F[X]) (d e : ‚Ñï) :
    hilbertPoly (p * (1 - X) ^ e) (d + e) = hilbertPoly p d := by
  induction e with
  | zero => simp
  | succ e he => rw [pow_add, pow_one, ‚Üê mul_assoc, ‚Üê add_assoc, hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_succ, he]

lemma hilbertPoly_eq_zero_of_le_rootMultiplicity_one
    {p : F[X]} {d : ‚Ñï} (hdp : d ‚â§ p.rootMultiplicity 1) :
    hilbertPoly p d = 0 := by
  by_cases hp : p = 0
  ¬∑ rw [hp, hilbertPoly_zero_left]
  ¬∑ rcases exists_eq_pow_rootMultiplicity_mul_and_not_dvd p hp 1 with ‚ü®q, hq1, hq2‚ü©
    have heq : p = q * (- 1) ^ p.rootMultiplicity 1 * (1 - X) ^ p.rootMultiplicity 1 := by
      simp only [mul_assoc, ‚Üê mul_pow, neg_mul, one_mul, neg_sub]
      exact hq1.trans (mul_comm _ _)
    rw [heq, ‚Üê zero_add d, ‚Üê Nat.sub_add_cancel hdp, pow_add (1 - X), ‚Üê mul_assoc,
      hilbertPoly_mul_one_sub_pow_add, hilbertPoly]
